text: Retinäy debex mi yižo, retinäy q’ˤuyzix yegir.
translation: If you want, marry her yourself, or if you want, send her to someone else.

text: Esnazał xizaz ixiw raład boqno.
translation: And a big sea formed behind the sisters.

text: Neła žaqor beɣetow hibo teƛno.
translation: The eagle gave a small stick to the boy.

text: Bazarganbi guluzaxozo aħozider baynosi, nesiqayn hemedurtow bayan bisno, t’aza ħumukulibin roc’no ik’ix debełi sis uži c’oxinč’aƛin.
translation: When the merchants came to the shepherd with the horses, they wanted to take his information, if

text: Nesis ɣˤutku ʕaƛoz č’imäza zown.
translation: His house was located in the suburb of a village.

text: Bilq’isdis bercinłin rukayn, ečruni žek’us hič’č’a eženi užä nełqor eƛin, mi däx nexa yayƛin.
translation: When he saw Bilqisdi's beauty, the old man said to her: "You must come to me!"

text: Ža nesi žadäɣorƛin yik’iƛ’oräy, nełał hunix, oc’ira ƛˤeba adäztow nełqor roƛ’i boqno zawru, k’ačaɣˤ c’oxno.
translation: While going to this boy, she met a bandit on her way, who ten years before fell in love with her.

text: Hemedur žedu yałzay, sida bečedaw žek’a xabar bayrno bertenyołxor horoƛin.
translation: While they were like this [i.e., married], a rich man spread the word: 'Come to my marriage!'.

text: Bitazoni ɣˤutkarno bik’in bezunosi, elo yizir mesedyos q’ˤano saħ besun.
translation: After they went to look into the next room, they found 2 Sah (5 kg) of gold there.

text: Harizi boqno ža:
translation: The dragon begged:

text: Nesis zownƛax sistow sis uži.
translation: He had just one single son.

text: Maħor boqnosi, bara xediwq esirno, yedu q’urec’nibin, žedaƛ’ ƛ’irisi q’ayn łasey besanƛin.
translation: When they went outside, the wife asked her husband: "Whose might those mules and the wares on them be?"

text: Zirur ɣˤutkun, kosin zawru sadaq qˤičin, c’ik’iw maʕišatno ƛexun, paraxat zirun bičin.
translation: The house, the harvest that had been gathered and the entire fortune remained for the fox, and so the fox lived happily ever after.

text: Sasaq qˤašizaƛ’or yiłzaqay rok’łinč’inäy, aždaħä meži rac’ƛin.
translation: When you don't leave this place before dawn, a dragon will eat you."

text: Sidä his xuxix zown, sis ek’anił c’oxno, ɣoq’ˤot’aniza butnin, xaličabin rodin.
translation: One was combing the wool, one was hitting it to spin, the others made the burka and mats.

text: Sida quł aždaħ baq’ˤułno zawruni kikxar łäħor Zeyes Aħya ik’in.
translation: One day Bear Ear went to the spring the dragon was covering to get water.

text: Eženi esiya eɣeniq reƛ’a caƛix kumak nesir boyx zown, yedakin ok’ełxanaƛin.
translation: The older brother was helping giving his hand to the younger one, so at least he might escape.

text: Sida zamanaƛ’aza baɣmar mesedyos yic’äasi ɣamasno yayrno q’ˤano žek’u ayn.
translation: After a while two men appeared in the garden carrying the box filled with gold.

text: Nesis łˤono uži zown.
translation: He had three sons.

text: Yizi bok’äłrun, kidbä ƛ’iräy ħošin boɣno, bercinaw šeƛ’un šeƛ’un, bašiłgon bašiqˤoyn erno bertenyołxor yik’in.
translation: After they went away, the girl took off the hide, put on beautiful clothes and the ring on her finger and went to the marriage.

text: ʕOƛno ħonno xecin yizi bitaɣor bok’ełno.
translation: They went over seven mountains and escaped into the distance.

text: Neł zamanał bełiqanä eƛin, howła purqay ħalkiłiƛ’aɣor ok’łace sabru bodin bičiƛin.
translation: That time the hunter said: "Wait in patience until I go away to relax from that field."

text: Bišan ħadur rodin ičäsi yałzay, mamalayƛ’no zown, ruk’ice mešok’la yołäsi sis t’omce žek’u oƛixno k’oƛin salam teƛno, assalamu ʕalaykumƛin.
translation: While he was preparing the food, a man in the size of a span with a moustache in the length of one cubit came riding on a rooster, jumped off and greeted: "Peace be with you!"

text: Howži nesi žek’a ža uži debez mi hunix ik’iƛin egirno.
translation: Now the man let the boy go: "Go on your way!"

text: Mi saɣ yayruni kid däz žax yaqˤuł yowxƛin, xanä madał adäzar boɣno berten.
translation: "My son marry today that girl, you are cured," and khan moved the wedding into the yard.

text: Acno yaɣˤin babiw iduɣor oqƛ’oräy, bic’äsi t’as maħor čaƛˤanix bisno yik’ix dandir kid c’oxno.
translation: Opening the door, the father got home, as he met his daughter carrying a full washbowl to pour it out outside.

text: Hudun ži ɣˤuruš neƛnäy sisgon bodan deber di ʕaq’luƛin razi oqno eženi.
translation: "Nevertheless, if you give me a ruble, I'll give you another advice.", agreed the old man.

text: Ɣˤutkun, ƛen rodin łiyzaƛ’, žoyä eniw k’ox äƛirugon kidno esirno xander yegirno.
translation: When the house and the bridge were finished, the boy sent his mother to the king a second time to ask for the girl.

text: Howt’or bayza imadun łiynƛax.
translation: And the tale also ends where it came to, here.

text: Ɣˤaɣˤun izin nesä ža butni bazaryay bisno bižin.
translation: He got happy and bought the burka from the market.

text: Sosi yisä ħumukulis bet’erhanno goƛ’in nesis murad esirnosi, nesä eƛin:
translation: At first he called the owner of the camel and asked what he desired, the owner said:

text: Xanes ħillan čuqˤno, kidbä ʕansaqor, bišodyołäysi zahru bok’ek’ƛinno eƛin, xizyo bercingo bišno.
translation: Noticing the king's trickery, and the daughter told the walking stick to remove the poison in the food, whereafter they ate well.

text: Neširu mežaɣor bizinosi, Ismaʕila q’ˤunełno roƛƛo bˤałayn erno eƛin nełqor, howłaq ged yitirłi debi meč’, dey bˤałayƛin.
translation: In the evening, when they got onto the bed, Ismail put a dagger in the middle between the two and said to her: "If your shirt touches it, so will my dagger [touch] your neck."

text: Nełaƛ’ yeda razin oqno, kumakyorgon ža ɣˤanabin yižin, žedu łˤoran ƛˤeba sadaq beynoyn.
translation: He agreed with it, took the woman as an aid and they worked together during three years.

text: Nełƛ’ay soder didiwnkin mišayat boyx zownanu neł ʕaƛoz žamaʕatyor, ya posu t’ok’ow narnokin rok’ełnoanu, ya räƛiru qˤiči łukin rexurnoanu.
translation: After that no obstacle occured to the village's population anymore; neither did any cattle escape, nor did anyone kill the planted harvest.

text: ʕAnsa sadaq xanes askar bexurnosi dandi xan harizi oqno, dey sadaq xanłin, kidno deber yiča yoł, mi dey askar č’agu bodinäyƛin.
translation: After the walking stick had called the king's entire army, the king now requested: "My entire kingdom and my daughter will be yours, if you make my army alive again!"

text: Ža gagirun, suƛi gula neł iħay ɣˤay ħaƛurun, gulun, užintown ɣˤuler butix.
translation: Right after it had escaped, the horse suddenly drank the milk from that river and the horse and the boy turned to stone.

text: Hag oqno ƛexun ečruni žek’u.
translation: The old man was amazed.

text: Xedya eƛin, wey dey baru, beroz gup’rezaƛ’or batiru xalq’i č’agu odoƛin.
translation: The husband said: "Hey, my wife, revive the people who turned into icy hills."

text: Neširu xanä elo nasin sidaɣor butirnosi, xanä eƛin, di łi ħaƛuxosi t’akan sasaqor howt’or yayäru žek’ux kid yegira yołƛin.
translation: In the evening the king gather everyone together and said: "I will marry my daughter off to the man who can bring my water-drinking-glass here tomorrow!"

text: Ox̌oyoƛin łu eƛäƛin, esirno yisi žek’a yiziq.
translation: "Who said Ohoyo?", that man asked him.

text: Nełasgon łinasnokin hunar anusi kid zownƛax.
translation: She had a daughter with no talent whatsoever.

text: Užä eƛin, anƛ’imarsi där hunixor qˤašo ero, mesedyos bašiqˤoy yałruni kid k’eda di ik’ix.
translation: The boy answered: "Pack provisions for me for a way of one week, I'm going to search the girl with the golden ring."

text: Debi iš anu žaƛin, bežet’ani esnabi bixzin boqno xabaryayn yisiqor rok’ƛ’osi murad t’ubazi boqinč’izaƛ’.
translation: "That's not your business.", said the older brothers angrily because their hearts' desire was not fulfilled.

text: Yisä eƛin, mi barułun yižanix debeł xizay nexäsi yoł diƛin.
translation: He said: "I came to you in order to take you as a wife."

text: Pat’imatä esirno, łinałäy mežus daʕbaƛin.
translation: Fatima asked: "What is your dispute about?"

text: Ħumukuliƛ’ kut’yoƛ’aza puƛ’aza nucos sik zowwäƛin?”
translation: "Was there a waterskin with honey on the camel's right side?"

text: Hudun žeda diduräyƛa gulun k’irik’no, ƛoħo rutirno caƛin.
translation: Nevertheless they frightened the horse somehow and it turned the pan and threw it to them.

text: Maħix micxir t’aħiruni ɣˤʷay neƛƛin eƛo mi nesiqor.
translation: Say to him: 'Give me the dog with money tied to its tail!'"

text: Zira äƛiru łinaƛ’no nasin razi roqno.
translation: All agreed on what the fox said.

text: Sida idu q’ˤuna xaner moči boqxosi anu.
translation: In one home there is no place for two kings.

text: Ozzałer č’uran rayrerno, yiła xediw howlotow ičirno.
translation: She brought the light back to his eyes but let her husband wait there.

text: Esin šebi, xecin šebi zownƛax zown anuƛax sis žek’u.
translation: Once upon a time there was a man.

text: Nełde idur baynosi, šakno oqno eɣeni esiya kidbeq ħaƛanix łi esiro.
translation: When they came to the house where she was, the youngest brother became suspicious and asked the girl if she wanted to drink some water.

text: Zamanałäy čanyan, cek’bintow ħalaq’ roqa roqno.
translation: After a while, the she.goat and the goat kids began to become meager.

text: Nedur resunäy bik’i huduƛin” - eƛin zeyä.
translation: "If that's the case, then go!", said the bear.

text: Ist’olowä äsuruni q’ebedzo užä eƛin, ɣudes ʕazart’a ɣuruš yiqixozo mečoƛ’or mi di egiranƛin.
translation: The smith's son said to the one he found in the dining room: "I'll send you to a place where you can get thousand rubles a day."

text: Hemełƛ’ay soder bˤeƛ’ ocät’a ričirxo zown.
translation: Afterwards he brought the sheep to the threshing floor.

text: Ħusen eženis ɣˤutkoxor ayn.
translation: Hussein came to the old man's house.

text: Howžigoƛ’aygon c’odorawnä esirno dibirqo, debez baruq šilabi zowwäƛin.
translation: This time the clever one asked the mullah: "Did your wife have horns?"

text: Haq’uł q’ˤano buci boɣnosi, eniya daʕba bičirno yiłał, debez mi ɣˤutkoƛ’or yuti, xedyor rätinč’iƛin.
translation: After spending two months at her family's, the mother had up a dispute with her: "Go back to your house and your husband won't like it."

text: Nedur resunäy, hi enda dowqor sis dey musno, debeƛ’ ɣudi rayza quł yedu mi bik’urƛin nesiqorno eƛin, zeyä ciqaɣor zebu yoɣno.
translation: "In that case, take this hair of me and when you're in big trouble one day, burn it!" it said to him and ran off to the forest.

text: Neła quł neširu ža äčiza ɣutkaɣorgon baħarayn yižin.
translation: That evening, he lead his fiancée to the house were he lived.

text: Nedur resunäyƛin, eƛin nesi žek’a, sis žek’u egir debez bˤeƛ’łäy biƛurox aluk’ani bˤeƛ’q’ˤu bowanix.
translation: "If that's the case," said the man, "send a man to bring the sheep from your flock which is white on the belly!"

text: Ža kaɣat t’et’erno łiynosi, łˤoran xexza nesiqor eƛin, eli łˤonon bik’a yoł debez halmaɣ Tawadidäɣor.
translation: When he finished reading the letter, his three children said to him: "We three will go to your friend Tavadi.

text: Sida quł bet’erhanno bet’erhanes užin ƛˤoreł bik’inosi, yiła neł ʕaƛos dibir iduɣor goƛ’in.
translation: One day, when the master and the master's son went to war, she invited the village's imam to their home.

text: Ik’in, ik’in babiwƛ’or kaɣat yegirno zawruni ečruni žek’uder ayn.
translation: He went and went and came to the old man who had sent the letter to his father.

text: Eniya äƛiru rožbin rok’er ʕeze roqinč’ey, eženi užä eniwq q’ˤano magalun hunixor ererno, iday maħor oqno.
translation: The words that the mother had spoken didn't please his heart enough, so the oldest son let the mother give two breads to him for the way and left the home.

text: Yizi esnazaqor Ismaʕilä eƛin, howži mežin xizoxno sisxoli di žedude dandir egirƛin.
translation: Ismail said to those brothers: "Now withdraw and let me go against them alone!"

text: Tełäzay teqxo där, šida mi dey kid yaqˤuł nece kʷat’izi yoqäƛin, insanzo mecrexor eƛix.
translation: I was hearing from inside: "Why are you, my daughter, so late today?", said in a human voice.

text: Žedus baqru nasin xabar teqno žedu q’ˤunan nukarzar - režet’ani esnazas halmaɣzar.
translation: Their whole discussion was overheard by those two henchmen - the friends of the older sisters.

text: Dey xexbi, di exun xizyo t’ubazi odin łiynosi, meži łˤonon esnabi bitazoni ɣˤutkar bezu, elo mežur ʕuraw mesed yołƛin”.
translation: "My children, when I have died and the funeral is done, you three brothers look into the next room, there is much gold for you."

text: Howlo lilyoxtow yedan xecin, xanderno bik’in ziru c’ok’inayn:
translation: When they left the river bank there and went to the king, the fox scolded:

text: Sida ħiƛ’oquqno rutirno xanƛ’or regirno, ele enda ħaramaw reƛ yołƛin.
translation: They gathered it in one handkerchief and sent it to the king: "There is the prohibited meat."

text: Yiła maq’anyabi qˤaƛixanu, ust’arder yedu yayrƛin yiła ɣarabašzaqor eƛin.
translation: "It doesn't play melodies anymore, bring it to the master!", she said to the servants.

text: Roƛik’u xanłin teƛno, xanä bisno ža.
translation: The king gave him half the kingdom and bought the bird.

text: Aħozä c’aq’ xal bodin zown, nar ža čanya gugixƛin.
translation: The shepherd examined closely, where this goat escaped to.

text: Nesä neła bełis łˤono but’a yodix zown, sis but’a awarager, sis miskinzar, sideni q’ˤimä q’ˤimzo haq’ur iduɣor yowx zown.
translation: He always made three parts of what he hunted, one part he gave to the prophet, one to the poor, and the other part he brought to his own family's home.

text: Kʷaxatow bertenno boyn yedu kid nesi žoyä yižin.
translation: Soon they celebrated their wedding and the boy took the girl with him.

text: Yisä kinaw šebinon esin, q’ˤunan esnazar gulubi sayɣat rodin.
translation: He told everything and gave his two brothers horses as a present.

text: Mi nedurä hudu zownƛin, eƛin Ražbadinä, nedur resunäy, ela sosi debi berten boda yoł, xizyo di baru yowa yołƛin.
translation: "So that way you are," said Razhbadin, "if that's the case, we will first prepare your wedding and afterwards I will marry my wife."

text: Aždaħägon risno yałzatown erno.
translation: But the dragon took it and put it back where it was.

text: Xizyogon zira šebi ža debez ħiroƛ’ƛin esirnosi, k’et’a tupi yołƛin eƛin.
translation: When the fox asked: "What is that on your shoulder?", the cat said: "It's a rifle."

text: Bet’erhanes kidno ħamimyołxor yegirno, nełałtow xizaygon dibirno egirno, micxir neƛanƛin.
translation: She sent the master's daughter to the hot springs and sent the imam behind her, "I'll give you money."

text: Kidbägon elayn bisno neła nełaz pardagix bicin.
translation: The girl then went and took it and tied it to her own scarf.

text: K’uruč’, k’eč’ ɣudi rocu buq bay.
translation: All's well that ends well.

text: Howži ža ɣamasno hokomƛ’o erno, eɣeni esiw xanes kid yałruni qalamxaɣor ik’in.
translation: Now they put the box onto a carriage and the young brother went to the fortress where the king's daughter was.

text: Siryos bet’erhanä q’ʷaridno oqno sadaq šebin ik’in ec’noni xanzo baruq esin.
translation: The mare's master became sad and told the king's new wife how all the things happened.

text: Yisä eƛin, dey t’o ƛirba Ražbadin yołƛin.
translation: He said: "My comrade here is Razhbadin."

text: Xizyo xan qˤaƛanada oqno, eleley, yedu didiw karamałi, debez buɣa däz buɣaz axʷay mo yac’xoƛin.
translation: Then the king began to yell: "Behold, what miracle is that, your bull is eating the fat out of my bull's belly!"

text: Ac ħišäsi yesuzaƛ’, ƛ’ˤuƛ’orno bizin, gondun yoɣno, tełerno boqno, łenon užin igin kurno.
translation: Because it found the door locked, it got onto the roof, made a hole, got inside and gobbled the five sons up.

text: Elo ʕomoyä bayäruni bexasi boc’i bukaynosi, nasin boc’a elin rexar yizäƛin, aɣˤʷa tełäy roxin.
translation: When they saw the dead wolf that the donkey had brought, all the wolves said: "They will kill us!", ran out of the mill and away.

text: Idur baynosi, yiła nesiq žeduƛ’osi qaca ret’urerno, xizyogon łäxor egirno, didiw zawru, didiw mežus di uži odäƛin.
translation: Having come home, she let him hack the firewood, sent him to get water [and said]: "How was your son and what have I made out of him?"

text: Yiłagon q’ˤanon xanes xexbi - užin, kidno k’emot’ya tełno eqerno, iħułxor kurno.
translation: But those two children of the king - the son and the daughter - she put into a wooden case and threw them into the river.

text: Ħumukulin bisno yizä, tełäy axorekasin roɣno, nełaz axä tełxor q’ebedes uži egirno.
translation: They bought a camel, removed its giblets and let the smith's son inside its belly.

text: Zownč’uƛin neła ot’ bisno, esan retinč’ey.
translation: "No, you didn't.", she rejected and didn't want to tell it.

text: Žedaxay bitor di q’ˤida ičinč’ey bixno kosix zows.
translation: Because of them, I didn't sit down but mowed grass.

text: Užä ža pardagi eniwł adäza ret’irnosi, riguƛ’ay rigu biša roƛixno elo ƛ’iri.
translation: The son laid down the scarf before his mother and a lot of good food appeared on it.

text: Xizyo yeda neła šaharyoz xandäɣor ik’in.
translation: Afterwards he went to that city's king.

text: Babiw exun q’ˤanoqu ɣudi rik’inosi, idu sida quł rac’ace at’no ƛexasi resun, rečuƛgon sis miƛ’in besun.
translation: When the father had been dead for forty days, at home only remained wheat to eat for one day and one ram in the shed.

text: Exunosi alžan deber biqiƛƛin, nesir bara saɣłin rodin, encetow qacan rižin, kʷaxatow iduɣor yik’in.
translation: May you get the paradise, when you die!", the wife wished him health, picked up some firewood and quickly went home.

text: Ik’in k’oƛin nukarä bełiquz žaqor eƛin, mi xanä goƛ’ixƛin.
translation: The henchman ran off and told the hunter's son, "The king invites you."

text: Žeda q’orolaq harizi rodin, yizi xexbi mi kikoƛin.
translation: They asked the widow: "Please feed these children!"

text: ʕAƛar xabar bayn, hunix zigolun yegirno, raq’ˤu bˤeƛ’ rexunƛin.
translation: Into the village came the news that on the way it had hailed and many sheep had died.

text: Xizyo gulučinin bišno.
translation: Afterwards the male one ate as well.

text: Baq’ˤu mix bik’izaƛ’ortow, eɣeni esiwr teqxo xabar, yisiz eženi esiws cudi nukarza c’unzi bodix sida qunyox yołƛin.
translation: Before much time has passed, the younger brother was hearing the story that his older brother's corpse was at a farmstead being guarded by henchman.

text: Ik’in nełaq reƛ’iqoy rukarnosi, didiw ža yałru sosisi riynč’ey neła eƛin:
translation: He went and showed her the glove, and at first, when she didn't know what kind of glove this was, she said:

text: Neła nesiqno esirno, deber heresi esa kaƛ’iyä kaƛ’inč’aƛin.
translation: She asked him as well: "Can you tell a lie or not?"

text: Xizor utin Pat’imatno yižin nesä nesiz ʕaƛaɣorno ik’in, mi däƛ’ay ʕaq’ilawn, c’odorawn zownƛin xanłin nełaqor teƛno žan paraxat ičin.
translation: He returned with Fatima into his own village: "You are more intelligent and cleverer than me!", gave her the kingdom and lived calmly ever after.

text: Aluk’ani biqirnäy, neła mi ƛ’irizoni raƛ’moƛ’or ayär, qˤabani biqirnäy waħazoni raƛ’moƛ’or ayärƛin, - eƛi- mołin egirno yizä yeda.
translation: When you catch the white one, it will bring you to the land above; if you catch the black one, it will bring you to the land below", they taught him and let him go.

text: Nesiztow bˤeƛ’eł yałru biƛurox aluk’ani bˤeƛ’q’ˤus reƛ rac’näy, ža xan saɣ oqxosi yałrułin xalq’imor riyxanu.
translation: When he eats the meat of a sheep from his flock, which is white on its belly, this king will be healed; but the people don't know this.

text: Žigoƛ’aygon xanes yeženi kidno yok’ek’no, xizor utin idur bežet’ani esnazaƛ’ay ader ayn.
translation: And now he stole the king's oldest daughter and returned home before his older brothers.

text: Zirun, k’et’un yołi boc’än, zeyän bayäru dawlaƛ’ žin yołƛax.
translation: And now the fox and the cat went to the prey that the wolf and the bear have brought that day.

text: Neširur mamalay howožatow at’iw qacan rayrno xizor butin baynosi, onoča esirno:
translation: At night, when the rooster came back bringing this wet firewood again, the hen asked:

text: Gulun howlo xecin, nełaz maħiłäy baɣruni łˤono musno butirno k’ukya tełxor eqerno, babiwz šudaƛ’ q’ulhun begirno, uži iduɣor ik’in.
translation: The horse left and the boy gathered the three hairs he took out of its tail and put them inside his cap, then he spoke the funeral prayer on his father's grave and went home.

text: Babiya eƛin, wallah, howda uži ʕarab ʕilmu t’et’ranix ižixƛin.
translation: The father said: "By God, I am leading my son here to learn the Arabic science."

text: Neła quł sasaqtow moƛaxayn bizin, ixinno risno, q’ˤanon gagalazał xizay ħonƛ’aɣor boqno.
translation: The next morning, the two of them got up from their sleep, took a back and went to the mountain for flowers.

text: Ħayran boqno yizä sidä sidqor eƛin, eloš nesi žek’uł adäza anusi šebinonwa anu, hudun nesiq łinaƛ’orkin reƛ’a regirełxanuwaƛin.
translation: They were surprised and talked among each other: "Isn't there everything possible on the table, but he nevertheless can't let his hand to any of it?"

text: Sis nełarno kurno siskin där ƛexandaƛin, aɣzo rok’ƛ’orno rayn, dicego rok’er zaħmat roqłin, zirur sis xexoy kurno.
translation: "Throwing one down, there will still remain one for me.", thought the bird and although it was difficult, it threw one nestling to the fox.

text: T’ok’ow bäxuru onočas xexoycekin elay iziłno anu.
translation: He couldn't get up anymore, just like the little chicks of a dead hen.

text: Q’ˤano äƛiru ɣudƛ’ogon zap’aniłnotow bayn zira eƛin, kurxo besunäy xexoy kuro, yałi di maħin rok’no ɣun recura yołƛin.
translation: On the second day, the fox came firmly and said: "Throw me down a nestling, or else I'll knock down the tree with my tail!"

text: Barus yołi kid łinasnotow hunarno, maxšelno, bažarin anusi zown.
translation: The wife's daughter however had no talent or skill whatsoever.

text: Beynoda retinäy däde mežur ħalt’in yoł, deytow iłno kidno yoł mežux regiranixƛin, eƛin yiła yizirabi iduɣor bižin.
translation: If you want to work, there's work for you at my place, and also my six daughters to be sent to you." she said and let them into her home.

text: Sida quł neła kidbes eniw qˤišoder sis mučarin rižin ħonƛ’aɣor yik’ix zown.
translation: One day that girl's mother went to the mountain with a cumin cake.

text: Nełquł sasaq č’ˤurqotow bizin onoču qaciƛ’or bik’in.
translation: The next day, in the early morning, the hen got up and went after firewood.

text: Xizyo yeda xizor utin babiwdäɣor nexno.
translation: Then he came back to his father.

text: Ža xabar yisä aħyaxorkin begirnoanu, baru yowač’inƛin di hedizi oqäsiƛin, t’ok’ow ža xabar meža haqazay maħor baɣnoƛin.
translation: These words he didn't let to his ears, "I won't marry another wife!"... "I swear!"... "Don't say that anymore!",

text: Sasaqtow izin žoyä ros bič’anix bašla bodin.
translation: In the morning the boy got up and began to dig the base.

text: Sis gut’ riłe šebin rukayxƛin, eƛin bełiqanä.
translation: "I see something like smoke.", said the hunter.

text: Elay mekoyx yałru yisä bet’urno neł ɣunoqay c’udani heneš bac’nosi, nesiłäy ɣun roqno.
translation: Then, he - being hungry - plucked a red apple from that tree and ate it, he became a tree.

text: Nasin yizi xan yałru mečoƛ’or bayn.
translation: They all came to the place that belonged to the king.

text: Žedaq di łˤora ɣudir ħonce qaca ʕaƛaɣor riħurno łiyrsi.
translation: I made them pull a mountain of firewood to the village for three days.

text: Užä izin, miƛ’in bexayn, ƛäxuruni at’es magalun boyn.
translation: The son got up, slaughtered the ram and made a bread from the remaining wheat.

text: T’omceni žek’us bełiqaner nasin ric’q’in ƛexun, barułungon nesis kidno yown, maʕišat bodix howlotow žan ičin.
translation: All the span-sized man's wealth remained for the hunter, he married his daughter, and lived there making a great fortune.

text: Howži ža uži iduɣor ik’in.
translation: Now the boy went home.

text: Elo yizir qazaqzas bˤeƛ’es reqenyabi resux.
translation: There they found herds of sheep from the Georgians.

text: Ik’in, ik’in yeda sida osiw qoqƛer aynƛax.
translation: He went and went and arrived under a high cliff.

text: Howži žedu nesiƛ’or butin, neƛ elur araq’iƛin.
translation: Now they turned on him: "Give us the vodka!"

text: Ža uži qˤilon ok’ełno, sidaɣ ɣˤuɣˤura iħuz lilyoxor ayn.
translation: The boy went a wrong way and came to a muddy river bank somewhere.

text: Yigtow ža ƛ’iri yičizaƛ’ riynoxa eƛin: di sabawyabin caxxosianu, šidaƛin eƛiłiri šayt’anza där ʕaq’uba neƛxoƛin, amma dey nesirsi sis daru yołƛin.
translation: But because she insisted so much, the sheikh said: "I don't write a talisman, and if I told you why, the devils would give me torture, but I have one medicine for him."

text: Bik’in ečrut’ani xalq’in łˤebozałäyn oɣno ža uži iduɣor own.
translation: The old people went, took the boy out of the leaves and brought him home.

text: Esin šebi xecin šebi zownƛax, zown anuƛax sis aɣi.
translation: Once upon a time there was a bird.

text: Yeda ƛirba mekoyxo äsuƛin, yizi bišʷa rodanił c’oxno.
translation: "The guest seems to be hungry", and they they met up to make food.

text: Bertenyoł šeƛ’anix baħarčis šeƛ’ureł xizay yiłagon q’orolay yegirno, neła nełas šeƛ’un nełar xecin.
translation: In order to dress up for the marriage, she let the widow look after young men's clothings and left her own clothes with her.

text: Mečołer raynosi, boƛƛo baq’ˤu zaman bik’izaƛ’ortow, zira im-m-m-m, im-m-m-mƛin eƛin.
translation: After coming to the field, not much time passed until the fox said: "Mhmhmh, mhmhmh!"

text: Ža čuret’no nˤoƛał buqˤno.
translation: The whip he hid in the ashes.

text: Neła mixƛ’o yisä k’oƛin nełas kodi biqirno.
translation: At that time, he ran and took her by her hair.

text: Neła bertenyołer din goƛ’is.
translation: I was also invited to this marriage.

text: Nesä xanqor eƛin, zam-zames łi ħaƛunay, saɣ oqno ciqqosi bełi iłe aq miƛin.
translation: He said to the king: "If you drink the holy water, you will get cured and become [as healthy] as a forest deer!"

text: Där eniwn, esnabin bukada betinƛin.
translation: I wanted to see my mother and my brothers."

text: Xediyan di neła bayru q’ut’imoxor izmun neƛno nesi žadaɣor yegirsiƛin.
translation: And my husband let me go to this boy and also gave her the permission to have a (marriage) contract.

text: Mežus kinaw šebin di xizor rutirno mežus mežuqor neƛan, meža ža karamałi yołalini hibo dey däqor bayrnäyƛin, žawab teƛno žoyä žedur.
translation: "I will give all your things back to you, if you give me back my magic wand," answered the boy them.

text: Äzuru yisir ciqez raʕalłix yałruni sida ixiw ɣunoq soder bizix aždaħ-bikori bukayn.
translation: Looking around, he saw a dragon-snake climbing a tree that was standing at the edge of the forest.

text: Dey xediw nexxoƛin, yiła yeda sida yeže ɣamasya tełxor uqˤno.
translation: "My husband is coming!", she [said and] hid him inside a large box.

text: Bara eƛin:
translation: His wife said:

text: Meža mežuł raqru halmaɣłi eli sabawłun rexarno.
translation: Don't destroy the friendship that is between you because of us!

text: Nedur resunäyƛin, eƛin užä dahawcen ičin, mažgityaɣorno ik’ač’in, idun kecač’in.
translation: "If that's the case," said the boy, "I'll wait a little, and will neither go to the mosque, nor sleep here at home.

text: Xanez kidbes eniw yoł di, där ža xedyox yik’izaƛ’or yukada retinƛin, yiła ader xot’o boɣno.
translation: "I am the king's daughter's mother, I wanted to see her before she'd go to that husband.", and she put her foot forward.

text: Elo yiłar ʕuraw q’ʷariłiƛ’ sis ečru žek’u esun.
translation: There she found an old man in big sadness.

text: Ža nece bercinawni aɣin bižin, łacez guluƛ’no zown, yeda hawaƛ’ k’oƛin iduɣor ik’in.
translation: The boy took this beatiful bird and rode his wind horse home.

text: Hemełƛ’o xizyo doxtur ʕuraw ɣˤaɣˤ izin.
translation: Then afterwards the doctor was very happy.

text: ʕIyayx yedun xecin C’irduxno, nesis esnabin elaygon bitor bik’in.
translation: Tsirdukh and his brothers left her crying and went away from there.

text: Ciqqay qacan t’it’in, ruk’un rayrno esnabi bayn.
translation: From the forest the brothers came, having chopped wood, with the bundle.

text: Žedu nasin maħor boqnosi, reɣeni xaličan, beɣeni magalun bäqiruni nukarä xizyo yałruxor esin, anƛ’ima baħna-bik’inkin räqinč’iru eluq reƛ, hemesi dowzoniƛ’aygon rigu ɣˤutku yałruni užä neƛsi, debi xexbikin besač’iney žeduƛin.
translation: After they all went outside, the henchman who got the small mat and the small bread afterwards told him: "We were walking for a week and this boy with the house even prettier than yours gave us the meat that we didn't get; they even might be your children."

text: Žoyä ɣˤutku rodixƛin eženi žek’uqno esin meɣyoqor eƛin, xanes riłe ɣˤutkun, č’ikays ƛen boqoƛƛin.
translation: The boy told the old man: "Building a house." and said to the udder: "May there become a house like the king's and a bridge of glass!"

text: ʕAƛaza ec’no xabar šebiƛin yisä esirnosi, sida maduhalä esin, xanez yeɣeni kidbeƛäy bitor sidä sis esnabi bexursiƛin.
translation: When he asked: "What's new in the village?", a neighbor told: "The brothers killed each other because of the king's youngest daughter."

text: Xanä hemedurtow pakmos ʕabasin cuƛer kurno eƛinosi, Kusagon mesedyos ʕabasin cuƛer kurno eƛin, k’ešonad mi, mesedyos ʕabasi, dunyalƛ’o debeq roqxanusi šebintow anuƛin.
translation: When the king threw the copper coin upward and said his phrase, Kusa threw up the golden coin up and said: "Play, golden coin, there is nothing in the world that you couldn't become!"

text: Bara yisir ičin močitown xecinč’izaƛ’, xizyo yisä eƛin, ža esinäy, di äxuƛin.
translation: Because the wife didn't leave him in peace, he then said: "If I tell you, you will die."

text: Nedurä mi dey wasi t’ubazi bodix zownƛin, elayn bizin ziru xanes kidbederno bik’in esin nełaq ža ɣˤutkun, maʕišatno ɣˤʷays yałrułin, žatown habihan yołƛinno.
translation: "So you have fulfilled my wish?", [said] the fox[,] got up from there, went to the king's daughter and told her that the house and the fortune all belong to the dog and that he is just a miller.

text: Howła imadał hič’č’a ixiw šebi yoł: isä, ceyä, t’ekayä, aħoyä, ziruyä, q’orol ɣˤanabiyä?
translation: Who is the biggest one in this tale: The bull, the eagle, the goat, the shepherd, the fox or the widow?

text: Aždaħes yaqru nˤoƛun łacqo yižirno, nełas zawru šinaw xazina mesedno bižin, Baxumesedun guluƛ’ zowrno Kidiro Žabulawder ik’in.
translation: He let the wind carry away ashes of the dragon, took all the gold from its treasures, seated Bakhumesed on a horse, and went to Jabulaw in Kidero.

text: Bayn zira, mušin pˤaƛun, noci užiƛ’or butirno.
translation: The fox came, thrashed and turned the louse into the boy.

text: Kʷaxatow bełiqanä barun yown, ƛˤeb ħišiłzaƛ’or žedur q’ˤuƛas q’ˤano uži odin.
translation: Soon the hunter married a wife, and before a year was over, two twin boys were born to them.

text: Bilq’isdä nesiqor eƛin, mi Q’urʔan yaqino, där teqa retin didur mi ža t’et’erxoƛin.
translation: Bilqisdi said to him: "Don't close the Qur'ân, I want to hear you reading it."

text: Howžäy soder nediw šebin radinƛin, reƛ’an ozzaq riħin č’uran rukarerno, Bilq’isdä ža maħor egirno.
translation: "From now on, don't do such things anymore!", Bilqisdi put her hands onto his eyes, gave him back his eyelight and sent him outside.

text: Eženi esiya eƛin:
translation: The eldest brother said:

text: Howžiƛa rˤałay roda zaman bayxƛin Alimaħama ciqaɣor ik’in, isʷaqor reƛ’u rodanix c’araʕyor qaca rowanixƛin.
translation: "Now it's time to do the threshing.", Alimagomed thought and went into the forest to bring wood for building the yoke for the bulls.

text: Nełaq mi rasgi q’ʷarid yaqnoƛin, žoyä nełas rok’u čučzi royn.
translation: "Don't be sad at all about it!", he calmed her down.

text: Hunix bik’iƛ’orey, žedur ħumukulis ruɣu resun.
translation: While being on their way, they found the hoofprints of a camel.

text: Eženi esiya eƛin:
translation: The eldest brother said:

text: Waħħo gibu bukayn gurow, iħu taqˤor baɣnoƛinno, eƛin šeyxä, žaqay ɣˤurušno yoɣno.
translation: "Don't cross the river unless you see the sand below!", said the sheikh and took the ruble from the boy.

text: Sida bečedaw žek’uderno ik’in, nesiz k’udoz baɣman eynoyn, micxirno bodin, bižin babiwqor teƛno.
translation: He went to a wealthy man, worked on his vineyard, earned money and carried it to give it to his father.

text: Enda dey ečuy t’et’ra ižixƛin, esin Ħasanä raqru šebin.
translation: "I am bringing my grandson here to school.", Hassan told what had happened.

text: Xan, č’agu ƛexuƛ mi!
translation: "King, [long] may you live!

text: Nesä ƛirbaq esirno, yedu ɣˤanabi šida qˤaƛanayx ʕiyayxƛin.
translation: He asked the guest: "Why is this woman yelling and crying?"

text: K’ak’aryar baynosi yisä eƛin, howt’äy bitor di ik’äłinč’iƛin.
translation: When they came to a narrow point, he said: "I cannot go on from here."

text: Xanes kid c’unzi yodanix sis oz bayn, xizay biłetow sisgon bayn.
translation: In order to protect the king's daughter, an army came, and behind it another similar army came.

text: Roqnäy däł xizay rexadanix sadaq’ałer ħonƛ’äy bˤeƛ’ rowan ik’an, elazay aynosi, debeq žan esanƛin.
translation: "In that case, I will go and bring the sheep from the mountain for slaughtering them for alms behind me(?), and when I come back here, I will tell it to you."

text: Nesis hut’-mˤalay ƛ’er bisäsi bukayzaƛ’ hunixoza esirno, šebi deber roqnoƛin.
translation: When they saw the color vanishing from his face, the travellers asked: "What happened to you?"

text: Didurnokin ħal bayxanusi biyzaƛ’, kidbä siħirno yoqno dibir šeƛ’u roɣa egirno.
translation: When she knew he had no trouble [following her], the girl became a sly person and let the imam take off his clothes.

text: Ceyä yisir bigu gulu teƛno.
translation: The eagle gave him a good horse.

text: Hemece rigu ɣˤutkun ričin, łinaq mi q’ʷarid oqxoƛin, esirno eženä.
translation: "That is such a pretty house, what are you so sad about?", asked the old one.

text: Yedan ʕabdal yołƛin, bazarganbi elaygon bitor bik’in.
translation: "He is an idiot as well," [said] the merchants [and] went away from there.

text: Bełiqanä esirno nełaq, mi nesis kidä, baruyä yaqƛin.
translation: The hunter asked her: "Are you his daughter or his wife?"

text: Nełƛ’ay soder ža ɣˤanabi xedyoz zulmuqayn yok’ełno, Allahzo ašunoz kumakyoƛaza raħatyoƛ’ zown.
translation: After that, the woman got repose from her husband's tyranny and his beatings with the help of the rainbow.

text: Yeženi esiya žawab teƛno:
translation: The eldest sister answered:

text: Bˤeƛ’no adäz roc’no neširur iduɣor ik’iƛ’oräy, Alimaħamar teqno bˤeƛ’ sidä sidqor xabaryayx.
translation: When he drove the sheep home in the evening, Alimagomed heard them talking to each other.

text: Saparyox oqno, qˤašdes zaman baynosi, yeda sida ɣunoƛ rešt’izi oqno.
translation: He went on the journey, and when it was time for lunch, he took a rest under a tree.

text: Esin šebi, xecin šebi sida beɣe ʕaƛa res anusi, baru yowace oqäsi ʕoloqanaw uži zown.
translation: What is to be said, what is to be left out -- there lived a young boy, without the necessary subsistence, being in the right age to marry a woman.

text: Xanä wazirzaqor amru bodin, ʕAt’id xexłiƛ’ däder ayroƛin.
translation: The king commanded his viziers: "Bring Atid to me quickly!"

text: Sidaɣ maqˤir ciqaɣorno yisä yedu yižin, hemedurtow gondun yič’in, ƛ’irigon zozis ƛ’ˤun bodin howlo žan xecin babiw xizor utin nexno.
translation: The father led her to a place far away in the forest, likewise dug a hole and built a roof of moss ontop, left her there and returned.

text: Moƛaxay žan č’arik’no, nesä eƛin, di malaik yoł, asatow maharno erno, mi yowanix nexäsi t’aɣor, neširu di dowde puħo keca ayƛin.
translation: He woke her up from her sleep and said: "I am an angel, I will arange a wedding in the heavens for I have come here to marry you; I must sleep next to you at night."

text: Esnazaq sipat esin łiynosi nesi žek’a eƛin:
translation: After they finished telling him about its appearance, the man said to the brothers:

text: Q’ˤano äƛirugon nesä žaqay ɣˤurušno yisno, mi qˤišodäy soder ħon xäcinƛin, ʕaq’lun bodin.
translation: For the second time he took a ruble from the boy and gave him the advice: "Don't go up the mountain after it got dark!"

text: Xizyo žedagon ža ižin xaner teƛno.
translation: Afterwards they brought and gave him to the king.

text: Netintow mamalayä at’iwt’a qaca rayrxo zown, nełaqgon c’i era onočar isałxo zown.
translation: The rooster always brought wet firewood, and it was difficult for the hen to light a fire with it.

text: Žałäy t’umin boqno, kidbägon akin raɣˤin begirno.
translation: The boy became a pigeon again, and the girl opened the window and let it out.

text: Hudayzikotow Bac’ali žedu k’eda ik’inosi, zeyn keze boqno nełaq esirno, meža dey čanyabi rac’iyäƛin.
translation: The next day, when Batsali went to search them, he met a bear and asked him: "Have y'all eaten my goats?"

text: Mamalay ɣudes ciqaɣor qaciƛ’or bik’ix zown, onoču iduza beynoyx, iduzasi royx bičix zown.
translation: Every day the rooster went into the forest for firewood, and the hen was at home doing the housework.

text: Hemełƛ’o xizyo xedyos esiw tełer ayn.
translation: After that, the husband's brother came inside.

text: Sis zownƛax Goqiƛin eƛäsi miskinaw žek’u.
translation: Once there was a poor man named Goqi.

text: Mi di naɣor ižixƛin, esirno yisä.
translation: "Where are you leading me?", he asked.

text: Elay bitorgon ik’iƛ’orey, deber sis žek’u äsu kʷayä zamzames łi roqłin ža ħaƛa roqinč’ey ƛexasi.
translation: And when you go further from there, you will find a man with holy water in his hands, but he won't drink it.

text: Xizyo, babiya äƛiruxor, ža uži bazarganzar teƛno.
translation: Afterwards, the father sold his boy off to the merchants, as he said.

text: Hoboygon xan žedu raqˤanix žek’u iqinč’ey ƛexun.
translation: But now the king didn't have anyone to sew them.

text: Nełƛ’aza di aq’ˤu pikrazał ƛexux roda šebin koƛ’inč’eyƛin, žawab teƛno xedya.
translation: Because of this, I - remaining very thoughtful - don't know what to do.", answered the husband.

text: Howži xanä, sida quł neširu eženi uži egirno baɣmox ičanix, heneš bok’ek’anix šebi elor bayxosi yałru riyraniƛäy.
translation: One day in the evening the khan left his elder son in the garden to find out who comes and steals the apples.

text: Babiwn razi oqno nełaƛ’, bełiqus uži uqˤłanix egirno.
translation: The father agreed and let the hunter's son hide himself.

text: Žeduƛ’ Ħasan sidƛ’o, Ħusenƛin sideniƛ’ cirabin ceyn.
translation: They named one of them Hassan and the other one Hussein.

text: Howži yeda č’ʷadmoƛ’or iħanadanix utin:
translation: Now he began to fight with the ruin:

text: C’irduxän adäza biłetown žawab teƛno.
translation: And Tsirdukh gave the same answer as before.

text: Žedu sasaq ħonƛ’aɣor roxirnosi, neširaɣor žedar žedu rutin iduɣor rayx zown.
translation: In the morning he chased them onto a mountain, towards the evening they came back home by themselves.

text: Eloza bezu-bˤoƛ’unosi, zirur boƛik’u bac’äsi ƛeles q’ulaʕi besun.
translation: When he looked around there, he found the carcass of a half-eaten lamb.

text: Sasaq qˤašinosi, Ismaʕil hunixor oqanix ħadur oqxo ukaynosi, ʕArabuzanä gulu bˤaƛoyn, din dowde sadaq yik’ixƛin.
translation: After she got up in the morning and saw that Ismail was preparing to get on his way, Arabuzan harnessed her horse: "I am going with you."

text: ʕAliqiličä rok’no mač’an, aždaħes meč’ bet’urno boɣno kurno.
translation: Aliqilich stroke with his saber, cut off the dragon's neck and threw it away.

text: Hudayziko xanez kidbä neła nełas ɣarabaš yezu-yoƛ’ˤanix łeno minutno beč’no bazaryaɣor yegirno.
translation: The next day, the princess sent her own servant to the market for five minutes to have a look.

text: Haqayn roɣno ža ƛuza Ismaʕila akiq karu šebinon rˤołun maħor roɣno rižirno.
translation: Ismail took that bone out of his mouth and threw it outside, where it bored itself through the window and everything.

text: Yiła q’orolagon yeda ʕAliqilič ayrno xander.
translation: And the widow brought Aliqilich to the king.

text: Ža aɣin bižin bełiqus uži xander ik’in.
translation: The hunter's son carried the bird to the king.

text: Wey Goqi, mi di kikinäy, Allahä deber kiʕangi maʕišat bičärƛin”.
translation: "Hey Goqi, if you feed me, God will set up a fortune for you."

text: Elayn boqno dahawtow bitaɣor bik’inosi, yisi žoyä žeduqor eƛin, di ža kid yowanix ik’ix, däz ža c’aq’ rok’äwa yisäƛin.
translation: When they had gone just a little further, that boy said to them: "I am going to marry this girl; did she really take me into her heart?"

text: Elo azbaryoł adäza mesedyos č’iħo roqxosi baɣ yołƛax.
translation: There, in front of the palace, was a garden where golden fruits were growing.

text: Bišanon bišno, Kusaqor siskin xabar boynč’ey, łˤonon esnabi maħor boqno.
translation: Having eaten their food, the three brothers went outside without having talked to Kusa.

text: Howži kidbä esirno, na ža uži yoł, didiw žek’u ža yołƛin.
translation: Now the girl asked: "Where is this boy? What kind of man is he?"

text: Nesä užiqor eƛin, ža aɣi däqor neƛ xaner bižanixƛin.
translation: He said to the boy, "Give me that bird, so I can carry it to the king."

text: Neła ža beƛ’q’ˤu bac’anix łet’ix yałrułi nesir čuqˤnosi, c’eruc’a caƛin.
translation: When he noticed that she was picking that sheep in order to eat it, he shot with his bow.

text: Sida zamanałäy eɣeni esiya nasin žeda roƛäk’ru šebinabi yizas xot’ozaxor kurnosi, łˤonontow yisił adäzarno rayn esirno, šebi deber elaqay ħažat roqnoƛin.
translation: After some time, the young brother threw the things that they listed to their feet, and they went before him and asked: "What do you need from us?"

text: Žigoƛ’aygon mi cek’qo qˤaƛu rizirłi, ela mi bexura yołƛin, ʕomoyä eƛin.
translation: If you make the goat kid shout again, we will kill you!", said the donkey.

text: Qˤayä hiboq neła nełas mecno bitirno, xizyo xexoyn begirno xizor, aħoziqor bižin teƛƛin.
translation: The rabbit touched the stick with its tongue and then sent the young one back: "Carry it back and give it to the shepherd!"

text: Osaxor hawaƛ’orno bizin, tupäsi hil biłe wˤałerno nexno bik’in ɣunoq c’oxno.
translation: It got up into the height through the air, and came down like a gun's bullet again and pounced on the tree.

text: Yisä eli baƛ’irxoƛin žedu ʕaƛoxarno bezun bik’in.
translation: "He's deceiving us," they [said and] went to look toward the village.

text: Oƛƛozo žar bazarya teƛxo butni besun.
translation: The middle son found a burka on sale on the market.

text: Nesis zown łˤono uži.
translation: He had three sons.

text: Ražbadin ħadur oqxon iyn, ʕaƛoz ʕoloqza nesiq harizi rodin, elin Huläɣor dawlaƛ’ bižoƛin.
translation: Knowing that Razhbadin was preparing, the young people from the village asked him: "Lead us to Georgia for hunting as well!"

text: Išno oqnosi yisir siskin roqinč’uƛax.
translation: After he ate, nothing happened to him.

text: Sebixasi zaman baynosi, Paxrudinä Alimaħamaqor eƛin, ixdaxor ɣutanłäzay ħonqaɣor bˤeƛ’ rownosi, bˤeƛ’q’uł xizay kami anusi ƛeli bayrnäy, boƛik’u reqen deber xeca yołƛin.
translation: When fall came, Pakhrudin said to Alimagomed: "Until spring, after your brought the sheep to the mountains onto the winter pastures, if you lead a lamb that doesn't get lost behind every sheep, then I'll leave you half of the herd."

text: Q’ˤunƛ’orno ƛ’irir č’edon rayrno, žedun šušin, iduɣor xediwn nexno.
translation: The husband put earth onto the two, burried them and came home.

text: Ža kid nesä yižixosi yałrułi Tawadir riyx zownanu.
translation: Tavadi didn't know that this girl was the one he was going to marry.

text: Baq’ˤu mesedno besuroqur teƛno, yisä ža besuro nesiq raładyołxor begirerno.
translation: He gave the fisherman a lot of gold and had him let the fish into the sea.

text: Xizyo c’odorawni esiw oqno ʕantawnä bukaruni moči bič’a.
translation: After that, the clever brother went to dig at the place the stupid brother had shown.

text: Barusno nełaz etalisno rok’ƛ’o yałru šebin xedyor čuqˤäsi zown.
translation: The husband noticed the thing that the wife and her lover planned.

text: Yisä yisir łˤora ɣudi bertenno erno, bercinawƛ’ay bercinawt’a kidbabin rukarno, howt’aytow barun yižoƛin harizin rodin, roƛik’u xanłin riso howt’otow uyn iči, mi iłe baħarči naynkin t’ok’ow elur äqinč’iƛin.
translation: He arranged a 3-day lasting marriage for him, presented girls that were prettier than pretty, "Choose a wife from here!", he begged, "Settle down here and take half of the kingdom, from nowhere will we ever get such a brave young man as you, anymore!"

text: Yeda bełiƛ’ ik’a aqzay soder xanez nukarzaskin bełis bax bexun.
translation: After he began to go hunting, even the king's henchmen's hunting luck deteriorated.

text: Elo neł ħukmatyozał di aq’ˤu aħnays, łukin bertenyozałxorno goƛ’inč’u, uyn išrinč’u.
translation: I was walking around much through these countries, but no one had invited me to the weddings and didn't even gave me to eat.

text: Xizaɣor ɣˤutkun, idusi maʕišatno, daruyabin teƛno micxiryosno, mesedyosno ħurženyabin ric’no, yeda saparyox oqno.
translation: Finally, he sold his house, fortune, medicine and went on journey with the saddle bags filled with money and gold.

text: Elo aw käczar hibon bok’no ža bexarur räqi ixina tełsi bečełiƛin.
translation: And there beat a stick on where the mouse sleeps to kill her and get the riches from inside her bag!"

text: Žeda sadaqay ʕumru boyx zown.
translation: They where living together.

text: Elo yeda zamanałäy mekoda utirno.
translation: There he got hungry after a while.

text: Zamanaƛ’aza ɣˤutka tełäzay sis qˤaƛu-bˤaɣu rizin, hiba gugu žek’ƛ’oräy, bečedawni maduhalqo k’ʷač’ni regin.
translation: Some minutes later one could hear noise and shout inside the house: as the stick beat the wealthy neighbor on his back, he gave a loud groan.

text: Way dey užiƛin, regin babiwq, howži deber žukkin roqinč’inäy, di eƛix zownč’a dowqor neła aɣis tupi caƛinƛin.
translation: "Oh dear, my son!", said the father, "Have I not warned you that it would become bad for you now, if you shoot that bird with your rifle?"

text: Sida quł sasaqosi bara eƛin, di yaqˤuł xexbi royxosi, mi däde igono näxno, č’uħaraw šeƛ’un šeƛ’un madaħarno oƛäxnoƛin.
translation: One day in the morning the wife said: "Today I am giving birth to the children, do not come near me and do not go outside in gorgeous clothing!"

text: Zeyes Aħya bˤałay rok’xo aždaħes q’ˤim ret’urxo roɣxo zown, nełaz meča ʕoƛnot’a roƛixxo zown.
translation: Bear Ear hit with his dagger and chopped off a head of the dragon, and instead of it, seven [more heads] were appearing.

text: Raɣäza akix där muži kuroƛin.
translation: Spread out a mattress for me at the window on the veranda!"

text: Uži idur aynosi, babiya nesił adäza zaɣrušaw biša erno.
translation: When the son came home, his father put poisoned food in front of him.

text: Akiłno q’ˤida di ičinosi, nesä howt’o deber kokoru qˤiƛci riqixyołiƛin eƛis.
translation: When I got tired and sat down, he said: "If we only could get you a soft pillow to sit down here..."

text: ʕAt’idä qaħłi begirzaƛ’or aħinyoƛ waħo gondu yič’inosi, tełäzay roč’iw łäs qˤuqˤu boƛixno.
translation: Atid digged a big hole under the rock before the dawn set in, and then a stream of cold water appeared from inside.

text: Zira hemedurtow idurno bik’in sadaq q’ut’uma yałru rił ragin łiyrno.
translation: So the fox went home and licked up all the butter that was in the earthen jug.

text: Heresi mec esixozo eƛin:
translation: The liar said:

text: Didurgon, šebigon resułin xal bodanix elor ɣˤanabi yegira yay, yałi - ela badiru ħukmu meq’aw bäčiƛin, diwan boyxozo xalq’imo xan wˤałer ezurerno.
translation: "How- and whatever it is, we must send a woman there to make an examination, or else the decision we make will be wrong.", the judges said and calmed down the king.

text: Nar yik’a koƛ’inč’ey, sasiw ɣudin roqzaƛ’, ža kid sasaqor ciqqotow ƛexun.
translation: Not knowing where to go, and because the day became gloomy, the girl stayed in the forest until morning.

text: Buqbetałer ʕaƛarno bik’in, dahaw iduzan bičin, godek’anyaɣor ʕoloqzałxor boqno.
translation: At sunset they went into the village, spent some time at home, and went to the people at the godekan.

text: Xanä ža maħortow yegirxo zownanu.
translation: The king didn't let her go outside.

text: Xalq’imo yeda ħaladtow ižin elo ʕaƛos žamaʕat batiza mečoƛ’or.
translation: The people lead him with trouble to the place were the village's assembly met.

text: Ža ɣˤutkun bˤeƛ’es reqenno Mamali-Maħamas yołƛin.
translation: This house and the flock of sheep is Mamali-Magomed's.

text: Aħmadä ečruni žek’uqor eƛin, howt’o deber q’ˤida iča kokoru qˤiƛci riqixyołiƛin.
translation: Akhmed said to the old man: "If we only could get you a soft pillow to sit down here..."

text: Xanno, diwan boyxosi xalq’in, kidbeder ays žaƛin, Kusaƛ’ bužzi boqno anu.
translation: The king and the judges did not believe that Kusa had come to the girl.

text: Tabanaw hunäsi hunä yik’in yedu sidaɣ ɣˤutkoxor yaynosi, tełxor yoqno.
translation: When she came to a house somewhere on the long way she was going on, she went inside.

text: Iduɣor nexƛ’oräygon, bahanar šeƛ’urqo ħurno biħisƛin”.
translation: Also, when he comes home, he smears dirt on his clothes as an excuse."

text: Mi ʕiyaynäy, ahlimos šakno bäk’i, yižin xander min karƛin.
translation: "If you cry, people will get suspicious and throw you before the king."

text: Nedur resunäy, däde sadaq ƛaƛin, xanä žedu łˤonon iduɣor rižin.
translation: "If that is the case, come with me!", said the king and lead the three of them to his home.

text: Nesiz guluƛ’no zown, bok’no yisä čuret’, ʕoƛno ħonno xecin raładyoz lilyoxor ayn yeda.
translation: He sat on the horse, beated the whip and having left seven mountains behind him, he came to the coast of the sea.

text: Rigu ƛirbałin rodin, ža čantan saɣyatyos teƛno, ceyä bełiqan hunix egirno, neła čantaƛ’osi iƛ’u idur ayzaƛ’or yizärnoƛin.
translation: Being a good host, the eagle gave the pocket as a present and sent the hunter on his way [saying]: "Don't lift the pocket's lid before you came home!"

text: Aždaħes xexoyn bižin yeda idur aynosi, yisä yedu ɣˤʷay biłe ɣˤutkox ɣuɣudoxor bicin.
translation: He carried the dragon's young home and tied it on a chain to the house, like a dog.

text: Adäz elor bit’araw mec esixosini ayn.
translation: The one who always said the truth went ahead.

text: Eže izäsin oqzaƛ’, ɣudi räk’iruce nesis tatu bexux bik’ix zown.
translation: Because he was getting old, his power was dying as a day passed.

text: Zownƛax zownanuƛax yiła eluz wilayatyoł sis barun yexun ƛexasi žek’u.
translation: Once upon a time there was a man in our province, whose wife had died.

text: ʕAƛor ʕeziyaw k’udi däq biqira bayxƛin.
translation: I must deliver enough vines to the village."

text: Xalq’ima ža gamušyozaq ƛisirno ʕaƛaɣorno yižin, žeda žedus c’ik’iw ʕaƛno kušin, neł c’iɣuƛ’ ƛ’iri bodin.
translation: The people made the buffalo pull it into the village, demolished their entire village and rebuilt it on the shoulder.blade.

text: Žedun razi roqno, kurno yisä c’eruc’as hil.
translation: They agreed and he shot the arrow.

text: Sida quł babiya nesiq esirno, šida mi nece q’ʷarid oqno ičäsi, šebi deber roqnoƛin.
translation: One day the father asked him: "Why are you so sad, what has happened to you?"

text: Roɣno rok’no mač’a, ret’urno q’ˤim kurno.
translation: He took his saber, hit him and cut the dragon's head off.

text: Łˤora ɣudi yaħ bodin nesä, elay soder yaħ boqnoanu nesiq.
translation: Three days he had his conscience, then his conscience wasn't enough anymore.

text: Nesi žaz babiya sidaquł q’orol ɣˤanabin yiqirno nełaq esin, hemedur ɣudƛ’ay ɣudƛ’or dey uži ħalaq’ oqxo ik’ixƛin.
translation: One day the boy's father found a widow and told her that his boy is becoming meager day by day.

text: Nesä užiq esirno, yedu ziya teƛxanaƛin.
translation: He asked the boy: "Don't you sell that cow?"

text: Qˤišodgon yizi bišanix elor baynosi, hemedurtow siskin šebin rac’inč’ey, q’ˤunan reƛ’izad q’ˤimno riqirno esun Kusa esnazar.
translation: When they came there to eat dinner, the three brothers found Kusa there without eating anything and with his two hands supporting his head.

text: Hemedur din nexsiƛin.
translation: And so I came here."

text: Esin šebi, xecin šebi sis zown q’ˤano halmaɣ.
translation: Once upon a time there were two friends.

text: Xizyogon nesä q’ˤuna äƛiru t’alamqosi eniwq madazartow rekan kurerno, acno serun, kidno iduɣor yegirno, yeda eniwdäɣor ik’in.
translation: After that, he let his mother throw the keys to the second floor outside, unlocked the door, let the girl into his house and went to his mother.

text: Aždaħägon yedan hemedurtow ašuƛayd cucik’no.
translation: So the dragon put him in up to the waist as well.

text: Besura neła nełaz gugyoƛ’no zowerno, bełiqus uži neła kidbez ɣˤutkoł adäzar ižin kurno.
translation: The fish took the hunter's son onto his back, carried him to that girl's house and threw him off again.

text: Žeduł teł cek’i ešunanu.
translation: The goat kid was not among them.

text: Azbaryoz mada gulun bicin, nełał adäzar ʕuraw henešno čˤaƛin, elotow ičix zawru iłe iduɣor ik’in.
translation: He tied the horse outside the palace, poured many apples in front of it and went into the house like someone who lived there.

text: Kidbä eƛin, ʕilmu yiyxosi nasin babiya bexurxosi, ele hemedurt’a bexaruzas ƛuzabi yoł yiziƛin.
translation: The girl said: "My father kills all who know about science, these here are the bones of the thus killed."

text: Nełƛ’ay soder yizä sadaq paraxat ʕumru bižin.
translation: After that, they led a peaceful life together.

text: Eniwrno ʕuraw aq’ˤuba č’aguyaw mixƛ’o babiya teƛxo zown.
translation: The mother was tortured very much as well by the father, when she was still alive.

text: Aħmadägon boƛƛik’u magalun bac’no eƛin, asno rocasi yoł, bucin bic’äsi yołƛin.
translation: Akhmed ate half of the bread and said: "The fog has become clean and the moon has become full."

text: Xizyogon Bilq’isdider nesi eženis łˤonon užibin bayn, xanez madaxosini žek’un, nełastow xediwn, xedyosgon esiwn.
translation: Then to Bilqisdi came the three sons of the old man, and the king's guard, her husband and her husband's brother.

text: Hudayziko qˤaħłiqtow babiw kidbabi saparyox boqno.
translation: The next day at dawn the father (and) the daughters went on their journey.

text: Ža lel yoƛ’ˤun ƛ’iräzay nesis koyär yayn.
translation: The feather fell down and into his hand.

text: Bik’i hudu cin cedin horoƛin” - eƛin zeyä.
translation: "So go, give him a name and come back!", said the fox.

text: Nełar žawabtow biqinč’izaƛ’ ƛexasi yoł di q’ʷariłiłerno egin.
translation: Because I could not get an answer to this, I got so sad."

text: Sasaqor ħimun yagin, č’urqˤo boqno bik’in.
translation: It licked the tombstone until the next day, and when the morning set it, it went away.

text: Di žeduqƛin, žawab teƛno nesä, där bˤeƛ’xo yałzay yukayru moƛu esinč’uƛin.
translation: He answered "I didn't tell them what I dreamed of while herding the sheep."

text: Netin t’ut’ƛ’orkin reƛ’a rizärinč’iru, yedu šebi där yaqˤuł roqäƛin, bikorin bexurno yisiz rok’uƛ’ nexełno.
translation: "I never even lifted my hand to a fly, what has happened to me today?!", he realized that he killed the snake.

text: Qaca royn łiynosi, ʕomoybin ric’no Mirza ʕaƛaɣor ik’in.
translation: After Mirza finished collecting firewood, he charged the donkeys and went into the village.

text: Hudayzikotow bertenno bodin, miskinaw oqłin ʕaq’ilawni žax nesi žek’a äƛiruxor, kidno yegirno.
translation: The next day the wedding was arranged and the girl was - as this man had told - married off to the intelligent though poor boy.

text: Xaner iyn anu yeda nesis uži yałrułi.
translation: The king didn't know that he was his own son.

text: Xexłiƛ’ k’oƛin babiwderno yik’in, hemesi tusnaq’äzo užir teƛxa mi izmu guluƛ’ zowanirƛin, nełaq eƛin rok’äłrun, xan kid žek’anixƛin iza oqno.
translation: She quickly ran to her father, and when the words "Do you give the permission that the boy in prison can ride the horse?" broke out of her, the king got up to slap his daughter.

text: Neła quł neširu yizirabi kecno baq’ˤu mix bik’izaƛ’ortow, Tawadis uži ayn.
translation: On the same evening, before they slept too long, Tavadis son came.

text: T’ok’ow k’ek’-bik’łinč’ey howlortow bexun ža - iłno q’ˤim yołali aždaħ.
translation: The dragon with the six heads couldn't move anymore and died there.

text: Ħusen ukayrun aždaħä eƛin, dowqo däz enda ɣˤul łox caƛin č’aq’ˤinäy, mi di boƛi begir, c’oxinč’inäy mi di ac’a yołƛin.
translation: Right after he saw Hussein, the dragon said: "If you throw and hit me with this stone within three attempts, break me apart in the middle! If you don't hit me, I am going to eat you."

text: Yisägon bizirno q’ˤidan bok’no aždaħ ašuƛäyd raƛ’moƛxor cucik’no.
translation: Then he beat the dragon down and put him into the earth up to the waist.

text: Sida quł ruzmanƛ’o xanä nazołno adäza sual erno, hič’č’an qˤaba hič’č’an kokoru, hič’č’an ʕedeʕaraw šebin šebiƛin.
translation: One Friday the king asked a question before everyone: "What is the blackest, softest and quickest thing?"

text: Hunixor ʕeziyaw micxirno teƛno, xanä Bilq’isdi yegirno, deber rätizaɣor yik’iƛin.
translation: The king gave her a lot of money for the way and sent Bilqisdi away: "Go wherever you want!"

text: Aq’ˤu urɣizi oqxo ičinč’ey izin Ražbadinä bˤałayn risno barude kecno äsuruni žek’us gič’orabi rodin kurno.
translation: Without thinking too much, Razhbadin got up, took his dagger and cut the man whom he found sleeping with his wife to pieces.

text: Łiya yižin k’emot’ sida aɣˤuz łiłiroł adäzan c’oxno yičin.
translation: The water carried the case before a mill's ditch, it hit it and remained there.

text: Guħƛ’orno bizin, łˤoran esnaza kurno c’eruc’a.
translation: The three brothers climbed up the hill and shot with their bows.

text: Sosisi ža dey anuƛinno yičin, yedu xizyo ža teƛanix razi yoqno.
translation: At first she was like "I don't have it.", afterwards she agreed to give it away.

text: Dandi yizi huniƛxor k’oƛin bok’ełno, žedu zawza mečoƛ’ ža ɣunno rik’in c’oxno.
translation: They had just gotten out of the way, and that tree hit the place where they had been.

text: Haq’udär yik’anix xedyoqay izmu esirnosi, nesägon łˤora becän yičin, hulon rexurno horoƛin yegirno.
translation: When she asked her husband for permission to go to her family, he let her go with the words: "You may stay three months, then, after [you killed the boredom(?)] come back!"

text: Ža nesä eƛizaƛ’, yeda xan ʕuraw ɣˤaɣˤun izin, xalq’in žaƛ’ razi boqno.
translation: When he said that, the king got very glad, and the people were okay with the boy.

text: Hemedur riguƛ’ay rigu rac’xo, riguƛ’ay rigu šeƛ’ux yisi žoyä yizide šomoƛa buci boɣno.
translation: So, eating the best [food] and wearing the best [clothes], the boy spent many months with them.

text: Hič’č’a adäza, sasaq mi däz gander biħanada ganin bown horoƛin, egirno nesä.
translation: The first one: Come tomorrow and bring a young bull to fight against my young bull.", he [said and] sent him away.

text: Xan urqizin oqno ičäsi zown, hoboyä hik’eyä yizi bexuxƛin.
translation: Then the king started pondering if they're still alive or already died.

text: C’aq’ bit’araw eƛisƛin, dibirän eƛin, q’ˤimbin wˤałer rˤoq’urno bazarganbin bik’in, žar q’aymos ric’äsi ħumukulibin xecin.
translation: "Very well said," said the imam and the merchants hung their hads and went away, leaving the camels with the goods to the boy.

text: Xanzo barus naysinan ci rik’inƛax ɣˤʷays k’ucin, k’et’us xexoyn neła ƛuƛin rodinƛin.
translation: The king's wife became known everywhere for the fact that she gave birth to a puppy and a kitten.

text: Neła sualyor žawab neƛxosi žek’u elo mužmareł oƛixnoanu.
translation: A man who could give the answer to that question didn't appear there on this Friday.

text: ƛirba, ig ayƛ!
translation: 'Welcome, guest!

text: Suƛƛisi oƛixno k’oƛin ɣamasyayn uži oxin maħor ok’ełno, ža iqiranixƛin nesił xizaygon č’ariƛ’ zawruni xediwn k’oƛin.
translation: Suddenly the boy jumped out of the box, ran outside and escaped; and in order to catch him ran the husband behind him, who was awake.

text: Boƛƛo baq’ˤu zaman bik’izaƛ’ortow, babiwn exun, ža uži sisxoli ƛexun.
translation: Before much time has passed, the father died and the son remained alone.

text: Tełerno bik’in bezunosi, esnazar ža micxiryos šemno bodin t’et’erxo esun.
translation: When they went inside and looked, the brothers found him making piles of money and counting it.

text: Acno yaɣˤin tełer aqruntow, suƛƛi dandir nexno bara xedyoq ałni bic’no.
translation: As soon as he opened the door and stepped inside, his wife came toward him and hugged him.

text: Hemełƛ’o xizyo nucos bic’alini q’ilimay yeda maħorno oqno, eženi esiws q’ˤimno risno iduɣor oxin.
translation: After that, he got out of the honey-filled pot, took the older brother's head and ran home.

text: Nesis sistow sis uži zown.
translation: He had one single son.

text: Užä žawab teƛno, yaqˤuł di besurozaƛ’or ik’ixƛin.
translation: The son replied: "Today I am going fishing."

text: Žedu esnabi ɣudes bełiƛ’t’a bik’ix zown.
translation: Every day the brothers went hunting.

text: Idurno yayngon yiła, mi gulu bayrno zown anuwaƛin, esiwqor eƛinosi, nesir neła gulu bac’rułi čuqˤno.
translation: When she went back into the house and said to the brother: "Didn't you bring a horse along?", he noticed that she had eaten the horse.

text: Neširaɣor bełiqanbi baynosi, žeduq raqru šebin esin.
translation: In the evening, when the hunters came, they told them what had happened.

text: Xanä madaxozoniqor eƛin, mesedyos q’ˤuri užiƛer teƛ, micxiryos - kidbeƛer, pakmos - ƛirbaqor, qacis q’ˤuri imyoqor yicin zawruni ɣˤanazaqor teƛƛin.
translation: The king said to the guard: "Give the golden chair to the boy, the silver one to the girl, the copper one to the guest and the wooden one give to the woman who was tied to the pole."

text: Xanez užä zey ax roqace bišerno.
translation: The khan's son fed down the bear.

text: Šebi roqno, ziruƛin?” - zeyä esirno.
translation: "What happened, fox?", asked the bear.

text: C’odorawnä esirno, eleley dibir, debez baruq maħi zowwäƛin.
translation: The clever one asked: "Lo and behold, mullah, did your wife have a tail?"

text: Iłira äƛiru ɣudƛ’o užä dahawtow koyä bixno risno ik’in, rečuƛäy mäħorno roɣno, ƛ’iri reƛ’un erno xizazgon gutanno yicin mečołxor rižin.
translation: On the sixth day, the boy went to take a little hay in his hand, took them out of the shed, put a yoke around them, tied a plow behind it and lead them into the field.

text: Boƛƛogon zaman bik’inosi, hemedurtow sosigon neła esirno.
translation: After a while, she asked it again.

text: Hunixor yisä łäs q’ˤano sikno eqerno, bišʷan erno, kumakyorgon, ħaƛirxo ik’anix, xizay k’oƛin yayn bara hibon teƛno.
translation: He put two waterskins onto the way, packed some food, and came running behind in order to go put(?), when his wife came running behind him and gave him a stick to go with, which he should put down for help.

text: Yisä tupi teƛno anu, žedä äƛiru mesedno teƛno, yisä žeduqay zey xʷasar bodin.
translation: He didn't give them the rifle, but gave them as much gold as they wanted and rescued the bear from them.

text: Maħor yoqƛ’oräy yiłas rorqosi xot’oqay roɣełno.
translation: While she got outside, a shoe fell from her foot.

text: Kidbazał xizay yedu kid elar yaħnayx, t’ar yaħnayx yičin yiłaq ɣudi sasiłno.
translation: While the girl walked around hither and thither behind the girls, it got dark around her.

text: Ečruni žek’a kot’aq reƛ’an riħin, ɣˤutku k’emot’ya tełxor rutirno.
translation: The old man rubbed his hand on the beard and the house turned into the wooden case.

text: Ax roqace nuci raganix egirno xecinäy neƛanƛin eƛin užä.
translation: "If you let me lick a belly-full of honey, I'll give it to you.", said the boy.

text: Sida quł nesider ziru bayn, debex di xanes kid yowanƛin.
translation: One day a fox came to him [saying]: "I will marry the princess off to you!"

text: Yizä nesi užiq esirno, debeqkin ana xaner daruyarsi reƛ, nełas siskin t’it’u anusi xizor butanix bˤuƛ’xo eliƛin.
translation: They asked that boy: "Do you not have a meat for medicine for the king? We are afraid to return without any piece."

text: Hemedurtow imyoq ħobo bok’nosi, pandur bˤoƛ’un bayn.
translation: So he kicked the pole again, and a lute fell down.

text: Aɣi bukayruntow nełaz maʕdinłiƛ’no ezun xan haqa ogun ƛexun.
translation: When the king saw the bird, he stood there gaping about its beauty.

text: Bełiƛ’ esnabi bäk’äsi yałzay, eɣeni esiwz bara k’orin boyn nazon q’ˤidan ričin bac’ƛ’oräy, Umukusez haqa bašiqˤoy šišin, k’ori boyƛ’orey eɣeni esiwz baruqay rˤaƛ’uruni.
translation: When the brothers were going hunting, the youngest brother's wife made an oatmeal and when everyone sat down and ate, a ring that fell off the youngest brother's wife when she made the oatmeal, got stuck in Umukus' mouth.

text: Musa suƛƛitow eƛin:
translation: Then Musa suddenly said:

text: Ža Qartay zown.
translation: That was Qartay (the witch).

text: Eniw iduɣorno yik’in, žaq xanä äsiru xabar esin.
translation: The mother went home and told her son what the king said.

text: Xizyogon urɣizi oqno xan, c’ohores q’ˤim anunč’uruni cudi sidaɣ qunyoxorno bayran, nesiz ʕagarłä ža šudał šušanix elay bižinč’ey xäcinč’i, hemełƛ’o där šebi ža c’ohor yałrun äyƛin.
translation: Then the king pondered: "I will bring the thief's headless corpse to the farmstead, his relatives won't leave without taking him from there to bury him on a graveyard."

text: Kʷaxatow bertenno boyn, yeɣeni kid nesä yown.
translation: And soon they organized a marriage and he married the young daughter.

text: Min däqor łˤora ɣudin q’adaraw roži äƛinƛin”.
translation: And don't you say a bad word in these three days!'

text: Hemełƛ’o ozzaq reƛ’an riħin, xedyoz esiwq Bilq’isdä č’ura rukarerno.
translation: Thereupon, Bilqisdi laid her hand on his eyes and restored the eyesight for her husband's brother.

text: Neła eƛin, darno rac’anix šebin neƛ, dowƛ’or ɣudi rayza quł, nagon boqłin din bäsu debez ħalarƛin.
translation: The bear said, "Give me something to eat, one day I will find you, if you will be in trouble."

text: Xizyo neła yisiqor mešok’lałäy bet’urno mus teƛno, debeƛ’ ɣudi rayza mečoƛ’ bik’ur mi yeduƛin.
translation: Then it pulled out a hair from its whiskers and gave it to him, "When one day you are in trouble, burn it!"

text: Zamanałäy bet’un kodin nexno, begirno gaman xecin yedu kid raładyołxor yiħun.
translation: After some time, the hair came off and the girl let go of the boat and went into the sea.

text: Užä žeduqor eƛin, ža bikori sida ħala xec meža, neła mežur royxosi žukłi šebiƛin.
translation: The boy said to them: "Let go of the snake, what's the evil thing it has done to you?!"

text: Didurgo šebigo roqsi eƛin ži ƛ’iräy din oqsi min oq.
translation: Whatever and however it happened, [said?], I forgave you now, so forgive me too.

text: Bik’anon bik’in, xexoyä eniwq esin, yaqˤuł sida aħozä elus tušman bexursiƛin.
translation: The rabbit's young went and told its mother: "Today a shepherd has killed our enemy."

text: Bazarganbi mešizaxozo aħoderno bayn esirno nesiq, howt’aza bitor łeno ħumukulin bižin ik’ix žek’u ukaynč’a deberƛin.
translation: The merchants came to the shepherd with the calves and asked him: "Have you seen a man leading five camels going there?"

text: Nesiƛ’ Alimaħamaƛin ci zown.
translation: His name was Alimagomed.

text: Ža ik’in kʷaxatow Ħusenno ayn.
translation: He went, and soon also Hussein came.

text: Nesir riyx zown suƛƛitow nełas q’ˤimbi reč’no roɣinč’inäy, neła ħal bayrxanusiłi.
translation: He knew that if he didn't cut off all the heads at once, he wouldn't defeat it.

text: Žin č’agu yołƛax ža baħarči.
translation: It is said that this brave younge man is still alive today.

text: Žan šušin q’ulhu bet’unosi, žedus xabar boqno bełiƛ’ bik’anƛin.
translation: After they burried him and the funeral feast was over, their plan was: "We'll go hunting."

text: Xizyo q’ˤidayn izin di dowderno nexno babiwqor eƛan, horo howži yiłas meč’ beč’oƛin.
translation: Then, when I got up, I will come to you and say to my father: 'Come now, cut off her neck!'

text: Nełäz ruɣuƛ’ bercingo xal boyzaƛ’, neła bˤula gibaq bitirxot’a bižin.
translation: When I examined its hoofprints thoroughly, [I saw that] it only touches the dust with one hoof.

text: Igtow ɣˤaɣˤun izin Tawadä nesis roƛ’i baqruni kid Ražbadinqo yukarno.
translation: Tavadi became very happy and showed Razhbadin the girl that became his love.

text: ƛ’iri xabarkin boynč’ey yeda razin oqno, hunixor eniya erno zowali žedu q’ˤunqorno sist’a magalabin teƛno.
translation: Without further discussion he agreed and gave the breads that his mother made for the way to the two of them, one for each.

text: Hunix nełade dandir c’axru boc’ä nełqay ža dawla boɣƛ’oräy, zira eƛin:
translation: On the way, when a wolf that was coming towards him wanted to take the prey away from him, the fox said:

text: Radiru rigłin riynč’ey, bežet’ani esnaza daʕba bodin, hič’č’a bercinaw kid deber šida yiča yayxƛin.
translation: The other brothers didn't know what good he had done, and began to argue: "Why must the most beautiful girl be for you?"

text: Xanez ɣˤutka tełxor yik’a nično boqno eniw madaxay bitor yik’iłnoanu.
translation: The mother was ashamed to go inside the king's house and wasn't able to go further than the yard.

text: K’ox äƛirugon bełiqu ƛ’irin zowerno, cey raładyoƛ’aza boržizi boqno.
translation: A second time the eagle seated the hunter above and flew towards the sea.

text: Yiła nełaq (ašunoq) harizi rodin där ukrus nak’ila yołäsi kid yodiroƛƛin.
translation: She begged it (the rainbow), if she could get a girl with a silver pinkie.

text: Idurno ik’in, kidno yukarno užä eniwqor eƛin, eni, deber kumakyor di kid yowsƛin.
translation: When he went home, he showed the girl to his mother and saying: "Mother, I brought this girl for helping you."

text: Zamanałäy žedus žeduł c’aq’ muši biłin.
translation: Suddenly a very strong smell approached them.

text: Nedur resunäy mi däde sadaq ƛaƛin ižis neła di.
translation: 'If that's the case, you go together with me!', it [said and] lead me.

text: Elo yizi sidä sidƛ’o łeɣin, q’ˤanon akiłace biħanayn.
translation: There they grasped each other and fought until the two of them got tired.

text: Ža elay bok’ek’xo asazo t’awusä.
translation: The heaven peacock steals them.

text: Sida zamanał ža maħor oqno, nesä nesir eynodanix močin k’edin.
translation: After some time, he went out and searched a place for himself to work.

text: Musaqilično, ʕIsaqilično babiwz šudaxaɣor bik’ixƛin sida q’orol ɣˤanazaz ƛ’emiƛxorno bik’in kecnot’a bičix zown.
translation: Musaqilich and Isaqilich pretended to go to their father's grave, and went to a widow's hayloft to sleep there.

text: T’ogon žade puħorno yik’in, ƛ’iri yutin eniw, eso däq, šebi deber moƛał rukayx zowäƛin.
translation: There, the mother went beside him and insisted: "Tell me, what did you see in your dream?"

text: Rok’un rexun ʕomoyä zeyn iduɣor begirno.
translation: The donkey felt sorry and let the bear into the home.

text: Ža nesiq eƛin rok’äłrun, oƛixno sis eže žek’u k’oƛin, łu di goƛ’äƛin.
translation: Right after he said that [to him?], an old man appeared jumping: "Who called me?"

text: Ukayyä mežur diƛin, esiranix yeda iduɣor ik’in.
translation: He went into their home to ask, "Did you see me?"

text: Sida ɣudi ik’in, q’ˤuna ɣudi ik’in, łˤora äƛiza quł ʕAliqilič sida maydanƛ’or ayn.
translation: He went one day, two days, and on the third day Aliqilich came to a clearance.

text: Nesiderno yik’in yiła eƛin:
translation: She went to him and said:

text: Yaɣˤin tełer bezunosi, marɣalyos k’icu yołäsi užin esun, mesedyos žežu yołäsi kidno yesun.
translation: When they opened it and looked inside, they found a boy with a pearl tooth and a girl with a golden ponytail.

text: Neła ɣˤanazas raq’ˤu bˤeƛ’ zown.
translation: That woman had many sheep.

text: Nesä eƛin, wallahi, xedyor mi dunyalƛ’ay yaq’ˤun yetix, šebi roqłin nesä mi bat’a yadinč’iƛin.
translation: He said: "By God, your husband loves you more than the world; no matter what happens, he won't leave you."

text: Iqira yisä iħurƛ’oräy ʕUmar onočaƛ’or utin oxin.
translation: When he began to catch up, Umar turned into a hen and ran away.

text: Yiła reƛ’a atinč’eytow ža k’emot’ q’ˤidar yegirno.
translation: Not touching it with the hand, she let the crate down.

text: Nazon yedu, q’ˤidakin yiča yegirinč’ey, łočyaƛ’ yoɣxo zown.
translation: Everyone, not even letting her sit down, invited her to a dance.

text: Boƛƛäɣor bayzaƛ’ortow, gulun, nełaƛ’gon ƛ’irisini ža č’uħarawni žek’un ɣˤuɣˤuruni neł iħä butirno caƛin, boɣno bižin ƛ’iräy wˤałer.
translation: Before they reached the middle, the horse and the proud man on it got stuck in that muddy river, and it pulled them downward.

text: Tawadä Ražbadin bužzi odin, nediw ħalikataw šebin debeł teł roda dey pikrukin zownč’u, rodäsin anuƛin.
translation: Tavadi convinced Razhbadin: "I would not have he idea to do such a mean thing to you, I haven't done anything."

text: Žan eƛin, ʕomoyä baqruce haraƛ’no boyn ħoʕoƛin.
translation: The donkey said that, and shouted as loud as he could.

text: Howlay bik’in idur baynosi, bertenno bodin, esnaza žeda žedus esiw Ismaʕilxo yegirno.
translation: From there they went home and arranged the marriage and the brothers married off their own sister to Ismail.

text: Mi ʕadalaw uži äsuƛin, Maħama nesiqor eƛin.
translation: You are a stupid boy.", Magomed said to him.

text: Ža yiqiranƛingon soder izin ƛ’irir aynosi, ža elo yesuxanu - hudu di qoqoƛix soder izixƛin.
translation: I go upward to take her with me, but I can't find her -- so I am laughing, when I go up.

text: Howlotow xanez kidbes nesiqor roƛ’i boqno.
translation: Then the princess fell in love with him.

text: Nedur resunäyƛin, - eƛin eženi esiya, - eli łˤonon xander bik’a bay - ele howlo räy łu mesed bäsrułiƛin”.
translation: "If it turns out this way," said the eldest brother, "we three must go to the king -- there it will be known who has taken the gold."

text: Baq’ˤu mix bik’izaƛ’orgon hemedurtow weeƛirno zeyä cek’i.
translation: After quite some time has passed, the bear caused the goat kid to bleat again.

text: Ismaʕilä esirno, t’o sasaqor iča behizi aqäƛin.
translation: Ismail asked: "May I spend the night here?"

text: Meč’oƛäyr bayäru cedisƛin” - eƛin zira.
translation: "He was named 'up-to-the-neck'", said the fox.

text: Elaɣor yizi gamiƛ’no zown, raładno boɣno bik’a bayx zown.
translation: They came back in a boat to cross the sea.

text: Nełaz ža q’aʕidaƛ’ žedun šušin, nesä nesir azbarno riqirno, barun yown, eniwn nesä nesidäɣor yižin.
translation: He burried them in his own manner, took the palace for himself, married a wife and brought his mother to himself.

text: Haqoƛer reƛno risno ažoƛ’ bayn ɣʷˤadi heqnosi, zira nełxor eƛin:
translation: When a crow sat down onto the tree with a piece of meat in its beak, the fox said to him:

text: Q’ˤimzo ʕaƛar ža t’umi baynosi, akä q’ˤida yäčiru sida kidbez koyä heqno.
translation: When the pigeon had come to its own village, it sat down in the hand of a girl who was sitting under a window.

text: Esnaza eƛin, mi äxuzay iłe izin ayzaƛ’, debez ħurmatyoxay ela beže bertenno bodin sis kid debex yowa yoł.
translation: The brothers said: "Because you came back like from the dead, we will organize a big marriage out of respect for you and marry of a girl to you.

text: Hemełƛ’otow bik’in žeda žedu esnazaq esin.
translation: Immediately they went and told those sisters of this.

text: Yizä yeda uži, elus ʕeziyaw maʕišatno yołƛin, žeda žedude iduɣorno ižin, deber rätiru šeƛ’u łet’in šeƛ’oƛin bitazoni ɣˤutka tełer egirno.
translation: They lead this boy into their own home[, saying]: "We're very rich, pick any clothes you want and put them on.", and let him into the next room.

text: Elay bitor nesir roda rayxosi šebin debertow räyƛin.
translation: And what you have to do to him from then on, you know yourself."

text: Ɣʷˤadi q’ʷaridno boqno, hawaƛ’osi oz rutira bik’in.
translation: The crow became sad and went to gather the airborne army.

text: Sadaq zawru halmaɣłi šebi yisir roqäƛin ħayran boqno ƛexun.
translation: All the friends who were there wondered what happened to him.

text: Xanä nesir roƛik’u nesä nesis xanłin teƛno, kidno yegirno.
translation: The king gave him half of his kingdom and married his daughter off to him.

text: Eɣenigon esiya eƛin:
translation: The youngest brother said:

text: Elo yisir besun bexasin, untaraw bexaniƛ’or bayäsin xalq’i.
translation: There he found dead and terminally ill people.

text: Babiws esiw bečedaw žek’u zown.
translation: The father's brother was a rich man.

text: Ža nesä äƛirun, bet’un k’oƛin ɣunoqayn boqno babiwz koyär nexno.
translation: After he had said this, it jumped up and ran [flew] off the tree and came into the father's hand.

text: Sasaqosi moƛaxay yizinosi, Bilq’isdä Allahqo harizi rodin, eli q’ˤanon debi laɣbi deber betixozołäysi besunäy, howt’otow elus ruħ boɣoƛin.
translation: In the morning, when she got up from her sleep, Bilqisdi prayed to Allah: "If we two are not among the slaves you love, then take our souls right here!"

text: Dey xediw nexxoƛin yiła yeda nuci teƛxosi bazargan nesi žade puräza ɣamasya teł uqˤno.
translation: "My husband is coming!", she [said and] hid that honey-selling merchant in the box next to the boy.

text: Hemedur oxin yeda ik’iƛ’oräy, yisił dandir q’ˤanon esnabi c’oxxo - sis c’odoraw, sideni - ʕantaw.
translation: While he was running, he met two brothers - one was clever, the other one stupid.

text: Deber rig räyƛin, eƛin Tawadä, deber t’o č’alʕizin roqno räsu.
translation: "You know well," said Tavadi, "that you've grown bored of here.

text: Mi žek’anix ɣˤilya bowsƛin eƛin, Alimaħama baruz gugya ɣˤilya basada oqno.
translation: "I brought a stick to beat you!", said Alimagomed and began to hit his wife's back with the stick.

text: Žan nesis bit’araw yołƛin, eƛin kidbä, dowqor beč’no bigu hibo neƛsiyołi, nełan ʕuraw bigu kumak badiƛin.
translation: "That of him is true," said the girl, "if he carved a good cane and gave it to you, it would have been a good help."

text: Łox äƛirugon esirno bara xedyoq, šida mi q’ʷaridƛin.
translation: For the third time, the wife asked his husband: "Why are you sad?"

text: Dey qoqoƛu łinaƛ’ rägiru riya retin resunäy, esan debeq di.
