translation: If it is the case that you want to know what I laughed about, I'll tell you.

text: Exa adäza babiya užizaqor wasi bodin, were, dey xexbi, di exunosi, šibaw ruzman ɣudƛ’o däz šudaƛ’orno nexno meža q’ulhut’a t’et’roƛin.
translation: Before he died, the father did his testament: "Well, my children, after I have died, come to my grave every Friday and read a funeral prayer each time."

text: ƛ’irir žas muʕalim ayn.
translation: Suddenly the son's teacher came.

text: T’ok’ow heresi mec esanix rok’ƛ’or rayärnoƛin.”
translation: Don't come up with the idea of telling lies again.

text: Yedu q’ay idur bayrƛin baruqorno eƛin, babiw užił xizay k’oƛin.
translation: "Bring these goods into the house!", the father told his wife and ran behind his son.

text: Yisi žar moƛał neł gogaƛäz łäs oreznin, bišʷan rizäsi c’in, qacisgon ɣeryabin rukayn.
translation: In his dream, the boy saw a pond of water under the meadow, food, a fire and a pile of firewood.

text: Neširu sadaq kecnosi, Ismaʕilä baħarayłno, nesä nesiłno roƛƛo bˤałäyn eqerno, debi ged ent’aɣor yaynäy, debi di meč’ beč’a yołƛin riyrno.
translation: At night, when they were sleeping together, Ismail put a dagger inbetween his bride and himself: "If your shirt comes onto my side, I will cut your neck!", he explained.

text: Sida quł, babiya igorno goƛ’in kidbazaq riyrno, sasaq eli bet’erbaqi bodanix maħor boqa bayƛin.
translation: One day, the father called them to himself and explained to the daughters: "Tomorrow we have to go outside to do work."

text: Hudayzikon uži bełiƛ’ ik’in, qˤayn č’aq’ˤirno xizor utin ayn.
translation: Next day the son went hunting and came back with the hare.

text: Zirulaw, mi äƛiru šebinon rown eli nexsi!
translation: "Mr.(?) Fox, we have come to bring what you said!

text: Nełaz ħamorok’uƛ’ zawru muhruzaxor, ža Maryames yałru riyn.
translation: From the birthmarks that were on her breast, he knew it was Maryam.

text: Sida quł žedu sadaq hunix boqno.
translation: One day they went together on a way.

text: Din zows elo bertenyoł, hudun mešok’labi dandi at’iłsi.
translation: I was there at the wedding as well, but only moustaches got wet.

text: Bežet’anibi ʕurawtow ɣˤaɣˤun bizin, nesiƛ’ Tabuldiƛin cin erno.
translation: The oldest ones were very glad and gave him the name Tabuldi.

text: Žeduder ƛirbałix din aysi, där žeda rigu ƛirbałin roysi, din nexsi mežuq yedu kesu esanix.
translation: I came to them as a guest as well, they treated me great and I have come to tell you this story.

text: T’ay rił łu ragäƛin?”
translation: "Who licked the butter out of it?"

text: Yisä užin bogmoƛ’ goƛ’in, nesir beže barkalan bodin, ganin teƛno egirno.
translation: He invited the boy for dinner, thanked him thoroughly, gave him one bull and let him go.

text: Xizyo ečruni ɣˤanabi ʕaƛos xalq’i batizarno yik’in eƛin, däde idur sis uži ayäsi yoł, šebigo boqłin hunar boqinč’inäy ža t’orkin keze aqinč’iƛin.
translation: Afterwards, the old woman went to where the village's people gathered and said: "A boy has come to my home, if he had no talent, he wouldn't have come here."

text: Sisxoli ciqqo ƛäxuru kidbä ɣunoz gonda teł sasaqorsi ɣudi qˤaširno.
translation: The girl who stayed in the forest alone [VERB] inside a hole in a tree, then the next day started to dawn.

text: Nełaq yoqäsi q’ˤano ƛˤeb yoł.
translation: There are two leaves growing on it.

text: Bexuratow bexurxo yiła di hoboyƛin, boc’i bet’un k’oƛin bizin boxin elay.
translation: "He's going to kill me now!", the wolf jumped up and ran away.

text: Eženi esiya eƛin Ismaʕilqor, mi č’uħizi oqinč’inäy, debex ela elus esiw yegiranƛin yoł eli.
translation: The oldest brother said: "If you are not too proud(?), we will marry off our sister to you."

text: Oƛƛozo esiwq esirnosi, nesä eƛin:
translation: When he asked the middle brother, this one said:

text: Zowsƛin”, eƛin nesä.
translation: "It was", said he.

text: Welečo, aq’iw maħ yołalini žek’u, oxi uqˤło.
translation: "Look out, man with the foreign spirit, run away and hide!

text: Hemełƛ’o yisir raładyoz bitazoni lilyox neła kidbes ɣˤutku rukayn.
translation: Thereupon he saw that girl's house on the other side of the sea.

text: Biša rodanix barun yegirno, yeda maħor oqno, bˤeƛ’ rowa ħonƛ’aɣor ik’ixƛin.
translation: He let his wife prepare the meal and went outside: "I am going to the mountains to bring the sheep."

text: Yisis rigu rok’ƛ’o anuƛin, nesiqay zebun yoɣno boc’i boxin.
translation: "He's bad-tempered", [thought] the wolf [and] ran away from him.

text: Elo teł esiw esuzaƛ’, roda šebin łaħin ƛexun yeda.
translation: When he found his brother inside there, he didn't know what to do.

text: Yedagon et’un k’oƛin guluƛ’orno izin, xizazagon ħurženyabin ricin, q’ˤimƛ’ogon k’ukno erno elay bitor oxin.
translation: He, however, jumped onto the horse, tied the bags onto it, put the cap onto his head and rode away from there.

text: Xizyo Ħon-Bahadurä babiwq nesir yukayru moƛałäy esin, howža moƛał yukayru kidbeł xizay ik’a retinƛin.
translation: Then Mountain~Hero told his father about the dream he had seen: "I want to go after the girl that I had seen in that dream."

text: Ža ħalt’i mežur di buytown badinč’iƛin, uži razi oqnoanu.
translation: "That work I won't do for you for free.", the boy disagreed.

text: Mi där madaħ esus yołi exura zows.
translation: Had I found you outside, I would have killed you.

text: T’ok’ow roda šebinon koƛ’inč’ey, hisno, hiƛun, ʕoƛ’no, nucosgon bankan bisno, yedu kid beqaɣor yoqno.
translation: Not knowing what to do anymore, the girl took the wool, the comb, the spindle and a jar of honey and went into the sun.

text: Elo zawru miƛ’es c’ik’iw reƛ k’et’ur ƛexun.
translation: So the ram's entire meat, which was there, remained for the cat.

text: Sis zown beže, oc’ira łˤora kilometra bexora is.
translation: There was a 13 kilometer long ox.

text: T’akanyar bayru mesedno ħiƛ’oqax butirno čantan eqerno, łˤonon besuron bižin uži iduɣor ik’in.
translation: The boy gathered the gold that came int the glass into a handkerchief, put it inside his pocket and carried the three fish home.

text: Nesis ʕomoy bičinč’ey ħoʕƛix zown.
translation: His donkey was shouting without stopping.

text: ʕOƛno ħonno, ʕoƛno iħun xecin ik’inosi, sida ʕaƛar ayn.
translation: After he walked past seven mountains and seven rivers, he came into a village.

text: Kaɣat t’et’ernosi, Ražbadin ħayran oqno ƛexun yedu šebi daq roqäƛin.
translation: After he had read the letter, Razhbadin wondered in surprise: "What happened to me?"

text: Xizyo madaxosinin ayn, xandärno yižin neła nełas muradyołäy esin, wallahi, di žax debi kid esira nexäsi yołƛin.
translation: Then the house-serf came, lead her to the king, and she told [him] of her wish: "By God, I have come to ask out the kings daughter to marry my son."

text: Hudu t’ok’ow šebi rodanƛin, užin ižin babiw iduɣor ik’ix zown.
translation: "So what more shall I do?", the father [said and] lead the boy home.

text: Elo yisir siskin ukayza žek’utow esun anu.
translation: There he didn't find any person, wherever he looked.

text: Idurno yik’in užin č’arik’no eniya eƛin, eliz mada łuƛa ixiw ɣˤutku rodinƛin.
translation: The mother went home, woke up the boy and said: "Someone built a big house outside of ours."

text: Meži łˤonon esnabin yoł, mežułäy sider gurow di yuyn yäqinč’i.
translation: "You are three brothers and I won't get to any of you except for one.

text: Neła ukruƛ’aygon xiriyaw kesu däqno ƛexus mežuq esanix.
translation: But the tale is more valuable than the silver and remains for me to tell you.

text: Ħonƛ’ay ža bˤeƛ’q’ˤun bayrno, bexʷayn nełas reƛ xanqo rac’ernosi, ža saɣ oqno.
translation: That sheep was brought from the mountain, slaughtered and when he let the king eat its meat, he got cured.

text: Šebintow roda koƛ’inč’ey kid ƛexunosi, nełder zey bayn.
translation: When - not knowing what to do - the girl was remaining there, a bear came to her.

text: Qalameł adäza butirno gulun bičirno, qˤaƛin Ismaʕilä, ʕArabuzan, hič’č’a deber rukada rätiru łinaƛ’no yezanix maħor yoqƛin.
translation: Ismail came before the fortress and let his horse wait there and shouted: "Arabuzan, come outside to look at what you want to see most!"

text: Žedaƛ’or ʕišq’un erno yisis bežet’ani esnabi žeda žeduz ʕomoyzaƛ’no zown ɣudes elor baħnayx zown.
translation: His older brothers fell in love with them and rode to there every day on their donkeys.

text: Alay-bulayƛin eƛixozo guluqorno qˤaƛin, ʕoƛira putce yoq’ˤiw kedonno yisno, ža Ismaʕilƛ’or yoqno.
translation: She shouted after her horse names Alay-bulay, took a 7-pood heavy sledgehammer and went to Ismail.

text: Sida quł ciqqo yałzay, xedyoder eže žek’un ayn, esirno, šebi deber roqno, šida mi q’ʷarid oqnoƛin.
translation: One day, while being in the forest, and old man came to the husband and asked him: "What happened to you, why are you so sad?"

text: Mi äƛiruxor rodanƛin, eniwr rožin teƛno, uži ʕaƛay maħor hunix oqno.
translation: The son gave his mother his word: "I'll do what you said!", and went on his way out of the village.

text: Xizyogon hemedurtow yeženi esiya ža uži c’enno ʕiyayrno.
translation: Then the eldest sister pinched the boy again and made him cry.

text: Elaɣor bik’iƛ’oräy, zirude dandir ixiw bˤeƛ’es reqenno, žedał tełgon bik’ix ixiw ɣˤʷayn keze boqno.
translation: When they went there, the fox met a big flock of sheep and a large dog walking among them.

text: Hunix mekʷa c’oxnosi, žeda sosi iguni halmaɣes bišʷa rac’no łiyrno.
translation: When they became hungry on their way, the first ate up the good friend's food.

text: Ditow nexanƛin, eƛin eniya.
translation: "I'm coming myself!", said the mother.

text: Eženi esiya beɣet’ani esnazaqor eƛin:
translation: The eldest brother said to his younger brothers:

text: Yizi bukayrun, aždaħä zudäy roɣno ixiw riris ɣun yiził adäzar rayrno kurno.
translation: When he saw them, the dragon tore out an alder tree from its roots and threw it before them.

text: Yisiq qu ƛˤeb yaynosi, riynč’ey reƛił ƛuza riqin.
translation: When he became twenty years old, he found bones in his meat.

text: Howłaƛ’ dey emadun łiys, keca zamanno bays.
translation: With that my story ends, now sleeping time has come.

text: Nesiz kumakyoƛ’aza di elo č’agu ƛexus exurxo zawru.
translation: They wanted to kill me, but thanks to his help I stayed alive there.

text: Hemeła maqˤizo ʕaƛasi där moƛał yukayru mesedyos nak’ila yałruni kid yiqinč’inäy di anƛ’imoq’az äxuƛin.”
translation: "Then, if I don't get the girl from the village far away with the golden pinky, that I saw in my dream, I will die in a week."

text: Dey di roɣno ozuri rac’xo, debi min rac’oƛin” - žawab teƛno zira.
translation: "I eat the eye I have removed, eat yours too!", answered the fox.

text: Łoxäƛirugon xanä ʕaq’lus xalq’i sadaqor butirnosi, Kusa mesedyos ʕabasin yižin elor ik’in.
translation: When the king gathered his advisors for the third time, Kusa carried a golden coin with himself.

text: Qazaqza eƛix zown, yisir ʕazabno teƛno exurno kuranƛin.
translation: The Georgians were saying: "We will torture him, kill him and then throw him away!"

text: Babiwn kidno maħor boqno, na ža uqˤełnoƛin bezu-bˤoƛ’anix.
translation: Father and daughter came out to find out where the boy had hidden.

text: Hudu där riys nełas keč’oƛ’asi ozuri rexasi yałrułiƛin”.
translation: So I knew that it has a left eye that is blind."

text: Uži xabar esa oqno, di ʕaƛa zows.
translation: The boy began to tell a story: "I was in a village.

text: K’edin esurno yizä Ražbadin žeduz odoman Tawadider ižin.
translation: They searched and found Razhbadin, and lead him to their leader Tavadi.

text: Xizyogon žoyä eƛin kidbeqor:
translation: Then the boy said to the girl:

text: K’eneč’ k’eč’ ɣudi rocu.
translation: All's well that ends well.

text: Žan bitor yiq’ˤirno waħaz egin ik’inosi, deber elo ixiw gondu yukay, elogon tełxor oqnosi, q’ˤida yičäsi eniw yäsu.
translation: After you have moved it aside and went below it, you will see a big hole, and inside you will see your mother sitting there.

text: Ža hunix oqnosi xalq’imo eč’erxo zown, äk’ino elaɣor min ok’äk’ƛin.
translation: When he got on his way, the people were stopping him: "Don't go here, you will be stolen as well!"

text: Murad šebi debi yoł?
translation: "What is your wish?

text: Horo eli sidqo sis biłaniƛäyn, dey rok’u riq’ˤaniƛäyn, łˤonon debi užibi däz žaz mečʷa bexuranix dadäɣor begirnäy där räti.
translation: So that we resemble one another and that you approach my heart, I'd love if you could send your three sons to me so I could kill them instead of my son."

text: Žedu sadaq ričix zown.
translation: They lived together.

text: Aħos nesix rok’un rexun, ža icin zawruni ročyabi reč’-t’it’in roɣno.
translation: The shepherd had deep sympathy for him, and cut off the ropes that restrained him.

text: Madaħ mečoƛ’ ža c’aq’ t’eq’erbašiƛ’or ičix zown.
translation: In places outside, he was very cautious.

text: Elay ža maħor łi roƛäk’rur, žamaʕatyo ʕaƛos boƛƛik’u ric’q’i neƛxoƛax.
translation: They say that the assembly of the village would give half their wealth in order to get the water out there.

text: Neła ža imyoq yicin zawruni ɣˤanabi elar yutirxo, t’ar yutirxo esayn q’ˤimaku yiłe yodin yegirno.
translation: She washed the woman who was tied to the pole, turning her hither and thither, and let her look like a grown-up.

text: Nedur resunäyƛin, - eƛin kidbä, - meži łˤoran esnaza sidä sidƛ’ay bežin šebin riszaƛ’, mežatow xal bodo di łux yik’a yayƛin”.
translation: "If that is so", said the girl, "you three brothers found such things, one better than the other, now figure out: to whom I shall go?

text: Sasiłzaƛ’or xan idurno ik’inanu, č’uħaraw šeƛ’un šeƛ’unanu.
translation: Until it got dark, the king neither went home nor put on gorgeous clothing.

text: Hemeła xabaryoł yizi yałzay, nesi nukarä xanqo esix zown, yaqˤuł bełiquz žoyä bercinaw aɣi č’aq’ˤirno bižisƛin.
translation: While that conversation was going on, that henchman was telling the king, "Today the hunter's son killed a bird and carried it home."

text: Hemełƛ’o elo akiłäza łäs bic’äsi tungin bižin Ražbadinxo esäruni kid yik’äsi zown.
translation: The girl that Razhbadin asked [= his fiancée] carried a copper jug filled with water and was going to a window up there.

text: Yedagon onočaƛ’orno utin at’ rac’a iħun.
translation: The other one turned into a hen and began to eat the wheat.

text: Tabanaw manzilno xecin ik’inosi, yisir his xuxix yeže ɣˤanabi yesun.
translation: After having gone a long distance, he found a woman combing wool.

text: Nesän esin, di dunyalƛ’otow ci rik’äsi doxtur yołƛin.
translation: He said, " I am a doctor known in the world."

text: Barur riyx žaqˤuł xediw exuxosi yałrułi.
translation: The wife knew that today the husband would die.

text: Asoqtow bitin žedu ɣˤaɣˤu bizizaq, qiƛ’orok’uƛ’äy ƛ’irir best’alawni uži ayzaƛ’.
translation: They were happy sky-high, when their step-son happened to come.

text: Yeza yak’iru ɣˤanabi xizor yutin yayn eƛin, xanez kidbä xexbi riqarzäy soder łˤono buci yołƛin.
translation: The woman who had gone looking returned and said: "It has been three months since the king's daughter has conceived her child."

text: Ražbadinä reƛ’ibin risno, Tawadisno nesiz halmaɣzasno hunin regirno, xizor iduɣor utin.
translation: Razhbadin took the hands of Tavadi and his friends, sent them on their way and returned home.

text: Neła quł sasaqozoxgon ɣurox žade sadaq babiwn ik’in.
translation: The next day in the early morning, the father went together with the boy to pasture the cows.

text: Ceyä eƛin, mi di bexarno, xizyo di nagon boqłin debez rigzar bäsu, mežustow kici yoł, radiru rigłi šuƛ’ixosi anuƛin.
translation: The eagle said: "Don't kill me! I'll will be a help for you somewhere; you have a proverb: 'A done good is not forgotten'."

text: Ža debeq äsirus haqu azirodoƛ, mi där łˤora ɣudi ʕeze roqace biša eroƛin, esiwqorno eƛin, išno-utin kecno yeda.
translation: "May the mouth of the one who told you that freeze over! Prepare me food enough for three days!", he said, ate and slept.

text: Nesis łˤono kid zown.
translation: He had three daughters.

text: Žan risno iduɣor ik’iƛ’orey, xizayn bayn neł bikorä esirno, deber ža ħiƛ’oqu reynoyrakin koƛ’ixosi yołäƛin.
translation: And he took it and while he was going home, that snake followed him and asked: "Do you know how to get this handkerchief to work?"

text: Idur ayzaƛ’or sidä ħaƛun, sidä ħaƛun ža łi nesiqayn hemedurtow łiyrno.
translation: When he went home, someone wanted to drink and another one wanted to drink, so this water was empty as well for him.

text: Qacisni gulu ɣˤʷayƛ’orno butin, k’oƛin bik’in xanes kid xanno.
translation: The wooden horse turned into a dog, ran and bit the king's daughter.

text: Yeɣeni kidbeq esirnosi yołi, deber rätiru zamanał mi äƛiru šebin roda ħaduraw di yołƛin, eniwqor eƛin.
translation: When she asked the youngest daughter, she said to her mother: "I'm ready to do whatever you say, whenever you want."

text: Hoboy q’orolaz užä yizi baƛ’ärułi riynosi, ʕoƛiran esnaza q’ut’i bodin ža exuranixƛin.
translation: Now, after the seven brothers knew that the widow's son had cheated them, they agreed to kill him.

text: Debeƛ’no gurħizi oqno bexurƛ’oräysi mi di xʷasar boysƛin, di nełqor eƛis.
translation: 'I felt sorry for you and rescued you from getting killed', I said to it.

text: Xizyo eženi esiya eƛin, q’ˤanon bexa adäza sis exun riguƛin.
translation: Then the older brother said: "Before two die, it's better if [just] one dies.

text: Rigu bišan rodin, sida quł neširu babiya Ismaʕil iduɣor goƛ’in.
translation: The father prepared good food and one day in the evening he invited Ismail into his house.

text: Šida mi elo nece mixƛ’or yičäƛin, xanes kid c’ok’inayn nełqor.
translation: "Why did it take you so much time?", the princess scolded her.

text: Elay Ražbadin Tawadiƛ’ ƛ’irir ik’in, nesiqor c’ok’inayn, yedu šebi šebin mi där rodä bertenyołerno goƛ’inƛin.
translation: From there, Razhbadin went up to Tavadi and scolded him: "What is that thing you did to me, inviting me to a marriage?!"

text: Bayn yizirabi ƛirbałix sid žek’ude idur.
translation: Then came home to a man as guests.

text: T’eker šebi eƛakin riyn anu nełaz šilazaƛ’ roqxosi šebin.
translation: The goat didn't even notice what happened on its horns.

text: Hunix ik’iƛ’oräy, neła ħalaq’awni gula nesiqor eƛin, aždaħ kecäsi yałzay, buq bayäsi bičixosi, q’ˤiday bizinosi ža bičixozoni gogaƛ’ ɣedu boqxosi, elo nełaqgon šet’ur k’ešonayx xexoybi ričixosiƛin.
translation: While he went on his way, that thin horse said to him: "While the dragon sleeps, the sun goes up, and after he gets up a shadow appears on the meadow, there around it the youngs are playing.

text: Užir łinasnotow čaran bexun, ži ik’ix di dunyalqoƛin, eƛin oqno ik’in elay sida baydanƛ’or ayn.
translation: Now that all the possibilities are gone for the boy, he said "I shall wander through the world now", and began to go away until he came to a field.

text: Ža ɣudes ciqaɣort’a ik’ix Allahqo harizi oqxo zown: däłäy žek’ułersi žek’u odoƛin.
translation: Every day, he went into the forest and asked God: Make me a respectable man.

text: Howła gulułäy ɣˤʷay bodoƛin”.
translation: Make a dog out of this horse!

text: Kut’yoƛ’aza puƛ’aza nełaƛ’ nucos sik zown”.
translation: On its right side there was a waterskin with honey.

text: Žan sadaq yižin uži babiwz ʕaƛaɣor ik’in.
translation: The boy took her together to his father's village.

text: Nasi yołƛin, išara bodin esirno ʕArabuzanä.
translation: "Which one is she?", signaled and asked Arabuzan.

text: Howži yisi ʕAliqiličä yiziqor eƛin:
translation: Now Aliqilich said to them:

text: Baruƛ’ ci Bilq’isdiƛin zown.
translation: The wife's name was Bilqisdi.

text: Mi äƛiruxor ričiƛƛin, razi oqno neł ɣˤutkos bet’erhan.
translation: "May it be as you said!", agreed the master of that house.

text: Boyxosi ħalt’i łiynosi, esnabin iduɣor rik’in.
translation: When they finished the work, the sisters went home.

text: Paxrudinno zown, nesisno kinawtow bˤeƛ’ rexun räsuƛin.
translation: Pakhrudin thought that also all of his sheep must have died.

text: ƛ’iräy reku roɣnosi, čanyas maħi roƛixno.
translation: After they had taken away the manure, a goat's tail appeared.

text: Nełaz rok’ƛ’or rayn, yeda däder igu žek’u aynč’uƛin.
translation: She thought: "He didn't come to me as a good man."

text: Bˤeƛägon qˤay łäƛ’or begirno.
translation: And the pig sent the hare for water.

text: Elay bik’in cek’ä iħäy łäs yic’no mušno yown nexƛ’oräy, nełał xizay k’oƛin zey nexxo zown.
translation: The goat kid went away from there, brought a pot filled with water from the river and when it came back, a bear was running behind it.

text: Mamalayägon, di rešonazałäy šebin rut’ix bäčiƛin, eƛin.
translation: The rooster then said: "I will be collecting things out of the garbage."

text: Xizyo xanä nazon nukarzaq riyrno, c’ohor yoł t’oza sis reƛ’a anusi, meži nasin nesił xizay boq, ža meža iqira ayƛin.
translation: Afterwards the king explained to all his henchmen: "The thief lacks one hand; y'all get behind him, you must catch him!"

text: Ža bac’run, nełłäy yałru xanes kid yoqno.
translation: After she ate it, she turned back from what she was, into the princess.

text: Hemedur ƛˤebrebi rik’in nesis uži aq’ˤiłno ʕoloqan oqno, baru yowa mix bayn nesis.
translation: So the years went by, his son grew up and became a young man, and the time came for him to marry.

text: Zamanaƛ’aza ža rutin rik’in eženä egirno zawruni uži äčiza puƛ’aɣor.
translation: After some time it fell to the side where the old man had placed the boy.

text: Hudu neła mežur łi neƛxosi anaƛin? - esirno yisä neła q’orolaq.
translation: "So he's not giving you water?", he asked the widow.

text: Elur yiłaƛ’ay mustaħiq’aw močin bäsunč’i, ži eƛnoqor ħadur roqanix zamanno bayn, howt’otow ričanƛin, žedas ħukmu boqno.
translation: "We won't find a place more suitable than this one; now the time has come to prepare for the winter; we will live here." was their decision.

text: Žan eƛin hemedurtow ozƛäy gugin.
translation: Having said this, he flew out of sight.

text: Nedur resunäy, däqay mežur ħumukulibin, žedaƛ’osi šebinon räqinč’iƛin, eƛin oqno yeda užin ičin.
translation: "If that's the case, you won't get the camels and what is on them from me," said the boy and remained.

text: Ziyan tupiƛ teƛno, yeda uži uyxor bazaryaɣor ik’in, žan teƛnogon šeƛ’u risanƛin.
translation: The boy sold his cow for the rifle and went straight to the market with the intention of selling it and buying clothes.

text: Neła eƛin nesiqor:
translation: She said to him:

text: Šebi t’or yayäsiƛin?”
translation: Who came here?"

text: Eɣenigon esiw ža biqiranƛin oqno, hemedurtow nesiqayn boxin bok’ełno.
translation: The young brother tryed to catch it, and just as before it escaped and ran away from him.

text: Biš-butin bizinosi, zeyä yisiq esirno, łinał xizay, šebin retin mi maħor oqäsiƛin.
translation: After the bear has eaten, he got up and asked, "What are you looking for in your trip?"

text: Mi badiru ħurmatno där ʕezen boqno ƛ’iräza k’oƛäsi yoł.
translation: And your respect was enough for me, more than necessary.

text: Xizyo xanä nesä nesider goƛ’in barus esnabin, nukarbin, ža q’orolayn.
translation: After that, the king invited to himself the wife's sisters, the henchmen and that widow.

text: Yałi ža c’aq’aw baħarči zowsƛin.
translation: "Otherwise he was an excellent brave young man."

text: Nediwtow q’ʷariłi bayzaƛ’, dahawtow yuyn ʕiyayn, bisno sis bexora hibon łoxt’a ža bok’xo nasin boc’an, zeybin maħor roxirno.
translation: Because of such a tragedy, she began to cry a little, took a long stick, beated it three times and chased off all the wolves and bears.

text: Tełxor k’oƛin cek’i bok’äłrun, aceƛ zeys meč’no biqin.
translation: As soon as the goat kid ran inside and escaped, the bear's neck got stuck in the door.

text: Naysinan bezun yizi.
translation: They looked everywhere.

text: Mešizaxozo eƛin, buq bäturuntow muqno caƛin yizi mešibi ʕaƛaɣor rik’ixosiƛin.
translation: The one with the calves said: "Right after the sun sets, " ...

text: Bešerxotow bižix zown neła xanes oz, xizaɣor ƛäxurubi boxin bok’ełno.
translation: She took out and brought to fall that king's armies, and finally the remaining ones fled.

text: Bahadurä eƛin, howži di exasi žek’u yoł.
translation: The hero said: "Now I am a dying man.

text: Mi dey t’ok’ow baru anu esiw yołƛin.
translation: You are not my wife anymore, you're my sister."

text: Nesä eƛin, were meži dahaw wˤałer bik’i, bertenyołäy xalq’i maħor roxira rayxosi anu.
translation: He said: "Well, calm down a little, one must not chase away people from a marriage."

text: Yaqˤuł iłe ɣuden goƛ’a ay nesiq di iduɣor.
translation: "Tomorrow too, just as today, he must invite me to his home.

text: Halmaɣza eč’erno yeda, debez eniwr nełas hut’-mˤalin, kočorin rätinč’iƛin.
translation: The friends stopped him[, saying]: "Your mother won't like her face and hair!"

text: Bayzatow ʕoƛiran kawumoqosi rekarbi rixäsi resun.
translation: Where he arrived, he found keys hanging on the seven gates.

text: Xizyo neła esirno, ya heneš, yagi geni bet’uriyä mi t’ayƛin.
translation: Afterwards she asked: "Have you plucked an apple or a pear from here?"

text: Madax doxtur adoru ukayzaƛ’, acno yuqin ža tełxor ik’in.
translation: Having seen the naked doctor, the khan went inside and closed the door.

text: Din zows žeduz bertenyoł, hudun k’onč’abin roƛno, łočyaƛ’ k’oƛa oqinč’u.
translation: I was on this marriage as well, however, my legs hurted, so I didn't dance.

text: Elo tełäzay zurmasno, q’ilisno sas teqxo zown.
translation: There they heard the noise of a zurna and a drum from inside.

text: Baħarčä biłe salamno teƛno, yedu tełxor yoqno.
translation: She greeted him like a young man and came inside.

text: Adäz riłetow muhumabin teƛno egirno šibawz akä eqroƛin.
translation: As before, she gave him grains and sent him away[, saying]: "Put those in every window!"

text: Sasaqtow yizin, užin q’ˤogxo isno yedu eniwdäɣor yik’in.
translation: The next morning she got up, put her son into a hay bale and went to her mother.

text: ƛ’iri ayäsi ƛirba oqzaƛ’ aždaħa p’estow anusi kikxay łäs q’ut’u bic’a begirno.
translation: Because it was a guest who was coming, the dragon let him fill his jug with water from the spring without saying anything.

text: Howlotow kidno xecin bełiqubi žeda žeduz hunix bik’in.
translation: Leaving the girl there, the hunters went on their own way.

text: Ričiƛƛin, razi oqno ža, amma imadu esa adäza meža yiła ɣˤutkoq acno ħišin reka däqor neƛa ray.
translation: "So be it!", he agreed, "but before I tell the tale, you must lock the door of this house and give me the key."

text: Sida quł eženi esiya eƛin, yaqˤuł di bełiƛ’ buq boƛaxza puƛ’aɣor ik’ix.
translation: One day the oldest brother said: "Today I'll go hunting in the east.

text: Yiła esirno, yaqˤuł debez bara xexbiya royxosi yołƛin.
translation: She asked: "Is your wife giving birth to the children today?"

text: Uži yołi sida šaharyaɣorno ik’in, elo raht’ateles ħalt’i k’eyx aħnayx zown.
translation: The boy however went to a city and walked around searching for a work as a servant.

text: Ič’an eɣeniƛ’ C’iɣruk’ƛin ci zown.
translation: The name of the youngest was Tsighruk.

text: Hemedur däł adäzarno bayn, insanzo mecreƛ’ xabaryayn aɣä di mi ɣurox egiranƛin odäsi zownč’u, ži roda šebin anuƛinno eƛin ozƛäy gugisƛin.
translation: "So, a bird arrived in front of me and talked in human language, saying: 'I didn't give birth to you to have you pasture the cows. But now there's nothing to do.', and escaped out of sight.

text: Kidbez babiya, xanä, nasin doxturbi goƛ’in nesä nesiderno butirno eƛin:
translation: The girl's father - the king - called all doctors, gathered them around himself and said:

text: Sida quł kidbeder halmaɣbin rayno, yizä yizade sadaq yedu ełuyax yižin.
translation: One day, some (female) friends came to the daughter, and took her with them to the bilberries.

text: Nełar žukłi roda retizaƛ’, di xaner hič’č’a betixozoni gulus maħin reč’no, qˤirič’ nełaz mežiƛ ruqˤsi, nełƛ’or rutiraniƛäy.
translation: Because I wanted to harm her, I cut off the tail of the king's most beloved horse and hid the scissors under her mattress, so it would turn onto her.

text: Žar čuqˤno nesi bečedawni maduhales piša yedu yałrułi.
translation: The son noticed, that it was a deed of that wealthy neighbor.

text: Nediwt’a t’as elzox oc’ino bäsuƛin”.
translation: "We have ten of such washbowls."

text: Magalu boɣanixƛin eženi žek’a ħoyäɣor reƛ’a regirnosi, Ħusenä yeda q’ˤiƛin tełxor kurno.
translation: When the old man let his hand into the oven in order to pull out the bread, Hussein pushed him inside.

text: Xizyogon hemesitow bečedawni žek’a naysinan xabar begirno, dey kid yeynodaniƛ’or ruhun yayru žek’ux yegira yołƛin.
translation: Then that very same rich man spread the story everywhere, that he would marry his daughter off to the man who would teach her how to work.

text: Xanes uži yisä akiłno q’ˤidar ʕoƛ’ace naysinan kerozaq ezu-ʕaƛ’urno, amma ƛ’irizoni ɣˤutkaɣor ezura ižinanu yisä yeda.
translation: He lead him through the ravines and showed him everything until he got tired and fell down, but he didn't lead him to show him the room above.

text: Sidaɣ žopono ciqa tełer yaynosi, yiła ħošin šeƛ’un, sida osiy ažoq soder yizin.
translation: After she came into the dense forest, she put on a hide and climbed up a high tree.

text: Nedur, ža t’as mi där neƛnäy nexan debex diƛin”, - eƛin xanes kidbä.
translation: "So, if you give me this washbowl, I will come to you.", said the princess.

text: Neła yisiqor eƛin, mi aynosi di bežigon bˤuƛ’si, šida mi di xʷasar bodäƛin.
translation: It said to him, "When you came, I was even more afraid; why did you save me?"

text: Taxƛäzayn boƛixno, yisiłtow adäza łoħra ƛ’iräy qalno kurno, tełäzay bercinaw kid yoƛixno.
translation: From the ottoman, that frog appeared, teared off his skin and a beautiful girl appeared.

text: Kusa nesir raqru łinałäyn esin eɣeni esiwq, dicegon baq’ˤu micxirno, mesedno boqłin, däq siskin šebin roda roqxanuƛin.
translation: Kusa told the younger brother what had happened to him: "No matter how much money and gold I have, I don't manage to do anything.

text: Hunix ik’iƛ’oräy, hič’č’a adäza ža sida gondaƛ’or keze oqno.
translation: While he was going on his way, he came to a hole in front of him

text: Di guluƛ’ zowan, mi däde sadaq zownäyƛin”.
translation: "I will ride that horse, if you ride together with me".

text: Debez čanyazaƛ’ č’aq’ˤäsianu diƛin, zira eƛin, deber bukayxana di ħežƛ’azay qizanyor šebinno rižin bik’ixƛin.
translation: "I didn't assault your goat," said the fox, "don't you see I am coming from the hajj with things for my family?"

text: Beže bertenno boyn, xanä ža kid barułun yown.
translation: A big marriage was arranged and the king married that girl.

text: Sida ɣunoz ɣeduƛ kecäsi besun yiłar yizi.
translation: She found them sleeping in the shade of a tree.

text: Däderno yayn neła eƛis:
translation: She came to me and said:

text: Miyä däz čanyazaƛ’ č’aq’ˤaƛin, Bac’ali ziruƛ’or oqno.
translation: "Did you assault my goats?", Batsali began on the fox.

text: Bertenyołäy ža bašiqˤoy yałruni kid guginosi, yisi žas tatun bexun, xexłiƛ’ idurno ik’in, mežä pun bok’no ičin.
translation: When the girl with the ring had disappeared from the marriage, the boy lost his cheerfulness and quickly went home and to bed.

text: Igtow xan ƛ’iri utinosi, yisä roži teƛno.
translation: After the king insisted so much, he gave his word.

text: Eženi esiya nesiqor eƛin:
translation: The eldest brother said to him:

text: Iduɣor beza bik’inosi, Tawadir exurno gič’orabin rodin kuräsi uži esun.
translation: When they went to look into the room, Tavadi found his son cut dead into pieces.

text: Ismaʕila pikru bodin, ʕoƛiran esnazar kumak bodinäy, dandir biħanayxozoz rok’ƛ’or ray, dice č’uħaraw žek’u yoł yedaƛin, žedurgon kumak bodinäy, esnazaz rok’ƛ’or ray, dice ħalikataw žek’u yeda yołƛin.
translation: Ismail thought: "If I help the seven brothers, the ones they are fighting will notice what a proud man I am, but if I help them instead, the brothers will notice, what a mean man I am."

text: Howži mi ader ik’iƛin eƛin, yedu ozyoƛ’ yiħanada yoqno.
translation: "Now go forward!", she said and began to fight the army.

text: ʕAdada huni raq debiƛin.
translation: Your way will be of no purpose.

text: Yeda ukayrun, šebiƛa sis kid yoxin yik’iƛ’oräy, nełał xizay k’oƛin yisä nełas xot’oqosi riqirno.
translation: And right when he saw that there was some girl who was trying to run away, he ran behind her and took what she had on her feet.

text: Mi eƛis, där rätiru šebinon risno eni- obiydäɣor yik’iƛin.
translation: You said, I could take what I want and go to my parents.

text: Xizyogon žedu hemełatow ɣunoxor bik’in.
translation: Then they went to that tree again.

text: Posures mec nesir biyxozołäy Alimaħama łaqnokin esin anu, esinäy mi äxuƛin nesiq riyräsi roqzaƛ’ qˤayz xexoyä.
translation: Alimagomed told to no one about his knowing the animals' language, because the rabbit's young had explained to him: "If you tell it, you'll die!"

text: Nesä naysinan nesiz raƛ’ma xabar begirno, c’ohor däƛ’ay ežis, di nesił q’ˤim rok’siƛin.
translation: He spread the story everywhere that "The thief has won over me, I declare my defeat.

text: Sosi moƛaxay uži č’ariłno - tamaša oqno ƛexun yeda, xanzoƛ’aygon rigu ɣˤutku moƛaxay izizaƛ’or elo resuzaƛ’.
translation: At first, the boy woke up from his sleep - he was surprised because he found the house to be prettier than even the king's one, when he got up.

text: Mi näƛru ʕaq’ˤlun rok’ƛ’or bayn, bikorin xʷasar bodin nexƛ’oräy, däłtow xizayn bayn neła esirsi, nazosnotow tušman šida mi bexura begirno xecinč’uƛin.
translation: "I remembered the advice you gave me and rescued the snake, and when I came back, it came behind me and asked: 'Why didn't you let them kill the enemy of everyone?'"

text: Däq nełas baha bäziƛin užä eƛinosi, yiła yeda idur kidbeder ayrno.
translation: "I will be able to afford its price.", the boy said, and she led him home to the girl.

text: Idur Ražbadin aynosi, kidbez babiya nesił adäza bišʷa ħaƛin.
translation: When Razhbadin went to the home, the girl's father put food before him.

text: Däz pikruxor, meža ža dey ħumukuli bok’k’äsi yoł, dey däqor xizor neƛƛin!”
translation: Methinks, you have stolen my camel, give it back to me!"

text: Zeyä q’ˤogedtow qˤiči kosix zown, zira sist’a ƛ’ara but’ix bičäsi zown.
translation: The bear packed the harvest together in hay bales, the fox was the one who ripped out the ears one after another.

text: Xizaɣorsi magalu boɣƛ’oräy, Ħusenä ža q’ˤiƛin tełxorno kurno, ħoyos ac yuqin yičirno elo ža ik’un łiyace.
translation: While he was taking the last bread out, Hussein pushed him inside, shut the oven's door and restrained it until he was burned up there.

text: Xizyo magalu boɣa ža oqƛ’oräy, ħaƛin tełxor kuroƛin, mołin nesiq neła qˤaƛumo.
translation: Afterwards, while he is starting to take out the bread, push him inside!", taught him that voice.

text: Nazon žeda sidaƛ’orno rutin ixiw ɣˤutku rodin.
translation: They all came together and built a big house.

text: Hemedur hunix ik’iƛ’orey, boxin bik’ixosi ziru yisił c’oxno, nełałgon xizay k’oƛin nexxo łˤono boc’in yołƛax.
translation: While he was on his way like that, he met a fox who was fleeing and behind it three wolves came running.

text: Žigoƛ’aygon uži ceyder ik’in.
translation: The son went again to the eagle.

text: Telawä dey sisgon Tawadiƛin esiw yoł, ža t’or ayzaƛ’or di berten badinč’iƛin.
translation: In Telavi there's one brother of mine, named Tavadi; before he comes here, I won't marry."

text: Nasin ʕoloqbi waħƛin bik’in, didur aħyałäy bˤulamzałer sis hil c’oxxoƛin.
translation: And all the people went "Whoa! How could one bullet hit it from the ear to the hoofs?"

text: Kʷaxatow xanes kid habihandäɣor nexno.
translation: Soon the king's daughter returned to the miller.

text: Xediw ičäsi zown žiƛbon xecin, k’oxäygon meši bodinč’inäy, bexʷadanƛin.
translation: The husband remained [in his opinion]: "We'll leave it this year and if it doesn't calve again, we shall slaughter it."

text: Sis ixiw ceyn bayn ža is elay neła bok’no bižin.
translation: And a big eagle came, struck this bull down and carried it away from there.

text: Nełƛ’osi ɣudbi rik’inosi, sida quł iħuz lilyox yisir bikori besun.
translation: From then on the days went by, and one day he found a snake at the river bank.

text: Sida ƛˤebaƛ’aza yiłas di roƛxosi ričiranƛin, eƛin doxturä.
translation: The doctor promissed to cure her in one year.

text: Babiya k’eneč’yabi risno kidbeqor teƛno.
translation: The father took the eggs and gave them to the daughter.

text: Boc’ä keč’ qˤaƛa baybik bodin, dalay, dalay sida bogmor ʕomoy, dalay, dalay sidagon bogmor mamalay, sidenigon bogmor k’et’un, mešinƛin.
translation: The wolves began to sing a song: "Dalay, dalay, a donkey for one meal; dalay, dalay, a rooster for another meal; and a cat and a calf for yet another meal."

text: C’urun-c’aranƛin” eƛin yayxosi k’emot’ mi yiqir, ža hoq’ˤoni reƛ’a atinč’ey q’ˤidar yegirƛin.
translation: Take away the crate that came down when you said 'Tsurun-tsaran!', let the other one down without touching it with the hand!"

text: Xanes wazir č’ariłno puħo t’akan yukaynosi, nesis cim bizin, łu yedu mesedyos t’akan t’or yayrnoƛin.
translation: When the king's vizier woke up and saw the glass at his side, he became angry[ and asked]: "Who brought this golden glass here?"

text: Miƛin, eƛin nesä, k’ot’ono, c’ec’mabi c’uda, ozurabi niga, kodi aluk’a ɣˤanazaƛ’ bužzi aqno, nełqay maqˤixno ičiƛin.
translation: "Don't", he said, "believe in a short woman with her eyelashes red, her eyes green and her hair white, but keep away from her!"

text: Nesisgon łˤono uži zown.
translation: He had three sons.

text: Anu där t’ok’ow łinaqazaykin ħinq’iƛin, žawab nełan teƛno.
translation: And she answered: "There's no dangerous place for me anymore."

text: Xizyogon žeda q’orolay yegirno, žedu xexzar rek’ bodanix.
translation: Then they sent the widow to play a trick on the children.

text: Hemedurtow nasin sider sis bätirut’anibin dandixoƛ!
translation: May all lovers come together likewise!

text: Neła qułno yisi žaq ɣuro kamin.
translation: On that day the cows got lost to the boy again.

text: Q’ˤano äƛirugon ɣudƛ’o eɣeni esiw raładyoz raʕalłixor ik’in.
translation: On the second day, the younger brother went to the edge of the sea.

text: Xan č’agu ƛexuƛ mi!”
translation: King, long may you live!"

text: Dandir aždaħ c’oxnosi, Ħuseneł biłetow yiła yisiłno q’ut’i boyx, łox ɣˤul caƛin sosikin nełaz c’oxnäy, yisä ža boƛƛi begira bayx, sosikin c’oxinč’inäy, aždaħä ža igix.
translation: When he came to the dragon, he was making the same agreement as with Hussein with him, that if he throws a stone three times and hits him at least once, he must break him apart in the middle, if he doesn't hit him at least once, the dragon would be swallowing him.

text: Yeda hunix ik’iƛ’oräy, yisir qˤaƛu teqno, mi nesi žek’uł xizay äk’ino, nesä mi exarƛin.
translation: While he was going on the way, he heard a shout: "Don't you go behind that man, he will kill you!"

text: Elo yiłar istowlix q’ˤida ičäsi bercinaw uži esun.
translation: There she found a handsome boy sitting at a table.

text: Allah šebitow roqä yedu där?
translation: "Allah, what the heck has happened to me?

text: Mi dey ħal bayrinč’uƛin, xanä sadaq barun yoɣno, žeda žeduz ɣˤutkaɣor nexno.
translation: "You win", the king took his wife and they went to their house together.

text: Ezun dibirno ičäsi zown.
translation: And the mullah waited and looked.

text: Igtow yeda ƛ’iri utizaƛ’ Tawadä esin, mi yowxozo barurno, deberno saɣłin rodin ħaƛanix di t’akan yizärun, gugyoq tungin bisno akiƛäza yik’ix yukayru kidbeƛ’ ozurin c’oxno, nełaz bercinłä ʕadal oys.
translation: Because he insisted so much, Tavadi told him: "Right after lifting my glass to drink on your and your soon to be married wife, my eye met this girl whom I saw going under the window and taking a jar on her back, and her beauty was intoxicating."

text: Mi dey halmaɣ esunäy, yedu kid debi mi yižo.
translation: If you are my friend, marry this girl yourself.

text: Nesä eƛin, ele eže žek’us ʕaq’lu biszaƛ’, Allahätow xʷasar oyn miƛin.
translation: He said: "Because you sticked to an old man's advice, God himself has rescued you!"

text: Ele hoboda debi mi eɣʷedin et’no biqiräsi micxir boqzaƛ’, c’idaɣor kura baynč’iƛin, eƛin babiya, užä nesä nesir micxir boda oqsiƛin rok’u č’eze roqnosi.
translation: "There, because it's the money you earned with your own sweat, I don't have to throw it into the fire.", said the father, and was convinced that his son earned money for himself.

text: Nesiƛ’ bezanixƛin bayru ahlimor žoyä bˤeƛ’es šebint’a teƛxo zown.
translation: The son gave out one sheep each for every person who came to look after him.

text: Hemełƛ’o xizyo xanes nukarbi yiłade igorno bayn esirno, yeda šebi deber oqxo, šida mi ʕiyayxƛin.
translation: After that, the king's henchmen came near her and asked: "What is he to you; why are you crying?"

text: Xizyo yiła idäy rätiru łinasno hokon bic’no, wazirzaqorgon eƛin, hokomƛ’aɣor xan izirƛin.
translation: Afterwards, she filled a carriage with what she wanted and said to the vizier: "Lift the king onto the carriage!"

text: Xizyo pardagin risno, gulun boɣno nesiqay bižin, uži eniwdäɣor ik’in.
translation: Afterwards the boy took the scarf, the horse and went to his mother.

text: Łˤora äƛiru ʕomoyä žawab teƛno, di qˤaƛanayru mixƛ’o, deber roqxosi šebin rukay hoboyƛin.
translation: The third donkey answered: "When I cry late at night, you'll see what happens then!"

text: Hudayzikon teqno yisir bˤeƛ’ sidä sidqor xabaryayx.
translation: The next day he heard again that the sheep were talking among each other.

text: Žeda yeda ader egirno eli xizaz bayanƛin.
translation: They sent him ahead, [saying]: "We'll come behind."

text: Täƛru łinaƛ’no razin oqinč’ey, t’omceni žek’a žigon esirno bišʷa, yiłaq dey ax roqinč’uƛin.
translation: Not having accepted what he was given, the span-sized man asked again for food: "I won't get my fill from that."

text: ƛ’irir ayru užiqor žeda eƛin, zey bexuranix tupi neƛƛin.
translation: To the approaching boy they said, "Give us your rifle so we can kill the bear!"

text: Baq’ˤu zaman bik’izaƛ’ortow nesi bečedawni žek’a q’ˤuya baru yižisƛax.
translation: After much time had passed, the rich man took another wife.

text: Igorno ik’in ezuzaƛ’, guluƛ’ ƛ’iri q’ˤanon micxiryos ric’äsi ħurženyabi resun.
translation: When he came closer to look, he found two bags filled with money on the horse.

text: Ža bac’no łäyrun, xanez kidbełäy ɣun roqno.
translation: Just when she finished eating it, the princess turned into a tree.

text: Ža xabar best’alawni eniwrno teqno.
translation: Also her step-mother heard that story.

text: Ħumukuliƛ’ ƛ’iräy riguƛ’ay rigu šeƛ’un roɣno, žan aħoziqor teƛno eƛin, nagaħ howt’aza oc’ino žek’un, łeno waranin boƛixnäy, šebi roynon žedu mi ričok’ƛin.
translation: Having pulled the prettiest clothings from the camel, he gave it to the shepherd and said: "When suddenly there appear ten men with five camels, do something to detain them."

text: Idur xediw ayrun baru daʕba boda c’oxno, ɣudi adäy eluq mešori bexada bayx, šidaƛin eƛiłiri nełqay didiwnkin mumpaʕat anu, ʕadada käkirułi gurow.
translation: As soon as the husband had come home, the wife started to insist: "Soon this day we have to slaughter the heifer, because there's no benefit whatsoever from it except for feeding [it] in vain."

text: Yexoriłƛin eƛinosigon, ele yexoriłinč’aƛin hečk’erno yizin.
translation: And when he said "Get longer!", she said "Here, am I not getting longer?" and got up upright again.

text: Neširu elo nełax sasaqor šebi ičixozoł boɣnayx ƛexasi eliƛin.
translation: "We are arguing who will wait at night until the morning, there.

text: Kidno razi yoqno.
translation: The girl agreed.

text: Eniya hibon bok’no ža q’ˤidar bˤoƛ’urno.
translation: The mother struck with a stick and let it fall down.

text: ʕUraw q’ʷariłiƛ’or yegin baru, howži šebitow rodanƛin.
translation: The wife became very sad [and said]: "What shall I do now?!"

text: ʕAt’idä c’in rit’erno qˤiči riħurxozoni koboraxaza uqˤełno ičin.
translation: Atid put out the fire and hid in the granary where one pours the harvest.

text: Yaynosi eniya esirno nesiq, łinaq nece q’ʷarid oqno ičäsi, debi ruyä roƛxoƛin.
translation: When the mother came home, she asked: "What are you so sad about, are you sick?"

text: Bełiƛ’ay xizor utin idur aynosi, yisir pašmanno yoqno yičäsi esiy yesux.
translation: When he came back from hunting, he found his sister being sad.

text: Yisiz idu lagiƛ baħa mužikin ƛexun zownanu.
translation: And at his home not even a mattress remained to be laid under his body.

text: Howži ʕuraw mixƛ’or ezun ičin babiw uži ayxanaƛin.
translation: Now, after much time, the father was looking why his son didn't came.

text: Elo yizi nucos bic’äsi q’ilima teł šišin.
translation: There they got stuck in the pot filled with honey.

text: Elo yedu q’ˤuya gamiƛ’arno yizin ħaƛ’ala xʷasarno yoqno lilyoxar yok’ełno.
translation: There she got to another boat, was bearly rescued and escaped to the shore.

text: Elo teł mesedyos heneš boqxosi sis ɣun zown.
translation: There was one tree, which had golden apples.

text: Sidaquł neširu šudax yeda yałzay, roƛiquraɣor xizor šilabi yołäsi, ƛ’iri hisno raqˤäsi sis miƛ’i bayn.
translation: One night while they were at the grave, at mighnight came a ram that had its horns backwards and accumulated whool.

text: Sasaq idurno ik’in užä eniwq esin kidbełäy, bˤeƛ’ rac’xosi boc’i” idutow zown, gugira yay ža yiłzaqayƛin.
translation: In the morning, the boy went home and told his mother of the girl: "The 'wolf' who eats the sheep was in our home, it has to be removed from this place."

text: Waħ, łu nesis žedu xexbi rexura rayx, - esirno ʕAliqiličä neł kidbeq, - žeda nesi baħarčir šebi zaral bodäsiƛin?”
translation: "Whoa, who would kill his children?", asked Aliqilich the girl, "What did they do to this brave young man?"

text: Där howža heneš neƛnäy, nexan debex diƛin”.
translation: "If you give me that apple, I will come to you."

text: Žedur bigu bogno boyn buyn bišerno, łˤonontow sida taxƛ’o sadaq kecerno.
translation: He made them a good meal and indeed fed them, and let the three sleep together on one ottoman.

text: Elor bayrun xanes oz partalq’ay butiranił c’oxno.
translation: When the king's army arrived there, they started gathering the belongings.

text: Howt’ay yäk’irun, där moƛał rukaysƛin babiwqor eƛo mi, soderno kurno ƛ’ˤiyor nexƛ’oräy, geres hibos boq’ˤugani pu wˤałxor butin nexno raƛ’moƛxorgon šäšiƛin.
translation: "When you go again, tell your father that you have seen it in a dream: He shall throw it upward and when it comes down again, the iron stick's heavier side will turn downward and get stuck in the earth."

text: Nełƛ’osi, łˤono ɣudin rik’in uyra äƛiru ɣudƛ’o sidä sideł daʕban boqno yiłader q’ˤano žek’u ayn.
translation: After that, three days passed and on the fourth day two men came to her who had a dispute among them.

text: Di deber łˤora ɣudi ƛirbałi rodinosi, xabaryada yoł dowqor.
translation: After I have been your host for three days, I will talk to you.

text: Kʷaxatow žax bertenno bodin eniwn, užin, barun nełƛ’ay soder łinasnotow q’ʷariłi anusi bičix zown.
translation: Soon the mother arranged a marriage for the boy and the boy and his wife henceforward were living without any sadness whatsoever.

text: Best’al eniya mect’a boyx babiya q’ʷarid yoyx zown eniwn yexun ƛäxuruni kid.
translation: The step mother slandered the stepdaughter and the father hurt his daughter whose mother had died.

text: Šebi deber roqno, mi c’aq’ pašmanłiƛ’wa yičäsiƛin, esiya esirnosi, yiła eƛin, maqˤiƛ’ay łi ric’a yik’iƛ’oräy, ʕoloqzas rok’uƛ’ yołƛax di yok’ek’anƛin.
translation: "What happened to you, to make you so sad?", the brother asked, and she said: "When I went from afar to get water, some young guys planned to kidnap me.

text: Eniya eƛin:
translation: The mother said:

text: Di k’emot’yabi royx aħnayxosi qacis ust’ar yołƛin, nesä žawab teƛno.
translation: "I am a carpenter working around making wooden cases." he answered.

text: Idur ayruntow yexurno k’eneč’ žedu q’ˤunzontow ozzaq yiħinosi, sider sis bukayłin, ɣudi qˤašizaƛ’orkin žedu bužzi boqno anu, ozzałer kanłi baysƛin, hemece ɣˤaɣˤu bizizaq.
translation: After he came home, he crushed an egg, smeared it on each of their eyes, and although they could see each other again, they couldn't believe it when it got dawn, and were even more happy when the light came into their eyes.

text: Elo roqno sis ixiw bercinaw ɣˤutkun, sidadäza puƛ’aza bik’ixƛax ɣˤays iħu, sidadäzagon łäs iħu.
translation: There came a big beautiful house into being, with a milk river flowing on the one side and a water river on the other.

text: Debeł halmaɣłin risno, łäƛ’ ƛ’iri xanłi rodaniƛäy anu di dowder iħada nexäsiƛin, eƛin rizirno yisä mač’a.
translation: "I won't accept your friendship with you and I won't rule over the water, I came to you in order to fight!", he said and lifted the dagger.

text: C’aq’ maʕdinay, ozäy ozuri boɣxanusi yiłe šebin zown ža.
translation: She was very lovely and one couldn't take one's eye off of hers.

text: Užä žawab teƛno, där retin Beqes ʕUneyzat barułun yowanixƛin.
translation: The boy answered: "I wanted to marry Sun's Uneyzat."

text: Zeygon bet’un k’oƛin bičäsi zown, ħurženyabin, gulun där biča bayƛin.
translation: And the bear jumped and was like: "The bags and the horse must be for me!"

text: Xaner šebi yałru iyač’iniƛäy mi dey q’ˤimno reč’o, iyos orezni boqnosi min ok’äł t’ayƛin, xizyo nesiqor amanat bodin, mi dey q’ˤimno ruqˤo, yeɣeni esiwqno rukarno, nagaħ dey laga debeq biqinäy, q’ˤimde sadaq buyn šušoƛin.
translation: For the king won't know who it was, cut off my head and escape from there after there is a large pool of blood!"; then he gave him the order: "And hide my head, don't show it to our little sister, if you can get my body, bury it together with my head."

text: Kʷaxatow babiw exunƛax.
translation: Soon the father died.

text: Xizyogon žoyä sisgon ʕaq’lu mołoƛin nesiqor eƛinosi, hudun mi di bayru ʕaq’luƛ’ razi oqxanuwaƛin eƛintow ičin.
translation: When the boy said to him again: "Give me another advice!", he said: "You won't accept my advice anyway."

text: Boƛƛo tabanaw mix bik’inosi, Ražbadinƛ’or Tawadis kaɣat yayx, ec’noni barun yown pulanaw ɣudƛ’or Telawär horo, Tiblisä t’et’erno łiyn ayru užis berten boyxosiƛin.
translation: After quite a long time has passed, a letter from Tavadi came to Razhbadin: "Come to Telavi on a certain day and bring your new wife; we are preparing the marriage of my son who came from finishing his studies in Tbilisi."

text: Dibirä esirno nesiq, žaqˤuł mi šida kʷat’izi oqäƛin.
translation: The imam asked him: "Why were you late today?"

text: ƛ’iri bičin onoča muhubin rut’in, siskin t’ok’ow muhu xecinč’uƛin rok’u č’odinosi, onoču akäza maħorno k’oƛin boržizin boqno bik’in.
translation: The hen tried hard and pecked up all grains, and after making sure that it didn't left out any grain, the hen jumped out of the window and flew away.

text: Užä nesä nesir micxir boda oqno.
translation: The boy started to earn money himself.

text: Maharno erno xanä kid Kusaxno yegirno, nesä nesis mečar xanłunno egirno.
translation: The king organized a wedding, married off his daughter to Kusa and gave him his kingdom.

text: Xizyo nesi nukarä xanqor eƛin, howža bełiqus uži egirčey neła aɣił xizay, nesä biqär žaƛin.
translation: Then the henchman said to the king, "Send that hunter's son after the bird, he will catch it."

text: Q’orola radirun, habihander yizi didur keze baqrułäyn, elo žedudäy xizor bäk’irułäyn, esnaza boyx zawru rek’mołäyn - nasin šebin Beqes ʕUneyzatä esin.
translation: About what the widow did, and about how they met the miller, and about how they went back from there, and about the tricks that their sisters played - all these things Sun's Uneyzat told.

text: Xizay žek’un egirno goƛ’in ayrno, sis kepaw imadu esoƛin žeda ža t’amizi odin.
translation: And they sent a man, invited and brought him; "Tell us a merry tale!", they forced him.

text: Maħor k’aƛirun, yisir bukayn gulu t’umiƛ’orno butin boržizi boqno hawaƛ’ boxix.
translation: As soon as he ran outside, he saw the horse had turned into a pigeon and was flying away in the air.

text: Neła ziys reƛno rac’no łiyrno, ħaħno ƛäxuru ƛuzazaq nesis raɣin ric’erno, ʕoƛnon esnabin boqno bik’in, yedan žeda žedudäy et’urno.
translation: After they had eaten up that cow's meat, the brothers left his veranda filled with the gnawed bones and left him behind.

text: Kidbes babiwgon nesis halmaɣ zown.
translation: The girl's father was his friend.

text: Adäz ƛeles boƛik’uni q’ulaʕin erno, ziran, ɣʷˤadan q’ˤunän sadaqay rac’no.
translation: Having put the lamb's half carcass down, the fox and the crow, they both ate together.

text: Di nesir sadaq bˤeƛ’ teƛsi.
translation: I gave Him all my sheep."

text: Zeyä xizyogon esin, eluz maduhalłiq yałru ʕaƛa łi ħaƛanix posu sida maqˤi yałruni keroqor rik’ixosi.
translation: The bear then told: "In a village in our neighbourhood, the cattle has to go to a ravine that is far a way in order to drink water.

text: Howła šadał aqˤuros maħ yołƛin”.
translation: "This wine smells like urine."

text: Babiws wasi t’ubazi boqinč’izaƛ’, nesä äƛirun rodin, meč’oxor q’aq’at’un kurno exʷa ayx diƛin.
translation: Because my father's testament won't get fulfilled, I must do what he said, put a noose around my neck and die."

text: Žeduq neł gogaƛ’or baynč’ey łˤono bucin, łˤono ɣudin, łˤono saʕatno rik’äsi zown.
translation: They didn't come to that meadow for three months, three days and three hours.

text: Xizyogon nukarä xanqor eƛin, raładno boɣno bitaz puƛ’aza yołƛin teqxo bokobin, k’ukyabin raqˤuxosi kidƛin, nełał xizaygon elor egiranix bełiquz užiƛ’ay mustaħiq’aw žek’un anuƛin.
translation: Again the henchman said to the king, "On the other side of the sea, as I have heard, lives girl wo sews coats and caps; there is no man more suitable than the hunter's son to be sent there after her."

text: Xanä esirno, debi murad šebi, šebi deber retinƛin.
translation: The king asked: "What is your wish, what do you want?"

text: Ža šebin yisi eɣeni esiwr teqno.
translation: And the young brother heard of that.

text: Nełƛ’o ža bežigon q’oq’oƛix zown.
translation: Then it crowed even more.

text: Ħaƛuxozoxay uyxor aynosi, xanä esirno:
translation: After the king came to senses, he asked:

text: Oka eƛin, däde sadaq egirno xec yeda debi uži, xizyo ƛˤebaq ƛˤeb ħišiłnosi, hobołatow mečoƛ’or horo mi yisił xizayƛin.
translation: Oku said: "Send your son together with me, and when the year after next year is over, come to this very field and follow him."

text: Neła eniw yuƛ’ˤerxo zown, där sis kid neƛinč’inäy, yexura yoł mi diƛin.
translation: It scared the mother: "If you don't give me a girl, I will kill you!"

text: Elor ža q’orolayn yayn.
translation: And the widow came to there.

text: Hemełƛ’ay soder doxtur xanez ʕaƛa ičin.
translation: From then onward, the doctor lived in khan's village.

text: Šebi roqä yaqˤuł bełisno, debinƛin?”
translation: "What happened to the deer and you, today?"

text: Ža ažo ałni bayłxanuce yečiw, ƛ’iräz žek’u oqła bihayaw yiłe zown.
translation: The tree was so thick that one couldn't give it a hug, and it was easy for a man to get inside from above.

text: Dowqosi ged där ħažan yetin ʕiyayx yedaƛin”.
translation: "He cries, because he wants that I exchange your dress with mine."

text: Yiła žeduqor eƛin:
translation: She said to them:

text: Behizi raqƛin, roži nełan teƛno.
translation: "I permit it.", she gave her word.

text: ʕArabuzanqor ža xabarno boyn, Ismaʕil eženidäɣor ik’in.
translation: After he told this to Arabuzan, Ismail went to the old woman.

text: Žedu teł iduɣor boqnosi, ceyä bełiqus uži žeduz ɣˤutkoxor egirno.
translation: When they had gone into their house, the eagle let the hunter's son down to their house.

text: Esiya małiruxor, č’ikays tungin bisno, yedu łäħor yik’in.
translation: According to what the brother had said, she took a glass jug and went to get water.

text: Elor baynosi, č’ikin boqno c’aramäz begin iduɣorno bik’in, žedus xabarno teqerno bičin.
translation: When it arrived there, it became a flea, went inside through a crack and listened to what they said.

text: Xizyo zeyä yisiq esirno, mi oƛixnosi di bežigon bˤuƛ’si, šida mi di bexurinč’ey xecäƛin.
translation: Then the bear asked him, "After you appeared I was all the more afraid, why didn't you kill me?"

text: Azbaryoxor baynosi, xanes biguni ɣira erno Goqix kid yegiranir.
translation: When they arrived at the palace, the king wished to send his daughter to Goqi.

text: Kidbä akrošaw magalun bižin, Aħmaddäɣorno yik’in, babiya äsiru xabarno nesiq esin.
translation: The daughter took the cheese bread, went to Akhmed and told him what her father told her.

text: Di mi žeduq ukarač’inƛin, eƛin zira.
translation: "I will not show you," the fox said.

text: Sasaq gamiƛ’no zowrno, yizä yeda uži ɣˤayz iħuƛ’ ƛ’iri ižix.
translation: The next day, they seated the boy on a boat, and took him out on the milk river.

text: Rečuƛer yizi bik’inosi, anuƛax elo gulu.
translation: When they went to the shed, the horse wasn't there.

text: Yisä eƛin, debex di esiw yegirłin, mi di ƛ’irizoni ɣˤutka tełer ezura egirinč’uƛin.
translation: He said: "Although you married your sister off to me, you didn't let me look into the upper room."

text: Migon nazay t’or yiħułäƛin, esirno yisä q’orolaq.
translation: "Now where have you come from, here?", he asked the widow.

text: Žeda yisiq esirno, šebi roqä deber, šida mi oxin ik’ixƛin.
translation: They asked him: "What happened to you, why are you running away?"

text: Elo Ražbadinä ʕoloqzaqor eƛix, pulanaw mečoƛ’or ʕaƛa yałru kidbabi rutirƛin.
translation: There, Razhbadin said to the young people: "Gather the girls from the village on some place!

text: Idur aynosi, yisir ciqaɣor erno bišʷan łiyn eniwn nan mekoyx yesuzaƛ’ žoyä meɣyoqor eƛin, wele meɣu deberno, därno, eniwrno ʕeziyaw bišʷa roƛik’ƛin.
translation: When he came home, he found the food put for [the way into] the forest finished and his mother being hungry, so the boy said to the udder: "Look out, udder, let appear much food for you, me and the mother!"

text: Boc’i maħla ɣˤunoqay räšruni ƛˤebrezał buqˤełno, zey hemełtow ažomzo kosyar bizin.
translation: The wolf who ate from the goat hide in the garden between the leaves, the bear climbed up on the crest of a tree.

text: Yizi elor bayzaƛ’, imyoq yicäsi nesä nesis barun yukarno, xanä eƛin, naʕanan bodin eloš neła ɣˤanazas nocu caƛoƛin.
translation: Because they came there, the king showed them his wife, tied to the pole, and said: "Curse and spit on that women over there!"

text: Yisägon rok’no mač’a ret’urno aždaħes q’ˤim kurno.
translation: Again(?) he pulled out his saber and beated the dragon's head off.

text: Mekoyx oqłin, išinč’ey baq’ˤu zamanno zowzaƛ’, yeda uži sosisi išxanuƛin ičäsi zown, kʷat’izi oqnäy di t’otow äčiƛin.
translation: Although he was hungry and hadn't eaten for a long time, the boy waited at first: "I won't eat. If it gets late, I will wait here."

text: Xizyo yiził adäza biša erno.
translation: Afterwards food was served before them.

text: Hemełƛ’o aħozä, ƛelzas zigari täqruntow, koyän risno yisä žedu rižin.
translation: Then, when the shepherd heared the lambs' complaint, he immediately took them onto his arms.

text: Zownƛax zownanuƛax sis bečedaw žek’u.
translation: Once upon a time, there was a rich man.

text: Bilq’isdis duʕan q’abul bodin, ozzataw oqno xediwn, xedyos esiwn, ža xanez madaxosini žek’un, eženis łˤonon užin, yedu teƛno zawruni gamiƛ’osi žek’un.
translation: Bilqisdis prayer was received, the husband, the husband's brother, that king's guard, the three sons of the old man and the sailor to whom they sold her got blind.

text: Yeduyä ža mesedyos bašiqˤoy zawruni kidƛin eniya esirnosi, p’es anusi užä eniw kidbeƛ’ yezura ƛ’iri t’alamäzoni ɣˤutkaɣor yižin.
translation: When the mother asked: "Is she the girl with the golden ring?", the boy looked at her silently and lead her into the room upstairs to let her look at the girl.

text: Sasaqosi moƛaxay yizizaƛ’or kidber ɣamas yexasi yesun.
translation: When the girl got up in the morning, she found that the box was broken.

text: Žaq oč’ino ƛˤeb yaynosi, babiwz rok’ƛ’or rayn, ʕarab ʕilmu t’et’ra ižanƛin.
translation: When the son became nine years old, the father had an idea: "I will send him to learn the Arabian science."

text: Ruy neł best’al eniya yiła kidbeƛ’ ʕuraw ɣudi rayrxo zown, c’ik’iw iduzasin, maħasin ħalt’i neła best’al kidbeƛ’or kurno xecix zown.
translation: But the stepmother tortured the girl many days and threw all the work in the house and outside onto her stepdaughter.

text: Nełas keč’oƛ’asi ozuri becono yołƛin”.
translation: Its left eye is blind."

text: Salamno bisno ečruni žek’a yisiq esirno, debi t’o ƛirba yołäƛin.
translation: The old man greeted him and asked him: "Do you have a comrade here?"

text: Čanno suƛƛi boƛixno sis hil karun, bˤulamzałäy aħyałerno c’oxno łˤora k’onč’aƛ’or boxin bik’isƛin”, - žawab teƛno heresi mec esixoza.
translation: "A deer suddenly appeared and the bullet that was shot, hit it through the legs and pierced the ear, so it ran away on its three legs", answered the liar.

text: Sisxoli idu yičin där ʕuraw žuka riyx.
translation: "I feel very bad sitting alone at home.

text: Bełiƛ’ay xizor utin yeda aynosi, yisir q’ʷariłiłer yegäsi esiw yesun.
translation: After he came back from hunting, he found his sister being sad.

text: Xanä eɣeni žaqor eƛäsi zown:
translation: The king said to his younger son:

text: Žeduł teł obiy anusi best’al uži zown.
translation: Among them was an orphan boy who had no father.

text: Xizyo mi däder horo debertown aq’ˤlu betin besunäyƛin, bełiqa xandäɣor uži egirno.
translation: Then come to me, in case you want some advice," and the hunter sent his son to the king.

text: Łˤono qacis hoko t’it’inäy, łˤono ɣˤuruš neƛanƛin q’ut’in bodin, eynoda egirno xecin.
translation: "If you fell three carriages with firewood, I'll give you three rubles."; they made an agreement and he let him work and left.

text: Ža bayzaƛ’or łˤonon esnabi iħun boɣno taqˤaɣorgon bok’łäsi zown.
translation: Before he came, the three brothers crossed a river and escaped to there.

text: Elor yaynosi, yiła xanqor aɣi teƛxo zown.
translation: After she arrived there, she wanted to give the bird to the king.

text: Sida ɣudi bik’in, q’ˤuna ɣudi bik’in łˤora äƛiza ɣudiƛ’o yizirabi sidaɣ qunyoxor bayn.
translation: They went one day, they went two days, and on the third day they came to a farmstead somewhere.

text: Kutak q’ʷarid boqno aɣi q’anon xexoy zira bac’zaƛ’.
translation: The bird became even more sad because the fox has eaten two nestlings now.

text: Esanayn łiynosi, yizir xanez kidbes šeƛ’u resunanu.
translation: After she finished swimming, they couldn't find the princess' clothes.

text: Sasaqor žeda äƛiza mečoƛ’ yizir bercinaw magazinno, elotow teł xanzarsi biłe bišxosi močin, icozaƛ’gon biguƛ’ay bigu q’ayn besun.
translation: In the morning, on the place where they spoke, they found a beautiful shop with the best articles on the shelfs and a place to eat like for a king in it.

text: Elayn bizin, q’ˤanon boqno hunix ʕaƛaɣor.
translation: The two got up from there and on their way to the village.

text: Q’ˤunan esnaza rigut’a rodin ɣutkabin paraxat ʕumru bodix bičin.
translation: And the two brothers built pretty houses and lead a calm life.

text: Hunix bik’iƛ’oräy nełade dandir mamalay c’oxno.
translation: While going on his way, he met a rooster.

text: Mi didiwtow siskin šebin riyxanusi uži iča ayxƛin, Oku aƛ’in nesiqor.
translation: "What kind of boy must you be, who doesn't know anything.", spoke Oku.

text: ƛ’iriɣor boƛixnosi, Bac’alä q’ˤimqo šebinon rok’no žan bexurno, nełqay ħoši boɣno.
translation: When he appeared above, Batsali beated something over his head, killed him and took off his hide.

text: Mamalay qaciqorno, yił- nełqorno bezux bičinč’ey durimoƛ’ bik’in onočaƛ’ biħanayn nełasno łoħros yiłetown k’onč’un yecurno, nełdäy hozur boqno, neła nełaz ɣˤutkaɣor bik’in.
translation: Without looking for firewood or any other things, he ran off to the hen, had a fight [with her] and broke her leg just like the frog's, seperated from her and went to his own house.

text: Hemedurtow, dey xediw nexxoƛin, reƛ teƛxosini bazarganno yizide sadaq ɣamasya teł uqˤno yiła.
translation: Again she said "My husband is coming!", and also hid the meat-selling merchant inside the box together with them.

text: Esiƛ’ nesiq xabarno xecirno elo yoƛor k’oƛin kidbä eƛin, neł rožes maʕna deber meq’ bič’zi boqno.
translation: "Let him cease the story while he's talking!", the girl ran into the middle of there and said: "You misunderstood the sense of these words.

text: Idur aynosi yisä esin, hemediw šebin roqno di kid xäciza mečoƛ’ƛin.
translation: When he came home, he told: "Such a thing has happened on the place where I left my daughter."

text: Bišƛ’oräytow nesä kidbez babiwqor xabar boƛik’no, hemedur sis beže q’ʷariʕelno dowqor keze boqno, mi ža t’ubazi badiƛin debeƛ’ rok’un rayn nexäsi yoł di dowderƛin.
translation: While they were eating, he started a conversation with the girl's father: "So there is a big necessity you have to do, and I have come to you and am convinced you can fulfill it."

text: Sosisi zirusno, qˤaysno ʕuraw zer rok’u regin, xizyo ža k’et’u yałru biynosi łˤonon žedu reƛ raħira rik’in.
translation: At first, the fox's and the hare's hearts where shuddering, but after they knew it was the cat, the three of them went to cook the meat.

text: Ža baynosi, šet’urtow dunyal č’urno.
translation: As he came, he illuminated the earth around.

text: K’oƛin ik’in žoyä kidbeq pandur bukarnosi, neła eƛin:
translation: They boy ran to the girl and when he showed her the lute, she said:

text: Siskin urɣizi oqxo ičinč’ey, kusa nesiqor eƛin, ʕomoyä łox ħoʕni roɣnosi äxu miƛin.
translation: Without thinking, the beardless man said to him: "When the donkey has shouted three times, you will die."

text: Sida quł yeda ža c’eruc’an risno bełiƛ’ ik’in.
translation: One day he went hunting with this bow.

text: ʕAƛoz ɣˤanaza nediw šebin radinč’iƛin, eƛin xanä.
translation: "The village's woman wouldn't do such a thing," said the king.

text: Nesi xanzo raƛ’mar aynosi, ʕAt’idä naysinan xabaryabi regirno, di boƛ’araw unti saɣ boyxosi yołƛin.
translation: When coming onto the king's land, Atid spread the story everywhere: "I can cure any illness!"

text: Mežur beže barkala yoł eli nedur beže boqace käkirus, howži elur mežudäy bik’a retin.
translation: "Thank you very much for feeding us until we got that big, now we wanted to leave you.

text: Deber ža ħumukuli bukayn besunč’inäy, nazay deber nełas kut’yos k’onč’u šiqˤono yałrułi yiyxƛin?”
translation: "If you haven't seen this camel, how do you know that its right foot is lame?"

text: Habihanägon k’onč’azaxno bisno šemqaɣor boɣno bižirno.
translation: The miller took it at its legs and carried it to the dump.

text: Yiziri gut’ zawru mečoƛ’or bayn.
translation: They came to the place where the smoke was.

text: Łäs tungin bic’no nexxo yedu yukayrun, yeda nełqay oxin ik’iƛ’oräy, mi ži daqäy ok’ła moči anuƛin, tungin q’ˤidar kurno yeɣeni esiw xizay k’oƛin.
translation: When he saw his younger sister coming back with a copper jug filled with water, he ran away from her, while she said: "Now there's no place you could escape from me [to]!", threw the jug down and ran behind him.

text: Ža yołi elay k’ek’kin ik’no anu.
translation: But he didn't even move from there.

text: Žan hemedurtow mat’i boɣa c’oxnosi, zira eƛin:
translation: And he also came to take away the duck, so the fox said:

text: Ela esirxosi kid yexux, xexłiƛ’ nełder bayr eliƛin!”.
translation: "The girl that we asked is dying, quick, bring us to her!"

text: Hoka k’eč’oli badiru raładyołäy madaħor besurobi raqruyä raq’ˤuƛin”.
translation: "Is it really that much (of a surprise) fish came out of the sea, than a foal was born on the carriage?"

text: Yeženi kidbä, din yegir yiłde sadaq hunin regirnoƛin, eniwq esirnosi, yegirzaƛ’ šida yik’inč’uƛin bixzin yoqno eƛin.
translation: When the eldest daughter asked her mother: "Let me go with her together so I can see her off!", and the mother angrily said: "Why didn't you go when I let you?"

text: Neła quł elo yeda Muħamadno zown.
translation: On that day, Muhammad was there.

text: Ela nełar naʕanan bodač’in, nocun caƛač’in, ža sadaq anusi eli debez ɣˤutka tełxorkin buyn baqinč’iƛin, Beqes ʕUneyzat yičin.
translation: "We will neither curse her, nor spit on her, and we will not even get into your house without her!", Sun's Uneyzat insisted.

text: Išno łiynosi, xan ƛ’iri utin, ža t’akan där neƛƛin.
translation: When he finished eating, the king insisted: "Give me this glass!"

text: Sida sider sisini muhukin boƛi bok’xo zown.
translation: They were even sharing every single grain among each other.

text: K’ox eƛa yawruni barur best’al kid yetix zownanu, neła nełar urɣizi boda ħilla xecix zownanu, nełdäy yet’u-t’it’iran yetin.
translation: His second wife didn't like her step-daughter, she didn't leave out any tricks to think about and wanted to get rid of her.

text: Hunin resun žedu nexłin, sadaq mesed gogaƛ’orno boɣno čuret’no bok’no nełas di nˤoƛu yadi.
translation: And even if they come, when they find the way, I will take away all the gold onto a meadow and with the beat of my whip, I'll transform it into ashes.

text: Habihan ziru ƛ’iräy boɣa ik’in, t’ok’ow debeł teł di nediw šebin ʕumruł rodač’inƛin.
translation: The miller went to apologize to the fox: "I won't do such a thing to you in my life anymore."

text: Eɣeni esiw yołi, šibaw ruzman ɣudƛ’ot’a babiwz šudaƛ’or ik’ix zown.
translation: The younger brother, however, went to his fathers grave every Friday.

text: At’ rac’anix q’ˤanon aɣi ocär bayn k’oƛin.
translation: Two birds came onto the threshing floor to eat the wheat.

text: Užä eƛin, ƛa boqzaƛ’ beza bik’an nełaƛ’ƛin.
translation: The boy said: "Let's go, if it is, we'll look at it!"

text: Kinaw žedu bałgołibin esin halmaɣbi maħor roqno.
translation: The friends had told all the secrets and went outside.

text: Nesis ozuri roƛa rutirzaƛ’, ʕaƛaɣor žamaʕatyoƛ’or xabar begirno, däz ozäɣor räk’iru šebi eƛa räynč’iru šebin roɣʷa horoƛin.
translation: Because his eye got hurt, he told it to the people in the village, "Something I don't know came into my eye; come and take it out!"

text: Elo žedus daʕban boqno ħukmu bodin, boƛ’arawgo roqłin sida esiƛ’oräy p’es anusi bičanixƛin.
translation: Then they argued and decided: "No matter what happens, when someone speaks, the other has to wait silently."

text: Neła reƛił xizay bełiƛ’ xanes łˤonoqu nukar oqno.
translation: Sixty of the king's henchmen went behind that meat.

text: ʕOmoyqor cek’ä eƛin, din iduɣor begirƛin.
translation: The goat kid said to the donkey: "Let me into the home!"

text: Sidaquł Musader xanes kid yayn.
translation: One day, the king's daughter came to Musa.

text: Xizyo neła gulułäy yałru užin oqno, babiwdäɣor ik’in.
translation: Afterwards, that horse became the boy that it used to be again and went to his father.

text: Šebi deber roqnoƛin yisä esirnosi, yiła yisiq q’orolayä äsiru xabar esin.
translation: When he asked: "What happened to you?", she told him what the widow had told her.

text: Ražbadinä eƛin, däder ayru ƛirba deber ukayn esunäy, ža qazaq yoł.
translation: Razhbadin said: "You saw that the comrade that came to me is Georgian.

text: Nagaħ mi ža yownč’inäy, mi din šuƛ’ir, di min šuƛ’iran, ela elułsi halmaɣłin łiyranƛin.
translation: Suppose you won't marry her, then forget about me and I will forget about you, and we will finish the friendship between us.

text: Tungi becunosi, mi ʕiyada behizi yaq.
translation: After the jug broke, you may cry.

text: Salamno teƛno, yisä esnazaq esirno, šida meži boɣnoyxƛin.
translation: He greeted and asked the brothers: "Why are you arguing?"

text: Ža yuyxor yutäru žek’ux yegira yoł di yedu kidƛin”.
translation: I shall give my daughter out to the man who turns her back into her true self.

text: Teł zawru ceyä yiłaq esirno, yedu insanes maħ nay mi t’or bowäƛin.
translation: The eagle that was inside asked him: "Where did you bring that human smell from?"

text: Mi di saɣ yoys, däx horoƛin”, - eženi esiya eƛin.
translation: "I cured you, come to me", said the eldest brother.

text: Nełas kuckinwa bigu anu, łinar ža mi yowäƛin, nesir žawabyor, bixzi yoqno yiłetow, eƛin eniya.
translation: "She doesn't even look pretty, why did you bring her?", answered him the mother as if getting angry.

text: Mužmarłäzay iduɣor aynosi, Pat’imater babiw q’ʷarid iłe ukayzaƛ’ yiła nesiq esirno, šida mi nece pašman oqno ičäsiƛin.
translation: After he came home from the Friday (prayer), Fatima asked her father because she saw him being so sad: "Why are you so sad?"

text: Xanez gulude biħanayƛ’oräy, šidqay ƛ’iyorno kurno, ƛ’irirno bizin ža di xot’ozad łełanƛin, eƛin kidbä.
translation: "While I am fighting next to the king's horse, I will throw it down from the street wall, get up again and trample it down with my feet

text: Gogaƛ’or baynosi, užiƛ’orno butin, ik’in ozyode sadaq kecno ičin.
translation: When it arrived at the meadow, it became the boy again and went to sleep next to the army.

text: Ič’an yeɣenägon eƛin, digon qema regirinč’ey asno riqirno yičäsi zows.
translation: And then the youngest one said: "And I was catching the sky and didn't let it rain.

text: Sida xanes zownƛax sis kid.
translation: Once there was a king who had one daughter.

text: Hemełƛ’o xizyo xanä Bilq’isdiqor eƛin, deber rätiru šebinon risno, mi t’ay xizor yik’iƛin.
translation: After that, the king said to Bilqisdi: "Take what you want and leave here!"

text: Ceyqor big bičiƛinno eƛin, neła näƛru sayɣatno bisno uži iduɣor ik’in.
translation: The boy said "Farewell!" to the eagle, took the present given to him and went home.

text: Oč’ino mučari ɣudest’a rac’zaƛ’,
translation: Because you ate nine cumin cakes a day,

text: Xizyogon, łi ħaƛa yołin yetas yeduƛin, rok’ƛ’or pikrun bayn, xecin.
translation: Then he thought, it might be useful to drink water with, and so he left.

text: ʕAƛoz č’imix yisił xanes nukar c’oxno.
translation: On the village's edge he met the king's henchman.

text: Haraƛ’ badirun, ɣˤanabin yoƛixno hemedurtow šebi retinƛin esirno eɣeni esiwq.
translation: After he shouted, the woman appeared and again she asked the younger brother: "What do you want?"

text: Xexza eƛin, eli maħor xalq’i q’ʷarid bodanix boqäsi anuƛin.
translation: Then children said: "We did not come outside in order to offend people."

text: Q’ˤida muɣno hiƛirno, neła ža hič’č’a rečiwsi riqirno qaca q’ˤidar karun, nełłäy bikorin boqno, bet’unč’ey žedałtow xizay nexxo zown.
translation: She put the bundle down, and the thickest piece of firewood that she threw down, turned into a snake, which didn't stay behind, but followed them.

text: Di žek’anč’e babiw nexxoƛin, yoł yałzatow q’ayn xecin užin oxin.
translation: Thinking that the father came to beat him, the boy left the goods where they were and ran away.

text: Ža užin, baħarawn sadaqno bičirno, žeduł roƛƛo hečk’er eqerno ciqqay reč’no rawruni ɣun.
translation: nan

text: Baħarčiƛin yizä yedu žeda žeduz kumakyor xecin.
translation: Taking her for a young man, they let her help themselves.

text: Salamno teƛno, bazarganza yisiq esirno, šebi roqno nece q’ʷaridno oqno ƛexasiƛin.
translation: The merchants greeted him and asked: "What happened, that you are so sad?"

text: Ceyez gugyoƛ’no zown, asoł teł mi ʕuqłäsi zowsƛin, eƛin kidbez babiya.
translation: "You were riding on the back of an eagle, and hiding inside the sky," the girl's father said.

text: C’odorawni esiya ʕantawni esiw ukrun, micxirno rasada q’ilimoł xizay dibirder egirno.
translation: The clever brother sent his stupid brother to the mullah for qilis to measure the amount of silver and money.

text: Neła deber kumak badiƛin.
translation: She will help you.

text: Eženä mołin, ent’o q’ˤim rok’oƛin.
translation: The old one explained: "Hit your head here!"

text: Xizyo Bilq’isdä ƛ’iräy k’ukno boɣno, bexorani neła nełas žežun bukarno, žedaqorno eƛin, howži meži iduzaɣor rik’iƛin, xizor žedun regirno.
translation: Then Bilqisdi took off her cap, showed her long ponytail and said to them: "Now go to your homes.", and she sent them back.

text: Reɣeni xaliča räqiruni nukarertow biqin beɣeni magalun.
translation: And the henchman who got the small mat also got the small bread.

text: Neła quł sasaqtow łˤonon esnabi q’ˤač’azin boqno xander bik’in.
translation: On that day in the morning, the three brothers dressed up and went to the king.

text: Ozyode sadaq yeda ƛˤoreł ik’a keze oqno.
translation: In the army, he had to go to the war.

text: Oƛixno k’oƛin tełer reƛ teƛxo zawruni bazargan.
translation: The merchant who had been selling the meat jumped inside.

text: Sasiłacen ičin, iduɣorno bižin yisä yedu ħumukuli buyn bexayn, waħazoni ɣˤutka nełas reƛno ruqˤno.
translation: She waited until it got dark, lead that camel home and indeed slaughtered it and hid it's meat in the cellar.

text: Q’orola äsiruxor rodin xanä.
translation: The king acted as the widow told.

text: Ruyxor mi esinč’inäy deber daru buyxor c’axinč’iƛin yiła eƛinosi, esin yisä yiłałno nesiłno raqru łinałäyn.
translation: When she said that the medicine won't have an effect if he doesn't tell the truth, he told her what had happened to him.

text: Esnaza Bilq’isdi yižin gamiƛ’zoni žek’ur teƛno.
translation: The brothers took Bilqisdi and sold her to a sailor.

text: Debeq ža äsirus haqu azirodoƛƛin, esiya eƛin.
translation: "May the mouth of the person who told you that freeze off!", the brother said.

text: Łˤora ɣudƛ’aza maduhalłiq yałru ʕaƛoł berten bičixosi.
translation: Three days later in a neighboring village there is a marriage.

text: Howži elay izin oqƛ’oräy, xanez žaq esiwz xediya esirno, šebi deber di t’ay neƛanƛin, micxirä, mesedä, q’ˤuya maʕišatäƛin.
translation: Now, while he began to get up from there again, the prince was asked by his sister's husband: "What shall I give you from there? Money, gold or another fortune?"

text: Zownƛax zownanuƛax q’ˤano halmaɣ netintow sadaq bełiƛ’ bik’ixosi.
translation: Once upon a time, there were two friends, who always went hunting.

text: ʕOloqan žek’a nesiq esirno, di netiey exanƛin.
translation: The young man asked him: "When will I die?"

text: Ža kaɣat yayrun, Ražbadinno, barun Telawäɣor halmaɣdäɣor bik’ix.
translation: Right after the letter arrived, Razhbadin and his wife went to Telavi to their friend.

text: Elo yisir besuroqu esun, gamušyozaq ƛisirno sis ixiw besuro bižix.
translation: There he found a fisherman who was carrying one big fish by buffalos.

text: Däq hemedur eƛin rok’łinč’us yołiƛin, eƛin qartayä, di mi aħirno, ešayn ac’a zowsƛin.
translation: "If I hadn't said so," the with said, "I would've cooked, baked and eaten you!"

text: Yiziqor doxturä eƛäsi zown, besuro bac’näy neła roƛxosi ričär mežusƛin.
translation: A doctor had said to them: "If you eat fish, this will stop you from being sick."

text: Waħ, debiya nece huinaw reƛ zownƛin, bečnayn roƛik’no yiła ža reƛ.
