translation: "Whoa, that oh so delicious meat was yours?!", he said and reurgitated the meat.

text: Hemedur di oc’ino ƛˤeb yoɣsi.
translation: I spent ten years like this.

text: Howlotow eniwqor xabarno boƛik’no, žan razi yoyn, zamanałäygon kidbeqay rožin riqirno, nełƛ’osi kʷaxatow bertenno boyn, aħozä ža kidno yown.
translation: Right there he started talking to the mother, she accepted it, and after some time he took her word and soon after that they celebrated the wedding and the shepherd married that girl.

text: Ɣudezoni ɣudä šow roƛäx deber.
translation: and tomorrow who will appear to you?

text: Idazayn oƛixno nesä yizi tełxorno goƛ’in, ƛ’irir zahrun ƛešun adäz bišʷa ħaƛin, bišƛ’oräytow bexuran yiziƛin.
translation: Coming out of the house, he called them inside, organized dinner and poured poison in it, to kill them when they eat.

text: Sid bazarganes xexbi zownanu.
translation: One merchant had no child.

text: Aždaħ bigza ħalał buyn akiłno, boɣno kurno ɣirno, idurno nexno ɣoɣorik’no hibon užizaz akix eqerno.
translation: The dragon got tired with all those difficulties, took the sieve and threw it away, came home, heated the staff again and put it to the boys' window.

text: Däzno uyran kidbełäy, ele howda sistow sis ƛexus.
translation: And of my four, only this one here remained.

text: Xande idur aynosi, nesir elo teł cemä ozzataw kid yukaynosi, iguni žek’a esirno, di nełaz ozzałer kanłi bayrnäy, šebi mi där baha neƛxoƛin.
translation: When he came to a house with the king and saw a blind girl in a corner inside there, the good man asked: "When I bring back her eyesight, what price do you give me?"

text: Ismaʕilä bašiqay roɣno bašiqˤoy teƛno yeženiqor, enda xanez kidbeqor teƛa bažarizi raqä debeqƛin.
translation: Ismail took a ring from his finger and gave it to the old woman: "Could you give it to the king's daughter?"

text: Xanä dowqor neti deber raładyołäy besurobi madaħor roqno rukadäƛin eƛinosi, mi eƛo, qaciz hoka k’eč’oli boyzaƛ’, raładyołäy besurobi madaħor raqruyä raq’ˤuƛin”.
translation: After the king said to you: "When did you saw fish coming out of the sea?", say that because the foal was born on the wooden carriage, is it more (of a surprise) for fish to come out of the sea?

text: Mix bik’äsi zamanno boqzaƛ’, yiła ɣˤanaza yeda neła nełdäɣor ƛirbałix ižix.
translation: Because some time had passed, that woman let him be her guest.

text: Neła yisiqor eƛin, mi di q’ˤuya guluƛno ħažin, din xecin q’ˤuya guluƛ’no mi zawnoƛin.
translation: It said to him: "Do not change me for another horse, and do not leave me and ride on another horse!"

text: Žan hemedurtow kecnon ičin, sasaqosi babiwderno nexno eƛin, där c’ohor ukaynč’uƛin.
translation: He also fell asleep and came in the morning to his father and said, "I did not see the thief."

text: Di bertenyołer goƛ’in ayäsi yoł ža.
translation: I invited him to the wedding and he has come.

text: Eženi esiya esin, č’ikays tungin bisno łäħorno yik’i, kikxar yik’iƛ’oräy yaħno bodo, elazay tungin bic’no nexƛ’oräy, esiwz žanazaxor yaynosi, tungi q’ˤidar bˤoƛ’a begirno xec.
translation: The older brother told: "Take a glass jug and go to the water; when you go to the spring, be patient and when you fill the jug there, come to your brother's corpse, let the jug fall down and leave.

text: Qura ƛˤebaƛ’aza di äƛiru šebin neƛanix mi ƛ’iri risnäy, di yedu nasin šebin tełxor čantaɣor rutiranƛin, eƛin eženä.
translation: "If you promise to give [me] after 20 years all the things I said, I will bring all the things into the inside of the pocket again.", said the old one.

text: Užiq žedur besurobi rukayzaƛ’, elur neƛƛin harizi rodin.
translation: When they saw the fish, they begged the boy: "Give them to us!"

text: Q’ˤano äƛiru ɣudƛ’o sasaq izin bazaryaɣor teƛanixƛin čanyan adäz boc’no yedan ik’in.
translation: On the second day in the morning, he got up, and went to drive the she.goat ahead in order to sell it on the market.

text: Esnazagon ža q’orolayq esin.
translation: And the sisters then told it to the widow.

text: Bazarganzar k’ukyoƛ tupin ħažin, žedudetow sadaq xizor utin nexxo zown ža.
translation: Having exchanged the rifle for a cap with the merchants, he returned with them.

text: Doxturä nełaz babiwƛ’or xabar begirno, debi mi kid xizor yižanix horoƛin.
translation: The doctor said to her father, "Come and take your daughter."

text: Neł t’umä ža t’akan yižin xaneł purłäzan eqerno, elaygon bitor hawaƛ’ žedu biħada bik’ixozo xanzo akixor bik’in.
translation: The pigeon carried this glass, put it next to the king and from there flew to the king's window, from which they went out to fight.

text: Łäs tungin bic’no xizor yutin yedu nexƛ’oräy, esiwz žanazał adäzar yaynosi, gugyoq caƛin bowx zawru tungi begirno xecin yiła q’ˤidar.
translation: She filled the jug with water and while she was coming back, she came to the corpse of her brother, let the jug fall down, which was [bringing/thrown at her back???], and left.

text: Žoyä eƛin, däz baɣma mesedyos heneš boqxosi sis ɣun yoł.
translation: The boy said, "There is a tree with golden apples in my garden.

text: Nesä ža ɣamas hokomƛ’on erno iħuxaɣor yižiƛ’oräy, łas haraƛ’ teqnosigon, nełaq roč’uganitow qˤaƛu regin.
translation: He put the box onto a carriage and while carrying it to a river, but when he heared the water's voice, it emitted a frosty shout.

text: Wˤałzoni dunyalƛ’or aynosi, užir elo Okus bercinaw kid yesun.
translation: When he came into the underworld, the boy found Oku's beautiful daughter there.

text: Roda šebin koƛ’inč’ey, yeda maduhalłiq yałru ʕaƛaɣor ik’in.
translation: Not knowing what to do, he went to a village that was in the neighbourhood.

text: Ayn yeda zeyä äƛiruni qoqƛer, elo q’ˤim ritäruntow qoq baɣˤiłno bik’in, teł yisir łaces gulubi resun.
translation: The boy came to the cliff, which the bear was talking about, touched the cliff with his head and after it has opened he went inside and found wind horses.

text: Didurnotow ʕaƛos xalq’imos ħal bayrxo zownanu.
translation: And the village's people couldn't defeat him in any way.

text: Bazarganbin boxin biłe bik’in nełzaqay, hibo boɣanix hoboyä hik’eyä uži xizay oƛixxoƛin.
translation: And the merchants went away from there hastily, [being afraid that] sooner or later this boy might appear behind them to take the stick away.

text: Oƛƛozogon esiya eƛin:
translation: The middle brother said:

text: Howži di nełał xizay oqäsi yołƛin.
translation: That's why I decided to find it."

text: Sida zamanał, neširuxayt’a šebi eƛa riyxanuzo łinan c’iɣaƛ’ ƛ’iri yałru ʕaƛ k’ek’-bik’erxot’a zown.
translation: One time, at night the village on the shoulder.blade was moved by something unknown.

text: Zira bik’in, xanä täƛruni, Goqiq šeƛ’un šeƛ’urno, guluƛ’no zowerno xandäɣor hunix boqno.
translation: The fox left, let Goqi put on the clothes and get on the horse given by the king and so they went on their way to the king.

text: Hut’-mˤaliƛ’ häqruni t’ut’ƛ’orkin nesä reƛ’a rizirxo zown anu, žatow boxin bik’ace xecix zown.
translation: Even to a fly who was sitting on his face, he did not lift the hand, but he left it there until it flew away.

text: Elo q’orolay dandir c’oxnosi, nełaq esirno, debi uži ukayxanuwa na ža yołƛin.
translation: When they met the widow there, they asked her: "We can't see your son, where is he?"

text: Xizaɣor esiwz kidbä yiłqor eƛin, yisiq’ay igun, bečedawn deber uži äqinč’i kid xedyox yegirinč’ey min yok’äłinč’iƛin.
translation: Finally the brother's girl said to her that she won't find any man as rich and wealthy, so that the daughter couldn't do anything else than to take him as a husband.

text: Ža kid dicegon bac’ałiƛ’ yoqłin, neła xediwƛ’or kaɣat caxno, debi kid neširuxayt’a q’ʷat’azaq yaħna-yik’ix yičixƛin.
translation: Although the daughter was very cleanly, she wrote a letter to her husband: "Your daughter is being wandering through the streets at night."

text: Elus xan oxisƛin, nesiłgon xizaz wazirbin čˤaƛin bik’in.
translation: [Saying] "Our king ran away!", his viziers went running behind him.

text: Riqixa hudu yisiq beqes rešambi?
translation: How could he get the sunrays like this?

text: Alimaħama maduhalłiqozoni ʕaƛar ayn.
translation: Alimagomed came into a neighboring village.

text: Elo yizi xexza žeda žedur ɣˤutku rodanix ros bič’a bašla bodin.
translation: There these children began to dig a base for building a house for themselves.

text: Sida mixƛ’o ƛ’iräzay qemas ixiw ix boƛixno.
translation: At some point in time, a big torrent of rain came from above.

text: K’ox äƛirugon užiłäy mesedyos šilabi yołäsi miƛ’i boqno.
translation: For the second time, the boy became a ram with golden horns.

text: Idur aynosigon, eniyan, kidbäntow, rok’u č’eze roqaniƛäy, best’alawni kid yexuriyäƛin, esirno nesiq.
translation: After he came home, the mother and her daughter asked him: "Did you kill the stepdaughter?" to become convinced.

text: Yedu haqa yogun ƛexun, nece bercinawn, č’uħarawn šeƛ’u yiła best’alawni kidber naey riqisƛin.
translation: She was gaping: "Where the heck did the 'stepgirl' get so beautiful and gorgeous clothes from?"

text: Ža ik’a ayxosi ʕaƛ ʕuraw maqˤi zown.
translation: The village where he had to go, was very far away.

text: Tawadi bercingo ezun ičäsi yałzay, sid žoyä Ražbadinxo yowxozo baħarayz hibon bok’no łočyaƛ’ yoɣno.
translation: While Tavadi was looking carefully, one boy hit the bride that was to marry Razhbadin with a stick and took her away to dance.

text: Yedu šebi nece bercinaw šebinƛin, yeda rešambi riqiranix c’oxno.
translation: "What beautiful thing is this?!", he tried to catch the rays.

text: Elo babiya kidbazaqor eƛin, meži däqor rezun howt’o ričinčey, di ezu-ʕaƛ’a ik’an sasaqor ɣudi qˤaširanix elur t’ozoƛ’ay bigu moči yołäƛin.
translation: There the father told them: "Wait for me here, I will go and have a look around if there is a better place to spend the night than this one here."

text: Qˤišdes zaman baynosi, boƛik’u magalu yiła bet’urno ɣˤʷayr kurno.
translation: When the dinner time came, she pulled out half the bread and threw it to the dog.

text: Nełaƛ’no zown ʕaƛarno nexnogon eɣeni esiya eƛin, dey eženi esiw esurƛin.
translation: Sitting down on it, the young brother came home to the village and said: "Find my big brother!"

text: Debez tawakalyoƛ’aza riqäsi bečełi yoł yeduƛin, xediw q’urec’niƛ’ay q’ay boɣaniłer oqno, ža iduɣor bižanix kumakyorgon barun yizin.
translation: "Thanks to your patience we got such a wealth!" and the husband began to unload the articles from the mules and his wife helped to carry them home.

text: Bara ƛeli bowwäƛin esirnosi, yisä nełaq raqru sadaq šebin esin.
translation: His wife asked, if he brought the lamb, and he told her everything that had happened.

text: Užä eƛin, pardagi eranix dey ya esiw anu, ya baru anu, där retäsi šebin anu žaƛin.
translation: The son said, "I have neither sister nor wife to wear this scarf."

text: Anu, xan, deyno, debin kidbabi nełar howži ħaram roqsi, šidaƛin eƛiłi ža aždaħ bexursiƛin”, - esin q’orola.
translation: "No, king, he won't get my and your daughters, because he was killed", said the widow.

text: Hemedurtow žan xecin, užin oqno iduɣor ik’in.
translation: The boy left her that way and went home.

text: Bik’in yiziri neła ceyder, žedus murad bič’zi boqnosi neła eƛin:
translation: They went to that eagle, and when he had understood their wish, the eagle said:

text: Neła elay harizin rodin nasin ħalt’uqanbin boxirno, Musał adäz bečnazaƛ’orno c’oxno eƛin:
translation: She asked all workers to get away from there, felt on her knees before Musa and said:

text: Žeda eƛixƛax, howlo rečuƛ dice buħi bičix, hohomaw mečoƛ’ ocä xecix yołi dice rigu räči, elaƛ’ ƛ’iri hisno rigu raq łemuƛin.
translation: They were saying: "There in the shed is so much heat, if he would leave us in a chilly place, on the threshing floor, how good that would be, and then our wool would be better, too!"

text: Idur ayza qułtow, neširu kʷat’izi baqru mixƛ’o, iday maħorno oqno, ɣˤutkoł adäzarno ik’in, yisä k’emot’ yaɣˤirun, elo ixiw ɣˤutku roqno.
translation: On the day when he came home, when it has become late at night, he got outside his home, went in front of his house and right when he opened the box, a big house came into being there.

text: Elo ža išno-utin, łin ħaƛun, elaygon bitor hunix oqno ik’in neł ɣˤanazaz baɣmoxor ayn.
translation: There he ate and drank the water, and went on on his way and came to that woman's garden.

text: Žeda yisiqor eƛin, wey uži, mi łinar t’aɣor nexä, eluƛ’ yałru ʕazabno, ʕaq’uban bukayxanä deberƛin.
translation: They said to him: "Hey boy, why have you come here; don't you see the torture and pain that is upon us?

text: Didurnotow yiziq sidaƛ’or hut’ rutinč’izaƛ’, yizis šarʕimoƛ’ bik’anix q’ut’i boqno.
translation: Because they could not reach a consensus, they agreed to go to the Sharia.

text: Cuƛer yäzurun, yiłar ɣunoq bäzirubi bukayn.
translation: When she looked upward, she saw those who got up into the tree.

text: Yisä eƛin, Ismaʕiles netitow ƛuzabi q’iq’isƛin.
translation: He said: "Ismail's bones had rotten a long time ago."

text: Žedun hemedurtow łek’irełinč’ey bayn.
translation: And so they also couldn't stir it and came back.

text: Xediwqorno eƛin yiła, yeda t’o ʕaƛay maħor oxirƛin.
translation: And she said to her husband: "Chase him out of the village here!"

text: Yisisgon šeƛ’u nesän šeƛ’un, aħon iħuz lilyox ičin.
translation: And the shepherd put on his clothings and waited at the river bank.

text: Q’ˤim rätiruntow žan baɣˤiłno nexno.
translation: He touched it with his head, the cliff opened and he came in.

text: Žeda esirno, nay yedu deber nece raq’ˤu his riqäƛin.
translation: They asked: "Where did you get so much wool from?"

text: Neł č’uramxor ɣˤutkoxorno ik’in akäz ezunosi, sis ozuri yołali esiya q’ˤiryoƛ’ geler žek’uzas q’ˤimbi kurxo yukayn.
translation: He went to the house with light, looked into the window and saw his one-eyed sister throwing people's heads over the floor.

text: Ža neła royxosi šebin rukaynosi, C’iɣruk’ä ħamori kurno.
translation: When he saw what he was doing, Tsighruk coughed.

text: Allahä bižaraw šibaw ɣudeł yiła oč’inot’a mučari rac’xo zown.
translation: Every day she ate nine cumin cakes.

text: Hemełƛ’o guluƛay dahaw bero becin bik’in.
translation: After that, a little ice thawed away from the horse.

text: Nełas maʕna yoł, keze aqza ƛirbałi rodoƛin.
translation: The sense of his words was: 'Be a guest whereever you go to!'"

text: Sisgon xalq’imor riyxanusi šebin, łiłiroƛ’ ƛ’iri sis ažo yoł.
translation: "One thing that the people don't know is that there's a tree above the ditch.

text: Ižin waħazoni raƛ’moƛ’or ayrxo yeda.
translation: It took him and brought him to the land below.

text: Ža k’eč’oli hokomxo zown.
translation: The foal was at the carriage.

text: Deyn sida där etix zawruni žasno q’ut’i boqäsi yoł:
translation: The agreement I have with the boy I was in love with, is:

text: Sida quł Goqix kidno esirno xanderno bik’in zira eƛin:
translation: One day, the fox went to the king to ask for his daughter to be promised for Goqi, and said:

text: Xanä eƛin, Ismaʕil äxuzay soder nesiz gulude puħor ik’iłxosi žek’u oqinč’u, min hemedurtow ik’äłinč’iƛin.
translation: The king said: "Ever since Ismail died, there was no man who could go near his horse; and you won't be able to do so either."

text: Mi šebi, hunizaxno dowƛer guluyä bičira betin.
translation: "Why do you want to restrain a horse under you on the ways?

text: Ža ɣˤanabi yałrułi žedurno yiynanu.
translation: And they didn't know that she was a woman.

text: Łˤono äƛiru ɣudełno, babiws ħal bayrinč’ey, besurozaƛ’or užitow ik’in.
translation: And on the third day, although the father was against it, the boy went fishing nonetheless.

text: Xanes kidbabin ʕuraw ɣˤaɣˤu rizin.
translation: Also the king's daughters got very happy.

text: Yizi q’ˤunän bigu q’ut’i bodin, hudu yisi babiyagon yisiq bałgołi esin:
translation: The two of them made a proper agreement and so the father told him the secret:

text: His- musoƛ’or rutin, aħozi oqzaƛ’.
translation: and because there was a shepherd, they turned into wool.

text: Bilq’isdi xanłun xecanix žamaʕat razi boqno.
translation: The population agreed to let Bilqisdi become king.

text: Qartay maħor yäk’irun, C’irduxä izin užibi käczaɣor kidbabi, kidbabi käczaɣor užibin butirno, neła łi rayrace ičin.
translation: Right after Qartay went outside, Tsirdukh got up, put the girls to the place where the boys slept and the boys to the place where the girls slept, all before she brought the water.

text: Sida quł neširu yisir moƛał rukayn: ħonzaq aħnayƛ’oräy, yisir łˤono gagali besuxƛax - sis mesedyos biłe ƛ’er yołäsi, sideni žawharyos biłe ƛ’er yołäsi, sidenigon yaq’utyos biłe ƛ’er yołäsi.
translation: One night he had a dream: while walking around the mountains, he found three flowers - one in the color of gold, another one in the color of a pearl and yet another one in the color of ruby.

text: Yisiq riyrno, dibirqor mi äƛino, ukrun, micxirno rasadanix q’ili betinƛin, mi eƛo at’ rasadanix betinƛin.
translation: He explained to him: "Don't tell the mullah that we want the qilis to measure silver and money, but tell him that we want the qilis to measure wheat!"

text: Neła zira yisiq harizi rodin, deber taliħ neƛani, žeda boc’aqay di xʷasar bodoƛin.
translation: That fox pleaded to him, "May fortune be given to you, please rescue me from those wolves!"

text: Babiwqay kidbes rožin riqirno ɣˤaɣˤun izin Ražbadin iduɣor ik’iƛ’oräy, dandir keze aqru esiwq babiw razi oqsiƛin esin.
translation: Razhbadin took the girl's word from the father, got happy and while he went home, he told his brother whom he met, that the father agreed.

text: Elo teł yexasi esiw yesunosi, yedu šebin šebi t’o idu roqxoƛin baruzaq esirzaƛ’, elar riyxosi šebin anuƛin, inkar bisno ričin.
translation: When they found their dead sister inside, and asked their wives: "What happened here at home?", they rejected: "We don't know anything about this here."

text: Eɣeni esiw šet’ur ezu-ʕoƛ’ux, xal bodix ik’ix zown.
translation: The young brother went examining and looking around.

text: Hudu dey šak bik’ix yizä bok’ek’no bäsuƛin”.
translation: So my suspicion will be that they appear to have stolen it."

text: Hemedur k’ox bizin cey soder hawaƛ’ay wˤałer uži kuranix.
translation: So he got upward into the air a second time to throw the boy downward.

text: Hudun beže taliħ boqsi: nesiztow hunaryoƛ’aza lilyoxaɣor ok’ełsi.
translation: Nevertheless, the luck was on his side: Because of his talent, he escaped to the river bank.

text: Eženi žek’a kid kikxor yegirno, elo deber biša räsuƛin.
translation: The old man sent the girl to the spring: "There you will find food."

text: Bełiqan sodxorzoq iziƛ’orey, sidaɣ ħalkaraw mečoƛ’ ɣunoƛ q’ˤida ičäsi łˤonon žek’u esun.
translation: While the hunter went upwards, he found three men sitting under a tree on an even place somewhere.

text: Ik’in, ik’in ža sidaɣ kikxor ayn.
translation: He went and went and came to a spring somewhere.

text: Hemełƛ’o xizyo babiwz ozzałer č’uran rayn, ža nesis uži yałrułin iyn.
translation: After that, the father's eyesight came back and he knew it was his son.

text: Elay nesä ik’in baruq raqru łinałäyn esinosi, neła eƛin, neširu t’ekan bexʷad, užin goƛ’o, xizyo di nesiq esiranƛin.
translation: He went away and told his wife what had happened, and she said: "Slaughter a goat tonight, call the boy and afterwards I will ask him."

text: Allahzo ħalk’ołiƛ’aza ɣˤutkoxor ayzaƛ’ortow gamiƛ’ozoni žek’uz ozzałer č’ura rayn.
translation: Through Allah's might, the light came back to the sailor's eyes even before he had come to his house.

text: K’oƛin bik’in yizirabi sist’a c’oxłin, q’ˤanot’a c’oxłin, c’ik’iwtow c’oxłin aždaħes q’ˤim žeduq q’ˤiday k’ek’-rik’no anu.
translation: Although running around, they came together one at a time, two at a time, and then all together, but couldn't lift the dragon's head up to them.

text: Žeda eƛin, ʕaƛos boƛik’u ric’q’i neƛa yołƛin.
translation: They said: "We will give [you] half the wealth of the village!"

text: Mi elur raładyoz iƛ’aq besuroz haqa ukaysƛin, eƛin kidbez babiya.
translation: "We have seen you at the bottom of the see in the fish's mouth," said the girl's father.

text: Xizyogon eženi esiw ik’in, nesiqno šebin roqinč’izaƛ’, q’ˤim ƛ’iyoħor rutirno cugu kurno.
translation: Then the eldest brother went and nothing happend, so he lowered his head and threw the barrel over.

text: Exurnäy exuroƛ, exunäy exuƛ, rätiru roqoƛƛin, eƛin Ħusenä, eženił xizay ik’inč’ey dey res anuƛin.
translation: "If he kills me, he shall kill me; if I die, I shall die; may it happen whatever wants to.", said Hussein and followed the old man, "I don't have the possibility not to go."

text: Nesi užä yołi nˤoƛas ixinabi ric’no, ʕomoyƛ’no erno hunix oqno, dicegon žedu, ža nˤoƛugon łinar yižixƛin, nesiqor baƛ’ix bičiłin.
translation: The boy however filled his bags with ashes, put them onto the donkey and went on his way, although they were scolding him much: "Why are you carrying these ashes?!"

text: Di däz raƛ’mar ayro mežaƛin, - eƛin yisä yizuqor.
translation: "Bring me to my land!", he said to them.

text: Užä žeduqor eƛin, neła ziys ħošis režigon reɣet’a t’it’urbi roys yołi, bežigon baq’ˤu łemu där micxir bäqiƛin.
translation: The boy said to them: "If you had made even smaller pieces, then I would've gotten even more money."

text: Däz kidbes cin rexurno, buhtan erayä oqäsi miƛin, cimyarno izin xan nesiƛ’ łeɣaniƛ’or ayäsi zown.
translation: "Did you dishonor my daughter's name and ...?", the king got mad and was about to pound on him.

text: Daʕba łiyranixƛin žedu q’ˤanon xander bik’in.
translation: In order to resolve the dispute, they both went to the king.

text: Uži yeženi esiya ižix zown.
translation: The eldest sister carried the boy.

text: Ozurazaq reƛ’an riħin egirno yiła yeda, kʷaxatow debez ozzar č’ura rukayƛin.
translation: She lead her hands over his eyes and sent him away: "Soon your eyes are going to see the light again."

text: Žeda eƛix zown, buqbetaxtow aħozä eli iduɣor rižinč’ey, mark’ač’uxor xecix yołi rigu räči yał.
translation: They were saying: "If the shepherd wouldn't lead us home when the sun sets, but would leave us until the twilight, that would be good!"

text: K’oƛočo yiguni, ƛ’ˤuƛ’or yezu šida yedu wˤałer rešnada roqä, yagi qemayä regirxoƛin?”
translation: "Run, my dear, look at the roof why it is beginning to leak (downward) or if it's raining."

text: Moƛaxayn izin žoyän esin, ža žedus ɣˤutku yałrułi.
translation: The boy got up from his sleep and told that it is their house.

text: Mi dey hič’č’a eženi užin yoł.
translation: "You are my eldest son.

text: Užä eniwq bazarganzał raqru šebin esin.
translation: The boy told his mother what had happened about the merchants.

text: Hunix bik’iƛ’oräy, zira reƛiƛ’ sis haqu caƛinanu.
translation: While going on his way, the fox didn't put his mouth on the meat.

text: Zownƛax zownanuƛax Mamali-Maħamaƛin ci yołäsi habihan.
translation: Once upon a time there was a miller named Mamali-Magomed.

text: Zownƛax, zown anuƛax sida ʕaƛa busurmanaw xan.
translation: Once upon a time in a village there was a Muslim king.

text: Sasiłnosi narnokin rik’an koƛ’inč’ey łˤonon esnabi ciqqotow ƛexun.
translation: After it got dark, the three sisters didn't know where to go and remained in the forest.

text: Howži elorno ik’in, žedu miƛ’bi roƛixace ʕAliqilič ezun ičin.
translation: So now Aliqilich went there and looked for the rams to appear.

text: Elo ɣˤutka teł babiwn, kidno besun.
translation: There in the house he found father and daughter.

text: Howži yołi yizide sadaq xannonan ax roqace išno.
translation: Now, however, even the king ate together with them until his belly was full.

text: Zira iqäru žoyä ceyz tupi caƛinosi, igo yałru ariƛ’no c’oxno, cey k’ek’-bik’noanu.
translation: The boy that the fox had found, shot the eagle with his rifle and hit a branch that was near, the eagle didn't move.

text: Neširur ža aderzotow sida šušuliłäsi aɣˤuxor aynosi, sasaqor howlotow ičanix ħukmu boqxo.
translation: By the evening he came to a demolished mill and decided to wait there until morning.

text: Elo yisir šeƛ’urez meča ħošan lagiq šet’u rutirno ahli besun.
translation: There he found people wearing hides around their body instead of clothes.

text: Howži doxtur oqno xanez žarno, bečedawni žek’us kidberno daruyabi rodanix.
translation: After that the doctor began to cure the khan's son and the daughter of the rich man.

text: Hunix bik’iƛ’oräy, žedu sida iħixor baynosi, nesiq yałruni šeƛ’u roɣno iħiq rižirno.
translation: While they were on their way, after they came to a river, he took off Goqi's clothings and threw them into the river.

text: Ac ħišäsi yesuzaƛ’, boc’ä qˤaƛin, ħišixosi, ac seroƛin.
translation: Because the door was locked, the wolf shouted: "It's locked, unlock the door!"

text: Łi ħaƛun łäyrun, mesedyos ža yic’nosi, xanes kid tamaša yoqno ƛexun.
translation: When the princess finished drinking the water, it filled with gold and she was very surprised.

text: Žedu režet’ani esnazar retinanu yeɣeni esiw xanxo yik’izaƛ’, yizi urɣizi roqno, šebiey nełar elaq žukłi roqanƛin.
translation: The older sisters didn't want that, because the younger sister went to the king, he would think, that something evil with us might happen to her.

text: Guluƛ’no zown sosigon Musa čuret’ bok’nosi, reč’yałer yizirabi xanzo ɣˤutkox igor bayn.
translation: When he was on the horse, Musa first beated the whip, and in an instant they came to the king's house.

text: Ža posurƛ’ort’a butirxo xalq’i bac’xosi žek’u yołƛin, ʕaq’lu boyn neła kidbä.
translation: He is a man who turns people into cattle and eats them!", advised the girl.

text: Nełƛ’osi kʷaxatow, habihanno zirun xanes kid esiranix bik’in.
translation: Soon after that, the miller and the fox went to ask for the king's daughter.

text: Ɣʷˤadin zirun žeda žedudäɣorno rik’in halmaɣłin rexurno.
translation: The crow and the fox went to each other and ended their friendship.

text: Ža bertenyołkin ičinč’ey xizor ik’a oqno zawru, di ħalaxor eč’räsi yoł.
translation: Without even being on the marriage, he started to go back, and I could hardly stop him.

text: Sebixaza žedu rˤełayzał zown.
translation: In autumn they were on the threshing floor.

text: Elayn izin t’akanno yisno, et’un guluƛ’orno k’oƛin, elar bižin, t’ar bižin bigza ħalał žan akik’no, xizyo yižin xande t’akanno eqerno.
translation: He got up from there, took the glass and jumped onto a horse, lead it here and there, which tired him quite much, and then he carried the glass back and put it next to the king.

text: Yizi biħanayxozo mečoƛ’or ayn, yisä salam teƛnosi, ʕoƛiran esnaza yisis salam butirno, yiziƛ’ biħanada bayrut’anibi yołi täqinč’irus riłe royn bičin.
translation: He went to the place where they were fighting, and when he greeted them, the seven brothers gave back the greeting, the ones they were fighting however pretended not to have heard anything.

text: Xizyo xaner riyn neła q’ˤuna žek’us zawru daʕba łiyärułi.
translation: Afterwards the king found out that she had finished the two men's dispute.

text: Zownƛax, zown anuƛax sis bełiqu.
translation: Once upon a time there was a hunter.

text: Ɣudes uži bełiƛ’ aħnayx, bełiqanzo haq’us maʕišat bigełno (bigu boqno).
translation: Daily the son went hunting and thus the hunter's family's fortune improved (they got wealthy).

text: Telawärno bik’in xizor eli butinosi, bertenno bodan.
translation: After we returned to Telavi, we will organize a marriage.

text: Hay riguƛin, reynoyx neširur ričin.
translation: Well then, he said, so they worked until the evening.

text: Zamanałäy yizi sidaɣ q’ˤunader huni č’ˤaƛäłza mečoƛ’or baynosi, sidaɣaza hunä kid yegirno, sidaɣaza bikori bik’in.
translation: When, after a while, they came to a point where the road forked, the snake sent the girl the one way and itself went the other one.

text: Sida quł yizider babiw ayn.
translation: One day, the father came to them.

text: Nełƛ’osigon oƛƛosi esiw neł xanzo kidbeder, däx barułun horoƛin, esirno ik’in.
translation: Then the middle brother went to the princess, "Come and be my wife!"

text: Elus xan exursiƛin, ozno, xalq’in roxin rik’iƛ’oräy, sidaɣaza puƛ’azar Ismaʕil k’oƛin, sidaɣazar ʕArabuzan yoqno nasin ahli dandirno butirno, xanłun ʕArabuzanno xecin, Ismaʕilgon wizirłunno ičin.
translation: "They killed our king!", the army and the people ran away and at the same time, Ismail ran to one side, and Arabuzan ran to the other side, and they gathered all the people together and Arabuzan was elected king/queen and Ismail became vizier.

text: Siryoz bet’erhanä eƛin:
translation: The mare's master said:

text: Nesiq reƛ’a ritirerzaƛ’or łaqnokin ritirerač’inƛin.
translation: Before he touches me, no one else may touch me.

text: Elo teł yisir osiw qala besun.
translation: There he found a high fortress.

text: Eženi žek’a ɣudes ƛirba ayruxort’a bˤeƛ’es šebin rexayx zown.
translation: The old man slaughtered one of his sheep for guests who were coming.

text: Nesä eƛin, xanes kidtow yegir šeƛ’u risanix.
translation: He said: "Send the princess to take the clothes!"

text: Idur yayruntow yiła eƛin, xexłiƛ’ babiya mi goƛ’ixƛin.
translation: Right after she came [to his] home, she said: "My father invites you urgently!"

text: Xizyo eɣeni esiya nełaz c’eruc’a caƛinosi, sis nełas lel ɣunoƛer yoƛ’ˤun yayn, t’awusno hawaƛ’ k’oƛin boxin bik’in.
translation: Then the youngest brother shot with the bow and the peacock flew away, but one feather fell down under the tree.

text: Hudun nesä žedur bukaynč’iruxor araq’i q’ˤidat’a biħurxo zown.
translation: Nevertheless he poured the vodka down, so they didn't see it.

text: Bełiqanä iduɣor bižin kikin ža cey.
translation: The hunter carried the eagle home and fed it.

text: Ɣedu biqiranƛin bik’inƛax žedu, mark’ač’uxar xizor butin bayn, eluq ɣedu biqinč’uƛin.
translation: "We will take the shadow", and they went on and came back in the twilight: "We didn't get the shadow."

text: Bayru q’ut’oƛ’ xan razi oqno.
translation: The king agreed on the contract to do so.

text: Keč’oƛ’asi pu łiynosi nesis kanłi bäs, kut’yoƛ’asi łiynosi äxu.
translation: When he finishes the left side, he will lose his eyesight and when he finishes the right side, he will die.

text: Sidenägon eƛin, ɣamasno nesirtow xecingon boxinäy, režigon rigu räčiƛin.
translation: The other one said, "It is better to leave him the box and run away."

text: Ža šeƛ’un šeƛ’un best’al kidno bertenyołxor yik’in.
translation: The stepdaughter put on the clothes and went to the marriage.

text: Ħon-bahadurä kurno c’eruc’as hil, aždaħzo yoƛƛo hut’-mˤalä c’oxno.
translation: Mountain~Hero shot an arrow from his bow and hit the dragon in the middle of its face.

text: K’ox äƛiru rayäru bišadyołno besun zahru.
translation: And for the second time there was poison in the food they had brought.

text: Amma nesi žoyä xanłi risnoanu.
translation: But the boy didn't take up the kingship.

text: Ža bihayaw šebin yołƛin, Bilq’isdä nełaq yałru micxir ečruni žek’uqor teƛno.
translation: "That's an easy thing.", said Bilqisdi and gave the old man all the money she had.

text: Kidber riyn nełaz babiwł adäza t’et’erxo zawruni užiłäy gulu baqrułi.
translation: His daughter knew that the horse used to be the boy, who learned in front of her father.

text: Elay ža oxirƛ’oräy, t’omceni žek’a mešok’lałäy boɣno musno, bełiqan imyoqorno icin, kinaw bišan rac’no, et’un k’oƛin mamalayƛ’no zown, oqno ik’in.
translation: When he chased him away from there, the span-sized man took a hair out of his moustache, tied the hunter to a pole, ate up the entire food, jumped on his rooster and rode away.

text: Wallah, etixƛin, eƛin ečruni žek’a.
translation: "By God, I do!", said the old man.

text: Idur aynosigon, yexurno ža k’eneč’ žeduz ozzaq yiħoƛin.
translation: "After you come home, crack the egg and smear it on their eyes."

text: ʕUraw ħayran oqno xan, neła kidbes c’odorłin ʕaq’ilłin rukayn.
translation: The king became very suprised, and he saw that girl's cleverness and intelligence.

text: Žamaʕatyo ɣˤaɣˤu bizizaq nesir baq’ˤu mesedno teƛno, reže suħmatno rodin hunix egirno.
translation: Because the population got happy, they gave him much gold, organized a big party and let him go on his way.

text: Hemece raładya caƛi- ok’no yisiz lagiq siskin šebin ƛexun anu.
translation: The sea threw him many times, but there were no traces on his body.

text: Baq’ˤu šaharyasi ʕoloqbi boƛixno di, di ža aždaħ bexursiƛin.
translation: Many young men from the city came and claimed they had killed the dragon.

text: Elaygon bitor žedu bik’inƛax, rigu hawan, bac’adaw łin, raqˤäsi bix yałzarno, žek’utow anuza mečoƛ’or baynƛax.
translation: From there they went on and came to a deserted place with good air, clean water and where the grass was thick.

text: Badiru q’ut’imoxor, doxturä nesi bečedawni žek’uqor eƛin, där debi roƛik’u bečełi retin anu, där debi kid yowa yetixƛin.
translation: As it was arranged, the doctor said to the wealthy man, "I do not want the half of your riches, but I want to marry your daughter."

text: Esnaza esin, hemedur hunin łaħin, t’ok’ow nar rik’an koƛ’inč’ey, c’idoxor nexno howt’or raysƛin.
translation: The sisters told, that they lost their way and didn't know where to go anymore, so they came here to the fire.

text: Xizaɣor, hudun nełar riyx babiya užiqay bisxot’a micxir c’idaɣor kurxosi yałrułi.
translation: Nevertheless she finally found out that the father was throwing the money that the boy earned into the fire.

text: Idur baynosigon elon žeda ixiw berten bodin.
translation: After they came home, they also celebrated a big wedding there.

text: Žädgon oc’ino łenokin ɣudi yoł bˤeƛ’ ħonqaɣor rižanix zaman baya, eli edeʕizi boqa baynč’iƛin, eƛin žeda.
translation: "There are still fifteen days left to lead the sheep to the mountain, we don't need to hurry.", they said.

text: Neła kidno, užin rodäsi resun.
translation: She had given birth to a daughter and a son.

text: Elayn izin imyoq ħobo bok’nosi, nazay eƛa biynč’ey t’as bˤoƛ’un bayn.
translation: So he got up from there and kicked the pole, and out of who-knows-where a bowl fell down.

text: Sidakin kidbä nesix yik’anix roži teƛxo zownanu.
translation: Not even one girl promised to marry him.

text: Neła eƛin:
translation: She said:

text: Q’ˤanon q’ˤidan bičin biš-butin łiynosi, užä nełqor ħaƛanix łäsno yic’no besuroz haqa yesun zawruni t’akan teƛno.
translation: The two sat down and when they had finished eating, the boy filled the glass he found in the fish's mouth with water and gave it to her to drink.

text: Sidqor sis bicin howlotow žedun xecin, q’urec’niƛ’ ƛ’iri esiws cudin erno, oqno iduɣor žan ik’in.
translation: He tied them one on one and left them there, then he put his brother's corpse onto the mule and began to go home.

text: Mi däƛ’ay ežen yoł, däzoƛ’aygon rigu tupin yoł debeq, mitow č’aq’ˤirno nesir bižoƛin eƛin, teƛno anu užä ža aɣi xanez nukarqor.
translation: "You are older than me and also you have a rifle better than mine, kill one yourself and carry it to him!" the boy said and didn't give the bird to the king's henchman.

text: Ɣˤutkazoni imyoq ħobon bok’no rok’er rätiru šebin harizi roynäy roqxosi yołƛin.
translation: "If you kick a pole in the house and ask for what your heart desires, it will become true."

text: Hunix dandir c’axru ahlimo esirzaƛ’, žedur teƛxo užä łäs bic’äsi q’ut’u łiyrno.
translation: Because on the way, people whom he met on the way, asked him, the boy was giving them [water] and the jug filled with water was finished.

text: Nesä reƛ’a regirƛ’oräy, aɣi boxin.
translation: When he opened his hand, the bird flew away.

text: Eɣenigon esiya eƛin:
translation: The youngest brother said:

text: Łˤonon žedu rukaynosi, yeɣeni kid yezun ƛexun, yizigon nazay t’or keze roqäƛin.
translation: When the young girl saw the three of them, it remained looking [surprised] and asked, where they had come from.

text: Xizyo yizä ža iduɣorno own esirno nesiq, didur šebi roqno mi t’or keze oqäƛin.
translation: Then they let him into the house and asked him what had happened and how he came here.

text: Eƛa šebinon koƛ’inč’ey haqa ogun heresi mec esixosini ƛexasi yałzay, buyxor mec esixosini ħalarno oƛixno eƛin:
translation: While the liar stood there with an open mouth not knowing what to say, and the one who always said the truth came to help and said:

text: Šet’urno bezu-bˤoƛ’un, neła nasin č’urambi rukayxosi močibi äsiruxorgon, nesä ok’bi paqˤƛirxo ik’ix zown.
translation: He [Tsighruk] struck in nails to where he [the dragon], looking around and seeing all the light, told.

text: Hič’č’a adäzaytow soder eni- babiwde idu zawru yisä yisis ʕamal boda oqno, dicegon bara eƛiłin ya qaca ret’urxo zownanu, ya łi rowa ik’ix zownanu, ya q’ˤuya iduzo ħalt’oqor utin ezux zownanu.
translation: Right from the beginning he started to behave like in the house of his parents; how much his wife would say, he neither hacked firewood, nor did he go and bring water, nor did he do look for or did any other housework.

text: Xanä žedu t’umbi łˤora ɣudi quqan xecin rišerƛ’oräy, aqugani bišno łiyace gulučini t’umi šet’u butix bičin.
translation: The king left those pigeons hungry for three days, and when he fed them, the male one turned around until the female one had finished eating.

text: Hudayƛ’aygon maduhalłiqozoni xanä begirno q’ˤano gulu bayrno nasi yizałäy ʕoloqanaw, nasi ečru yałru bat’a boda koƛ’inč’inäy, di dowde dandir ƛˤori boda yołƛin.
translation: On the next day, the neighboring king had two horses be brought to him, [saying:] "If you don't know how to find out which of them is young and which is old, I will make war on you!"

text: Xizyo žoyä eƛin, eni, eli beynodäsi boqzaƛ’, elur boƛik’ut’a ħak’o keze boqxo yaqˤuł, yił kidber siskin šebin keze roqxanu.
translation: Afterwards the boy said: "Mother, we will get half of the khinkali each today, that girl will get nothing, because she hasn't done a thing."

text: Walah, eli paydax boqsi.
translation: "By God, we became...(???)

text: Elo bertenyoł žedur imadu esa koƛ’ixosi žek’utow iqinanu.
translation: They couldn't get a man there on the wedding, who could tell tales [to them?].

text: Elay yik’in kidbä, där hemedur moƛu yukaysƛin, babiwqor nesi užä äsiru łinałäyn eƛin.
translation: The girl went away and [saying] "I had a dream like this!", told to her father about what that boy had told.

text: Sida quł, kidno howlotow xecin, eniwn, užin ʕaƛa bik’ixozo sida bertenyołxor bik’in.
translation: One day, they left the girl there like this again and mother and son went to a marriage in the village.

text: Šebitow di žigon rodanƛin, uži wˤałer q’ˤimno rˤoƛ’urno oqno iduɣor ik’in.
translation: "And what shall I do now?", the boy hang his head and went home.

text: Užä žawab teƛno, šida mi di begirno xecixanu, dowƛ’aygon q’ʷariłiƛ’ din oqzaƛ’ƛin.
translation: The boy answered, "Why would I not leave you, when I'm much more sad than you?"

text: Beɣet’a yizi yałzaytow yizis eniwn yexun, babiya baru yownƛax.
translation: It is said that when they both were young, their mother died and their father married a[nother] wife.

text: Žeduƛ’ ci sadaq’a yołƛin.
translation: Their name is Alms."

text: Sida quł sasaqaza Kusa istolowäy išxo zown.
translation: One day in the morning, Kusa was eating in the dining room.

text: Neła t’umiqay bˤaƛ’uru ħuli babiwz k’ukyoł boqun, miƛ’iłäy räšruni mesedyosnigon his ħamarno čˤaƛin esirno, baba, req’ezi roqxaƛin.
translation: She put the feather that fell from that pigeon onto her father's hat, and threw the golden whool that fell from the ram onto the chest and asked: "Daddy, does it fit?"

text: Ozzaq reƛ’ibin riħin, č’uran rukarerno, Bilq’isdä žedu xizor begirno.
translation: Bilqisdi put her hands over their eyes and caused them to see the light, then she sent them back.

text: Yoɣno kurno yičäruni aɣˤux elus xabaryabin teqerno siskin buyn bäčinč’i, hudu t’o ela sidä sidqo kesubi esix sasaqor bičan.
translation: In this abandoned mill no one will hear what we say, so we will wait here until the morning telling each other secrets.

text: Elo yisä godek’anya q’ˤida bäčiru ʕoloqzaq łi esirnosi, sidä izin zok’är aqˤun kučin teƛƛ’oräy, nesä eƛin, eleley, yedu šebi šebin, insanä aqˤuyä łäz meča ħaƛuƛin, nediw šebin behizi raqinč’iƛin.
translation: There he asked a young person who sat at the village square for water, and one got up, pissed into a mug and gave it to him and he said: "Behold, what is this? A human is not allowed such a thing as to drink urine instead of water!"

text: ʕAƛox bitaz yałruni gogaƛ’arno yižin, ʕoloqza eč’ibi caƛix Bilq’isdi tełxor cucik’no.
translation: The young people led her onto a meadow that was distant from the village, threw pebbles onto her and buried her under them.

text: Režet’ani ƛoħazaɣor reƛno riħurno, waħaza c’i erno.
translation: They poured the meat into big pans and set up a fire below.

text: Ħumukuliƛa bukaynč’u elur, amma nełas sipat debeq esan elaƛin”.
translation: "We didn't see any camel, but we will tell you how it looks."

text: Žedu q’ˤunesno q’ut’i boqno šebi rodinon ža nesi xedyoxay yok’ek’anix.
translation: The two of them agreed to remove her from that husband by all means.

text: Q’ˤano äƛiru ɣudƛ’ogon yeda ayzaƛ’or resun istowliƛ’ riguƛ’ay rigu biša eqräsi.
translation: On the second day then, when he came, he found the richest food put on the table.

text: Howži yedu yexusƛin, yisä rečuƛerno yižin, rekuł šušin.
translation: "Now she died.", he (tought and) carried her into the cattleshed and buried her in manure.

text: Hudayzikotow beže bertenno bodin, xanä nesix, ziruz kumakyoƛ’aza, kidno yegirno.
translation: The next day they had a big wedding; due to the help of the fox, the king gave him his daughter.

text: Horo dädäɣor.
translation: "Come to me!

text: Guluza di akik’xanuƛin, žawab teƛno guluzaxozo aħa.
translation: "Horses don't make me tired," answered the horse shepherd.

text: Howlotow nełar riyn ža reƛ’a łas yałrułi, neła q’orolays reƛ’a yoł žaƛin esin neła xanqo.
translation: And there she knew whose hand it was: "It's this widow's hand.", she told the king.

text: Yeda ukayruntow, neł kidbes yisiqor roƛ’i boqno.
translation: Immediately when she saw him, she fell in love with him.

text: Hudun žedu nełaƛ’ ƛ’iri butin ɣudi rayrno, elułäy sidxokin mi nexinč’inäy, bazaryarno yižin teƛa yołƛin.
translation: Nevertheless, they tortured her again: "If you don't marry any of us, we will take you to the market and sell you!"

text: Yedu kidno t’otow xecixƛin.
translation: I am also leaving that girl here.

text: Badiru daru baqinč’iƛin Bilq’isdä nesi eženi žek’us łˤonon užibi muk’urno boyrerno, buyxor xabarno esirerno žeduq.
translation: "(Otherwise) the medication won't work.", Bilqisdi made the old man's three sons confess and made them tell the true story.

text: Užä eƛin, šida mi di xʷasar boda bayxanu, dowƛ’äygon q’ʷariłiƛ’ oqzaƛ’ diƛin.
translation: The boy said, "Why would I not save you, when I'm much more sad than you?"

text: K’ox äƛirugon Ħon-Bahadurä kurno c’eruc’a - aždaħez rok’eł č’aq’ˤin.
translation: Mountain~Hero shot a second time with his bow and hit the dragons heart.

text: Xizyo ɣamasya tełäzay xanez kidber riguƛ’ay rigut’a maq’anyabi teqnosi, neła ža micxiryoƛ yisinč’ey yäčinč’i.
translation: Then, after the king's daughter heard the prettiest melodies coming out of the box, she just had to buy it.

text: Di mi ɣurox egiranƛin odäsi zownč’u.
translation: "I didn't give birth to you to have you pasture the cows.

text: Yisiƛ’ bužzin yoqinč’ey nełas eniw yäk’iru, žan hemedurtow aždaħä yexurno.
translation: That dragon likewise killed her mother, who didn't believe this, and went there.

text: Žedu c’aq’ miskinłiƛ’ zown.
translation: They lived in great poverty.

text: ʕAƛoxor aynosigon dibirno goƛ’in, maharno erno, ixiw bertenno bodin.
translation: When he came into he village, he called the imam, organized a wedding and threw a big marriage.

text: Elor baynosi, yizä žas ɣˤutkun resurno, idurno, bik’in eƛin, mi eli baƛ’irno, elus ħumukulibin, žedaƛ’ ƛ’iri zawru q’ayn elus eluqor xizor butirno neƛƛin.
translation: When they arrived there, they found the boy's house, went inside and said: "You tricked us, give us back our camels and the goods that were on them!"

text: Adäztown rič’in zawru šuda teł žedun šušin.
translation: And they were buried in the grave they had digged before.

text: Bisno q’ˤano besuron bac’ad boyn, łˤono äƛiru boyƛ’oräy, nełaz k’esma aɣiw qˤod yesun.
translation: He took two fish and cleaned them, and when he took the third one, he found a hole in its gullet.

text: Xizyogon xabar esanix boc’i boqno, eluz maduhalłiq ičixozo xanez kidbes sis ozuri rexasi yoł.
translation: Then the wolf began to tell a story: "The daughter of the king who lives in our neighbourhood is blind on one eye.

text: Iqiran iqirno yizä ža beže iħuz lilyoxor ižin.
translation: They caught him and brought him to the bank of a large river.

text: Q’ʷarʕelyoƛ’ sida ʕaƛaɣorno ik’in, xizor utin nexƛ’oräy, nesił dandir nesistow ʕaƛasi Aħmadƛin sis ʕoloqanaw uži keze oqno.
translation: When he went to a village out of need and came back, he met a young boy named Akhmed from his village.

text: Mi dey uži yołƛin nesä eƛizaƛ’or, łox kikibi ƛisin užä nesis.
translation: Until he said "You are my son.", the boy pulled his breasts three times.

text: Xanä urɣizikin oqxo ičinč’ey nesir žawab teƛno, boƛƛik’u dey ric’q’i neƛa yoł deberƛin.
translation: The king wasn't thinking and answered him: "I will give you half of my wealth!"

text: Hoboygon šebi roqno, ziruƛin?” - esirno zeyä.
translation: "And what happened now, fox?", asked the bear.

text: Łox äƛiru rayäru bišadyoł zahru anuru biyn yizir, elay bet’urno magalu kurnosi k’et’u bexunč’izaƛ’.
translation: The third time they knew that there was no poison in the food they brought, because the cat for which a [piece of] bread was pulled out and thrown to, wouldn't die.

text: Ži roda šebin anuƛin”.
translation: But now there is nothing to do.

text: Raɣˤin tełer ezunosi, elo teł rigu biša resun.
translation: After he opened it and looked inside, he found good food in it.

text: Sasaqosi xanqo esin, sis xaliča dahaw k’ot’ono riłe yołƛin.
translation: In the morning they told the king: "One mat is a little shorter."

text: Onočas pikru boqno, ciqaɣor ža bik’inosi, nełał xizay xal bodin bičanƛin.
translation: The hen had the idea to follow him, when he goes to the forest.

text: Tawadä bigza ħalał xal bodinosi, elo nesir sosi ozuri c’oxno yätiruni kid yukaynanu.
translation: When Tavadi looked intently, he didn't see a girl that he fell in love with at first sight.

text: Hudun ža ozzatawni žek’u harihun ƛaƛaƛix ik’in, sidaɣ aɣˤuxor keze oqno.
translation: Nevertheless, this eyeless man went slowly feeling up and got to a mill someplace.

text: Elo yezun-yoƛ’ˤun yaynosi kidbä eƛin:
translation: After coming back from looking up there, the girl said:

text: T’ok’ow roda šebin koƛ’inč’ey, eniya sossi eženi kidbeq bikorxo yik’ixaƛin esirno, neła yik’ixanuƛin inkar boynosi yoƛƛozoqor eƛin, žan razi yoqno anu.
translation: Not knowing what to do anymore, the mother asked her eldest daughter first, if she would go to the snake; she rejected: "I won't go."; then she talked to the middle daughter, who also disagreed.

text: Di saɣ oynäyƛin, eƛin nesi iguni žek’a, šebi baha mi där boyxƛin.
translation: "When I cure you," said the good man, "what price do you give me?"

text: Hudu šebigo roqłin mi esirnosi, riyä nesir debez rok’ƛ’osiƛin, c’aq’ ʕašiq’no yoqno esirno bara.
translation: "So when you asked [???], he knows what's on your heart?", the wife asked very passionately.

text: Šebey roqsiƛin, idur bik’inosi, ɣˤutkos ac tełäz ħišäsi yesun.
translation: "What happened?", they asked and when they went home, they found the house door locked from inside.

text: Bazarganä roƛik’no eƛuk’yazay bišan, axbin rodin, kʷaxatow hunix boqno.
translation: The merchant pulled out food from a leather sack, they stuffed their tummies and soon went on their way.

text: Kʷaxatowngon nesä nesiz mečar xanłungon egirno.
translation: And very soon then, he even let him be the king.

text: Di mežur t’it’u neƛač’in, q’ulaʕitow neƛanƛin, užä žedu razi bodin.
translation: "I won't give you a piece, I will give you a whole carcass.", the boy agreed to them.

text: Hudayziko yoƛozoni esiwqor gegmus ric’äsi saħ teƛno, däz ozyor rac’anix ʕeziyaw magalabi rodoƛin.
translation: The next day, he gave a sah filled with flour to the middle sister, [saying]: "Make many breads for my army to eat!"

text: Neła quł Ħusen zown ciqaɣor ik’äsi.
translation: On that day, Hussein had gone into the forest.

text: Xizaɣorsini xabar neła šak yoqno biłe esin.
translation: The final story she told suspiciously.

text: Hudayziko č’urqˤotow izin yeda zam-zames łäł xizay oqno.
translation: The next day he got up early and went out to get the holy water.

text: Xizay bayru žedu ʕoloqzaqor yeženä eƛin, nesis hunar mežizoƛ’ay bežis, meža royxosi šebin šebi, dey xexbiƛin.
translation: The old woman said to those young peple who came behind them: "He is more talented than you all, what is what you do, my children?

text: Way, mi exurno äsuƛin ʕiyayx zowsƛin”, - esin yizä.
translation: "O dear, we were crying because we thought you had been killed!", they said.

text: Ħusenä elo q’ˤiryoł c’eruc’an riƛ’irno, nełqorno eƛin, di guluzał xizay ʕaƛaɣor ik’ix.
translation: Hussein plunged the bow on the floor there and said to her: "I am going to the village for horses.

text: Neła äƛiruxor, nełas č’aɣˤirno, žubin boɣno, iyon zok’är biqirno.
translation: As the dragon said, he took out his bowel and liver, and put the blood into the mug.

text: Kusa eƛin, baq’ˤu micxir boqnäyƛa, qalamäɣorno izin, ik’an di nełdäɣorƛin.
translation: Kusa said: "If I had a lot of money, I would go up into the fortress to her."

text: Sadaq bik’iƛ’orey, sida sidqo xabaryabi esix Ražbadinno halmaɣbin cezyazno qazaqzazno ʕurqixor bayx.
translation: While they were walking together, Razhbadin and his friends were talking with each other and came to the Tsez and Georgian border.

text: Xedya esin, sosi di bełiqur tupiƛ ziya ħažis, xizyo ža tupin teƛno bazarganzaqay k’uk bissi, ža k’ukgon iħay łi ħaƛaƛin wˤałer užinosi, q’ˤimƛ’ay bˤoƛ’un bik’in łiya boɣno bižisƛin.
translation: The husband told: "At first I exchanged the cow for a rifle with a hunter, then I sold the rifle and boughta cap from some merchants, this cap fell down from my head while I was about to drink from the river and the water carried it away."

text: Zamanałäy, yeda uži, ža yižinč’ey di ik’ixanu meži bik’iƛin, xizorno utin, kidno yižin ʕaƛaɣor ik’in.
translation: At one point in time, the boy couldn't bear it any longer and, [saying] "I won't go, you go [alone]!", he returned, took the girl with him and went towards the village.

text: Aɣin bisno, adäzgon boc’no t’ekan kid xandäɣor yik’in.
translation: Having taken a bird and chased a goat ahead, the girl went to the king.

text: Däz č’ikayägon mi yukarinč’us yołi, łarnokin debi roƛxosi yałrułin riya zownč’u, min yexa zowsƛin”, - eƛin eɣeni esiya.
translation: "If my mirror hadn't shown you, nobody would have known that you were sick, and you would have died", said the younger brother.

text: Hemediw žedaƛ’or bayru balahno bukayn, babiw best’al kidbedäɣor oxin.
translation: The father saw what misfortune had come upon tem and ran away to his stepdaughter.

text: Qema regirnosi, Bac’alägon harizi rodin, Allah, kuro däz ħirozaqor butniƛin.
translation: When it had started to rain, Batsali requested: "God, throw a burka over my shoulders!"

text: Zira eƛin, nedur resunäy, hawaƛ’osi mi rutir, q’ˤidasi di rutiran.
translation: The fox said: "If that's the case, collect what's in the air and I will collect what's on the ground.

text: Žek’us roži rayxosi šebin radin t’ok’owƛin”, - eƛin ičin babiw.
translation: "Don't do that again, so that other people don't scold you!", the father remained saying.

text: Yisä pikru bodin, didiwgon boqłin sida maxšelyoƛ’or ečula ʕUmar ruhun oqanix t’et’ra ižanƛin.
translation: He thought: "I'll lead my grandson Umar to learn so he can learn at least one skill."

text: Nedur resunäy di raziyaw yołƛin, nesä esace teqerno ičin.
translation: "If that's the case, I am ready.", and he listened until he told him.

text: Ziragon k’oƛin bik’in riqirno žan rac’no, ɣʷˤader barkalan boyn, neła nełaz hunix bik’in.
translation: The fox then jumped and took it away and ate it, thanked the crow and went on his way.

text: Hudayzikotow Tawadä Ražbadin Telawi šaharyarno ižin riguƛ’ay rigut’a daruyabin risno saɣ odix.
translation: The next day Tavadi lead Razhbadin to the city of Telavi, bought him the best medicine and cured him.

text: Sidas zown sis uži, sidenis ʕoƛno uži zown.
translation: One had one son, the other one had seven sons.

text: Ele hemedur eniwz bašiqˤoyz kumakyoƛ’aza taliħ besun best’al kidber, žukani best’al eniwqayn yok’ełno.
translation: Thus, the step daughter has found happiness with the help of her mother's ring and could escape from her evil step mother.

text: Eniya eƛin, nagon ža äči, eloš hisołäy tuturłi hok’xo yołƛin.
translation: The mother said: "Where could he be; he is pulling out the dirt from the whool over there."

text: Babiya uži eynodanixno egirno, nesä badiru micxir bukarerno.
translation: The father sent his son to work and made him show the money he earned.

text: Aluk’ani ɣˤulader aynosi, žan sidaɣar yiq’ˤirno imxaɣor oqno ik’inosi, žar eniw yesun.
translation: After he arrived at the white stone, he moved it aside and got into the cave and then he found his mother.

text: Nazay deber teqäsi ciqqo riynoxu ičixosiƛin.
translation: "From where did you hear that there's a sheikh living in the forest?"

text: Sida quł yedu dibirder yik’in, där kid yodixosi sabaw caxoƛin.
translation: One day she went to the imam [and said]: "Write me an amulet, which lets me have a daughter!"

text: Žedur žedu esnabi yałru biynosi, Umukusä nesiq kinaw raqru łinałäy esin.
translation: When they knew they were brother and sister, Umukus told him about everything that happened.

text: Neła äƛiruxor, xanez užä ač’urqor gulun bicin, k’ulä bäsuru ahlimoqor salamno teƛno eƛin, däqor meža maxsar boyłin, yiła guluqor badinoƛin.
translation: According to what she said, the prince tied the horse to the handle gave a greeting to the people he found in the side street and said: "Although you can make jokes about me, do not make jokes about this horse!"

text: Xedya žawab teƛno, neła xexbi rodixƛin.
translation: The husband answered: "She is giving birth to a child."

text: Łox äƛiru žoyä karuni tupis hil ceyde pureł č’aq’ˤin.
translation: The third time the rifle's bullet shot from the boy hit next to the eagle.

text: Hemedur iħuz raʕalłix yeda yałzay, guluƛ’no zown žek’u oƛixno.
translation: So while he was at the edge of the river, a man riding on a horse appeared.

text: Neširuxay maħor ža yaqrun, sadaq šet’urtow gʷanɣizi roqxo zown.
translation: Right after she had gone outside in the evening, everything around was getting bright.

text: Xizyo nesä tukamƛ’ozoni žek’uq esirno, mi ozurabin roɣno ƛexun zowali žek’u anaƛin.
translation: Afterwards, he asked the man in the shop: "Weren't you the man whom I took the eyes away from?"

text: Bik’ixanu - däz guluƛ’ bik’anƛin yedan ičin.
translation: "We're not going - we will ride on my horse," he insisted.

text: Hemedur roƛon risno, Alimaħama bˤeƛ’no rižin ɣutanyołxor ik’in.
translation: So they agreed and Alimagomed lead the sheep onto the winter pasture.

text: Yiła užiqor eƛin, kakno royn mi däƛ’ ƛ’iri zow, nełaz akixor bik’anix.
translation: It said to the boy: "Do a prayer with me and sit on me, so we can get in through her window."

text: Ža ħos ɣoɣoriłnosi, yiła žeduq esirno, šebi izäsi, šebi kecäsi yołƛin.
translation: When the stick has become red-hot, she asked them: "Who is awake and who is sleeping?"

text: Biči, hiboƛin, eƛizaƛ’or deber aciru žek’uz gugyoƛ’ yedu biħnayxosiƛin.
translation: "As long as you do not say 'Stop, stick', it hits on the back of that man, which bothers you."

text: ƛ’irir baynosi, bazarganza esirno nesiq, łeno ħumukulin boc’no howt’aza ik’ix deber uži ukaynč’aƛin.
translation: When they had come closer, the merchants asked him: "Have you seen a boy going in this direction driving five camels?"

text: Elo tełxor oqƛ’orey, yisir č’ikays rodäsi ɣˤutku rukayn.
translation: When he went inside there, he saw a house made of glass.

text: Elozo xanesgon iłno kid zown.
translation: The king there had six daughters.

text: Ražbadinan, baran Tawadir beže barkalan teƛno, bežet’asini q’ˤanon užibin bown ʕaƛoxaɣor nexno.
translation: Razhbadin and his wife thanked Tavadi very much and went to their village together with their two oldest boys.

text: Ža kid yowanƛin esiranix eženi uži ik’inosi, neła nesiq roži neƛaniƛ mesedyos saħ esin.
translation: When the older son went to ask if he could marry that girl, she gave him her word for the Sah of gold.

text: Sasaqosigon yizi ʕoƛnon esiw yałruni kidbedäɣor bizin bik’in, sadaq žan yižanix.
translation: The next morning, they set out and went to the girl who had the seven brothers to take her with them.

text: Bełiƛ’ bäk’izay esnabi baynosi, idu esiy Umukus yesunč’izaƛ’, baruzaq, nar ža yik’äƛin, esirno.
translation: When the brothers came back from hunting, their sister Umukus wasn't at home, so they asked their wives where she went.

text: Deber di yetixa, mi di yowa yołäƛin?”
translation: "Do you love me, will you marry me?"

text: Žedu bik’in xomi zaman bik’izaƛ’ortow, kidbä Ražbadinqor Tawadä caxruni kaɣat teƛno.
translation: Not too much time has passed since they had left, and the girl gave Razhbadin the letter that Tavadi had written.

text: Xanez kidbä mesed biqinosi, oxirno eɣenin esiw.
translation: When the princess got the gold, she chased also the youngest brother away.

text: Bäxuruni tungił xizay yedu ʕiyayxƛin xalq’imozgon rok’ƛ’orno rayƛin.
translation: And the people will think that you're crying for the broken jug."

text: Xizor utin yeda uži raładyoz raʕalłixor aynosi, yoƛixno łäłäzay neła ɣˤanaza esirno, šebi roqä däz esiws užiƛin.
translation: When the boy went back and came to the edge of the sea, that woman appeared out of the water again and asked: "What happened, my nephew?"

text: Sida zamanałäy akin raɣˤin ɣutkazay yoƛixno yeže ɣˤanaza yisiqor qˤaƛin:
translation: Suddenly an old woman opened a window, looked out of her house and shouted at him:

text: Maħaza bełiqun xecin, ža cey tełxor bik’in.
translation: The eagle left the hunter outside and went inside.

text: Nełƛ’ay soder yizä łinasno q’ʷariłi anusi bercinłiƛ’ ʕumru bižin.
translation: After that they lead a beautiful life without any sadness whatsoever.

text: Baydanyay dahaw bitay riłe nesir henešyos ɣun rukayn.
translation: A little further away in the field, he saw and apple tree.

text: Neła quł sasaqtow, qaħłi begiracekin bičinč’ey ixinabin rižin q’ˤanon esnabi č’ʷadmay micxir bowa bik’in.
translation: The next morning even before dawn, carrying a bag, the two brothers went to get the money out of the ruin.

text: Baħarayä eƛin, di debeƛ’ qoqoƛixanu.
translation: The bride said: "I am not laughing about you."

text: Neƛxanuƛin, - eƛin žoyä nełqor, - adäyt’an aƛ’irsi mi diƛin”.
translation: "I don't give it to you," said the boy, "you have cheated me before several times."

text: Eniwqorno hemedurtow, esa k’ʷeze yaqinč’i däqƛin ičin.
translation: To his mother he was the same: "I cannot tell you."

text: Bahadur igtow razi oqxo sadaqor halmaɣłi roqxoƛin.
translation: The hero accepted well that there were friends with him.

text: Suƛitow nesä iħä łäłxor ɣamasno kurno.
translation: And suddenly he threw the box into the water in the river.

text: Žek’uz raƛ’ma xanłun ičizaƛ’or, däztow raƛ’ma laɣłun rigu därƛin.
translation: "Before I become king over a someone's land, I would rather be a slave in my land.

text: Howlaygon yiła yeda izirno ok’no ʕAliqilič bečnazaxar raƛ’moƛxor cucik’no.
translation: And from there the dragon lifted and beat Aliqilich into the earth up to his knees.

text: Ža q’ˤimno ruqˤno yeda esiwde igor ayn.
translation: That head he hid and went toward his sister.

text: Xexłiƛ’ yeda maħor t’ay oxirƛin, sis xalq’i qˤaƛanayn.
translation: "Quickly chase him out of here!" the people yelled.

text: Hemedur nociƛ’orno utin, neła kidbez kočorał uqˤełno yeda.
translation: So he turned into the louse and hid himself in that girl's forelock.

text: Howži ʕoƛnon esnabi yisiq hardizi boqsiƛax, aħinyoqorno bicin eli iħułxor kuroƛin.
translation: Now it's said that the seven brothers asked him: "Tie us to a rock and throw us into the river!"

text: Elaygon bitor ža boc’ä äƛiruni untarawni xander ik’in.
translation: From there, he went away to the sick king that the wolf had told about.

text: Esinč’inäy bazarganzar teƛa yołƛin ʕuƛ’erxo zown.
translation: "If you don't tell, I will sell you to the merchants!", he scared him.

text: Yiłaƛ’no zown ik’inäy, beciz bolžalyos huni mi sida minutałer xäciƛin”.
translation: "When you're riding on it, you will need one minute for a month's journey."

text: Nedur resunäyƛin, eƛin dibirez barä, din yik’an dowde sadaq, därno ence betäsi ukrun, micxirnoƛin.
translation: "If that is the case," said the mullah's wife, "I will also go with you, because I also want silver and money."

text: Q’ˤidayn yisno č’ikayä bezunosi, žeda esirxosi kid yexux yukarno.
translation: And when they looked at the mirror, after taking it up from below, it showed the girl that they had asked out dying.

text: Babiwn, best’al eniwn, esiwn goƛ’in bertenyołxorno bik’in, best’al kid sisxoli idu ƛexun.
translation: The father, the stepmother and her sister were invited and went to the marriage, the stepdaughter remained home alone.

text: Yeda zek’no egin č’ariłno.
translation: He shuddered and woke up.

text: Hemełƛ’o elo ƛ’irir sis eže žek’u ayn.
translation: Then there came an old man.

text: Neł iħux sis ʕaƛ zown.
translation: Along the river was a village.

text: Bet’un k’oƛin łˤonon esnabi butnoƛ’no zown nełqor eƛin:
translation: The three brothers jumped onto the burka and said to it:

text: Ža šinaw šebin teqno acmox aħyan kurno bäčiruni bazarganzar.
translation: All these things heard the merchants who were waiting at the door spreading out their ears.

text: Eženi žek’u sadaq šebin riyxosi šayx zown.
translation: The old man was a sheikh who knew everything.

text: Imxuxgon maħor ža boƛixaceƛin Bac’alin ičin.
translation: And Batsali waited outside the cave until he (the wolf) would appear.

text: Ik’in däz guluzaƛ’no ezu, deber bätiru howlay łˤono gulu łet’oƛin.
translation: Go and see my horses, then pick the three horses that you want out of them.

text: Elor yiłar ɣudes bełiz čanyaza ɣˤay yayrxo rayx zown.
translation: The wild goats were daily coming to bring her milk.

text: Yedu kaɣat yaynosi, Ražbadinä łˤonon užin goƛ’in žeduł adäz t’et’erno.
translation: When the letter arrived, Razhbadin called his three sons and read it out to them.

text: Xizyo sasaqosi babiwderno ayn eƛin, där siskin šebin rukaynč’uƛin.
translation: Then in the morning he came back to his father and said that he did not see anything.

text: Elorno ik’in, teł c’in erno, c’idox tataniłxo ičäsi zown.
translation: He went there, set up a fire inside and waited warmthening himself on the fire.

text: Yedu q’ut’u łäs bic’no sasaqorno xecin, elosi łi ħaƛurnäy, exuƛ’osi žek’u č’agu oqxosiƛin”.
translation: "If you fill this jug with water and leave it until the morning and then give it someone to drink, a dying man will become alive again."

text: Xizyogon hemediwtow roži yołäsi qˤaƛu teqno yisir, eženiz mada reže ɣun yoł, nesä mi ža ɣun reč’anix egär.
translation: Then he heard a shout from the same voice: "In the old man's yard is a big tree, he will send you to chop that tree down.

text: Bełiqu eže izinosi, ozzas č’uran łiyn, koyär tupi risa ħalno zown anu.
translation: When the hunter got old, he lost his eyesight and he didn't have the strength to take up the rifle into his hand anymore.

text: Nełƛ’osi kʷaxatow nełas eniw yexun.
translation: Short time later, her mother died.

text: Xizyogon nesił boc’i c’oxno.
translation: Then he met a wolf.

text: Hudayzikon uži besurozaƛ’or ik’in, hemedurtow biqinč’ey aynosi, babiw igza ħalał aƛ’in.
translation: The next day when the boy went after fish again, didn't catch any either and came back, the father scolded him pretty much.

text: Lagiqay bat’an roqno bikores q’ˤim geler rik’in.
translation: The snake's head got seperated from the body and tumbled away.

text: Sabru bodin iči, zamanałäy debertow räyƛin”, - eniya eƛin.
translation: "Be patient, eventually you will know.", said the mother.

text: Ele enła onočas k’eneč’yabin rižin iduɣorno ik’in, sasaqor k’eneč’yołäy xexoybin roɣno däqor rown horoƛin.
translation: "Here, carry these chicken eggs to your home; tomorrow bring me the chicks that have hatched from the egg."

text: Mi nełas pikru bäsno, sasaq di ganƛ’orno yutin, xanez ganzo axarno k’oƛin, nełas mo yac’anƛin, eƛin kidbä uži ɣˤaɣˤu izirno.
translation: "Don't think about it; tomorrow I will turn into a young bull, run into the king's bull's belly and eat its fat.", said the girl and made the boy happy.

text: Neła elur łi neƛxosi, ƛˤebaxay sosit’a ela nełar yac’anix kid teƛnäy.
translation: "He gives us water, if once a year we give him a girl to eat.

text: Howžin hemedurtow bišadyos niʕmatyabi ašiłno nełaƛ’ ƛ’iri.
translation: And again now delicacies grew on it.

text: Nełƛ’ay soder Alimaħama sasiłnosi xizyo gurow bˤeƛ’ iduɣor rowx zown anu.
translation: After that Alimagomed only brought the sheep home after it got dark.

text: Esnabi ʕuraw ɣˤaɣˤun bizin, Ħon-Bahadurer saɣyatyabin rodin, riguƛ’ay rigu bišadyos ħurženyabin ric’no, yedan hunix egirno.
translation: The brothers got very happy, gave Mountain~Hero presents and filled a bag with the best food and sent him on his way.

text: Ziru k’oƛin bik’in xanqo ukaranix Goqi owa.
translation: The fox ran away to bring Goqi in order to show him to the king.

text: Yaɣˤin tełer yezunosi, elo neła best’alawni kidbes eniw yołƛax.
translation: She opened it and when she looked inside, there was the stepdaughter's mother inside.

text: ʕUraw zaman bik’inosi yizi sidaɣ šaharyar bayn.
translation: Much time passed by and they came to a city somewhere.

text: Bero beca baqruntow t’ay eli boxin bok’ła bayƛin yiłä äƛirun, bet’un guluƛ’orno k’oƛin, čuret’ bak’runtow, ʕoƛno ħonno xecin bitaɣor bok’ełno.
translation: "Immediately after the ice has begun to thaw, we must run away and escape from here!", she said and right away they jumped onto the horse, cracked the whip and immediately crossed the seven mountains and escaped into the distance.

text: K’uruč’ k’eč’ ɣudi rocu, buq bay.
translation: All's well that ends well.

text: Di insan yołƛinno, eƛin, kid ažoqay q’ˤidar yoqno.
translation: Saying "I am a human!", the girl got down from the tree.

text: Šebiƛa žek’u t’o zown äsuƛin, žedus xabar boqnosi, zeyä eloza naysinan bezu-bˤoƛ’a ziru begirno.
translation: After saying "Someone has to find the man here!", the bear sent the fox to look everywhere there.

text: Neširur ħalt’in łiyrno, ečruni žek’a näƛruni łˤono ɣurušno eqerno, uži eniwdäɣor iduɣor ik’ix zown.
translation: The boy finished his work late at night, took the three rubles that the old man gave him, and went home to his mother.

text: Razi boqno žedu nełaƛ’.
translation: They agreed on that.

text: Yedu gugirzaƛ’or diƛin, eƛin eniya cimyarno yizin, ʕoƛnon meži gugiranƛin.
translation: "Before I chase her away," said the mother in anger, "I will chase you seven away as well!"

text: Neła sida yečiw ačit’yoq muži bodäsi zown.
translation: It was building a nest on a thick pine tree.

text: Nełagon eƛis, ukayruntow etis mi därƛin.
translation: And she said: "Right when I saw you, I fell in love with you."

text: Soder iziłinč’ey hemedur yeda yałzay, aki riłe moči nesir elo besun.
translation: While he wasn't able to get upwards like this, he found a place there that looked like a window.

text: K’oƛin yik’in žader kid ixiwt’a muši kurxo yičin.
translation: His daughter came running and moaned heavily at the boy.

text: Teł iduɣor yoqƛ’oräy, yiłas qoqoƛu regix.
translation: Inside, while she came into the house, she bursted into laughter.

text: Bercinawni ɣˤanaza t’ok’ow ƛ’iri xabarkin boynč’ey, mi xiyantaw žek’u zownƛin däqorno eƛin, ceyqor amru boys, yeda žek’u yałzar ižin kuroƛin.
translation: The beautful woman, not having said anything; 'You were a traitor', she said to me, and ordered the eagle: 'Take this man to where he came from and throw him out!'

text: Šebigon nesä royłin, nełade igor qalamär yeda didurnokin egin anu.
translation: But no matter what he did, he didn't get into fortress near her.

text: Madaƛäz oƛäxrun, bełiqanä tupin caƛin žan exurno neła kidbeqor eƛin, t’ok’ow dunyalƛ’o deber ħinq’i yołäsi moči yołäƛin.
translation: Just when he appeared under the gate, the hunter shot from his rifle killed him and said to the girl: "Is there any dangerous place for you in the world anymore?"

text: Babiwn, kidno uži uqˤäłru moči k’edanix maħor boqno.
translation: Father and daughter went outside to search for the place where the boy was hiding.

text: Sasaqosi moƛaxayn bizin, aždaħä yeɣeni kidbeqorƛin eƛin, Maryam där bišan riso, ič’an eɣeni užiz iyos q’ut’un bic’o mečołxor raƛin.
translation: In the morning, after getting up from his sleep, the dragon said to - what he supposed was - his youngest daughter: "Maryam, take the food to me and fill the earthen jug with the youngest boy's blood and bring them to the field!"

text: Sidƛ’o ʕAt’idƛin, sideniƛ’gon Q’arumƛin cirabi yołäsi.
translation: One was named Atid, the other one Qarum.

text: Mesed biqinosi, xanez kidbä nesix yik’anix razi yoqinč’ey žan oxirno.
translation: After getting the gold, the princess didn't agree and chased him away.

text: Yisi užä yedu kid idurno yižin, sasaqozox nasin moƛaxay bizinosi, eƛin, eni, mi hek’uyax eč’uri roda yik’inäy, yiła iduzasi radi, di ciqaɣor ik’anƛin.
translation: The boy took the girl home and in the early morning, when everyone got up from their sleeps, he said: "Mother, if you go to weed the potatoes, she will do the housework and I will go into the forest."

text: Howła däz xexzas tatuwa quqix, behizi roqnäy, hut’bi rešak’ace łi neƛ, debertow ažru bäqi”.
translation: My child is getting weak; if it is possible, give her enough water to moisten her lips, she will recompensate you."

text: Kusa nesir žawab teƛno, mesedyoz ʕabasä royxanusi šebintow roqinč’izaƛ’ƛin.
translation: Kusa answered him: "There is nothing that a gold coin couldn't do."

text: Siħirno yoqno neła ɣˤanaza harizi rodin, gama tełäy maħor yoqa yegir diƛin.
translation: The woman was a sly person and pleaded: "Let me get out of the inside of the boat!"

text: Hay yezanix neƛ daqorƛin”, - eƛin kidbä.
translation: So then give it to me so I can have a look!", said the girl.

text: Sisgon däqor babiya eƛin zowsƛin, esin žoyä, yexoriłxosin, k’ot’oniłxosin, qˤaba, aluk’a yoqxosin gurow baru yawnoƛin.
translation: "My father said another thing to me:", told the boy, "Don't marry a wife unless she's becoming longer and shorter and is black and white."

text: Ža debeq äsirus haqu azirodoƛƛin, eƛin esiya.
translation: "May the mouth of the one who told you that freeze over!", said the brother.

text: Xexbi roda netiey zaman bayanƛin, xanä ɣudbi t’et’erxo ičäsi zown.
translation: The king waited and counted the days until the time when the children would be born.

text: Zewnoƛax oc’ino halmaɣ, ceyn, ɣʷˤadin, cek’eryon, elbon, onočun, mat’in, ʕomoyn, mešin, cek’in, ƛelin.
translation: Once there were ten friends: An eagle, a crow, a sparrow, a pigeon, a hen, a duck, a donkey, a bull calf, goat kid and a lamb.

text: Däde yoł deber eynodanix moči, bˤeƛ’xo aħołun ik’anix mi razi oqnäyƛin, eƛin Paxrudinä.
translation: "I have a place for you to work, if you agree to become a shepherd.", said Pakhrudin.

text: Elo nesir berten eräsi besun.
translation: There he found a wedding that was organized.

text: Hudun nesis bˤeƛ’ reɣułatow reɣułxo zown anu.
translation: Nevertheless, his flock of sheep never got smaller.

text: Ražbadines xexbi bexuranis mečʷa, Tawadä žedu nesis uži zawza Tiblisäɣor t’et’ranixno begirno, riyrxo žeduq, dey izmu anusi eni- babiwƛ’or kaɣat caxnoƛin.
translation: Instead of killing Razhbadin's children, Tavadi sent them to study in Tbilisi where his son was, and explained to them: "Don't write a letter to your parents without my permission!"

text: Ya yexun yäsu ža, ya č’agu yäsuƛin xanqorno eƛin, yedu režet’ani esnazadäɣor yik’in, xanesno yiłasno baqru xabaryołäy esanix.
translation: Maybe she is dead, or maybe she is alive." she said to the king, went to the older sister and told that story that happened to her and the king.

text: Hoboy kidbazar čuqˤno, žedu roɣno kurno babiw äk’irułi.
translation: Now the girls noticed that their father abandoned them and went away.

text: T’ok’ow babiw sabrun boyn ičin.
translation: The father remained silently.

text: Eženä p’es anusi nesä nesistow ret’urno.
translation: The old man hacked his own [wood] without saying anything.

text: Xanes zown sis bercinaw kid.
translation: The king had one beautiful daughter.

text: Nełaqno izin reƛ’iqor yiqi yäqiru ƛˤeb ozqo yiħixt’a kurxo zown.
translation: He reached up with his hand and smeared a plucked leaf onto an eye, and threw it away.

text: Boržizi boqno bik’a adäza ceyä yisiqor neła nełałäy boɣno sis ħulin teƛno eƛin, dowƛ’or ħal baynosi, yedu mi bik’urnäy, yiła deber kumak badiƛin.
translation: Before the eagle flew away, it plucked a feather from itself, gave it to him and said: "When trouble comes upon you, if you burn it, it will help you."

text: Žan elay yoɣno, sosi łäłxor kuranƛin oqno yeda.
translation: He took it out of there and at first wanted to throw it into the water.

text: Neła guluƛ’no zown, waħazoni dunyalƛ’o idur aynosi, Okä kidbeqor maħlazaytow qˤaƛin, xexłiƛ’ bˤałay maħor roƛik’ gulu bexada retinƛin.
translation: He rode home on that horse and when he came home into the underworld, Oku shouted to his daughter from the court: "Quick, get the dagger outside, I wanted to slaughter a horse!"

text: Žazasƛin elo q’ˤač’azi rodasi šudabi rukarnosi, eniw yigtow q’ʷarid yoqxo, babiya yołi eƛace ħal čuqernoanu.
translation: When they saw the graves that were made up as of their sons, the mother became very sad; the father, however, didn't reveal any condition.

text: Aq’ˤu mixƛ’or ičinč’eytow imxayn oqno šayxno ayn idur.
translation: Not waiting too long, the sheikh got out of the cave and came home.

text: Žäd di baru yowäsi anuƛin, doxturä žawab teƛno.
translation: "I am still unmarried," the doctor gave an answer.

text: Xizyo yiła nesiƛ’or qˤaradamu łäs bic’asi t’asno biħurno, ža mežärsi odin.
translation: Afterwards, she poured a washbowl filled with boiling water over him, and made him bed-ridden.

text: Žan xander yižin C’irduxno ik’in.
translation: Tsirdukh carried her to the king.

text: Yiła yiziqor eƛin, sis guluƛ’ zown, sideni q’ˤidan ik’in, buq betunosi xizyo žek’us ɣedun bown ent’or horo mežiƛin.
translation: She said to them: "One will ride on a horse and the other one will walk below; after the sun has set, get me a man's shadow and come here!"

text: Sosisi yisä esinč’ey iča oqäsi zown, xizyo kidbä xecinč’izaƛ’ esin, hemedur xanä mužmareł sual neƛsi, hič’č’an qˤaban, hič’č’an kokorun, hič’č’an ʕedeʕaraw šebin šebiƛin.
translation: At first he didn't want to tell it, but then, because his daughter wouldn't leave him, he told: "The king asked a question this Friday: 'What is the blackest, softest and quickest thing?'

text: Sida zamanał, maduhalłiqozo ħukmatyoz xanä yisiƛ’or begirno geres hibo bayrno naza boq’ˤiw, naza kikiw yałru biyrƛin.
translation: At one point in time, the king of a neighboring country sent to him an iron stick [with the message]: "Find out where it's heavy and where's it's light."

text: Eli t’ay narnokin bik’ixanuƛin, bet’un k’oƛin ziru bičin.
translation: "We are not going anywhere from here!", the fox jumped up.

text: Qiƛ’orok’uƛ’ay yeɣeni esiw yukaynosi, yiła nełar radiru kinaw šebinon roƛixno adäz ričin, howlortow rok’un ret’un žan yexun.
translation: When she happened to see her younger sister, everthing appeared in front of her(?), she got a heart-attack and died.

text: Šebi ci cedäƛin?” - esirno zeyä.
translation: "How was he named?", asked the bear.

text: Igorno ik’in babiya č’arik’nosi, užir retinanu, dahawgon šida di moƛał xecinč’uƛin.
translation: The father came closer and after waking him up, the son didn't like it [and said]: "Why didn't you let me sleep a little more?"

text: Där retinäy di guluƛ’no zawƛin, eƛin žeduqor guluzaxoza.
translation: "I will ride on a horse whenever I want," said the horseman.

text: Din nesi xander ʕarzaƛ’ ayäsi zows.
translation: And I also came to this king with a complaint.

text: ʕAliqiličä nełaz c’eruc’a caƛin.
translation: Aliqilich shot with his bow.

text: Ža ɣˤutku eli yołƛin, žoyä eniw ɣˤaɣˤu yizirno.
translation: "This house is ours.", the boy [said and] made his mother happy.

text: Łˤono äƛiru ɣudƛ’ogon xanä łˤonoqu nukar egirno ayrno, q’ˤanon esnabin, Beqes ʕUneyzatno sadaq yowanixƛin.
translation: On the third day, the king sent sixty henchmen and let them bring the two siblings and Sun's Uneyzat.

text: Hemełtow zamanał, k’oƛin rik’in yizi aħozis barur kumak boda.
translation: At that time they ran to help the shepherd's wife.

text: Dicegon k’ediłin yizir yeda esun anu.
translation: No matter how long they looked, they did not find him.

text: Sidaquł azu kosiƛ’oräy, zirun, zeyn hemedurtow sadaq mečołxor rik’in.
translation: One day, while gathering the harvest, the fox and the bear went to the field together.

text: Elor bayrun, muši begira Ħasan q’ˤida ičiƛ’oräy ox̌-x̌o-x̌oyƛin eƛin.
translation: As soon as they arrived there, while Hassan sat down to take a breath, he said: "Oh-ho-hoy!"

text: Q’arumä adäz äru šebin rac’no.
translation: Qarum ate the things that were put in front [of him].

text: Hunin regirno xizor yutin nexanƛin, yigtow yedu ƛ’iri yutizaƛ’, eniyan yegirno.
translation: When she insisted: "I will see her off and come back again!", the mother let her go.

text: Qura ƛˤebaƛ’aza oƛixno k’oƛin ža ečruni žek’u, sis uži där neƛa ayƛin.
translation: Twenty years later, the old man appeared [and said]: "One sone has to be given to me!"

text: Sosi reƛ’ibi risa min, kidno bitazoni ɣˤutkaɣor horoƛin”.
translation: "At first you and your daughter will go to the next room to shake hands."

text: Däzoq’aw ɣˤutkun rodoƛin, xanä eƛin kidbez xedyoqor, maday madargon mesedyos ƛen eroƛin.
translation: "Build a house like mine", said the king to his daughter's husband, "and construct a golden bridge from door to door!"

text: Neła quł sasaqtow ža ɣˤanabi bašiqˤoyn koyär risno rukarxo bazarya yäčizay, ayn sida užä nełas baha šebiƛin esirnosi, neła debeq yiłas baha bäzinč’iƛin eƛin.
translation: The next day in the morning, the woman was showing around the ring on her hand, and while being on the market, a boy came and asked: "What's its price?"; she said: "You won't afford its price."

text: Q’ˤanon esnabi sadaq hunix roqno.
translation: And so the two sisters went on their way together.

text: Xizyo aɣä nesir meɣu teƛno.
translation: Afterwards, the bird gave him an udder.

text: Meɣu där neƛ, deber di ʕansa neƛanƛin daʕba boyn ičin ečruni žek’u.
