translation: "Give me the udder and I will give you the walking stick!", the old man argued.

text: Roda šebinon koƛ’inč’ey yisä bečnaqay reč’no reƛ kurno, howlaytow ric’no iyos zok’in et’no haqa.
translation: Not knowing what else to do, he cut meat off from his knee and threw it to the eagle, them he filled the blood into a mug and poured it into its mouth.

text: Neła quł užä ɣuro kamirno.
translation: On that day, the boy lost the cows.

text: Nesiq bišʷa rodin łäyrun, mamalayƛ’no zown howžatow t’omceni žek’u oƛixno k’oƛin salamno teƛno, q’ˤida ičin łäyrun bišʷa esirno.
translation: When he was just finished making the food, that very span-sized man appeared riding on the rooster, jumped off, greeted, and right when he sat down, he asked for food.

text: Kʷaxatow baq’ˤu ahli kumakyorƛin bayn k’oƛin.
translation: Quickly many people came running in order to help.

text: Neł ʕaƛos xalq’i besun yisir asaɣor reƛ’ibin kurno, qema regirƛin Allahqo harizi boqxo.
translation: He found the people of the village spreading out their hands into the sky pleading God: "Send us rain!"

text: Sasaq bizinosi, ža uži ganƛ’on zown hunix oqno.
translation: The next morning, when he got up, the boy got on his way riding on the bull.

text: Nełan, dey užiƛin, žaq ałni bic’no.
translation: She also said: "My son!", and gave him a hug.

text: Moƛaq iqirač’iniƛäy yisä yisis baša bicin sasaqor t’eq’erbašiƛ’or ičin.
translation: In order not to fall asleep, he tied his finger and was cautious till the morning came.

text: Hemełƛ’o hudu babiya žazaqor eƛin, meža ža nełaz ža ħala xec, koyiq yałru šinaw micxirno däqor neƛno, neła meži tusnaq’äy boɣsiƛin.
translation: After that the father then said to his sons: "Leave her in peace, she has given me all the money she had oh her hands and released you from prison."

text: Sida quł neła nełasno, eniwsno sidaƛ’or šeƛ’un rutirno, žan rižin ciqaɣor yoxin.
translation: One day she collected the clothes of herself and her mother and carrying them escaped into the forest.

text: Qunyox yałru bežet’anizaz beƛ’ˤes reqenyoł sis čanya zown q’ˤano cek’i bodäsi.
translation: The oldest one, who was on the farmstead, had a goat in his herd which gave birth to two goat kids.

text: Baq’ˤu xalq’i nesider bayn, di ža c’ohor yołƛin.
translation: Many people came to him: "I am that thief."

text: Nełdäy xexbin rižin, k’ucin, k’et’us xexoybin nełder kurno.
translation: She took away the children from her and put the puppy and the kitten do her.

text: Q’ˤidan ičin his xuxix zawru bara esirno, łinaƛ’ mi qoqƛäƛin.
translation: His wife who was sitting there combing wool asked: "What were you laughing about?"

text: Xizyogon zira eƛin, walla, howža di eƛix zowali łiłiroƛ’ozoni ɣunoqay yaqˤułer ƛˤebno yok’ek’no.
translation: Then the fox said: "By God, someone stole the leaf from the tree on that ditch I talked about.

text: Dibirä esirno nesiq, mi žeduq cirabi esirinč’aƛin.
translation: The imam asked him: "Haven't you asked for their names?"

text: Esin xediya babiya äsiru xabar.
translation: The husband told what his dad had said.

text: Acxor wazir aynosi, nesir eženi esiya eƛix teqno:
translation: When the vizier came to the door, he heard the eldest brother say:

text: Nesis zown užin kidno.
translation: He had a son and a daughter.

text: Ža rukayxow, k’et’uz elo aw yołƛin rok’ƛ’orno rayn bet’un bˤeƛez q’ˤimƛ’or k’oƛin.
translation: Seeing this, the cat thought there was a mouse and jumped onto the pig's head.

text: Baq’ˤu q’ʷarid boqno ɣʷˤadi howži, didurtow di ziruł adäzar bik’anƛin.
translation: The crow became very sad, [thinking]: "Now, how will I go before the fox?"

text: Elaygon bitor žeda žedude sadaq hunix k’et’un boɣno.
translation: From there they took the cat with them further on their way.

text: Howsi ʕabdalƛ’o bužzi oqsi diƛin, izin elay oqno maħor ik’in dibir, q’ˤuya mečoƛ’ baru k’edanixƛin.
translation: "And I had believed in that fool!", said the mullah, got up from there and went away to search for his wife in another place.

text: Kidbazade sadaq di baɣmaɣor yoqan yaħna-yik’anix, elay di yok’ek’anix nesis hunar ʕeze boqnäy, nesis murad t’ubazi baqƛin.
translation: "I will go out into the garden together with the girls to take a walk, if his talent is enough to steal me from there, his wish will be fulfilled."

text: Xizyo nesä Ražbadinqor eƛin, riynč’ey raqru łinarno šebin rodan.
translation: Then he said to Razhbadin: "This happened by accident, what shall we do?"

text: C’ik’iw yizi kecnosi, neł aždaħä c’in erno ɣoɣoru ʕamołno eqerno geres hibo ɣoɣorik’a boqno.
translation: When all of them slept, the dragon made a fire, put an iron staff onto the coals and began to make it red-hot.

text: Tełxor ižiƛ’oräy, kidbä nesi žaqor aq’ˤlu bodin, barkatawni ħiƛ’oqu gurow q’ˤuya šebin eniwqay risanix mi razi aqno.
translation: When she led him inside, the girl gave the boy an advice: 'Don't agree to take anything else from my mother than the blessed handkerchief!'

text: Kurzak’ugon yisiłäyn boqno xizay k’oƛin.
translation: And he became a falcon and ran afterwards.

text: Kusas ža xabar täqruntow, q’ˤuyasi ʕaq’lus xalq’in maħor begirno, xanä nesiq esirno, šida mi mesedyos ɣurušno cuƛer kurno, nedur eƛäƛin.
translation: When he heard what Kusa said, the king immediately sent the other advisors outside and asked him: "Why did you throw a golden ruble up and said this?"

text: Sida quł ʕaƛoz xalq’imoq łin rixra regirinč’ey, sida kikƛ’o aždaħ baq’ˤułno.
translation: One day, a dragon covered a spring and didn't let the people of the village fetch water.

text: Neširutow bˤeƛä c’idoƛ’or ƛoħon rizirno, qˤay łäxay buta begirno.
translation: At night, the pig put the pan on the fire and sent the hare for water again.

text: Nedur resunäyƛin, - eƛin onoča, - sasaq qaciƛ’or di bik’a yołƛin”.
translation: "If that's the case," said the hen, "I will go after firewood tomorrow."

text: Buqbetaxar kidbä siskin ziya kamirinč’ey, posures reqenno ʕaƛar bayrno yayn.
translation: Toward sunset, the girl hadn't lost any cow and led the cattle's herd back to the village and came back.

text: Eženi esiya eƛin:
translation: The eldest brother said:

text: Xizyo xanä nesir žawab teƛno:
translation: After that, the king answered him:

text: Xizyo yisä eƛin, yedu dey baħaray meža šida xedyox yegirxoƛin.
translation: Afterwards he said: "Why do you marry off my fiancee to another husband?"

text: Elo bišanixƛin q’ˤida bičinosi žedus Kusaƛ’ ozuri c’oxno.
translation: After they sat down in order to eat, their eyes fell on Kusa.

text: Elaygon bitor yik’iƛ’oräy yedu sida beže ʕaƛar yayn.
translation: When she went further from there, she came to a big village.

text: Kaɣatyoƛ’ rukaräsi zown Tawadä, däz žade sadaq Tiblisä t’et’erxo zawru halmaɣbin bayxosi yołƛin.
translation: In the letter it was also pointed out that some friends who were studying together with the son in Tbilisi were coming.

text: Tabanaw biħanayn žedu q’ˤanon.
translation: The two of them fought very long.

text: Dey esnabi, babiya badiru wasiyatyoƛ’ req’un t’o mesedyos łˤono saħ biča bay, amma t’o q’ˤano taraw saħ anu.
translation: "My brothers, according to father's fulfilled legacy, 3 Sah of gold must be here, but here is nothing more than 2 Sah.

text: Sosigon žoyä łˤono ɣˤurušno teƛno, eženi žek’uqor ʕaq’lu bodoƛin eƛin.
translation: Again, the boy gave him the third ruble and said to the old man: "Give me an advice!"

text: C’unt’i, c’ʷant’i t’ekzo šilaƛ’, t’eq’urzo aħyaƛ!
translation: Tsunti-tswanti, over the horn of a goat, under the ear of a biennial goat.

text: Yegiranir mi di elo teł yišäsi anuƛin, yisän žawab teƛno.
translation: "I didn't lock you in there to let you out again", he answered.

text: Łox äƛirugon nełatow c’enno uži ʕiyayn, žigon šebi nesir retäƛin, eniya bayan bisnosi, yeženi esiya eƛin:
translation: When she pinched the boy for the third time and he cried, again, his mother asked, what he wanted again now, so the eldest sister said:

text: Ozno xizok’no biłe bičirno Ismaʕil ʕArabuzande dandir ik’in ʕoƛira putceni kedonno yisno, biƛira putcenigon kedon nełaqno zown.
translation: Ismail [kept the army as if they were pushed back?] and went towards Arabuzan, having taken the seven-pood sledgehammer, while she took the eight-pood sledgehammer.

text: Hemedur Kusa ayn osugani ezä teł babiya yuqˤno yičirno zawruni kidbeder.
translation: So Kusa came to the girl who was being grounded hidden inside the high fortress by her father.

text: Elay bitor ik’iƛ’oräy, yeda eɣeni esiw sidaɣ ʕaƛar ayn.
translation: While going away from there, the young brother came into a village someplace.

text: Babiya uži Okude sadaq egirno xecin.
translation: The father sent his son together with Oku.

text: Žedude sadaq kumakyor xalq’in zown.
translation: And they had people with them for help.

text: Elo yizir didiwnkin ukru besun anu.
translation: There they didn't find any silver at all.

text: Bečedawni žek’u razi oqno.
translation: The wealthy man agreed.

text: Tamaša oqno ƛexun eɣeni esiw, łuey yedu t’o idu nediw bercinłi rodisƛin.
translation: The younger brother got very surprised: "Who the heck has done such a beauty here at home?"

text: Q’aymoxor baynosi, bˤeƛä šida boxäƛin esirnosi, qˤayä elo didiwƛa žek’u yołƛin eƛin.
translation: After coming to the tent, the pig asked: "Why did you run away?" and the hare said: "There is some man there!"

text: Tupin ħiroq caƛin, gulun bˤaƛoyn, eɣeni uži lel yaƛ’ˤuruni aɣeł xizay oqno.
translation: The youngest son threw the rifle over his shoulder, harnessed the horse and went to find the bird that lost its feather.

text: Raładyołxorno yiħun, muhumozas qotno ric’no yoƛixno eƛin, šibaw žek’uz akä sist’a muhu eqro, elay bitorsi debi ħalt’i anuƛin.
translation: She went into the sea, filled her hand with grains, reappeared and said: "Put one grain into the window of each man, besides that there's no more work for you."

text: Zowsƛin”, nesiƛ’no hemedurtow ža žek’u razi oqno.
translation: "There was", the man agreed this time as well.

text: Łˤora ɣudƛ’az beže bertenno bodin, idur kidno yown.
translation: They organized a three days lasting marriage and he brought the girl home.

text: Sida quł babiyan, best’al eniyan yedu ʕaƛoz posurxo yegirno, qˤašder boƛik’u magalun erno.
translation: One day the father and the stepmother sent her to the village's cattle, preparing for her half of the bread for lunch.

text: Där exuntow riguƛin, žoyä eƛin.
translation: I would rather die.", said the boy.

text: Elozo ʕaƛos xalq’imo yisir micxir teƛa boqno, amma eɣeni esiya eƛin, där mežus bečełi ħažat anu.
translation: The people in the village there began to give him money, but the young brother said: "I don't need your riches.

text: T’ok’ow roda šebin koƛ’inč’ey, q’ˤuyzaɣaɣor dawlaƛ’ zirun bik’in.
translation: Not knowing what to do anymore, the fox went after prey somewhere else.

text: ʕOloqzas zigara boƛixno, howži šebi rodanƛin.
translation: The young people started to complain: "What shall we do now?"

text: Bertenyoł, keč’ qˤaƛoƛin, boc’ä ʕomoyq harizi rodin.
translation: On the marriage, the wolves asked the donkey: "Sing a song!"

text: Hemeł zamanał žader yexora kot’u yołäsi sis ečru žek’u oƛixno k’oƛin žaq esirno, šida mi nece q’ʷarid ičäsiƛin.
translation: Some time later an old man with a long beard came running and asked the boy: "Why are you so sad?"

text: Hudäyzikogon idu biša rodanix sideni bełiqan ičin.
translation: The [second?] day after that, another hunter stayed home to make food.

text: Meža barubi raƛin”.
translation: Find wives to marry!"

text: Esnabi, muši kuracekin ričinč’ey, aždaħqay raqruce maqˤir roxin rik’anił ričix zown.
translation: The sisters, not even waiting a single breath, were running away from the dragon to get far away from it.

text: Nesan hemedurtow kaƛ’inč’iƛin žawab teƛno.
translation: Also he gave the answer likewise: "I can't."

text: Neł bertenyołer hokobin sadaq gulubin rayrno tabanaw xalq’i bayn.
translation: To that marriage came many people, bringing carriages and horses.

text: Hemedur q’ˤunesno micxirno łiyn, babiya žarƛin c’unzi rodin xecin zawruni micxiryosno, mesedyosno ixinno łiyrno yizä.
translation: So the two guys' money was finished, and the bag of money and gold that the father left for his son they finished as well.

text: Nełaqno sis magalu beɣe biłe boqno.
translation: For her one bread became too small.

text: Roƛo räsruxor, kidno teƛno, kidbez babiya uži hunix nesä nesiz ʕaƛaɣor egirno.
translation: According to arrangement, the girl's father gave his daughter to the boy and sent him to his village.

text: Hudu šebi di rodinäy xedyoxay bat’an yoqno där etixosi äqiƛin, esirno riynoxuq.
translation: "So what shall I do to get rid of my husband and get my lover?", she asked the sheikh.

text: Sis zown c’aq’tow sider sis betäsi užin kidno.
translation: Once there was a boy and a girl who loved each other very much.

text: Didur roqłin, žoyä eniw muk’ur yodin xanes kid esira yik’a.
translation: Somehow the boy persuaded his mother into going to ask the king's daughter.

text: Nesä eƛin:
translation: He said:

text: Idurno yižin yiła yaɣˤin tełer yezunosi, bercinaw ƛ’iräy ozuri boɣa betixanusi, kid yesun.
translation: She carried it home, opened it and when she looked inside, she found a girl so beautiful that she didn't want to take her eyes off from her.

text: Xizyo xedya esin bazarganzan nesän roƛo räsrułäy.
translation: Then the husband told of what was between the merchants and himself.

text: Didurnokin t’omceni žek’a ħal bayrinč’izaƛ’, sideni bełiqanä roɣno k’onč’ałer tupi caƛin.
translation: As they couldn't overcome him, the other hunter took a rifle and shot him in the leg.

text: Babiwq žan biqira k’ʷeze boqnoanu.
translation: The father couldn't catch him either.

text: Di ayzaƛ’or xuxin ek’no hisno, där bigu, qema regirxanusi butni bodoƛin.
translation: Before I come back, comb and spin the wool, and make a good burka for me, that doesn't let the rain go through."

text: Howži yizi esnaza ħukmu bodin, yisi užä elur kumak bodinč’us yołi, yiła ƛˤoreł eli beɣa zows, horo ela yisix elus esiy yegiranƛin.
translation: Now those brothers came to a decision: "If that boy hadn't helped us, we would have been defeated in that war; so come, let's marry off our sister to him!"

text: Bišno boqnosi, esnabin maħor begirno, eɣeni esiya igorno ik’in Kusaq esirno, sasaqay soder q’ˤimno riqirno, bišadyoƛ’orno aq’ˤułnowa mi ičäsi, šebi deber q’ʷariłi boqäsiƛin.
translation: After having eaten, the young brother sent his brothers outside, approached Kusa and asked him: "What sadness has happened to you so that you are sitting there over your food holding your head."

text: Nełas yałru žedur mumpaʕat biynosi, yizä ža ʕansa muroƛ’orno butirno, kidbez kočorał caƛin.
translation: When they understood the benefit of it, they turned this walking stick into a needle and put it into the girl's hair.

text: Ža xabarno esin, aw adäzay gugin.
translation: Having told this, the mouse ran out of sight.

text: Šida yałru debertow räyƛin, kidbä uži ezuranix bitazoni ɣˤutkaɣor ižin.
translation: "You will know why", the girl [said and] lead the boy into the next room to show him.

text: Yizä yedu iday maħortow yegirxo zown anu.
translation: They didn't let her out of the house.

text: Nełas ʕoƛno uži gurow siskin kid zown anu.
translation: Except her seven sons, she didn't have any daughter.

text: Guluƛ’ zowa adäza Musa nesiqor eƛin:
translation: Before mounting the horse, Musa said to him:

text: Yedugon łinarey bˤoƛ’usƛin? - eƛin žoyä - ži bičas babiwz wasimoƛ’ req’unƛin”.
translation: "Why the heck has this fallen down?", said the boy, "Now my father's will will be fulfilled."

text: "Yaqˤułenč’e mi däder dandixno, narčey mi däqay yok’äłƛin?".
translation: 'Today you came to me, where will you escape from me to?'.

text: Elaygon ƛ’iyor oqno nexƛ’oräy, bˤeliz ciqłer ayn.
translation: When he came down from there, he came to the poplar's forest.

text: Nełqułtow žedus berten bodin.
translation: They married on the same day.

text: Etali pun bok’no neti yeda ɣut’xoƛin idu iłe taxƛ’o kecno ičäsi zown.
translation: And the lover lay down to sleep on the sofa, just like at home, and waited for him to pass away.

text: Tełer bik’in bezuzaƛ’, ʕoƛnon esnabi haqa bogun bezun ƛexun.
translation: When the seven brothers went inside and took a look, they remained looking with an open mouth.

text: Xander baynosi, yedu reƛ di riqirsiƛin sidä eƛin, ay di riqirsiƛin sis ičin, hemedur nukarza xanqo heresi esin.
translation: After they came to the king, one said: "I caught this meat.", one insisted: "No, I caught it!", so the henchmen lied to the king.

text: Sayɣat teƛanix ža uži tełxor aƛin eniya nełaq di goƛ’irersi.
translation: 'Bring the boy inside, in order to give him a present!', and the mother let her call me."

text: Žigoƛ’aygon eɣeni esiw nesiq esiranix oqno, šida mi nece pašmanłiƛ’ ičäsiƛin.
translation: And now the younger brother began to ask him: "Where is there so much sadness on you?"

text: Eɣeni uži aq’ˤiłno, ʕoloqan oqa isäsi zown.
translation: The small boy grew up, and soon he became a young man.

text: Izirno ƛ’irizoni dunyalƛ’or ayrno.
translation: And he brought him up into the upper world.

text: Nesiƛ’ ci Tawadi yoł.
translation: His name is Tavadi.

text: ƛ’iri bičin žeda kikizaƛ’, xexbi baq’ˤiłxotow bik’ix zown.
translation: They diligently fed them and the children grew up steadily.

text: Neła buqˤno zawruni q’ayn bižin, žeda žedus ʕaƛazaɣorno bik’in.
translation: They took out the article that she hid, and went to their own villages.

text: Neł mixƛ’o žoyä ʕansa begirno, xanes sadaq askar t’it’oƛin.
translation: At this time, the boy sent his walking stick: "Destroy the king's entire army!"

text: Mi aħyabin rˤoƛ’urno šida ičäsiƛin esirnosi, yisä eƛin, debex di esiw yegirłin, mi däq debi mi maʕišat bukarxanuƛin.
translation: When in the morning he asked: "Why are you sitting there with your ears fallen down?", he answered: "Although I married off my sister to you, you don't show me your fortune."

text: Yiziqgon Aħmadä esirno žiƛboyä yeda, eƛ’iyä exasiƛin.
translation: Akhmed asked them: "Has he died this year or last year?"

text: Eɣeni esiyan, łoħran sadaq ʕumru boda baybik bodin.
translation: The younger brother and the frog began to live together.

text: Nedurgon roqxa žaƛin, nełaq esirno rok’äłrun, rok’no bˤałayn eɣeni esiya ža yoƛƛi yegirno.
translation: "Did it happen like this?!" she accidentally said, and right after that the young brother hit her with the dagger and split her in the middle.

text: Šeƛ’ures k’emot’yar t’it’ubi rodäsi resunosi, yeženi esiya qˤirič’ roɣno maħor kurno.
translation: The older sister found the clothings in the wooden case that were cut to pieces, took the scissors and threw them outside.

text: Anƛ’ima baħnayłin žeduq nedugani reƛis sis t’it’ukin riqinanu.
translation: Although they walked around for a week, they could not get even a piece of such a meat.

text: Esin šebi, xecin šebi sis zown ɣˤanabi.
translation: Once upon a time, there was a woman.

text: Daɣaw bičintowgon zira im-m-m-m, im-m-m-mƛin eƛin.
translation: Waiting for a while, the fox said "Mhmhmh, mhmhmh."

text: Nesiƛ’ Zeyes Aħyaƛin cin cedin.
translation: She gave him the name "Bear Ear".

text: Där ħumukulis ruɣu hunä resus.
translation: "I found the camel's hoofprints on the way.

text: Neł ɣˤanazar uži odin.
translation: A son was born to the woman.

text: Mi šebi žek’u t’or ayäsiƛin?” - esirno aždaħä yisiq.
translation: What kind of man are you to come here?", asked him the dragon.

text: Žedus užin kidno zownƛax.
translation: It's said they had a son and a daughter.

text: Suƛƛi teł iduɣor aqruntow yisä yiłaq ħaƛanix łi esirnosi, lamusno yoqno eƛin, deber idu ħaƛanix łi anu, t’o kikƛ’o aždaħ baq’ˤułäsi yoł, ɣudes nełar yac’anix q’ˤimaku teƛinč’inäy, neła yiła ʕaƛor łi regirxosi anu.
translation: Immediately after coming into the house, he asked her for water to drink, and .......... she said: "You can't drink water here at home, there is a dragon sitting on the spring, and every day if someone doesn't give him a grown-up to eat, he won't let water to the village."

text: Aždaħa qˤaƛu rok’nosi kičuriču rayzaƛ’ riłe raƛ’no k’ek’-rik’no, yizin bižin taqˤo č’aq’ˤirno.
translation: The dragon released a shout, and moved the earth like by an earthquake, and thus caused them to fall far away.

text: Howži łeɣin biħadanix, sidä sis bizirxo bok’xo yiła aždaħä ħaltown bayrinč’ey baq’ˤu aq’ˤuba bukayn yisi ʕAliqiličer.
translation: Now they grasped each other to fight, lifting and beating each other up and that dragon tired Aliqilich out very much.

text: Bac’alä łi boc’i teł yałruni imxaɣor rutirno.
translation: Batsali turned the water into the cave which the wolf was inside.

text: Yisä esirno, šebi retin meži harizi boqxoƛin.
translation: He asked: "What are you asking for?"

text: Zamanałäy yisider sid miskinaw haq’us užä ayn eƛin, däq regirnäy, žedu di reynodanix ruhun rodanƛin.
translation: After a while, a boy from a poor family came to him and said: "If you send them to me, I'll teach them how to work."

text: Nełƛ’ay soder łˤoran haq’uzas bigu ʕumru boqno.
translation: As of then, the three families' lives became pretty.

text: Ža kid yižinč’ey, babiwde sadaq di äk’inč’iƛingon, xanes uži ičin.
translation: "I will not go with my father without that girl," the khan's son said.

text: Neła eƛin, dey uži eloš neła ɣˤutka teł micxir t’et’erxo ičäsi yołƛin.
translation: She said: "My son is counting money in that room over there."

text: Di nesix ʕiyayxanu, enła bäxuruni tungix ʕiyayxƛin, žagon där iyxosi žek’ukin anuƛin.
translation: "I am not crying for him, I'm crying for the broken jug. I don't know this man."

text: Nesä xʷasar badiruni ceyä, neła nełaƛ’no zowerno, yeda raładno xecin bitaɣor oɣno.
translation: The eagle he had rescued seated him onto itself and leaving the sea took him away into the distance.

text: Nesis zownƛax sis ʕoloqanaw uži.
translation: It's said that he had a young son.

text: Sis k’ucin, k’et’us xexoyn ałux ruqˤno žedun rižin q’orolay xanez barudäɣor yik’in.
translation: The widow hid a puppy and a kitten under her skirt and carried them to the king's wife.

text: Yałi eluł boƛƛ’o ƛˤori biča yołƛin.
translation: Or else there will be war between us!"

text: Eƛnin ɣutaneł yoɣno, ħonzaqaɣor bˤeƛ’ rowa oc’ino łeno ɣudi ƛexunosi, aħozir ɣˤʷadbi sidä sidqor xabaryayx teqno.
translation: They spent the winter on the winter pasture and when just 15 days remained to bring the sheep back to the mountains, the shepherd heard the crows talking among each other.

text: Eženi žek’a eƛin, ža Hulaɣor dawlaƛ’ äk’izay, qazaqza exursiƛin.
translation: The old man said: "While he went to Georgia for prey, the Georgians have killed him."

text: Ha, mežus huni regiroƛ, naɣor bik’ixƛin, esirno neła Qartayä.
translation: "Well, may you go your way, where are you going?", asked Qartay.

text: Užiz rok’ƛ’or pikru bayn, dice ixiw ʕaq’lu nesä däq mołäƛin.
translation: The boy thought: "How big an advice did he give me?!"

text: Hemedur q’ʷaridno oqno ičin zawru yeda, babiya nesiqor boyn zawruni wasiyat rok’ƛ’or bayn.
translation: So, being so sad, he recalled the legacy handed down to him by his father.

text: Yisiz babiws šudaƛ’osi ħimu yaganix biħun yedu.
translation: It came to lick the tombstone on his father's grave.

text: Zown zownanuƛax sida ʕaƛox igo ixiw aždaħ.
translation: Once upon a time there was a big dragon near a village.

text: Zownƛax, zownanuƛax sis xan, nesisgon łˤono užin zownƛax.
translation: Once upon a time there was a khan with three sons.

text: Ič’č’an yeɣeni esiya sasiłzaƛ’ortown, xexłiƛ’ iduɣor ƛaƛin daʕba bičirłin, nełqor žedu ʕenekizi roqnoanu.
translation: Although the youngest sister argued: "We have to go home quick before it gets dark!", the didn't listen to her.

text: Kidbezno baruzno rožƛ’ay iziłxanusi žek’u zown ža.
translation: The man could not get away from his daughter's and his wife's words.

text: Cimno bizin Bac’alä esirno, miyä dey čanyabi rac’äƛin.
translation: Getting cross, Batsali asked: "Did you eat my goats?!"

text: Bažarizi raq, šida däq roqxanuƛin, yeženä bašiqˤoyn risno yizin xanzo madazaxaɣor yik’in.
translation: "I can, why not?", the old woman took the ring, got up and went to the king's yards.

text: Bertenyoł zawruni ahli ʕažaib boqno nesä äƛiru łinaƛ’no.
translation: The people who were on the marriage were surprised about what he had said.

text: Doxturä nesiqorno hemedurtow eƛin, sida ƛˤebaƛ’aza debez žas di roƛxosi ričiranƛin.
translation: The doctor also said, "I will cure your son in one year."

text: Zeyn, zirun, boc’in elo sasaqorno ričin, qˤašiƛ’aɣor roqno rik’in.
translation: The bear, the fox and the wolf waited there until the morning and when it got dawn, they went away.

text: Yizis baqru xabarno teqerno, q’ˤanon xanes nukarbi - režet’ani xanes esnabi - bičäsi zown.
translation: Their talking was heard by two of the king's henchmen - the king's older sisters.

text: Łˤonon gulubi rayn adäza ričin aluk’an, qˤaban, c’udan.
translation: Three horses came and waited in front [of him], a white one, a black one and a red one.

text: Xizyo baħarayn yižin xanes užin ik’in nesä nesiz babiwdäɣor, sadaqgon nesiz esiws xedyos kidno yižin.
translation: Afterwards, the prince took the fiancée with himself and went to his own father, and together with him he took his sister's husband's daughter.

text: Bazarganza eƛin, debi hibo ela becursiƛin.
translation: The merchants said: "We broke your stick."

text: Łˤonon barubin rown, Ismaʕilä nełqay ža łˤora ɣudi bertenno boyn.
translation: Ismail married the three wives and three days in a row celebrated his marriage.

text: Bˤeƛ’xozoni aħozä eƛin:
translation: The shepherd said:

text: Šebiey yiłar roqsiƛin, yeda ezu-ʕoƛ’a ik’in.
translation: "What in the world has happened to her?", he went to visit her.

text: Elo nesä elo c’oxno, t’o c’oxno č’iħo rac’a bašla bodin.
translation: There, pouncing here and there, he began to eat the fruits.

text: Rišno łiynosi, p’es anusi baruza Umukus cugumä tełer zeruyałer kurno.
translation: After they finished eating, the wives silently threw Umukus into the bouza inside the barrel.

text: Naysinan ža qˤirič’ k’edixozos riłen rodin, xizaɣor Bilq’isdiz mežiƛäzay risno xanqor teƛno, ele debez gulus maħi räč’runi qˤirič’ƛin.
translation: He pretended to search for the scissors everywhere and finally took it from under Bilqisdi's mattress and gave it to the king: "Here, the scissors which cut off your horses tail."

text: Elaygon bitor ik’iƛ’oräy, yäsu deber aluk’a ɣˤul.
translation: And when you go further, you will find a white stone.

text: Elay izin ik’in bigtow manzil xecinosi, žedu esnabi biħanayx zawru mečoƛ’or ayn yeda.
translation: He got up and went away, and when he left a great distance after himself, he came to the place where those brothers were fighting.

text: Užä eƛin, Allahä didurƛa caxäsi resan, däz babiyan ƛirba ayruxort’a bˤeƛ’es šebin rexayx zows, sida qułkin ža bišan kamin (giħiłno) ƛexunč’u ža, din hemedurtow ƛäxunč’iƛin.
translation: The boy said: "God might have written it somehow; my father used to slaughter a sheep for every guest who came, and he wasn't without food for even a day; so I will be as well."

text: Hemedur guluƛ’no zown xanes uži maħor oqno yok’ek’alini esiwł xizay.
translation: So the prince rode on his horse outside behind his stolen sister.

text: Sida zamanałäza xabar teqxo, xanä nesä nesiz ozyołer ʕoloqbi bisxoƛin.
translation: After a while he hears the story that the king is taking young people into his army.

text: Xanä žedu nesä nesidäɣorno goƛ’in sosi eženi esiwq esirno:
translation: The king called them to himself and first asked the eldest brother:

text: Howt’aza bitor didiw ħumukuli bäk’iru di biyran mežur retinäy:
translation: If you want, I will find out for you what camel went from there.

text: Nełał xizay k’oƛin rayru ɣˤʷaybi maqˤiƛ’or rok’ełnosi, bełiqanä bikoriqor eƛin, maħor boq, howži debi tušmanbin rukayzay rok’ełsiƛin.
translation: After the dogs that were running behind it, went far away, the hunter said to the snake: "Come out, now your enemies went out of sight."

text: Ruysi reƛ’iqoybi resʷa behila mežzoxƛin, - eƛin žoyä, - amma endiw däzoq’aw anu žeduƛin”.
translation: "One might perhaps find simple gloves at your's," the boy said, "but they're not like the one I have."

text: Hemedur q’ecenyayx žedu ʕuraw mixƛ’or ƛexun.
translation: So they were arguing for a long time.

text: Kutak ʕuƛ’xo zowsƛin, eƛin žoyä.
translation: "I was extremely afraid.", said the boy.

text: Nełaq dicegon raq’ˤu biša roqłin, ɣˤʷayr neła magalas sis t’it’ukin kurnoanu.
translation: But no matter how much food she had, she didn't throw even one piece of the bread to the dog.

text: Bełiqa eƛin, horo, oxi där tupiƛ ħažoƛin.
translation: The hunter said: "Come, exchange it for my rifle!"

text: Nesiƛ’ cin Ismaʕilƛin zown.
translation: His name was Ismail.

text: Žeduqor bitor hunix ik’iƛ’orey siskin xabar bexarno.
translation: When you go further on the way, the story will go on.

text: Dahawtowgon zaman bik’izaƛ’or, xizyogon acmoq deqƛin.
translation: After a little while, it knocked on the door again.

text: Babiyagon nesiqayn bisno, ža micxir c’idaɣor kurno.
translation: The father than took the money from him and threw it into the fire.

text: Mi nełas rask’i urɣel badinƛin, sida q’urec’niƛ’ ƛ’iri ixin erno, hisos urgubi äruni sideni q’urec’niƛ’ ƛ’iri yeda zowerno.
translation: "Don't worry!", they put his bag onto the mule and seated him on the other mule that carried the sacks of wool.

text: ƛˤeb yik’inosi, sida quł dawlaƛ’ zey maħor boqäsi yałzay, kid nełdayn yoxin ʕaƛaɣor yik’in.
translation: A year went by, and one day while the bear went outside for prey, the girl ran away from him and went into a village.

text: Nełƛ’aza rayn kesubi esa adäza, eli bezu-bˤoƛ’a bay yołä t’oza xalq’i anaƛin.
translation: Because of that [...?], we must look if there are people here.

text: Elo nełade ƛirbałix yałzay, neširu Qartay maħor yoqacen ičin, izin nełaz mežił ok’bi rok’no xecin.
translation: When he was there at her's as a guest, he waited until Qartay got out at night, got up, beated nails into her mattress and left them there.

text: Žeda eƛixƛax, oc’ira łera ɣudƛ’aza howži ɣutaneł ixiw zigolun yegär, raq’ˤu bˤeƛ’no räxu, rigza ħalał elur reƛno räqiƛin.
translation: They were saying: "Fifteen days from now there will be a big hailstorm on the winter pasture, many sheep will die, and we will have a lot of meat in the trouble."

text: Di t’ay oɣoƛin eženi qˤaƛanayłin, aħyakin kurinč’ey, nesä nesiz guluƛ’no zown, oqno iduɣor ik’in.
translation: Although the old man yelled: "Take me out of here!", he didn't even listen, sat upon his own horse and went home.

text: K’ox äƛiru caƛin, hil dahaw ceyde igo yiłe č’aq’ˤinosi, nełaƛ’no k’ek’-bik’noanu.
translation: He shot a second time, and when the bullet hit something near the eagle, it didn't move then either.

text: Ža gulu sis čuret’ bak’run hawaƛ’ bik’in, ʕoƛno ħonno xecin bitazar bayxosi zown.
translation: This horse went into the air after one beat with the whip, and leaving behind seven mountains, went far into the distance.

text: Boc’an, zeybin, ɣuro yałza rečuƛ, aɣˤnä teł rišin, aɣbi girimabin kurno idutow xecin, bełis čanyabin, resobin čolow yałza hiɣar regirno.
translation: She drove the wolves and bears into the cowshed where the cows were, erected poles for the birds to give [them] a home, and sent the wild goats and roes into the half-open shed where there was straw.

text: Kidbazä esin, eli babiwde sadaq saparyox roqäsi zows, babiwn gugin howt’o ƛexasi yoł, ela debez ɣunoqay dahaw henešno but’in bac’siƛin.
translation: The girls said: "We were travelling together with our father, and he ran away and we were staying here; and we picked some apples from your tree and ate them."

text: Yedu t’azay reɣet’ani bikorbi roƛixsiƛin, oxix yeda elay.
translation: "There are small snakes there!", he said and ran away from there.

text: Howži Ismaʕil ezunč’ey ʕaƛaɣor ik’in nesä nesis łonon barubin rižin.
translation: Now Ismail went into the village without looking and took his three wives with him.

text: Kidbä eƛin, nesis xabar bit’araw yołƛin.
translation: The daughter said: "What he said, is true."

text: Debeq esäru łinarno mi žawab neƛ, guluzałäygon łu debeq esiräƛin, yiziri ƛ’iri butin.
translation: "Answer the question we asked you! And who asked you about the horses?", they insisted.

text: Nełaƛ’ razi oqinč’izaƛ’, tamiħyorƛin žeda howt’o iħuz lilyoxor kuräsiƛin, rok’u rexałxor iłe xabaryayn yeda.
translation: Because we couldn't come to an agreement, they throw me here onto the river bank as a punishment.", he talked to evoke sympathy.

text: Xizaɣor tatu łiynosi, cey ħalica Ħon-Bahadurzo mač’iqay bok’ełno.
translation: Finally his power ended and the eagle escaped like through a wonder(?) from Mountain~Hero's dagger.

text: Yisił dandir c’axru bełiqa esirno, yedu ziya naɣor bižix miƛin.
translation: A hunter whom he met asked him: "Where are you bringing that cow to?"

text: Howži qˤays yxexoy aħo xäciza mečoƛ’orno bik’in, nełaz mecreq yisis mecno bitirerno, riya rodanix aħozis hibon bisno, xizor butin eniwdäɣortown butin.
translation: Now the rabbit's young went to the place where it had left the shepherd, touched his tongue with its own tongue and took the shepherd's stick for marking it, and returned back to its mother.

text: Elo azbarya teł ža ezu-ʕaƛ’unosi, taxeƛ yisir bexuräsi esnabi besun.
translation: When he examined inside the palace, he found his killed brothers under the ottoman.

text: Yisägon šinaw raqru łinałäyn esin žedaq.
translation: And so he told them everything about it.

text: Ħayran yoqno yezun ƛexun kid, moƛałä yedu di yaqˤułƛin.
translation: The girl was surprised and remained looking (at him), "Is this day a dream?"

text: Howži yisi Ħasanes ečriłno lagis tatun bexun, ħalt’i boda biynč’us oqʷa iħun.
translation: Now Hassan's old body ran out of power, and he started getting unable to do the work.

text: Däz butna eli ozuri buqin bˤaɣizaƛ’or dowder bayrinč’us yołi, mi łemu yexa zowsƛin”.
translation: "If my burka hadn't brought us to you before the wink of an eye, then you would have died."

text: Sis zownƛax bečedaw žek’u, nesis zownƛax q’ˤano kid.
translation: Once there was a rich man who had two daughters.

text: C’ik’iw moƛa bisnosi, geres ħosno bisno Qartayä ɣoɣorik’anix pečma teł ʕamoƛ’ erno.
translation: After everyone went to sleep, Qartay took an iron stick and put it on some coals inside the oven to make it red-hot.

text: Debertow yätiru sis barun yižo mi t’ay, roƛik’u xanłin riso daqäy, howt’otow ičiƛin”.
translation: "Take the wife you love with you, take half of the kingdom from me, but stay here!"

text: Žeda žedur ʕeziyaw reƛ raħiranixgon esiwn yegirno.
translation: The sister did that so they would have much meat for themselves.

text: Yisir rodixosi šebin därtow räyƛin.
translation: I'll know what we do to him."

text: Ža soder iziƛ’oräy, qoqoƛix izix zown, wˤałer oqƛ’oräy ʕiyayx oqxo zown.
translation: While he was going up, he laughed, and while he was going down, he cryed.

text: Zira sida quł nesiqor eƛin:
translation: One day, the fox said to him:

text: Yeženä Beqes ʕUneyzatno, xediwn mˤaliz qˤodreza tełäy maħor begirno, howži mežus req’el boqsiƛin.
translation: The old woman let Sun's Uneyzat and her husband out of her nostril [and said]: "Now you're at peace."

text: Goqi zira äƛiru łinaƛ’no bužzin oqno, nesä ža iduɣorno bižin, q’ˤanon sadaq bičix zown.
translation: Goqi believed what the fox said, he took him home and the two lived together.

text: Ža xabar šet’ur ʕaƛozałno bik’in.
translation: This story went through all the villages around.

text: Xizyogon Ražbadinä äƛiruxor božarawni ʕoloqza ʕaƛos baħarayƛ’ rayzaƛ’or nasin kidbabi rutirno.
translation: Then, according to what Razhbadin had said, the reliable young people gathered all girls of the village, including the bride.

text: Elaygon bitor ik’iƛ’oräy, yeda raładyoz lilyoxor ayn.
translation: When he went further, he came to the banks of the sea.

text: Nełaƛ’ žedun razi boqno.
translation: With that they agreed.

text: Hemeła zamanał ʕaƛa xabar boƛixno, qalamez ƛ’ˤuƛ’or gulun bizirno, elay kid yok’ek’a k’ʷeze yaqru žek’ux xanä ža yegirxoƛin.
translation: At some time, a rumor appeared in the village: The king would marry off his daughter to the man who is able to lift a horse onto the roof of his fortress and to steal his daughter from there.

text: Žedu sis ečru k’et’u zown.
translation: They had an old cat.

text: Nedur resunäy k’ot’oniłƛin žoyä eƛinosi, ele k’ot’oniłinč’aƛin kid k’omo yiłen yoqno q’ˤida yičin.
translation: When the boy said: "If that's the case, get shorter!", the girl said: "Here, am I not getting shorter?", sat down and became like a ball.

text: Xizyo xanä sis siħiray q’orolayn goƛ’in nesä nesidäɣor, nełaq esin, däz iday micxir nar eƛa biynč’ey gugix, łu bok’ek’xosi yałrułin didurnokin riyxanuƛin.
translation: Then the king invited a cunning widow to himself and he told her: "Money has vanished from my home to who-knows-where, I don't know whatsoever who might have stolen."

text: Q’ˤida bičanix yiziƛer uyno q’ˤuri rayrno.
translation: He brought four chairs for them to sit down.

text: Babiwƛ’ nediw ɣudi rukayzaƛ’, kid žigon yizin žader tusnaq’ärno yik’in akixor yaynosi, ixiw muši kurno.
translation: When she saw the hazzle on her father, the girl got up again and went into the prison to the boy, and moaned, when she came to the window.

text: Sida quł esnazas barubi urɣizi roqno sisxolisini žedaz xedyozas esiy zeroqozo cugumäɣor kuranix.
translation: One day, the brothers' wives planned to throw their husbands' lonely sister into a barrel of bouza.

text: Nedur resunäy, sis k’emot’ därno yodoƛin, ƛ’iri yedu yutin.
translation: "If that's the case, make me one wooden case!" she insisted.

text: Elo bäsuru mesedyoƛ rac’a šebin risno, bigu bet’erbaqi boqno žedus.
translation: In exchange for gold, that was found there, they bought something to eat and lived good.

text: Xizyogon bara yizin xediwq ałni bic’no, nełƛ’ay žuka elur rukaynoƛ, eli saɣłiƛ’ boqnäy, dunyales ɣez bihayaw šebin yołƛin.
translation: Then the wife got up and hugged her husband again: "May we not see something worse than this; as long as we are in health, it's an easy thing to find the world's "

text: Neširu kinaw ħalt’imabin łiyrno nasin iduzaɣor butinosi, sida sidqo badiru ħalt’ozałäy esin.
translation: By the evening, when everyone has finished their works and returned home, they told each other about the work they did.

text: Hemedur ħonbin, iħubin, xecin nexƛ’oräy, sidaɣ keroqor baynosi hoko buta boqno.
translation: So while they left mountains and rivers behind, they came to a ravine and began to turn the carriage.

text: Muʕalimkin nesä maħor egirinč’iey, žaq darsi mołirxo ičirxo zown.
translation: He didn't even let the teacher go outside until he finished teaching lessons to his son.

text: Nedur resunäy, mi därsitow ɣˤanabiwa zownƛin, howlotow babiwn nełas yałzay, maharno erno daramno boyn, nesi žoyä yacʕaltow yowx.
translation: "If that's the case, aren't you a woman exactly for me?", and while the father was hers(?) there, they organized a wedding, traded and that boy married his cousin.

text: Elor bayza quł yizir Tawadis uži ayäsi esunanu.
translation: On the day they arrived there, they didn't find Tavaid's son having come.

text: Xizyo xanä žedu esnazaq esirno:
translation: Afterwards the king asked the brothers:

text: Zamanałäy neła ɣˤanazas xediwn, nesisgon užin ozno bižin ƛˤoreł bik’inosi, nełar retäsi zown best’alawni kid elo iday yok’ek’anix.
translation: After some time, after the woman's husband, his son and the army went to war, she wanted to remove the step-daughter from their home.

text: Sasaq moƛaxay izizaƛ’or mesedyos qˤiƛurabi resuzaƛ’, bečedawni žek’a kidno yegirno nesi žax, neła qˤiƛurazaƛ’aza baħarayn iduɣor yižin, nasin ʕaƛos xalq’in goƛ’in bertenno bodin.
translation: The neex morning, before the wealthy man got up from his sleep, he sent his daughter to that boy, because he found the golden steps; [NELHA] lead his fiancée over the steps to his home, they invited all people of the village and married.

text: Di yoł debi xexbin rexurno, howži mi bexuranix nexʷasi žek’uƛin”, - eƛin ʕAliqiličä.
translation: "I am a man who killed your children, and now came to kill you", said Aliqilich.

text: Mi q’ʷarid yaqnoƛin, eƛin užä.
translation: "Don't be sad!", said the boy.

text: Sida quł roƛa yutirnosi, eniya kidbeqor eƛin, mi babiwqor eƛo, nagaħ di yexunäy hoboda bašiqˤoy c’oxxosi taraw nesä ɣˤanabi yawnoƛƛin.
translation: One day, when the mother started to feel pain, she told her daughter: "Tell your father that when I suddenly die, he may only marry a woman who matches this ring!"

text: Nełqazay Ħasaner riyn c’eruc’as hilno q’ˤiryoł caƛin, Ħusen guluzał xizay ʕaƛaɣor nexno zawrułin, nesirgon hunix aždaħno keze boqno, neła axʷaɣor ägirułin.
translation: From her he knew that Hussein sticked his bow's arrow into the floor and went to the village for horses, and that he met a dragon on the way, which swallowed him into his belly.

text: Howži yedu aždaħ yisiƛ’or bizin eƛin:
translation: Now the dragon came over him, got up and said:

text: Yigza ħalał barun qˤaƛanada yegirno xecin, xiszi rožin rodin, xediya eƛin, di ent’o yoł, šebi deber retinƛin.
translation: Letting his wife shout out quite loudly, the husband changed his voice and said: "I am here, what do you want?"

text: Elo ža dibir neła kidbeł xizay oqno.
translation: So the imam followed that girl.

text: Neła yeda gugyoƛ’no zowerno soder asaɣor izirno.
translation: It seated him on its back and brought him up into the sky.

text: Kʷaxatow yeda sida azbaryoz madaxor ayn.
translation: Soon he came to the gate of a palace.

text: Nełas ža ozuri saɣ radirur xanä beže sayɣat bodanƛin ƛ’iri risäsi.
translation: To he, who cures her eye, the king will give a big present.", he added.

text: Boƛƛi raładyołer baynosi nesi sadaq zawruni žek’a ža ozƛ’o yisa oqno.
translation: When they came to the middle of the sea, the man that was together with them, started to mock her.

text: Zownƛax zown anuƛax Mirzaƛin sis žek’u.
translation: Once upon a time there was a man named Mirza.

text: Elo där c’aq’ bercinaw ɣˤanabi yesus.
translation: There I found a very beautiful woman.

text: Xizyogon kidbezno, debezno, däzno čuret’no bok’no, ħolaq’awt’a ʕomoybin rodin, ža čuret’ nˤoƛał buqˤan łärnokin bäsunč’iza.
translation: Then with the beats of our daughter's, your and my whip, we will turn into thin donkeys and hide the whip in the ashes where no one can find it.

text: Elogon xanä wazirbin butirno, sosigon łu yedu dey mesedyosni t’akan yayräƛin esirnosi, oƛixno hemesitow wazirä eƛin, di yayrinč’a žaƛin.
translation: There, the king gathered his viziers, and when he asked: "Who again brought this golden glass of mine?", that vizier appeared and said: "Haven't I brought it?"

text: Gamiƛ’no zown baħna-bik’iƛ’orey, ža gamiƛ’osi žek’un, däx horoƛin, hemedurtow ƛ’iri utin.
translation: When they were walking around on the boat, that sailor insisted again: "Come with me!"

text: Elo esnazar q’ˤidar beč’ułäsi ukrun, micxirno besun.
translation: There the brothers found the silver and money that had flown down.

text: Hudu xedyox yik’inč’ey, nece yeže yizace šida yičäsiƛin bełiqanä esirnosi, neła esin nesiq, t’omceni žek’ur yegira retixanuƛin.
translation: When the hunter asked: "So why don't you go to a husband but wait until you get old?", she said to him: "The span-sized man doesn't want to let me [go]."

text: Yoqzaƛ’ yeženi esiwł xizaytow min yik’iƛin, xanä yoƛƛosin esiw maħor yoxirno.
translation: "Because this happened, go after your older sister!", and the king also chased the middle sister away.

text: Sida quł neł kidber łˤonon esnabi hunix dandir c’oxno.
translation: One day, that girl met the three brothers on the way.

text: Xizyo xexza esirno, eli mežusä yoł xexbi, žek’usä yołƛin.
translation: Then the children asked: "Are we your children or someone else's?"

text: Sosi mi där k’ałazi yoqa izmu neƛƛin”.
translation: At first, give me the permission to speak to you.

text: Xizyo nesä žeduqor eƛin, howt’o ʕaƛa di łi roƛik’näy, šebi meža där baha boyxƛin.
translation: Afterwards he said to them: "If I pull out water here in the village, what price do you pay me?"

text: Nełaƛ’no kidbes babiw razin oqno, berten boynč’eytown xanxo Pat’imatno yegirno.
translation: After that, the girl's father agreed and married Fatima off to the king without having a wedding.

text: Raq ža eluqƛin, yizä yeda ɣˤaɣˤu izirno.
translation: "We will do that!", they cheered him up.

text: Mi iynč’ey nesił xizazarno ik’in, riqirno łox kikibi ƛiso, mi dey uži yołƛin eƛace.
translation: Go to him from behind without him knowing, catch his breasts and pull them three times until he says "You are my son"!"

text: Puħorno goƛ’in, neširu babiya žaqor eƛin, howži mi bełiƛ’ ik’ace oqäsi oqzaƛ’ di ɣˤaɣˤu izäsi yoł, hudun mi dey sis ʕaq’lu teqroƛin.
translation: In the evening the father called his son to his said and told him, "I am happy that you have grown to be able to go hunting, nevertheless listen to one advice!

text: Didurnokin žedus bok’ełxosi ħal boqnoanu neła boc’oqaqay”.
translation: However, they were not able to escape from that "trap".

text: Goƛ’in aħon, xanä nesiq esirno:
translation: The king called the shepherd and asked him:

text: Xedyos zulmun č’alʕizi boqno yedu esiwz kidbedäɣor yik’in.
translation: When she couldn't bear her husband's tyranny any longer, she went to her brother's daughter.

text: Nedur resunäy, hi enda sis lel, debeƛ’ ɣudi rayza quł ža mi yik’urƛin, yisiqorno eƛin, cey hawaƛ’ k’oƛin bik’in.
translation: "If that's the case, take this feather, and on a day that brings trouble upon you, burn it!" the eagle said to him and flew away.

text: Elzox idu ƛirba yoł, elor nexa resno, sanaʕałin anu elusƛin, xizor begirno užä nukarbi.
translation: "We have a guest at home, we don't have a possibility or a chance to come there," the boy said and sent the henchmen back.

text: Elo ʕaƛox sis beže cey zown.
translation: In the village there lived a big eagle.

text: Guluzaƛ’ zown žedu bik’iƛ’oräy, yizi q’ˤuya ħukmatyołer bayn.
translation: While they rode on their horses, they came to another country.

text: Barur xediw etix zownanu.
translation: The wife didn't love her husband.

text: ƛ’iri xabar boynč’ey bazarganzadetow sadaq oqno užin ik’in.
translation: Without further conversation, the boy went together with the merchants.

text: Q’ˤimez ʕoƛoz raʕalłixor aynosi, Ražbadiner eže žek’u keze oqno.
translation: When he came to the edge of his own village, Razhbadin met an old man.

text: Sisgon barun yown, riynoxu žin paraxat ʕumru boyx č’agu yołƛax.
translation: It's said that the "sheikh" married another wife and still leads a calm life now.

text: Wallaƛin, eƛin boc’ä, howža xanez bˤeƛ’eł zawru biƛurox aluk’ani bˤeƛ’q’ˤu yaqˤułer narƛa bok’ełnoƛin.
translation: "I swear," said the wolf, "the sheep with the white belly which was among the flock of sheep of that king there escaped somewhere today."

text: Hudun ʕArabuzanä žedu igorkin nexa begirno anu.
translation: Nevertheless, Arabuzan didn't even let them come close.

text: Sadaq ʕon rižäsi zowzaƛ’, yaqˤułgon šida mi at’iw qaca rayräƛin?”
translation: "Why did you bring wet firewood today again, although you carried the axe with you?"

text: Xanerno hemedurtow yizi biynanu.
translation: And the king didn't know them either.

text: Sida zamanałäza acmoq deqƛin.
translation: After a while, it knocked on the door.

text: Nedur resunäy hoko xizor butirƛin, eƛin xanä.
translation: "If that's the case, turn the carriage back!", said the king.

text: Ħayran boqno ƛexun bežet’ani esnabi, nay yedu deber gulubi riqäƛin esirno.
translation: The older brothers were astonished and asked: "From where did you get these horses?"

text: Nediwt’a reƛ’iqoybi elzox oč’ino yołƛin”.
translation: "We have nine of such gloves."

text: Ža goƛ’anix xizay žek’u egirƛin.
translation: Send someone to invite him!"

text: Yisä eƛin, yexuran yexurno di ža rečuƛ rekuyał šušisƛin, dibirä ʕantawni esiw ižin baru šaširuni moči bukarera.
translation: He said: "I have indeed killed your wife and burried her in the manure in the shed.", and the mullah led the stupid brother to show him the place where he burried his wife.

text: Xexbi rodinäy, mi äƛiru din rodanƛin, xediyan roži teƛno.
translation: The husband gave his word: "If we get children, I'll do what you say."

text: Xanłi koyär riqinosi, Kusa žedu łˤonon esnabi nesä neside ust’arbiłun xecin, žedus eɣeni esiw hič’č’a adäsi nesis ʕaq’lus žek’ułun odin.
translation: When Kusa had the kingship in his hands, he named the three brothers masters for himself and named the youngest brother his foremost advisor.

text: Nesä sadaq bazarganbin č’arik’no, c’idoxaɣor bik’inosi, elogon łäs kikno besun, bišʷan resun.
translation: He woke up all the merchants and they went to the fire, where they found a spring of water and food.

text: Howlaytow yizi q’orolader k’oƛin, hemediw xabar elur teqsiƛin esanix.
translation: Right then they ran to the widow to tell her: "We have heard such a story."

text: Zownƛax sis ažoƛ q’ˤida bičäsi ziru.
translation: Once there was a fox sitting under a tree.

text: Xexłi bodin däł xizay uži egirƛin, eženi žek’u nesä nesiz hunix ik’in.
translation: "Quickly, send your son after me!", [said] the old man and went on his way.

text: K’uruč’ k’eč’ ɣudi rocu, buq bay!
translation: All's well that ends well.

text: Beɣe xexzas guru teqnosi, zer rok’un regin, aɣˤʷa tełxorno yoxin, xedyoq esin.
translation: When she heard the crying of the small children, her heart shuddered, she ran into the mill and told her husband.

text: Xanä xizyo sisgon ħilla urɣizi bodin.
translation: The king afterwards thought of yet another trickery.

text: Sis di äƛiru rodinäy, mežur xexbi boda yołƛin, eƛin nesi eženi žek’a.
translation: "If you do what I say, you will get children.", said the old man.

text: Raładyazay xizor utin ayzaƛ’or, yisir ža idu yesun anu.
translation: When he came back from the sea, he didn't find her at home.

text: Nełƛ’ay soder łˤoran paraxat, rok’uɣʷeymoł ʕumru bižix zown.
translation: After that, the three of them lived together happily and calmly.

text: ʕUraw ɣˤaɣˤu bizin habihanno, barun, xexbi anusi žedur nazay eƛa biynč’ey žedu kurzaƛ’.
translation: The miller and his wife, not having children themselves, got very happy because they were thrown to them from who-knows-where.

text: Amma k’eč’oli hokomeł xizay baħnayx zown.
translation: But the foal was walking around behind the carriage.

text: Nazay di t’or ayäƛin?”
translation: "How did I came here?"

text: Ciqez aɣzas iyoq šeƛ’un rodin (tutirno), xizorno nexno xanqo rukarno, ele debez žas šeƛ’uƛin.
translation: They put (stained) the clothings with the blood of forest birds, came back and showed them to the king: "Behold, your son's clothings."

text: Bełiqanä cey idun bičirno sidagon anƛ’ima kikin.
translation: The hunter kept the eagle at home and fed it for another week.

text: Elaygon bitor bˤałno gulun ik’in, neširur sidaɣ ɣˤutkoxor ayn.
translation: From there he stroke his horse and went on and in the evening arrived at a house in some place.

text: Ža däde igor aynäy, nesir di roƛik’u xanłin teƛan, kidno yegirno mezałin nesił di risanƛin.
translation: If he comes to me, I will give him half my kingdom, marry off my daughter to him and I'll take him as my son-in-law."

text: Xizor yutin idur yaynosi, best’alawni kid ziya bicix zawruni hisona kecno yičin.
translation: The stepdaughter returned and while she came home, she waited sleeping in the trough the cow was tied to.

text: Howlotow žan bet’irno ža beqa kecxosi yoł.
translation: And there it will make a bed and is going to sleep under the sun.

text: Xanƛ’o reže ɣudi zown, roda šebin koƛ’inč’ey.
translation: It was a big hazzle for the king and he didn't know what to do.

text: Yišan, kecan gurow t’ok’ow q’ˤuya ħalt’i boda koƛ’ix zownanu yiła kidber.
translation: That girl didn't know how to do any other work besides to eat and to sleep.

text: Xizyo yiła yisiqor eƛin, däqkin šida mi esinč’u, xexbi royxosi ɣˤanabi nedur sisxoli xeca yayxosi anu.
translation: Then she said to him: "Why didn't you tell me? One musn't let a woman who gives birth to children so alone.

text: Sidä eƛin, yisä däqay moƛał ʕazar ɣuruš yoɣäsi zows, hoboy ža yisä xizor neƛxanuƛin.
translation: One said: "He took a thousand rubles away from me in my dream, now he doesn't give them back to me."

text: Aždaħ t’aʕizi bayru C’iɣruk’er c’ik’iwzä barkala teƛno.
translation: And everyone thanked Tsighruk who has destroyed the dragon.

text: Bazargan yołi ħaƛaniłer oqno.
translation: The merchant however began to drink.

text: Kid yet’un k’oƛin yičin, di łočyaƛ’ k’oƛixosi anuƛin.
translation: The girl jumped up and was like: "I don't dance!"

text: Elay maħor aqruntow, yisä zirus mus bik’urno.
translation: As he was outside, he burnt the fox's hair.

text: Yizi kecäsi yałzay, yizir ɣˤutkun di roys, yiziz ɣˤutkoxay, debez ɣˤutkoxor mesedyos ƛen di ersi.
translation: "While they were asleep, I built a house for them, and I set up a golden bridge from their house to your house."

text: Enda užin, baħarawn sidä sidde puħon bičirno, yiził roƛƛo ciqqay reč’no rown hečk’er ɣunno eqerno, ža ɣun ładäɣor rutix, nesix yedu baħarayn yegira yayƛin.
translation: That boy and the groom we let stand side to side, and in their middle we'll put upright a tree that we cut from the forest and brought here, to whomever the tree falls, he must be married to the bride.

text: Šebi yiłas micxir bäčiƛin, esirno xanä.
translation: "What will its price be?" the king asked.

text: Zownƛax sis c’odoraw xan.
translation: Once there was a wise king.

text: Hemełƛ’ay soder dey užin na eƛakin iynč’u.
translation: I don't even know where my son went to, after that.

text: Xander yik’in esin neła, sis magalu beɣe biłe boqno, deber näƛru roži daq t’ubazi roqinč’uƛin.
translation: She went to the king and told him: "One bread became a bit smaller, the word that I have given to you didn't fulfill."

text: Iłnon kawu serun, nasin ric’q’is ric’äsi resun.
translation: He had unlocked six gates and found all filled with wealth.

text: Miƛ’zo raqˤyołäy bešnayx zawru iyoł xizayn ik’in sidaɣ žopono ciqär ayn.
translation: He went behind the blood that was dripping out of the ram's wound and came into a dense forest, someplace.

text: Howlotow yedu babiƛ’ ƛ’iri yutin, ža ɣamas micxiryoƛ yisoƛin.
translation: Thereupon she insisted that her father buy this box for the money.

text: Esin šebi xecin šebi, zownƛax sis q’ˤanon halmaɣbi.
translation: Once upon a time there were two friends.

text: Tełtow ɣˤʷayn bik’un, kinaw ža bˤeƛ’es reqenno, ɣˤutkubin, maʕišatno zirurno, habihanerno ƛexun.
translation: The dog inside burned, and the entire flock of sheep, the house and the fortune all remained for the fox and the miller.

text: Žedus šudabi rukaranix ža maduhal yisä sadaq ižin.
translation: In order to show him their grave, he took the neighbor together with himself.

text: Nełas ža ƛ’irisi reƛno rac’no xizor ƛäxuru ƛuzabi yiła ža bicixozoni hisonä teł eqerno.
translation: They ate its meat, and then put the remaining bones inside the trough that she [the cow] was tied to.

text: Raładyoz raʕalłixorno ik’in eɣeni žoyä qˤaƛu rak’run, yoƛixno k’oƛin sis ɣˤanabi.
translation: And when the young boy went to the edge of the sea and shouted, a woman appeared and jumped out.

text: Elo neła łˤono k’eneč’ et’äsi zown.
translation: There it layed three eggs.

text: Neł ciqeł teł ik’iƛ’oräy, yeda yoƛƛo ciqa teł yałru baydanƛ’or ayn.
translation: While going through the forest, he came to a clearance that was in the middle of the forest.

text: Ele howda ʕalamałi yoł nełas.
translation: This is the reason.

text: Kid yoxin yik’in rikinä kecno yičin.
translation: The girl ran away and went to sleep in her cradle.

text: Xizaɣor ža ayaniqaysi rok’un roč’iłno, guluƛ’no zown iduɣor ik’in.
translation: Finally he lost hope that he would come, and rode home on the horse.

text: Kid madaxor yaynosi, iduɣor ža yoqno yok’ełzaƛ’or noci q’ˤimƛ’ay k’oƛin madax ƛexun.
translation: The girl went home and just before the girl entered the house the louse jumped down and remained near the door.

text: Encegon elo neside mušin begirno, ader ħumukulibin roɣno, hunix yizin boqno.
translation: They rested with him there for a little while and then began to drive on their camels and got on their way.

text: Roƛxo deyƛin, eƛin xanä.
translation: "My ___ is sick", said the king.

text: Yisis q’ˤano dandi ʕagaraw žek’u zown ƛexasi, babiws esiwn, nesis kid yisis yacʕalno.
translation: He had only two relatives remaining, the father's brother and his daughter, his cousin.

text: Sida quł q’ebedes uži istolowäɣor išanix ik’in.
translation: One day the smith's son went to eat in the dining room.

text: Nedur resunäy, hi hudu deberƛin mač’an exuran exurno, taxeƛ žan eqerno idu akäza yezun yičin.
translation: "If that's the case, take [this]!", and a dagger killed him; she put him under the ottoman and waited looking out of the window at her home.

text: Neła ɣˤutka teł sis q’orolayn, nełas kidno zown.
translation: Inside this house was a widow with her daughter.

text: Nełar neširuxay rukayx zown anu.
translation: It couldn't see anything in the night.

text: Nesir siskin šebin retixanu, debez mecqo mec bitirnäy ʕeze raqƛin eƛis nesäƛin, esin xexoyä.
translation: "He doesn't want anything, if he can touch your tongue with his tongue, it will be enough, he said.", explained her young.

text: Nełƛ’ay soder raq’ˤu saɣyatyabi nesä xanez kidber regirno.
translation: After that he sent many presents to the king's daughter.

text: Xizyo bečedawni žek’a naysinan xabar begirno, howziri ganbi reynodaniƛ’or kuczi radiru žek’ur di sis gani teƛa yołƛin.
translation: After that, the rich man spread the word, that he would give one bull to the person, who would be able to make these bulls work.

text: Eniwn, užin hemedur big yołƛin teqnosi, hemesi bečedawni maduhalä neširu rečuƛäy ža gulun bižin, nełaz meča q’ˤuya gulu bicin.
translation: That wealthy neighbor heard about good life of that mother and her son and took away that horse out of cattle shed in the evening and tied instead of it another one.

text: Gulun rečuƛ bicin, ža kidno q’ˤuya ɣˤutka teł yuqˤno, esnabi bayaniqor idu ezun ičäsi zown.
translation: He tied the horse into the shed, hid the girl in another room and waited at home for his brothers to come.

text: Waħ, šebi nesir roqnoƛin, Qartayä esirno.
translation: "Oh, what happened with it?", said Qartay.

text: Žan zown małiru risxanusi, t’ubaraw ʕaq’lus anusi iłe.
translation: And he was disobedient and had a defective mind.

text: Kidbä yik’in mat’izaxay oɣo žaƛin babiwqor eƛinosi, nesä inkar bodin, eläy oɣłin nełƛ’ay bigza mečoƛ’or di ža egärinč’iƛin.
translation: When the daughter went and told her father: "Let him away from the ducks!", he rejected and said: "I won't let him to a place better than this one."

text: Q’ʷaridno boqno ɣʷˤadä eƛin, wallahi, šomon ħon xecin bik’is di, hudun šebintow riqinč’ey nexƛ’oräy, sidaɣ simildis muhu besun howdagon boqłin bižanƛin bowƛ’oräy, adäzar osiw ħonno keze yoqno soder biziƛ’oräy biynč’ey bigin bok’ełsi.
translation: The crow became sad and said: "By God, I passed so many mountains; nevertheless while I didn't get anything, I found a grain of maize somewhere and [thinking:] "I will take it anyway!", so while I carried it here anyway, I encountered a high mountain, and while flying higher, I accidentally swallowed and [thus] lost it.

text: Nełƛ’ay bigu eynodanix moči där anuƛin, Alimaħama razi oqno.
translation: "There is no better place for me to work!", Alimagomed agreed.

text: Yeda nełaƛ’ razi oqinč’izaƛ’, baqru q’ut’imoxor ƛ’irigon yisir gulun, tupingon teƛno.
translation: Because he didn't accept her and according to the agreement that had been made, they gave him a horse and a gun.

text: Qacan č’ˤaƛin, c’in erno eƛin eženä Ħusenqor, howži mi magalu bodoƛin.
translation: "Split firewood, put on a fire," said the old man to Hussein, "and then make bread!"

text: Howži yedu yeynodaniƛ’or kuczi yoqsiƛin žas rok’un č’eze roqnosi, kidbes babiw goƛ’a žek’u egirno.
translation: Convinced that she now has learned to work, the boy sent someone to invite the girl's father.

text: Hemedur nesiq łˤono buci bik’in.
translation: And so three months passed for him.

text: Debi didiw yoł ža huduƛin?”, - esirno kidbä.
translation: "So how is this one of yours?", asked the girl.

text: Din begirƛin, rigu raq’ˤu esin zeyä.
translation: "Let me in as well!", the bear begged.

text: Akbar roɣno łäyrun, bayn sida ceyä iqirno ižin di maqˤi mečoƛ’ sida c’aq’ bercinaw baɣmar kursi.
translation: At the end of my call for the prayer, an eagle came and took me away to a far place and threw me into a very beautiful garden.

text: Däz kidbełäy ɣun roqno.
translation: "My daughter has turned into a tree."

text: Sidaɣ lalašin łi räk’iza mečoƛ’, xanes kid teł yałruni k’emot’ ɣˤanazaq yiqin.
translation: In one place, where the river spread out, the wooden chest with the princess inside got to a woman.

text: Yoƛosini, hič’č’an bercinawni xanes kid yołƛin, yukarno Ismaʕilä.
translation: "The middle one, the most beautiful daughter of the king.", Ismail showed here.

text: Yiłar roda šebinon łaħin, esnazał xizay k’oƛin.
translation: He left the things that were to be done by him and ran behind the brothers.

text: Užin koyä isno yisä nełaqay, narce yedu yäk’iƛin gama tełäy yoqa yegirno xecin.
translation: He took the son from her into his hand, [thinking] "Where could she go?" and let her get out of the boat's inside.

text: Ɣudes sasaqaza elorno rayn sis aluk’a sideni qˤaba miƛ’bi riħanayxosi yoł.
translation: Every day in the morning, one white and one black ram came and were fighting.

text: Buciƛ’ay baq’ˤu zaman bik’in yiziq ža łi k’edix, hudun resunanu.
translation: More than a month passed that they were searching that water, but nevertheless they didn't find it.

text: Xizazgon yisän qˤaƛin nełqor, beže taliħ boqsi debi nedur mi boxin bok’äłruƛin.
translation: From behind he shouted: "Your luck was big for having escaped!"

text: Sidaquł yiła esnazaqor eƛin:
translation: One day, she said to her brothers:

text: Ciqqor baynosi, užä ʕiyada bašla bodin.
translation: When they came to a forest, the boy began to cry.

text: Nesä nedur harizi royzaƛ’, sabru bodin bičin bełiqan ħalkaraw mečoƛ’aɣor ok’ełzaƛ’or.
translation: Because he asked, the snake waited patiently until the hunter went away to an even field.

text: Zeyä gugu c’eyƛ’oräy, enduräz biynč’ey bik’in zira xizaz c’i atinosi, zeyq regin:
translation: When the bear heated his back, the fox secretly approached and when he set him on fire, the bear said:

text: Žigon xan q’ʷariłiłer egin.
translation: Again, the king was sad.

text: Č’uħaraw šeƛ’un šeƛ’un iduza, madazax aħna-ik’ix yołƛax xan.
translation: The king is said to have gone outside in his gorgeous clothes, taking a walk.

text: Bisno bazarganä yisił adäza raħiräsi reƛis ħit’u eqerno.
translation: The merchant took a pan of cooked meat and put it before him.

text: Zownƛax zownanuƛax ɣudes bełiƛ’ aħnayxosi sis bełiqan.
translation: Once upon a time there was a hunter who went on a hunt every day.

text: Hemedurtow, łox äƛirugon egirno xediw raładyoz raʕalłixor.
translation: And so, for the third time, she sent her husband to the edge of the sea.

text: Bužzi odin yisä Ražbadin.
translation: He trusted Razhbadin.

text: Xizyo xanä nesiq esirno, räk’iru mužmarƛ’o mi ža esinč’uwa, hoboy łu ža debeq esäƛin.
translation: Then the king asked him: "You didn't tell this past Friday, now who told this to you?"

text: Yegirxanuƛin et’un k’oƛin ičäsi zown yeda.
translation: "I won't marry her off.", he jumped up.

text: Hudun neła kʷanin bok’no, taqˤorno boqno, kidbabi riqira risƛ’oräy, yoƛƛozoni esiya q’ˤidar muri kurno.
translation: Nevertheless, it swam across it and got there, and when it tried to catch the girls, the middle one threw the needle down.

text: Roda šebinno koƛ’inč’ey, ža xan c’aq’ q’ʷaridno oqno ičäsi zown.
translation: The king became very sad and didn't know what to do.

text: Howlay idurno ik’in, xanez užä eniwq esirno, dey aqiw esiw zowwäƛin.
translation: He went home and the prince asked his mother: "Did I have a sister?"

text: Yik’inƛax ža kikxor, elo yiłar ʕuraw biša resun.
translation: She went to the spring and found much food there.

text: Babiya eƛin, moƛ’oqˤoy anusi gulus baha łeno ʕazar ɣuruš yołƛin.
translation: The father said: "The price of the horse without the bridle is 5,000 rubles."

text: Bara ɣˤaɣˤun yizin esirno, rowwä bˤeƛ’ƛin?
translation: His wife got happy and asked: "Did you bring the sheep?"

text: Iduɣor bik’iƛ’oräy, buyxor mec esixoza hersiqanqor eƛin:
translation: When they went home, the one who always told the truth said to the liar:

text: Xizyogon žoyä ʕansan begirno xanes oz č’agun rodin, xande sadaq iduɣor begirno.
translation: Then the boy sent his walking stick, made the king's army alive again and sent them(?) home together with the king.

text: Zira yisiqor eƛin, dice di boc’aq bˤuƛ’äsi zowä, žedaƛ’aygon di debeq bˤuƛ’äsi zows, šida mi di howži begirno xecäƛin.
translation: The fox said to him, "I was so afraid of the wolves, and even more afraid of you than of them; why did you leave me here now?"

text: Yeženä eƛin, hemeła pulanaw mečoƛ’ yoł ža, elor ayrun, kakno rodo, łox nełas ci qˤaƛo.
translation: The old woman said: "She lives at that certain place, when you arrive there, say a prayer and shout her name three times!"

text: Nełaƛ’ zown yizi bik’in sida šaharyar bayn.
translation: Riding on it, they went on and came to a city.

text: Ismaʕilqo oc’ino biƛno ƛˤeb yaynosi, muʕalimä xanqo riyrno, ƛuza teł yołäsi reƛ täƛno nesirƛin.
translation: When Ismail became eighteen years old, the teacher explained to the king: "Do not give him meat that contains bones!"

text: Xalq’in, ʕaƛno bukaynosi, aždaħ nełaq ža biqinč’ey neła nełaz iduɣor, ciqaɣor, boxin.
translation: When the dragon saw the people and the village, it ran back to its own home, towards the forest.

text: Cimyarno bizin neła eƛin, dey eniw yexaru tušman didur mi t’aɣor nexłäƛin.
translation: He, who got furiated, said: "How could you who killed my mother come here?!"

text: Sidaɣ rut’uga quqirxo bazargan esun.
translation: In some place, he found a merchant who was drying a carpet.

text: Žan riyn nesiłtow xizay Ox̌oyon k’oƛin.
translation: When he knew it, Ohoyo ran behind him.

text: Ža nesä eƛinosi yeda igtow lamus oqxo, qazaqä yisir bodixosi ħurmat bukayzaƛ’.
translation: When he [Tavadi] said that, he [Razhbadin] became very ashamed, because he saw the respect that the Georgian showed him.

text: Žagon šebiƛin, zira eƛin.
translation: "How's that?", said the fox.

text: Howlotow yiła ƛ’iräy uži oɣno, dey ʕayib boqsi idu yičin howžär nełqor yutin yezunč’ey yičizaƛ’.
translation: Then she apologized to the boy: "It was my fault, because I didn't pay attention on her again, since she has been living with us, until now."

text: Bukayxa eloš bitay qala?
translation: Do you see the fortress there in the distance?

text: Didur eli bišan, bäšru k’et’ukin bexusƛin eƛin xanqor Beqes ʕUneyzatä.
translation: "How could we eat, if even the cat who ate died?", said Sun's Uneyzat to the king.

text: Sis zown zirun zeyn.
translation: Once there was a fox and a bear.

text: Hudayzikotow maħlarno ik’in siryoz bet’erhanä xanez bara äƛiruxor qˤaƛin.
translation: The next day, the mare's master went to the court and shouted what the king's wife told him.

text: ʕOmoyä łinax riyx yedu elur ħažat boqaniƛin, halmaɣłiqorno eƛin, neła nełaz gugyoƛ’orno bizirerno, elay bitor bižin.
translation: The donkey said to his friends: "How does one know [how] it can become useful for us.", had [the wolf] lifted on his back and carried it further.

text: Xan ħayran oqno ezun ƛexun.
translation: The king remained looking surprised.

text: Xexłiƛ’ eƛin ɣˤʷaygon yałru qaciz guluƛ’or butirno.
translation: He quickly said [something] and what has been a dog transformed back into the wooden horse.

text: Xanqay roƛik’u bečełin risno, doxturä sider sis nece betixosi kidno, užin sadaq begirno.
translation: If they loved each other so much, the doctor took the half of the khan's riches and sent the boy and the girl together.

text: Xander ik’a adäza, babiya užiqor eƛin, howži roda šebin anu, dey uži, mi eƛo xanqor mesedyos bic’no q’ili neƛnäy, guluzaz reqenyołäy sis bigu gulu łet’an egirnäy, di neła kidbeł xizay ik’anƛin.
translation: Before going to the king, the father said to the son, "Now there's nothing one can do, my son, say to the king, 'If you give me a qili filled with gold and if you let me pick a good horse from the herd, I will go behind that girl'!"

text: Eniya aħozi iqäru deber.
translation: your mother has found a shepherd for you.

text: Šebi mežas maxšel yołƛin, xanä yizaq esirno.
translation: "What are your skills?", asked the king.

text: Xizyo xanä kidbeq esirnosi, t’akanyoł xizay howda wazir idur ayäƛin, ža wazir aynč’u, enda aysƛin, ganƛ’o zowali uži ukarno.
translation: Then, when the king asked his daughter: "Did this vizier come home for the glass?", [she said:] "This vizier didn't come, he came!" and showed him the boy on the bull calf.

text: Neła ƛˤeba q’ˤuyazis raq’ˤu bˤeƛ’ rexun, netin yegirxanuzoce zigolun, isin yegirno.
translation: In this year, many sheep of other people died, as it hailed and snowed as if it had never snowed before.

text: Xizaɣor eɣeni esiwr riyn, esnabi bik’ixosi moči.
translation: In the end the younger brother knew the place his brothers were going to.

text: Xizyo nełar uži odin.
translation: Afterwards, a boy was born to her.

text: Aluk’anin, qˤabanin q’ˤanon gulubi rečuƛ ruqˤno xecinƛax.
translation: The white and the black horses he left hidden in the cattleshed.

text: Esin šebi, xecin šebi zownƛax ʕoƛno bełiqan.
translation: Was has been told, what has been left, it's said that there were seven hunters.

text: Ražbadinä nazoƛ’no rayrno Tawadä nesiq regäru saɣyatyabi.
translation: Razhbadin brought to everyone the presents Tavadi has sent him.

text: Sis beže hibon bisno ʕAt’id aw bayace ezun ičin.
translation: Atid took a big stick and waited until the mouse came.

text: Raładno bigtow halag boqno, yeda ƛ’iri zawruni gama butix zownƛax.
translation: The sea ran high and the boat began to sink.

text: Žoyä babiwq raqru šebin esin:
translation: The boy told the father what had happened:

text: Nełaqorno aħya kurinč’ey, yeda oqno ħonƛ’aɣor ik’in.
translation: Not paying attention to them, he went onto the mountain.

text: Hemedur howloza rˤałayzał xexbi yałzay, eɣeni esiwr keroqazay nexxo aždaħ bukayn.
translation: While the children were there on the harvest like that, the young brother saw a dragon coming to them through the ravine.

text: Q’ˤim rak’za mečoƛ’ reže aki roqno.
translation: On the place where she hit her head, a big window appeared.

text: Neła imadał yoƛäk’ru ɣˤanabin di yołƛin, neła nełaƛ’ay aħozis k’uk boɣno.
translation: And I am the woman who appeared in this tale.", [she said] and took away the shepherd's cap.

text: Neł ʕaƛasi žamaʕattow yisir q’ʷariłiƛ’ bičäsi besun, ɣudes boc’ä, zeyza rexuräsit’a posu resuzaƛ’.
translation: The population of the village was being sad because every day they found their cattle killed by wolves and bears.

text: Žedus harimoxor ixiw qeman, zigolun regirno.
translation: And on their request, much rain and hail was sent.

text: Nełƛ’ay soder xanez baɣma ɣunoq boqxosini mesedyos heneš łukin bok’ek’xo zown anu.
translation: After that nobody stole the golden apples growing in the khan's garden.

text: Howła reƛił ɣˤʷays maħ yołƛin”.
translation: "This meat smells like dog."

text: Kidbaza łˤono pu yołäsi šebinno rayrno eƛin, deber rätiru šebinno radi yiła, ätizarno ayärƛin.
translation: The girls brought the things to their three sides and said: "This will do what you want and it will bring your where you want."

text: Žedułtow xizay iłegon ʕAt’idno oqno.
translation: And also Atid went behind them.

text: Ža igor yayƛ’oräy, awä äƛiruxor, meš kurnosi nełał adäz žopono ciq roƛixno.
translation: When she came near, he - as the mouse had said - threw the broom and before her appeared a dense forest.

text: Dey ʕarza nesä bissi.
translation: He dealt with my complaint.

text: Idur aynosi, Maħama nesistow ʕoloqanawni kidbeqor eƛin, žaqˤuł ʕaƛaɣor nexƛ’oräy, där Aħmad keze oqsi.
translation: When he came home, Magomed said to his young daughter: "Today, when I came into the village, I met Akhmed.

text: Hemedurtow meɣyoqorno eƛin, xanzoq riłäsi mada ɣun roƛik’no.
translation: So he said to the udder, and let a tree appear in the yard that resembled the king's one.

text: Howži xanä neła aɣiqayn bik boɣno.
translation: Now the king took away the hide from that bird as well.

text: Xanez užä žeduq šinaw raqru łinałäyn esin, xizyo žeduq neła t’awuses lel yukarno.
translation: The khan's son told them what had happened and showed the feather of that peacock.

text: Xizyo Kusa xanez kidbeden kecno, qˤašiƛ’aɣor kid moƛaq yiqirnosi, elayn izin ɣamasya tełerno oqno, tełäzgon žan ħišin, maq’anyabi rižixosi pelun yexurno.
translation: Afterwards, when Kusa had slept with the king's daughter, and the girl had stopped sleeping, he got up from there and went into the box, locked it from inside and destroyed the pipes which played the melodies.

text: Sida quł ciqzaq naysinan aħna-ik’in, bełin biqinč’ey yeda xizor utin nexxo zown.
translation: One day he walked through the forests everywhere for a while, and not getting any prey, was coming back.

text: ʕAƛos ʕoloqbi nesiƛ’ bužzi boqno.
translation: The village people trusted him.

text: Eƛni yayƛ’oräy, ža sida quł neła nełar ɣˤutku rodanix halmaɣłin k’eyn maħor boqno.
translation: When the winter was coming, he went outside and looked for friends to build a house for him.

text: Boc’ezno, qˤayzno, ziruznogon žedaƛ’or ixiwtow žanawar bays bäsuƛin rok’ƛ’orno rayn sidqo sis riqinč’ey k’iriłno roxin.
translation: The wolf, the hare and the fox thought that an animal, larger than themselves, was coming, they ran away, one running faster than the other.

text: Dibirä nesiq esirno, dey baru yukaynč’a deberƛin.
translation: The mullah asked him: "You haven't seen my wife, have you?"

text: Nukarza Ismaʕil ciqaɣorno ižin, exuran k’ʷeze roqinč’ey, ozurabin roɣno yizä ža howlotow ɣunoqor icin xecin.
translation: The henchmen carried Ismail into the forest, but couldn't kill him; so they pricked out his eyes, bound him to a tree and left him there.

text: Rok’ƛ’o anuzaytow, yayn eƛni c’oxno, yegirno isi, rizin roč’i.
translation: Suddenly, the winter came and sent snow, and frost came.

text: Qˤirič’yogon k’emot’ya teł zawru nasin šeƛ’u t’it’i-reč’no.
translation: But the pair of scissors cut all the clothes that were inside the wardrobe into pieces.

text: Nesä tupi caƛiƛ’oräy, čanmo bˤulamed aħya ħaħxo zownƛin”.
translation: When he threw the rifle, the goat was scratching its ear with a hoof."

text: K’uruč’ k’eč’ ɣudi rocu, buq bay.
translation: All's well that ends well.

text: Howži Ħon-Bahadurä k’oƛin ik’in hič’č’a bercinawni kidbes lelno yisno, ɣunoł xizaza uqˤełno ičin.
translation: Now Mountain~Hero ran and took the feathers of the most beautiful girl and ran back to hide behind the tree.

text: Yisi doxturqor taliħƛin sis q’ˤir koyiƛer riqin, nełaq t’it’aħinno ičin karačelyoza yeda lilyoxaɣor kurno.
translation: Luckily, the doctor got to clutch the board and waves threw him on shore.

text: Ražbadinzo halmaɣza ʕaƛos kidbabi sidaɣ mečoƛ’orno rutirno pˤaƛux zurma, bok’xo q’ili, k’oƛix łočyaƛ’.
translation: Razhbadin's friends gathered the village's girls in one place somewhere, thrashed the zurna, beated the drum and danced.

text: Där t’o baħarčiyaw žek’u k’ačaɣˤ iyxƛin”.
translation: "I know that the bandit is the brave man here."

text: Aždaħä raɣru ixiw qˤaƛuma, C’iɣruk’ łaymoƛ’ay iža dahaw šebin xecin.
translation: The shout that the dragon emitted distracted Tsighruk a little bit.

text: Sida quł bˤeƛ’łer boc’i k’oƛinosi, nełał xizayn k’oƛin nełaz di xarcelin caƛin žirbał šiširsi.
translation: One day a wolf ran into the flock of sheep, so I threw my scythe and made it stuck inside its ribs.

text: Hemedur yizi elo sasaqor bičin.
translation: So they stayed there until the morning.

text: Aħa igorno ayn esirno, šebi roqäsi šebin yoł, łinax mi zigaryayxƛin.
translation: The shepherd came closer and asked: "What happened? Why are you complaining?"

text: Nełƛ’osi yayn sida q’orolayä eƛin, ʕoƛira ɣudi uži meža sida ħala xec.
translation: At that time came a widow and said: "Leave the boy alone for seven days!

text: Bełiqa eƛin, däz madax yedu nediw ɣˤutku där roqa räti, didur eƛa riynč’ey howt’or rayza čantaƛ’osi iƛ’u yoɣełno yik’isƛin.
translation: The hunter said: "I would like such a house to be in my yard, but accidentally somehow the pocket's lid opened here where I came to and went away."

text: Zownƛax, zownanuƛax sis bečedaw žek’u.
translation: Once upon a time there was a rich man.

text: Deber riyxa ža huduƛin, esirno babiya kidbeq.
translation: "So you know it?", the father asked his daughter.

text: Dahowtow łi žeda elo teł xecin.
translation: They only left little water inside.

text: Šebi deber retix, šebi deber neƛanƛin, nazon xalq’imo yeda šet’u utirno, mi äƛiru šebin ela t’ubazi rodanƛin, koyä isxo yeda cuƛert’a kurxo zown, hemece ɣˤaɣˤu bizizaq.
translation: "What do you want? What shall we give you?", all the people gathered around him: "We will fulfill what you say."; and they took him into their hands and threw him upward [several times], because they got so happy.

text: Zira eƛin: rigu huni yałza sadaq rik’an, žuka huni yałza reƛ’ixno bisno di boɣanƛin.”
translation: The fox said: "We will go together where there's a good road, and I'll take you by the hand on a bad way"

text: Yedugon łinarey bˤoƛ’us?
translation: "And why the heck has this fallen down?!

text: Žedułäy sis zown netintow buyxor mec esixosi, sidenä heresi gurow esix zown anu.
translation: One of them was always telling the truth, the other one told nothing but lies.

text: Neširaɣor xizyo yiłaq ʕaƛoxor dahaw taraw posu raynanu.
translation: After it got evening, only some of the cattle came back to the village with her.

text: Elaɣor bik’anix ħadur boqƛ’orey, xedyoƛ’or aħin bayn, askarno boɣno ƛˤoreł ik’in.
translation: While they prepared themselves to go there, the alarm came unto the husband, he took the army and went to war.

text: Zira eƛin:
translation: The fox said:

text: Neła quł sasaq esnaza Umukusno šušin, nełas šeƛ’u šudaƛ’ ƛ’iri eqerno.
translation: The next morning, the brothers burried Umukus and put her clothes onto the grave

text: Nedur resunäy, ža tełxor goƛ’oƛin, eƛin beženi esiya.
translation: "If that's the case, invite him inside!", said the older brother.

text: Howži šebitow radi, šebitow räs diƛin, rok’ƛ’or pikrun bayn ža uži dunyalqo ik’in.
translation: "What shall I do now, what shall I take?", so the boy got the idea to go through the world.

text: Žan risno iduza ezu-ʕoƛ’uƛ’oräy, yisir bečedawni žek’us uži esun.
translation: Taking it into the house, he looked around and found the rich man's son.

text: Howži hudu xan oqno Ismaʕiles barubi q’ˤuyzix regiranix.
translation: Now the king began to send Ismail's wives to others.
