text: Sis xexoy yiłar kurnäy, q’ˤano där ƛäxuƛin, rok’ƛ’or pikrun bayn, dicego zaħmat roqłin, yiła zirur xexoy kurno.
translation: "If I throw him down one nestling, I will still keep two", thought the bird and although it was quite difficult, it threw a nestling to the fox...

text: Yałzatow rekan eqerno, taxƛ’o kecno ičin.
translation: He put the key where it was and slept on the sofa.

text: Boƛƛo sis zaman bik’inosi, xan roƛa utirno.
translation: After a while, the king became sick.

text: Neł zamanał nesä ʕuraw huni xecin.
translation: At this time, he already went quite far.

text: Kidbä eƛin, nay yedu debi biča bayx, däz koyä heqäsi bukayxa.
translation: The girl said: "From where must it be yours, it is sitting on my hand."

text: Neła eƛin, mi nazay t’or ayä.
translation: He said: "How did you came here?

text: Kidbezgon rok’ƛ’or rayn, di moƛałtow yołƛin, yedu t’o roqxosi šebinon där moƛał rukayxƛin.
translation: The girl thought: "I am in a dream, and what is happening here I am dreaming."

text: Bayru q’ut’oxor, Ħasanä rełimay bˤałayn roɣno aždaħzo rok’nosi, boƛƛi begirno.
translation: According to the agreement they did, Hassan took his dagger out of the sheath and stabbed it into the dragon and broke him apart in the middle.

text: Däxor bičinč’ey, qˤalmoƛ din uqˤo, xexłiƛ’ mežin xizor butiƛin.
translation: Don't wait for me, hide me under the brushwood and go back quickly!"

text: Zamanaƛ’aza elor äƛiruxortow q’ˤano miƛ’in bayn, riħadanix riħun.
translation: After some time, two said rams came there and started to fight.

text: C’irduxƛ’o ozuri c’axrun, sis qˤaƛun rok’no Qartay ɣunoq yiza c’oxno, žaqˤułgon narey mi däqay ok’łanƛin.
translation: Just when Qartay discovered Tsirdukh, she gave out a shout, pounced to/up the tree, [saying]: "Where will you escape to from me today?"

text: Xizyo žedu nasin Allahqo, qema regirƛin, harizi roqno.
translation: Afterwards they all asked God: "Let it rain!"

text: Mi där quluq bodinäy, žeduqay bałgo sis šebin esanƛin”.
translation: If you look after me, I will tell you a secret that the others don't know."

text: Sasaq yeda xanez ozyode sadaq qalamäɣor ik’in.
translation: The next day, he went to the fortress together with the king's army.

text: Madaxosini žek’un goƛ’in, xanä nesiq esirno, yedu däz gulus maħi łu reč’äƛin.
translation: The king called the guard and asked him: "Who has cut off the tail of this horse of mine?"

text: Raładyoz lilyox c’eruc’an resus, howža łoħron xizaz nexsiƛin, žawab teƛno žoyä babiwr.
translation: "I found the bow at the sea shore, [and] that frog came behind me.", the son answered the father.

text: Ža čanya nar eƛa biynč’ey reqenyołäy gugixt’a zown.
translation: The she.goat didn't know what to do anymore and escaped from the herd.

text: Nesän hudayziko sasaqtow izin gulun ħadur bodin xalmoƛ’or ičin.
translation: He got up the next morning, prepared a horse and examined.

text: Eɣeni esiya eƛin, horo igorno ik’in esiran nesiq di, šebi debeƛ’ necetow q’ʷariłi yołƛin.
translation: The younger brother said: "Come, I'll go to him and will ask what's wrong with him."

text: Zamanałäza nesił dandir bazarganbi c’oxno hisos urgubi q’urec’niƛ’no erno rižin bik’ix.
translation: After a while, he met some merchants carrying sacks with wool on a mule.

text: Mi yik’äsi zows yołi, elotow łemu ƛäxuƛin, žawab teƛno ɣarabašä.
translation: "If you had gone, you would have stayed there.", answered the servant.

text: Ražbadinä ik’in Tawadiqor eƛin, šebi deber roqä, šida mi neł rok’uɣʷeymołäy oxä.
translation: Razhbadin went and said to Tavadi: "What happened to you? Why did you run away from that banquet?

text: Howži Bilq’isdä nesiq esirno, howła xizaɣorzo zamanał mi łarnokin žukłi rodäsi zowwäƛin.
translation: Now Bilqisdi asked him: "Have you done anything evil to anyone lately?"

text: Qˤaba yoqƛin žoyä eƛinosi, kidbä adäzar kočori bayrno, aluk’a yoqƛingon eƛinosi, adäzay kočori butirno xizor kurno, ele yoqsiƛin.
translation: When the boy said: "Get black!", the girl brought her hair ahead, and when he said: "Get white!", he turned her hair away from the front and threw it on her back[, saying]: "Here, I got."

text: Howžäy soder łˤora becider xanez kidbedäɣor ezäɣor di iziłinč’inäy, nesä dey meč’ beč’xosiƛin.
translation: In three months after now, if I won't get up into the fortress to the king's daughter, he is going to cut off my neck."

text: Łäs q’ut’un bic’no xizor utin nexƛ’oräy, xizaz aždaħä qˤaƛin, t’ok’ow näxno mi łi ric’a, nagaħ nexnäy, mi di č’aguyawtow axaɣor igin kura yołƛin.
translation: He filled the jug with water and while he was coming back, the dragon shouted from behind: "Don't you come to fill [it with] water anymore! If you come, I am going to throw you into my belly and swallow you alive!"

text: Tełer ayrun yisä sida reƛ’id nełas kočori biqirno, sidenigon reƛ’id hibon bisno žek’no yedu, neła mi dey uži yołƛin eƛace.
translation: Immediately after going inside, he took her hair with one hand and took a stick with the other hand and hit her until she said: "You're my son!"

text: Žigoƛ’aygon mi eli baƛ’ärinč’iƛin, esnaza aħo qaciz ruk’uzaqorno icin iħuq egirno.
translation: "You're not going to cheat us again!", the brothers [said,] tied the shepherd to the bundles of wood and let him into the river.

text: Idur ža aynosi, šida mi meč’oxor akan begirno exux zowäƛin, babiwz esiya esirnosi, nesä eƛin, där babiya bodäsi wasi zows keze raqza ɣˤutkubi rodoƛin...
translation: When he came home and his uncle asked: "Why have you been dying, having a noose around your neck?", he answered: "My father stated in his testament, that I have to make houses where I get to..."

text: Elaɣor bik’iƛ’oräy meža dey kiki xiräsi boqzaƛ’, mežus di eniw yoqxo, hudu meža eniya äƛiru rodoƛin.
translation: When you go there, I become your mother, because you will have sucked on my breast, so do what your mother says!"

text: Užä inkar badiruce ža yežigon, neƛ henešƛin, ƛ’iri yutin.
translation: The more the boy rejected, the more she insisted on "Give me the apple!"

text: Užä eƛin, baba, elur caxinč’iru šebin riqa zaħmataw ričixosi yoł.
translation: The boy said: "Father, it is difficult to get a thing that is not destined for us."

text: Sida quł däder q’arza bˤeƛ’ roɣoƛin Allah ays.
translation: One day God came to me: "Take the duty for the sheep away!"

text: Mi eƛo, ža čanta taraw, q’ˤuya šebin där retinanu, q’ˤuya sayɣat di bäsinč’iƛin.
translation: You have to say: 'I don't want any other thing than this pocket, another present I won't take.'"

text: Elo igoza bäsuru yizir ɣˤays oreznar k’oƛinosi, gulun saɣ boqno, adäzoƛ’aygon rigu rexora maħin roƛixno.
translation: There the jumped into a milky lake that they had found nearby, and the horse gut cured; its tail appeared, prettier and longer than before.

text: Xizyo t’umi maqiz muhuzaƛ’or butin, onočaƛ’orgon Okun utin.
translation: Then, the pigeon turned into barley grains and Oku turned into a hen.

text: Sidaquł xanä idurno goƛ’in budumqor eƛin:
translation: One day, the king called the muezzin to his home and said:

text: Elo yizi q’ˤunän rok’uɣʷeymoł bičin sasaqor ɣudi qˤaširno.
translation: There the two had fun until the next morning.

text: Neła q’orolays zownƛax sis nełƛ’aygon siħiraw yacʕal.
translation: That widow, so it's said, had a (female) cousin who was more cunning than her.

text: Režigon rok’u q’ʷarid roqno yisis.
translation: He became even more sad...

text: Nełaƛ’osi ža ħal bukayn, yeda bełiqan tupin risno t’omceni žek’u ayace iqˤin ičin.
translation: The hunter saw the condition upon her, took his rifle and waited and watched until the span-sized man came.

text: Were hoboy mi, yedu bˤeƛ’es reqenno bižin, t’ay xizor ik’iƛin, nesä nesis šeƛ’un šeƛ’urno uži egirno.
translation: "Well, alright, you, lead my flock of sheep and go away!", he [said and] dressed the boy in his [own] clothings and sent him away.

text: Žigoƛ’aygon hemedurtow aɣzas xabar teqno yisir.
translation: Once again he heard the birds speaking:

text: Xizyo yizi bazarganza yisił roƛƛo risno, neširu debin barusno c’ok’ini roqnäy, debi yałru maʕišat elur biča yoł, bercingo bara mi iduɣor egirnäy, ele enza łˤoran q’urec’niƛ’osi q’ay ħalalgotow deber biča yołƛin.
translation: Then the merchants decided among themselves: "In the evening, if your wife makes a scandal, then your fortune will be ours, and when a good wife lets you into the home, all the articles on these three mules shall be yours."

text: Ži payda anuƛin, žan dunyalqo ik’in.
translation: "Now, that was useless!", he said and also went through the world.

text: Xediw adäzay ok’äłrun, bara kušin ža ɣˤutku rosyoƛ’orno rayrno, ec’no ɣˤutku rodanix bašla bodin ustarbin begirno.
translation: When her husband had gone away from her, the wife demolished that house and brought it down to the base, and she let the experts begin to build a new house.

text: Esin šebi, xecin šebi, sidaquł ʕomoyn bižin ciqaɣor qaciƛ’orƛin ʕoloqan žek’u ik’äsi zown.
translation: What should I tell, what should I leave; once upon a time a young man went with a donkey into the forest to collect firewood.

text: Šibaw ruzman ɣudƛ’o nesä nesiz ulkas nasin ahli sadaqor butirxo zown.
translation: Every Friday he gathered together all the people of his country.

text: Xan aynosi, doxtur išinč’ey ičin.
translation: As the khan came, the doctor refused to eat.

text: Hemeł mixƛ’o, yoƛi gogan č’ˤaƛiłno, ozuri buqin baɣˤizaƛ’or, Okun, užin wˤałzoni dunyalƛ’aɣor bok’ełno.
translation: After that, the middle of the meadow opened, and in the wink of an eye, Oku and his son had gone into the underworld.

text: Yedu mat’i di ciqez xaner bižix, sis miƛ’in bown min horoƛin”.
translation: "I am carrying this duck to the king of the forest, go get a ram!"

text: Walla xan, di ša bodanix k’udi q’uq’iƛ’orey, elo šet’u butäsi hadambi boqzaƛ’, aqˤu kuča maħorno oqa iynč’ey howlo tełer kučis.
translation: "By God, o king, while I was pressing out the grapes to make the wine, there were people who were walking around there and without me knowing took a piss in there."

text: Nesä nesiƛ’ yałru q’ʷariłi esin nełaq.
translation: He told her of the sadness that was on him.

text: Ɣˤutkoxor rik’in, acno yaɣˤin tełer rezunosi, elo žedar yeɣe kid yesun.
translation: They went to the house and opened the door, and when they looked inside, they found a young girl there.

text: Lamusyoq esinč’eyn ičin, xizyo nesän esin, sida bercinaw ɣˤanazar di rek’ bodäsi zows, däx horoƛin di eƛinosi, dey xediw yołƛin neła där roži neƛinč’izaƛ’.
translation: He was too ashamed to tell, but then he told: "I played a trick on one beautiful woman, because she said she already had a husband and didn't give me her word when I asked her to marry me.

text: Nełaƛ’ yeda užin razi oqno.
translation: On that also the boy agreed.

text: Elay bitor yizi łenon sadaq hunix boqno.
translation: The five of them went on their way together, from there.

text: Yisägon roɣno mač’an tełäza aždaħno t’it’i-beč’no, axaza egin maħorno oqno nełas q’ˤim ret’urno kurno.
translation: He took out his dagger and cut the dragon into pieces from inside, broke him apart from out of the belly, got outside and chopped off its head.

text: Kid neside igor yayrun, užä nełqay ħošos šeƛ’un roɣno mesedyos bašiqˤoy ernosi, adäyzoƛ’aygon bercinaw ža užir yukayn.
translation: When the girl came to him, the boy took off her hide-clothes and put the golden ring on her, and the boy saw that she was much more beautiful than before.

text: Bilq’isdiƛ’or šakno bižirerno xanes nesi madaxozoni žek’ä, xizyo yeda qˤirič’ k’edaniłer oqno.
translation: The guard let the king suspect Bilqisdi and then he began to search for the scissors.

text: Yizä xal bodinosi, bazarganzo čanta teł iyos ric’äsi, raɣˤäsitow t’aqˤ resunosi dandi yizi bužzi boqno.
translation: They did an examination, found the blood-stained, open knife in the merchant's pocket and now believed them.

text: Teł iduɣorno oqno, esiyqor nesä eƛin, ačqˤaq quqix di, ħaƛanix łi neƛ därƛin.
translation: He went inside into the home and said to his sister: "I am drying out because of thirst, give me some water to drink!"

text: Šebey roqsiƛin, q’orolayä šudaƛ’orno yik’in mamalayz k’onč’azaqay šeƛ’u roɣxo zown.
translation: "What happened?", the widow said and went to the grave and took the clothings from the rooster's feet.

text: Sisini łäs ric’alini sikno roƛik’no, žeduqor ħaƛa teƛno.
translation: He pulled out a skin filled with water and gave it to them to drink.

text: Ža bix yałza mečoƛ’or aynosi, rexorani bixiz ɣˤuč’ä eƛix teqno, yisi žek’us ʕantłi rukayxana mežur, barus ʕadlun boqinč’ey, nesä nesił xizaz rexʷadanix ħonƛ’ay bˤeƛ’no rown nexxoƛin.
translation: When he came to the place where the grass was, he heard the longest blade of grass say: "Don't you see the ... of this man? He can't control his wife, but brings sheep down from the mountain to slaughter them after himself."

text: Däz beženi ɣˤʷačulis madaxortow ɣˤʷačni teqzaƛ’ tełxorkin yik’inč’uƛin.
translation: Because I heard the chewing of my old goat at the door, I didn't go inside.

text: Xizyo diwanbegä ħukmun beč’no, žeduq riyrno, Bilq’isdin hečker yičirno, ža cucixace eč’ibi kuro nełaƛ’orƛin.
translation: Then the judge made a decision and explained to them: "Let her stand up and throw pebbles onto her until she is burried under them."

text: Łaymoƛ’or aynosi, Ismaʕil nełde puħor k’oƛin.
translation: When Ismail came to, he run to her side.

text: Rˤałayn rodin, at’no beqa ħolimoƛ’ roɣno, Alimaħama čardaħä q’ˤidan ičin ezun ičäsi zown.
translation: Having done the threshing, Alimagomed put the wheat on a mat in the sun, sat down on a balcony and was looking around.

text: Xaner ža šebi yałru iyn anu.
translation: The khan did not recognise him.

text: Neł q’ʷariłiƛ’ cek’i bukaynosi, ʕomoyä eƛin, iħäy łäs yic’no muš yownäy, begiranƛin.
translation: When the donkey saw the sad goat kid, he said: "If you bring a pot filled with water from the river, I'll let you in."

text: Howlotow yisä ceyes lel yik’urno.
translation: There he burned the eagle's feather.

text: Kid ʕuraw ɣˤaɣˤu yizin.
translation: The girl got very happy.

text: Łaxtow di nexanƛin?”.
translation: To whom shall I come?"

text: Xexoyzas eniya šebi deber retinƛin žaq esirnosi, p’es anusi ičin.
translation: When the chicks' mother asked the boy: "What do you want?", he remained silent.

text: Sis ħon xecin, q’ano ħon xecin, łˤono ħon xecin yeda q’ˤuya ħukmatyołxor ok’ełno.
translation: He left one mountain behind himself, left two mountains behind himself, left three mountains behind himself and came into another country.

text: Xanä kid yegiranƛin nesił q’ut’in boyn, ža uži kid yuyxor yutiranix egirno.
translation: The king agreed with him to give him his daughter, and the boy began to turn her back into a real girl.

text: Oqnäyƛin, eƛin qˤayz xexoyä, di eniw goƛ’an bik’an, deber rätiru łinałäyn di nełaq esanƛin.
translation: "If that's the case," said the rabbit's young, "I will go to call mother and you will tell her about what you want."

text: Xizyo zeyä neła nełas ozurabi roɣno rac’no.
translation: Then the bear took out his own eyes and ate them.

text: Nełas siskin urɣel boynanu bat’iyaw sisxolizoni q’orolaz užä.
translation: The son of the other widow who was alone, did not have any sorrow.

text: Debez eniwq baqru hunarä däq boqxanuƛin, aždaħä žedur miƛ’i bexayn.
translation: "Your mother's talent isn't mine", the dragon said and slaughtered a ram for them.

text: Suƛƛi sider sis bukayrun yizis roƛ’i boqxo.
translation: Immediately when they saw each other, they fell in love.

text: Nełaq ža heneš bukarnosi, esirno žaq:
translation: When he showed her this apple, she asked the boy:

text: Mežus halmaɣłi rexuzaƛ’or eli bexun biguƛin.
translation: Before your friendship dies, rather we would die!"

text: Q’ˤogce bix eqersiyołi, q’ˤida äčiza kokoru qˤiƛci roqxosiƛin.
translation: If one had put a bale of hay to where one sits, it would become a soft pillow.

text: Mekoq bexux yałru k’et’u sosi bik’in miƛ’eƛ’ c’oxno, xizyogon isƛ’o łeɣin.
translation: The starving cat first went and pounced on to the ram, then clinged to the bull.

text: Bik’i hudu cin cedin horoƛin” - eƛin zeyä.
translation: "So go, give him a name and come back!", said the bear.

text: Howlaytow bitor adäz bikorin, xizaz kidno hunix boqno bikorzo iduɣorƛin bik’in.
translation: From then on, the snake went ahead with the daughter following it, until it came to the snake's house.

text: Dey zikru netikin łiyxosianu.
translation: "My recitation never ends.

text: Nesis užin, kidno zownoƛax.
translation: It's said that he had a son and a daughter.

text: Xizayn k’oƛin ʕoloqan žek’a elaɣor reƛ’a regirnosi, beč’ułno elazay micxirno, mesedno bayn.
translation: When the young man ran behind him and put his hand into there, silver and gold came out of there.

text: Yiłƛ’ay rigu čorpa netikin riqäsi zownč’uƛin, neła quł neširu eniyan užän rigut’a ax rodin.
translation: They have never had a such delicious soup before, so that evening the mother and the son ate good.

text: Yeda uži žeduł teł rok’u q’uq’ix ik’a retinč’ey ik’ix zown.
translation: Pulling himself together the boy went with them against his will.

text: Buħi baqruntow yizi mešibi iduɣor raxiƛin, žawab boyn aħa žedur.
translation: "Just when it gets hot, these calves will run home", the shepherd answered them.

text: Elay bitor ok’ełnosi, boɣno ziyas ħošin kurno, oqno iduɣor ik’in.
translation: When he had escaped from there, he threw the cow's hide away and went home.

text: Šebitowey meža där rodanƛin pikrun ban, zey qoqoƛix bičäsi zewn.
translation: "What are you going to do to me anyway?", thought the bear and waited laughing.

text: Neside sadaqosin nesä łiboƛ’ beɣurxo zown, hemediw q’uwataw uži oqxo zown ža.
translation: He defeated all his peers in wrestling, and became such a strong boy.

text: Sida quł yizi hunix boqno sidaɣ yeže gogaƛ’or baynƛax.
translation: One day, they went on their way and came to a big meadow somewhere.

text: Žigoƛ’ay nexan debex di mi där howda pandur neƛnäyƛin”.
translation: "Now I will come to you, if you give me that lute of yours."

text: Xizyo eniya užiqor eƛix, bik’uranircetow micxir elzox anuƛin.
translation: Afterwards the mother said to her son: "There is no money here to be burnt."

text: Bik’in yizä xanesno nesi nukaresno baqru xabar q’ˤunan esnazaq esin.
translation: They went away and told the story that happened between the king and that henchman to the two sisters.

text: Hemełƛ’o bayn adäz qˤays xexoyn bičin yisiqor eƛin, mi siskin pašman aqno, yaqˤuł mi elus tušman bexursi, hoboy mi däz eniwdäɣor däde sadaq ƛa.
translation: Then came a rabbit's young and sat down in front of him and said to him: "Don't become sad! Today you have killed our enemy; now come with me to my mother!

text: Sidaquł yizi q’ˤanon bełiƛ’ bik’in.
translation: One day the two of them went hunting.

text: Žeduq bišʷa łäyrułer, mamalayƛ’no zown howžatow t’omceni žek’u ayn.
translation: When they finished the food, that very span-sized man came riding on the rooster again.

text: Nesä yisiqor eƛin, enda ɣun reč’oƛin.
translation: He said to him: "Chop down that tree!"

text: Biƛira putcegon kedonno yisno, yedan et’un guluƛ’or k’oƛin.
translation: He also took a sledgehammer of 8 pood and jumped onto his horse.

text: Idur aynosi, nesi užä kidbeq esin, hemedur xanzo łiboqande c’oxranix łiboqan elur žigoƛ’ay etixƛin.
translation: When he came home, the boy told the girl: "And this time we want a wrestler in order to fight with the king's wrestler."

text: Sidaquł yisä nesiz xanłiƛ’osi nasin ʕoloqbi sidaɣor butirno, yołä mežuł teł yiła guluƛ’ zowanix bažari yołäsi žek’uƛin esirnosi, nasin p’es anusi wˤałer q’ˤimbin kurno bičin.
translation: One day he let all young men of his own kingdom come together and asked who among them would be the one that had the skill to ride that horse, all were silent and kept their heads down.

text: Zownƛax, zownanuƛax sis dunyalƛ’otow ci rik’äsi doxtur.
translation: Once upon a time there was a worldwide famed doctor.

text: Eɣʷeni žoyä esin, łinas ħal yisiƛ’ yałrułi.
translation: The young boy told why he was in trouble.

text: Hunixor oqnosi, yisis k’onč’u yoƛa yoqno, t’ok’ow čaran bexun, elay bitor ik’iłnoanu yeda.
translation: After he went on their way, his leg began to hurt and he could not go on anymore.

text: Bisno yaraɣno, guluƛ’no zown yeda buq boƛäxzaɣor ik’iƛ’orey, ayn sida beže ʕaƛar.
translation: He took the weapon, sat on the horse and when he rode towards the sunrise, he came into a big village

text: Xizyo ɣˤanazas šeƛ’un šeƛ’un, nesidäɣorno yik’in, sasaqor sadaq ɣudi qˤaširno.
translation: Afterwards she put on woman's clothings, went to him and they stayed together until in the morning the day dawned.

text: Neła esirno, šebi deber retinƛin.
translation: She asked: "Whad did you want?"

text: Yisi bełiqanän ƛoħay boɣno sis ħak’on nełdergon met’rin teƛno.
translation: And the hunter also took one khinkali out of the pan and put a piece of meat next to it.

text: Maħor boqanƛin zawru yedu, gondaz beginč’ey howlotow ƛexun.
translation: He wanted to get out, couldn't get through the hole and stayed there.

text: Nedur resunäy, deber yätiruni kidbeł xizäy bik’an eliƛin, encetow k’ek’-yik’no yedu guluƛ’or yutin.
translation: "If that's the case, we will go behind the girl you want", she moved a little and then turned into a horse.

text: Zamanaƛ’azay bak’arizin boqno ʕagarłisno, ʕaƛosno ʕoloqbin bayn, reƛ’ibin ricin bexuranixƛin bižiƛ’oräy užä eƛin, mežur šebintow riynč’ey šida eli bexura bižix.
translation: After some time, the young persons from the relatives and the village came together, bandaged their hands and while they took them out to kill them, the boy said: "Why are you taking us out to kill us without knowing anything?!

text: Neła quł užiq siskin besuron biqinanu, hudu haq’un quqa ƛexun.
translation: On that day, the boy didn't get any fish, so the family remained hungry.

text: Užin, kidnogon sida sidƛ’or bezun ƛexun, žedur žedu betin.
translation: The boy and the girl were looking at each other and fell in love.

text: Q’ʷˤaɣˤƛin eƛin bitor bezunosi nełaz haqar reƛ kurxo zowno, q’ʷˤaɣˤƛin eƛin ninor bezunosi iyos zok’i et’xo zowno nesä nełaz haqa.
translation: When the eagle screaked and looked to the one side, Aliqilich threw the meat into the eagle's mouth; when he screaked and looked to the other side, he poured the mug with the blood into his mouth.

text: Šudaƛ’ yeda ʕiyayx ukayzaƛ’ maduhaler iyn ža žedus eɣeni esiw yałrułi.
translation: Because he saw him crying on the grave, the neighbor knew that he was their younger brother.

text: Sidä riłe nazon eƛin, eluƛ’ ci sadaq’a yołƛin.
translation: And as one they said: "Our name is Alms!"

text: Debi di č’iħotow rac’äsi anuƛin, žawab teƛno žoyä.
translation: "Your fruits I haven't eaten.", answered the boy.

text: Däzoq riłäsi mada ɣun roƛik’oƛƛin, xanä eniw sosigon madaƛäytow xizor yutirno.
translation: "May there appear a tree in my yard that resembles mine!", the king sent the mother back from his court again.

text: Zamanaƛ’äza xan saɣ oqnoƛax.
translation: After some time the king got cured.

text: Hemeła qułtow mogolxo elo aɣˤuxor zeyn, zirun, boc’in rayn.
translation: The next day, a bear, a fox and a wolf came there to the mill at the granary.

text: Xizyo nesi eženä boƛik’no xedyoqor q’ˤano heneš teƛno, sis nesir, sideni baruq bac’roƛinno eƛin, ozƛäy gugin.
translation: Then that old man pulled out two apples and gave them to the husband, one for him; "Give your wife the other one to eat!", he said and disappeared from his eyes.

text: Xanez kidber ža maq’anyabi teqnosi, akäzayn yoƛixno yezun, yedu šebi šebin eloƛin.
translation: After the king's daughter heard these melodies, she appeared at the window and looked out, wondering: "What is this thing there?"

text: Nełƛ’osi kʷaxatow xan exun.
translation: The king died very soon, then.

text: Howži yiła eƛin, didur di hoboy łaƛ’or yik’iƛ’oräy esiws žanaza bukaynosi, ʕiyaynč’ey yik’anƛin.
translation: Now she said: "How, while I'm going to fetch water, am I going to go without crying when I see my brother's corpse?"

text: Yisä ža q’orola xander yegirno, ža aždaħ bexursiƛin esanix.
translation: He sent the widow to the king to tell him that the dragon was killed.

text: Łˤora äƛirugon ɣudƛ’o goƛ’in babiya nesiqor eƛin, mi dey q’ˤano masala t’ubazi boys.
translation: On the third day the father invited him and said to him: "You fulfilled two of my tasks.

text: Xizaɣor ʕomoyn mešin gurow reynoynanu.
translation: Finally, only the donkey and the calf were working.

text: Idur yaynosi, xedyoz eniya, qidoq tungi bok’oƛin yiłqor eƛinosi, bok’no neła qidoq.
translation: After she came home, the husband's mother said to her: "Strike the jug against the wall." and she struck it against the wall.

text: Xanä wazires meč’no beč’no, t’akan nesiqor yayäruni žax, ixiw bertenno bodin, kidno yegirno.
translation: The king cut off the vizier's head, arranged a big wedding and gave his daughter to the boy who brought him his glass.

text: Bara eƛin, žedu č’agu boynäy, dowƛ’orno, däƛ’ornotow žeda sis balah bayär, xec žedu sida ħalaƛin.
translation: The wife said: "If we revive them, they will bring misfortune unto both you and me; leave them in their condition!"

text: Hudayziko, boc’no adäza ziyan xediw bazaryaɣor ik’in.
translation: The next day the husband chased the cow to the market.

text: Baq’ˤu urɣizi boqxo bičinč’ey, ɣʷˤadä q’ˤʷa-q’ˤʷa-q’ˤʷaƛin haraƛ’ bayrun, haqoƛäy reƛ q’ˤidar rˤaƛ’un.
translation: Without thinking much, the crow let its voice caw and the piece of meat fell down out of his mouth.

text: Uži howt’o waħozo raƛ’moƛ’ łäz kikƛ’o aq’ˤułno ičäsi zown.
translation: Our son was covering a spring of water in the land below here.

text: Ražbadinä eƛix, dey halmaɣ, di nexäsi yoł debez kidbes reƛ’an esirnoƛin.
translation: Razhbadin said: "My friend, I've come to ask for your daughters hand."

text: Na uqˤłäsi zowäƛin, esirno babiya.
translation: "Where did you hide?" the father asked.

text: Sidaƛ’or rutirno žan babiw ʕaƛaɣor ik’in.
translation: The father gathered everything together and went into the village.

text: Baq’ˤu mix bik’izaƛ’ortow babiwn exun nesis, xizaygon eniwn yexun, sisxoli uži ƛexun.
translation: Before a long time had passed, his father died and afterwards his mother died, and the son remained alone.

text: Sida zamanałäy, eženi esiya eɣeni esiw zeru łek’ira egirno.
translation: After a while, the eldest brother sent the youngest brother to stir the bouza.

text: Sosi däde puħor oc’ino q’ˤimakun yayr, q’ˤano äƛiru sidä sidde igot’a q’ˤano šudbin rič’o, łˤono äƛirugon där rigu ɣˤutkun rodoƛin.
translation: "First, bring ten women to my side! Second, dig two graves, next to each other! And third, build me a good house!"

text: Sida quł maħorno oqno uži nesicetow sida žaƛ’no iħanayn, yisiq nesis mecu q’ˤažun.
translation: One day the boy went outside and had a fight with a peer, and he dislocated his hand.

text: Mi äƛiru roži t’ubazi rodinč’uƛin, xanä yeženi esiw xizor yoxirno.
translation: "You didn't keep your word!" said the king and chased the oldest sister back.

text: ʕAƛos xalq’in nełaƛ’ ʕuraw razi boqno.
translation: The village people were very pleased with her.

text: Eženi esiya eƛin, ɣudes neširuxay eluz baɣmay sist’a č’iħomes ɣun rok’ek’äsit’a resux, łu rok’ek’xosin elur riyxanu.
translation: The old brother said: "Every night, there seems to be someone who steals one fruit tree from our garden, and we don't know who it is."

text: Yisägon bisno iduɣorno bižin, aɣzaqozoni bak’imar begirno.
translation: He took it, carried it home and put it into the hen coop.

text: Bikorä žawab bodin, maħor baqinč’iƛin t’otow bičin bok’ełxosi ħal yoł dey.
translation: The snake answered: "I won't come outside, I have the opportunity to live here."

text: ʕOƛiran ɣudi baɣʷˤacet’a rac’ace ʕeze roqno k’et’ur boc’esno, zirusno nełar ƛäxuruni dawla.
translation: And for seven days, there was enough of the prey that remained from the wolf and the fox, left for the cat to get sated.

text: Q’ˤanosi bazargan žeda žeduz hunixno bik’in, sideni ħumukuliƛ’ zowrno uži ʕaƛar ayra nexno.
translation: Two merchants went on their way, the other one seated the boy onto the camel and brought him into the village.

text: ʕOƛira ɣudƛ’aza Ismaʕilesno ričin, nesir darsi eranix muʕalimno ayn.
translation: After seven days, Ismail got well, and the teacher came to teach him again.

text: Akiƛer aynosi, ʕUneyzatƛin qˤaƛin, roži roɣnoanu.
translation: When they came under the window, he shouted "Uneyzat!", but there was no response.

text: Xabar anuzo hut’er, k’al yäsruce kiri bäči.
translation: "The silent mouth will get the reward, just like in the fast.

text: Ayruntow yisä nełas kiki xirno.
translation: Just when he came, he sucked at her breast.

text: Q’ˤunaäƛiru ɣudƛ’o miƛ’i bayrno boc’in, isno gugyoq caƛin zeyn ziruz ɣˤutkoxorno rayn, maħlaytow qˤaƛin:
translation: On the second day, the wolf came to the fox's house bringing a ram and the bear came with a bull thrown over his back, a loud voice shouted from the backyard:

text: Yisä esin, xanä nesił adäza äru masalałäy.
translation: He told about the task the king had set up before him.

text: Acno serun tełxor ik’in.
translation: He unlocked the door and went inside.

text: Di aždaħ bexursiƛin meži daƛ’ bužzi boqxo besunč’inäy, nełał mežus daʕban besunäy, bik’in aždaħes q’ˤim rowo xanqo rukaranixƛin”.
translation: "If you don't believe me that I killed the dragon and you are having a dispute on that, go and get the dragon's head to show it to the king!"

text: Žeduł xizay iłetowgon q’ˤuya šeƛ’un šeƛ’un, aluk’a guluƛ’no zown eɣeni esiw maħor oqno.
translation: But behind them rode the younger brother, dressed in different clothings on the white horse.

text: Žan eƛin, babiw gogaƛ’ay oqno bitor ik’in, kidbabin babiw ayaniqor rezun howlotow ričäsi zown.
translation: Having said that, the father left the meadow and went away, and the girls waited there for their father to return.

text: Žedu łˤorän esnaza sida kidbeƛ’ reƛ’a eräsi zown.
translation: Those three brothers were proposed to the same girl.

text: Oƛƛozoni esiya eƛin:
translation: The middle brother said:

text: Nesir didiw zaral žeda badirun biyxanu.
translation: Neither does one know what harm they did to him.

text: Ħaširu acmoqosi rekan čantaɣor eqerno, yiła imadu esa bašla bodin:
translation: Having put the key to the locked door into the pocket, she began telling the tale:

text: Boƛƛo baq’ˤu zaman bik’izaƛ’ortow, elo best’alawni kidbede igor sis žek’un ayn nesä eƛin, aki raɣˤoƛin.
translation: Before much time had passed, a man came to the stepdaughter and he said: "Open the window!"

text: Eleley, esiwƛin, eƛin yiła, din kumakyor anusi yedu nece reže ɣˤutku didur debeq rodiłä, dikin šida moƛaxay č’arik’inč’uƛin.
translation: "Wow, brother!", said she, "Without my help you have built such a big house; why didn't you wake me up from my sleep?"

text: Guluƛ’no zown, tupin ħirar kurno eženi esiw bełiƛ’ oqno.
translation: The older brother sat on his horse, threw the rifle on his shoulder and went hunting.

text: Reže nesiz ħurmatyoxaysi rok’uɣʷeyn rodin, sida zamanaƛ’aza Ražbadin haq’uzaƛ’ hulin, žeduƛ’ ezu-ʕoƛ’a ʕaƛaɣor ik’a retin.
translation: They organized the big banquet because of his respect, and at some point in time, Razhbadin missed his family and wanted to go to the village to visit them.

text: Sida zamanałäza raƛ’tow šešerxo eƛix bayn aždaħ:
translation: After a while, the earth shook when the dragon came and said:

text: C’iɣruk’ yołi iħuƛ’ taqˤorgon oqno ok’łäsi zown.
translation: Tsighruk, however, crossed a river and escaped.

text: Reɣeni xaličan, beɣeni magalun bäqiruni nukarqo, łi ƛäxurułi riyn yizir.
translation: They knew that the henchman who got the small mat and the small bread still had water remaining.

text: ʕOƛnon esnabi haqa bogun ƛexun esirno, nece ixiw yedu deber bˤeƛ’es reqen nay biqäƛin.
translation: The seven brothers remained gaping and asked: "Where did you get this large flock of sheep from?!"

text: Naysinan nesider bayx zown: beɣen, bežen, miskinawnin bečedawnin.
translation: All people came to him: young and old, poor and wealthy.

text: Qartayder aynosi, neła nesiqay bayan bisno, mi šebi, łina hunaryos žek’u yołƛin.
translation: When he came to Qartay, she wanted to know from him: "Who are you and what talent do you have?"

text: Qacis q’ili Qudi Musaz Maħama bok’si, ħošos zurma Xantuz ʕAlä pˤaƛus, keč’ Maʕat’iz Ruq’än, Amirħanzan qˤaƛis, istowlizaq šet’ur łočyoƛ’ Xaluman Ošk’oris Ramazanno k’oƛis, ɣodos ʕarada Metirozya pˤoƛirsi, dabaɣyos tupi K’it’i Ramazanä kursi, meys tamanča Qebozya ašuƛ ricis, Ešiƛ’oza łelmay butnos mač’ibi roɣsi, Newoza bexada qacis t’eka bayrsi, Kidiraygon ʕomoyƛ’no zown Šapk’ilaw ays.
translation: Qudi Musa's Magomed beated the wooden drum, Xantu's Ali played the zurna, Ma'ati's Ruqi and Amirhanza sang a song, Khaluma and Oshkori's Ramazan danced around the tables, people from Metrada fired a lime gun, Kiti Ramazan shot with a leather rifle, the Khebatli people put tied a birch pistol to their belt, people from Shaytl pulled burka daggers out of the sheath, the Mokok people slaughtered brought a wooden goat for slaughtering, and Shapkilaw came from Kidero riding on a donkey.

text: Nełan hemedurtow harizi rodin žedaq, din bižo sadaqƛin.
translation: And he asked them likewise: "Carry me with you!"

text: Hudun mi ya micxir bäsno, ya mesed bäsno, ya q’ˤuya bečełi räsnoƛin.
translation: Nevertheless, don't take the money, don't take the gold and don't take any other riches!

text: Neła kidbes ʕUmarqor šuliyaw roƛ’i boqno.
translation: That girl fell deeply in love with Umar.

text: Žan bižin yeda iduɣor ik’in.
translation: So he brought it home.

text: Maħor yaqrun yedu raładyołer k’oƛin yičin.
translation: As soon as she came outside, she jumped into the sea.

text: Babiya užizaqor eƛin, exunosi meža di azbaryan šušo, ƛ’irigon ciyon ƛešoƛin.
translation: The father said to his sons: "When [I] die, bury me in the palace, and pour salt on it!"

text: Ezuzaƛ’ oč’ino q’ˤim yołäsi aždaħ ƛ’irir nexxoƛax.
translation: He looked: A nine-headed dragon was coming to him.

text: Sidä muhubi ħolimoƛ’äytow rac’xo zown, sideni aɣä q’ˤidar räšru rut’ix zown.
translation: One ate the granules directly from the mat, the other bird collected those that had fallen down.

text: Mi ža bazaryaɣorno bižin łera ʕazara ɣˤurušyoƛ teƛ, moƛ’oqˤoy yołi łeno bišom ɣˤuruš neƛłikin täƛno, dey ruħ nełał bičixosi yoł, nagaħ mi ža teƛnäy, di exuxosi yołƛin.
translation: Lead it to the market and sell it for 5,000 rubles, the bridle however don't sell even if you were given 500 rubles, my soul will be inside; if you sell it, I will die."

text: Boɣno žeduqay bigu gulun, nełaƛ’no zown buq boƛäxza puƛ’aɣor ik’in.
translation: He took the good horse from them, and rode on it towards the sunrise.

text: Sosisi egirxanuƛin babiw ƛ’iri utin, xizyo necetow nesir retizaƛ’, egirno xecin.
translation: At first the father insisted in not letting him go, but then - because he wanted it so much - he let him leave.

text: Xanes q’ˤanoqu ʕaq’lu boyxosi žek’u zown.
translation: The king had 40 advisors.

text: Xexłiƛ’ yiła howlaytow ħaƛanix esiwr łäs zok’i ric’no teƛno.
translation: Quickly she then filled a mug with water for the brother to drink and gave it to him.

text: Nełƛ’ay soder halmaɣza sida sidƛ’or kaɣatyabin caxnoanu, sidä sidderno bik’inanu.
translation: After that, the friends didn't write any letters to each other and didn't visit each other anymore.

text: Ac ħišäsi zownč’us yołi mi eli rac’as łemuƛin, tełäzay ʕomoyä žawab teƛno.
translation: "If the door wasn't locked, you would eat us!", answered a donkey from inside.

text: Yiła łinasno payda anu därƛin pikrun boyn, tungin bisno yeda ʕAliqiličtow łäƛ’or ik’in.
translation: "I have no use for that", thought Aliqilich, took the jug and went to get water.

text: Di elor ayzaƛ’, ža ʕuraw ɣˤaɣˤun izin, nełquł sasaqtow nesä di eženi wazirłun xecis.
translation: Because I came, he got very happy and the next morning he made me the grand vizier.

text: Xanä eƛin:
translation: The king said:

text: Xizaɣor Ħusen ežin, ža ik’inƛax nesi eženił xizay, yaraɣno bisno guluƛ’no zown.
translation: Finally Hussein won [he will go, it's said???], he took a weapon, sat on a horse and followed the old one.

text: Eleley, dice bercinaw, rigu šeƛ’u rižixƛin, bezun nasin xalq’in ƛexun.
translation: "Behold, what beautiful and pretty clothes are being carried away!", all the people remained looking.

text: Q’ˤida raƛ’moƛ’or yizi baynosi, ceyä eƛin, däqay deber t’ok’ow kumak bäqinč’i, mi xexłiƛ’ iduɣor ik’iƛin.
translation: When they came down to earth again, the eagle said: "You will not get help from me anymore, go home quickly!"

text: Žigon šebi roqnoƛin, užä bayan bisnosi, neła kinaw raqru šebin esin.
translation: When the boy asked: "What happened?" again, she told all the things that happened.

text: Mesedyos saħno bižin ža uži nełqay roži riqira ik’in.
translation: Carrying the Sah of gold, the boy went to take her word (hand).

text: Nełaƛ’ ci Umukusƛin zown.
translation: Her name was Umukus.

text: Hoboy di šebi rodanƛin?”, - eƛin nesä bet’urno aluk’anigon heneš bac’nosi, yeda yałru insanƛ’or utin.
translation: What shall I do now?", he said, plucked a white apple this time, ate it and turned into the human that he was.

text: Di ža keč’oz reƛ’id aɣˤuroƛ’ eqersi, kut’yoz reƛ’id q’ˤidar yoɣsi, deber retinäy, iduɣor yižo ža, retinäy - däx yegir žaƛin, eƛin nesä xanez užiqor.
translation: I've put her on the wheel with my left hand, I took her down with my right hand, if you want, carry her home; if you want, marry her off to me!", he said to the prince.

text: ƛa huduƛin, ʕomoy mamalayn sadaq boɣno hunix boqno.
translation: "So let's go!", and the donkey took the rooster and went on the way.

text: Yeženi ɣˤanaza eƛin, mi berten łiyace yičinč’ey xexłiƛ’ idurno nexno, hisona teł šeƛ’un eqro, min nexno idu yičiƛin.
translation: The old woman said: "Before the marriage ends, come home quickly, put the clothes into the trough, get home and wait."

text: Šida mi yaqˤułgon at’iw qaca rayräƛin onoču c’ok’inayzaƛ’, mamalayä di kecno ƛexunƛint’a žawab teƛxo zown.
translation: When the hen scolded: "Why have you brought wet firewood again, today?", the rooster answered that he slept in.

text: Yedun teƛno där šeƛ’u risa retinƛin, eƛin yisä.
translation: "I want to sell it and buy clothes.", he said.

text: Žoyä babiya łinał xizay egärułi esin.
translation: The boy told what the father has sent him for.

text: Elus šeƛ’ures bic’äsi gama raładyoł boƛor bayrułer tełxor biħusƛin, žawab teƛno žeda nesir.
translation: "The boat filled with our clothes sank when it came to the middle of the sea.", they answered him.

text: Žedu rätiruxor riqira k’ʷeze roqxosi šebinabi yołƛin.
translation: "These are things that one can get wherever one wants."

text: Ozuri buqin bˤaɣˤizaƛ’or žedu neł kidbederno baynosi, yeženi esiyagon nesä bisno zawruni č’edosni q’ut’un łäs bic’no, kidbez haqan et’no, ža saɣ yodin.
translation: Before the wink of an eye, they arrived at the girl's; the elder brother filled the clay jug he bought with water, poured it into her mouth and cured her.

text: Babiw kodyox ik’is, eniw neside dandir ʕiyada yik’isƛin”.
translation: "Father went to an evening gathering, mother went to cry for him at the meeting."

text: Šebi deber ela roda retixƛin?”
translation: What do you want us to do?"

text: Ziruq ža eƛin rok’äłrun, nełał xizay ɣˤʷay k’oƛin.
translation: Right after the fox had said that, the dog came running behind it.

text: Din ik’ixƛin, eƛin Ismaʕil izin xanderno ik’in, nukarłun di ukarƛin nesiq harizi rodin.
translation: "I will go as well!", said Ismail, went to the king and asked him: "Appoint me a henchman!"

text: Elaz egin oqanƛin zawru yeda iħułxor ʕoƛ’un ik’in besuroz haqar keze oqno.
translation: He was about to go that way, but fell into the river and into the jaws of a fish.

text: Zowsƛin”, eƛin žek’a.
translation: "It was", said the man.

text: Hemedur yizi yałzay sida bečedaw žek’a bertenyołxor goƛ’ixƛin yiziƛ’or xabar bayrno, hemełatowgon zamanał xediw goƛ’in ƛˤoreł ižin.
translation: While they were living like that, the story came that a wealthy man invited to his marriage, and at this time, her husband was called to war.

text: Nełƛ’osi baq’ˤu zaman bik’izaƛ’ortow žedus babiw exun.
translation: Before much time had passed after that, their father died.

text: Žedu qunyox bičix zown.
translation: They were living on a farm.

text: Užä eƛäsi zows moƛ’oqˤoy sadaq täƛnoƛin.
translation: My son said: 'Don't sell the bridle [together [{with me}]!'

text: "Deyn hemesi pulanaw etixozosno q’ut’i boqäsi zowsƛin:
translation: 'I have a lover's agreement, which went like this:

text: Neła kidbä rok’un roɣno, yeda doxtur t’ok’ow roda šebin koƛ’inč’ey ƛexun.
translation: That girl took away the doctor's heart and he did not understand anymore what was left for him to do.

text: Howži sisgon berten bok’a bayƛin, eƛin xanä.
translation: "Now we must celebrate one more wedding," the khan said.

text: Babiw išƛ’oräy, kidbä nesił adäz mesedyos yic’äsi t’akan eqerno.
translation: While the father was eating, his daughter put the glass full of gold in front of him.

text: Zira eƛix zown, ħurženyabin, k’ukno där keze roqxoƛin.
translation: The fox was saying: "The bags and the cap belong to me!"

text: Hemedur c’odorłiƛ’aza q’ˤanon esnabi tambiħyoƛäy bok’ełno.
translation: And so the two brothers escaped from punishment, due to their cleverness.

text: Elo sida azbarya teł yisir žek’u esun, ixiw ƛoħon c’idoƛ’ erno, simildi raħirxo.
translation: There, inside a palace, he found a man who put a big pan onto the fire and cooked corn.

text: Bełiqanä roč k’ek’erłin, yizä bayru q’ut’oxor, ža izirnoanu.
translation: When the hunter moved the rope, they - according to the agreement they come upon - didn't lift him up.

text: Yeda rigzaxay t’ay äk’inč’iƛin, boc’ä harizi rodin, rigza ħalał qema regirƛin.
translation: [Thinking] "He won't get away from there anyway," the wolf requested: "Let it rain heavily!"

text: Elaz ik’iƛ’oräy, yisir ɣˤanazas roži teqno, daƛ’ gurħizi oqxosi žek’utow ana t’ozaƛin.
translation: While he was going there, he heard the words of a woman: "Isn't there a man here who pities me?"

text: Teł idu yiłar riguƛ’ay rigu bišadyos ric’äsi istowli resun.
translation: Inside the house, she found a table set with the best food.

text: Xizaɣor asoƛxor bezunosi, yizir yeda ceyez gugyoƛ’no zown, asoł teł ʕuqłäsi ukayn.
translation: Finally, when they looked into the sky, they saw him hiding in the sky, riding on the eagle's back.

text: Sida quł q’ˤanon esnabi urɣizi boqno xanez iday micxir bok’ek’anƛin.
translation: One day the two brothers thought: "We'll steal money from the king's home!"

text: Ħonƛ’or baynosi, sida beƛ’q’ˤa sasaqor oc’ino ʕazar ƛeli bodin.
translation: When they arrived on the mountain, one sheep gave birth to ten thousand lambs in the morning.

text: Dicegon žeda rek’ boyłin, yisis žedux rok’u rexun.
translation: No matter how much they played tricks, he felt sorry for them.

text: Ža maħor oqƛ’oräy, nesiłtow xizay yoƛixno Baxumesedagon ʕAliqiličqor č’ikay teƛno, reč’no nełas q’ˤim č’ikayä rezurinč’inäy, nełas ruħ baɣinč’iƛin.
translation: When Aliqilich got outside, Bakhumesedo appeared behind him and gave him a mirror, saying: "When you cut his head off and don't show it to himself in the mirror, you won't take away his power."

text: Neła baydanƛ’or aynosi, miƛ’zo iyos ruɣubin łiyn, šet’u utiƛ’oräy, yeda waħozoni dunyalƛ’orno ˤoƛ’un, sida ɣˤutkoz ƛ’ˤuƛ’ ƛ’iri c’oxno.
translation: After he came to that plain, the ram's blood spots ended; when he turned around, he fell onto the earth/world below, and on the roof of a house.

text: Xizyo baɣmaɣorno ik’in, ɣunoƛ waħo q’ˤida ičin zown ičäsi, Allahä sis sabaw yoynč’i diƛin.
translation: Afterwards he went to the garden and sat down thinking under the tree , "The God will not disregard me."

text: Hemedur q’ˤunan ozurazaƛ’ayn ok’ek’no iguni halmaɣ žukanä, howlotow žan xecin oxin nesidäy.
translation: So the bad one had stolen the two eyes from the the good friend, left him there and ran away from him.

text: Maharno erno, beže bertenno bodin, ža kidno bełiquz užä yižin.
translation: The wedding was set up and the hunter's son married that girl.

text: Ixiw qˤaƛun rok’no, łinasnotown čaran bexun, aždaħ xizor butin.
translation: The dragon emitted a loud shout, he had no possibilities and returned.

text: Łˤora äƛirugon ɣudƛ’o neširu babiya eɣeni uži baɣmoxor egirno.
translation: On the third day the father sent his youngest son to the garden.

text: Elo waħo gibu bukayx zownanu.
translation: He didn't see the sand below, there.

text: Nece žuka ħalał mi esunäy, hi dowqor enda musno, debeƛ’ ɣudi rayza quł bisno bik’ur ža miƛin.
translation: "If you're in such a trouble, take this hair from me, and on the day when it's worst for you, burn it!"

text: Kut’yoƛ’asi nełas k’onč’u šiqˤono yołƛin”.
translation: Its right foot is lame.

text: Di muši pˤaƛunosi, mi nociƛ’or ati, xizyo mi kidbez kočorał teł uqˤeł, ža iduɣor yik’iƛ’oräy, mi q’ˤidar k’oƛo, hemełƛ’o mi di nociƛ’ay užiƛ’or utiran.
translation: "When I blow, you will turn into the louse. Afterwards you will hide youself inside the girl's forelock. Before she go home, jump down and I will turn you back to the boy."

text: Nesiqosi šeƛ’u łiyän rižin, ža howlotow adoru ƛexasi dowdäɣor nexa oqinč’ey.
translation: And the water carried his clothes away, he remained naked there and could not come to you.

text: Hisonax ža bäciza mečoƛ’ sasaqort’a mesed besux zown.
translation: In the morning they found gold in the trough, where it was tied.

text: Harihun maʕišat bodix bäčiru yizirabi, sida quł xizor rutixanusi roƛnin c’oxno babiwn eniwn bexun iłnon esnabi best’alłiƛ’ ƛexun.
translation: They slowly made their fortune, and one day an irreversible disease hit them and the father and the mother died, so the six brothers remained orphans.

text: Rigutow riguƛin, yedan razi oqno ʕuqłanix ik’in.
translation: "Very good!" he agreed and went hiding himself.

text: Užä nesir rätiru šebin esinosi, zeyä yeda ʕoƛno ħonno xecin bitaza sasugani ciqa yałruni imxa tełxor ižin.
translation: After the hunter's son has told what he wanted, the bear took him to the cave behind the seven mountains in the dark forest.

text: Ader zamanał sida eniws roda risa šebin koƛ’ixanusi kid zown.
translation: Once upon a time there was a mother who had a daughter, who didn't understand how to do things.

text: Necetow žedu ƛ’iri butizaƛ’, yedan razi oqxo.
translation: Because they insisted so much, he also agreed.

text: Xalq’imo ʕadalawniƛ’ ayrxo zawruni žoyä, qaciz hiboreƛ łenon riguƛ’ay rigut’a šeƛ’ures bic’äsi ħumukulin ader boɣno, ʕaƛaɣorƛin hunix oqno.
translation: The boy whom the people called "the dumb one", gave his wooden stick away for five camels packed with the prettiest clothings, then he got on his way to the village.

text: Becurinč’ey di täƛru biłetow däqorno meža hibo bayrsi yołi, din mežus mežuqor rutirno kinaw šebin neƛa zowsƛin.
translation: "If you hadn't broken it, you could have brought me back one that were as the one I gave to you, then I would have given you back all your stuff."

text: Nełƛ’aza di nełas kut’yoƛ’asi k’onč’u šiqˤono yołƛin eƛis”.
translation: This is why I said that its right foot is lame."

text: Xizyo ʕArabuzanä qot ric’no micxir teƛno nełaqor enłaƛ elur retixosi šebin risoƛin.
translation: Then Arabuzan gave her a handfull of money: "Buy the things we want with it!"

text: Nełƛ’ay soder babiwz šudaƛ’or siskin baynanu.
translation: After that, no one came to the father's graveyard anymore.

text: Hemełaqułtow ža uži ayrno nesider.
translation: On this very day, the boy was brought to him.

text: Zownƛax, zown anuƛax sis giħono xan.
translation: Once upon a time there was a cruel king.

text: Insanzo mecrexorno xabaryayn neła eƛin, mi di t’äy boxärno, däz maħiłäy łˤono musno bet’urno iduɣor ik’i, debeƛ’ ɣudi rayza quł žedu rik’urnosi, debez ħalarno nexno boqxosi kumak bodanƛin.
translation: It talked in human language and said: "Don't chase me away from here; pull out three hairs from my tail and go home; on a day that you are in trouble, after you burned them, I  will help you to regain your strength."

text: Elus t’o raq’ˤu kidbabi łiyrsi yiła aždaħäƛin, - esin yiła.
translation: That dragon ate up many of our daughters", she said.

text: Hunixozo sida žek’a hi yac’oƛin ɣunoqay yet’urno łˤeb teƛno.
translation: One travelling man, [saying] "Take and eat it!", pulled out a leaf from the tree and gave it to him.

text: Xizaɣor bazarganä uži muk’urno oyn, nesä nesiz ƛirbadäɣor bik’inosi, elo sisxoli ɣˤanabi yesun.
translation: Finally the merchant persuaded the boy and when they went to his own comrade, they found a single woman.

text: Q’ˤuna äƛiru ɣudƛ’on neširu kecno äčizar xanez užis hemełatow ɣˤutkazay eč’i caƛin yayrno.
translation: And on the second day, at night, he threw a pebble from the prince's room down to where he was sleeping.

text: Xizaɣorgon tełtow žan xecin acmołnonan ok’bi rok’nosi, Qartayä eƛin, ha, howži, yił badiru ħalt’os deber keze roqxosi šebin neƛanix maħor yegir diƛin.
translation: Finally, he left her inside, and after he had beated nails into the door, Qartay said: "Well now, let me out to give you something for the work that you have done!"

text: Elaygon bitor ik’iƛ’oräy, yisir sis ħalaq’aw gulu besun.
translation: When he went on from there, he found a thin horse.

text: Neširu bežet’ani esnabi q’ʷarid boqno biłe baynosi, yisä esirno, šebi mežur roqno hemecetow pašman boqa keze boqnäy, šebi necetow beže q’ʷariłi mežuƛ’or bayäsiƛin.
translation: In the evening, when the brothers came looking sad, he asked: "What happened to you that you became so sad? What a big sadness has come upon you!"

text: Elo bˤeƛ’a sidä sidqor eƛix teqno, were, howži gušiwt’ani sida puƛ’aɣor, ƛ’ariyawt’ani sideni puƛ’aɣor roqƛin.
translation: There he heard the sheep say to each other: "Well, now the thin ones go to one side and the fat ones to the other side!"

text: Sis q’ut’u neƛčey däqor, di ħal biyrančey neła aždaħesƛin eƛin, uži kikxar łi ric’a ik’in.
translation: "Please give me a jug, I will find out the dragon's condition.", the boy [said and] went to the spring to fill it with water.

text: ʕAnsan koyä yołäsi ƛ’irir ayru ečru žek’a žaqay bayan bisno šebi t’o sasaqtow rodixƛin.
translation: An old man got up with a walking stick in his hand and wanted to know from the boy: "What are you doing here in the morning?"

text: Žeduq ža moƛugon esis yołi, di yałruxor ƛexa zowsƛin.
translation: Had I told them this dream instead, I would have remained what I was.

text: Razi oqno anu ʕAliqilič.
translation: Aliqilich did not agree.

text: Kaɣat koyär yäsruntow, babiya uži egirno, debi mi esiwn yexurno, nełas rok’u däq rukaranix raƛin.
translation: As soon as the father took the letter in his hand, he said to his son: "Kill your sister, and bring me her heart to show it to me!"

text: Hudayziko onoču mamalaył xizay biynč’ey ciqaɣor bik’in.
translation: The next day, the hen secretly followed the rooster into the forest.

text: Ža kid mi däx yegira yayƛin, yisä Muħamadqor eƛin.
translation: "You must marry this girl off to me!", he said to Muhammad.

text: Sida quł ža guluƛ’orno utin, ža di bazaryarno bižin teƛsi.
translation: One day he turned into a horse, and I led him to the market and sold him.

text: Uyno ɣˤurušno yisno, xediw hunix oqno.
translation: The husband took the four rubles and went on his way.

text: Nesir res boqnoanu ža kid yižinč’ey.
translation: He had no choice not to marry her.

text: K’uruč’ k’eč’ ɣudi rocu buq bay.
translation: All's well that ends well.

text: Bazarganä q’ˤunerno yic’xot’a t’akan et’nosi, nesä ħaƛux zown, yiła enduräz t’asmaɣor yiħurxo zown.
translation: After the merchant poured a glass full for each of them, he drank, and she poured it into the washbowl in the same way.

text: Sida quł yizä yeda režin rayn ɣurox egirno.
Sidaɣ bičix zownƛax babiwn, užin.
translation: It's said that in some place there lived a father and his son.

text: Kutak ɣˤaɣˤu yizix baru, hoboyƛa yisis kanłi bissiƛin.
translation: The wife became really really happy: "Now his eyesight has been taken."

text: Ža eženi ečuy Ħasan žaqˤułno ʕaƛox yołƛax ʕUmares xexbin šet’u butirno žeduq kesubi esix.
translation: They say that the old grandfather Hassan is still living in the the village and, surrounded by Umar's children, tells them tales.

text: Žedus zown sis uži.
translation: They had one son.

text: Nesir beže barkalan boyn, uži iduɣor nexno.
translation: The boy thanked him very much and came home.

text: Howlay äk’inoƛin qˤaƛanayx yeženi ɣˤanabin xecin ʕAliqilič k’oƛin ik’in.
translation: Yelling "Don't go away!", Aliqilich left the old woman and ran.

text: Xizyo šeyxä äƛiru šebin yisiz rok’ƛ’or raynosi ičiqeqełno.
translation: Then he remembered what the sheikh had said and paused.

text: ƛ’iri roži anusi Ħon-Bahadurä eženir gulu ħažin.
translation: Without saying another word, Mountain~Hero exchanged the horse with the old man.

text: Hudun aždaħ sis ɣun recurxo, sidaƛ’aza bet’un k’oƛix esnazał xizay k’oƛin nexxo zown.
translation: Nevertheless the dragon - breaking one tree - jumped onto [another] one and cam running behind the sisters.

text: Žedus eƛixosi šebin łiynosi, wazirä ža xabar ik’in xanqo esin.
translation: After they had said these things, the vizier went to the king to tell him about it.

text: Bukar hoboy eluq ciqes xanƛin!”
translation: Show us the king of the forest now!"

text: Yeɣenä eƛin, di nesir marɣalyos k’icu yołäsi užin odan, mesedyosgon žežu yołäsi kidno yodanƛin.
translation: The young one said: "I will give birth to a son with a pearl tooth and a daughter with a golden ponytail."

text: Ečriłno exʷa zaman baynosi, nesä žaqor wasi bodin, di exunosi deber rätiza ɣˤutkun rodo, yexoriłxosin, k’ot’oniłxosin, aluk’an, qˤaban yoqxosi gurow barun yawno.
translation: He got older and when the time has come to die, he gave his testament to the son: "When I have died, you build a house where you want, but don't marry a woman except for one being black and white, be she long or short.

text: Didurnokin eɣeni esiws ħal bayrxo zownanu neła, kodi begirinč’eygon yedan zank’izin oqno ičäsi zown.
translation: The young brother couldn't overcome her in any way, but he didn't let her hair go.

text: Nexno nesä xanqor eƛin, migon xanä yoł, xan oqxo debi uži, rown qalamä teł nesä raqˤru baruzaƛ’ eza ik’iƛin.
translation: He came to the king and said: "Aren't you the king? Your son is going to become the king; go to look at the wives that he brought and hid in the fortress!"

text: Babiwł xizay užä raq’ˤu sadaq’a rexurno.
translation: After the father's death, the son gave away much alms.

text: Gula eƛin, mi iłet’ani bahadurzas šudbi yoł t’oƛin.
translation: The horse said: "The graves of heroes like you are there."

text: Yaqˤułäy soder yeda Musa xanłun iča yołƛin”.
translation: From that day on he shall be king Musa."

text: Hemełƛ’okin gama xʷasar boda boqnoanu, ƛ’iri zawru nasin xalq’in raładyoł ɣanq’izi boqno.
translation: But it did not rescue the boat, all people drowned in the sea.

text: Howži yiła kʷaraħin, ɣirno bisno łi rowa yik’in.
translation: Now she took a colander and a sieve and went to bring the water.

text: Uži ƛ’iri utizaq, esin yiła, debi esiw qˤabani žek’a yok’ek’no yižisƛin.
translation: Because the son insisted, she told: "Your sister was stolen by a black man."

text: Zownƛax zownanuƛax ʕelmu t’et’räsi Maħamaƛin ci yołäsi sida ʕaƛa žek’u.
translation: Once upon a time there was a man learned in sciences in a village, whose name was Magomed.

text: Hay iguni uži, därƛa mi łi neƛinč’iłin urɣel anu.
translation: "So then, good boy, although you can't give me water, (I) don't worry (about myself).

text: Uži kecnosi, nesä mušin kurno nełar eƛix teqno:
translation: After the boy went to sleep, she heard him sighing and saying:

text: Neširaɣor qacis muɣbin rown nexƛ’oräy, där muɣ boq’ˤiłxoƛin eniya eƛinosi, sis rečiwsi riłe qaca tełäy roɣno q’ˤidar kuroƛin, kidbaza eƛin.
translation: When they were coming back with a bundle of firewood in the evening and the mother said: "The bundle is too heavy for me", the daughters said: "Take a thick bunch of firewood and throw it out."

text: Elor bayru aždaħä, ocä räsuru isʷa rigintow kurno.
translation: The dragon, having come there, swallowed the bulls that were on the threshing floor.

text: Kidbä eƛin, t’o sis aq’iw ʕoloqanaw uži yoł, eluz ʕaƛaysin anu žaƛin.
translation: The girl said: "There is a strange young boy and he is not someone from our village."

text: Bet’urinč’uƛin, žawab teƛno nesä.
translation: "I haven't plucked.", he answered.

text: Ox̌oya ʕUmar iduɣorno ižin, siħrus ʕilmu moła iħun.
translation: Ohoyo lead Umar to his home and began to teach him the science of slyness.

text: C’iɣruk’ä yeɣeni kidbes iyos q’ut’un bic’no, nełastow ħamorok’un roɣno adäz eqerno, maqˤäytow aždaħqor qˤaƛin, biša ħaƛis, howži idur bik’iƛin.
translation: Tsighruk filled the youngest daughter's blood into an earthen jug, took her breast and put it before himself and shouted from far away to the dragon: "I organized the food, now I'm going home!"

text: Neširugon žedu baħnayx zawrunitow hunäza q’ˤanon esnabi micxir bok’ek’anix bayn.
translation: At night, then, the two brothers came on the same way they had been walking on, to steal the money.

text: Łˤora ɣˤurušyoƛ yedu teƛxo yoqzaƛ’, yiłas hunar šebi yołƛin?”.
translation: "Three rubles does it cost, what is its ability?"

text: Howži yeda ža yičixozoni ʕaƛaɣorno ik’in, elo rukayru hič’č’a reženi ɣˤutkos ac yaɣˤiƛ’oräy, xan oƛixno maħor k’oƛin.
translation: He dropped into the village, where that girl lived, and saw the big house. As he wanted to open the door, the khan appeared outside.

text: Zamanałäy gulu ƛ’iriɣor boƛixno, ža žek’u na eƛa t’ok’ow iynanu.
translation: After some time, the horse appeared on the surface, but no one knew where the man was.

text: Din hemedurtow bˤuƛ’xo zowsƛin, eƛin ceyä, mi däƛ’orno rutirno tupi caƛiƛ’oräy.
translation: "And I was likewise afraid", said the eagle, "when you turned a rifle on me and shot."

text: Užä ħit’ures iƛ’u nec’irxotow yižix yukaynosi, reƛ teƛxozoni bazarganä ža xizor oxirno.
translation: When he saw that the boy was emptying the bottom of the pan, the meat-selling merchant chased him.

text: Horo, meža yaqˤuł dey sis ʕaq’lu bisoƛin.
translation: Come, take my advice today!

text: Šida mi nece q’ʷarid oqno ičäsiƛin, yiła nesiq esirno.
translation: "Why are you so sad?", she asked him.

text: Ħonƛ’orno izin, regirno bixiłer ɣuron muši begiranix yeda q’ˤida ičinosi, nazay eƛa biynč’ey sis aɣi boƛixno yisiqor insanzo mecreƛ’no xabaryayn eƛin:
translation: He climbed up the mountain and sent the cows onto the grass, and when he sat down to take a breath, a bird appeared out of nowhere and told him in human language:

text: Izin äk’iru ʕAliqiličä yołi aħyan rˤołun, bašiqno rixintow aždaħes q’ˤim rayrno.
translation: Aliqilich, who got up and came, pierced its ear, and brought the dragon's head on one finger.

text: Nełar ža ʕaq’ˤu etix zown, hudun Musa hiƛ’ar anusi ičix zown - miskinaw oqzaƛ’ ža däx yuyn yegärinč’i, yuyn näxinč’iƛin.
translation: She loved him very much, but Musa didn't care about this, thinking: "Because I am so poor, he wont give me to her and she won't come to me either."

text: Roƛƛo ʕuraw ɣudbi rik’inosi, zira bäxurusno rodin bičin.
translation: After many days had passed, the fox pretended to be dead.

text: K’ox äƛirugon mekoyn iguni halmaɣä biša esirnosi, nesä inkar boyn, deber di sosi neƛinč’aƛin.
translation: When the good friend was hungry for the second time, he asked for food, but he [the other one] rejected: "Didn't I give you something first?"

text: Sida quł ciqaɣor äk’izay, xedyor ƛ’iräzay yecasi, teł aɣiw ažo yesun.
translation: One day, while he was going into the forest, the husband found a tree that was hollow inside and had been broken from above.

text: Nesä kidbeqor eƛin, yedu t’umi dey yoł, xizor dey däqor neƛƛin.
translation: He said to the girl: "That pigeon is mine; give it back to me!"

text: Debi łˤono uži yoł, dey siskin anuru deberno iyx äsu.
translation: You have three sons and should know that I don't have anyone.

text: Pardagin risno uži idur aynosi, eniya esirno, ža łinar mi rowä, šebi nełas mumpaʕatƛin.
translation: As the son came home with the scarf, the mother asked, "Why did you bring it, what is it good for?"

text: Kʷaxatow sida igozo ʕaƛay nesi žax łet’in sis bercinaw kidno yown.
translation: Soon, that boy picked a beautiful girl from the neighboring village and married her.

text: Di yoł yiła eluz wilayatzo naib Žabulawes aždaħä yok’ek’no yowäsi kidƛin, - esin yiła, - däƛ’ Baxumesedoƛin cin yoł.
translation: "I am the daughter of our province's governor Jabulaw, who got stolen by a dragon." she said, "my name is Bakhumesedo.

text: Didur di ħažƛ’o bik’an, däz ozzar šebin rukayxanuwaƛin?” - zeyä eƛin.
translation: "How should I go on hajj, when my eyes can't see a thing?", the bear said.

text: Elo tełäyn ƛisin, łaymoƛ’ay ik’ace žeda ža uyn žek’no oɣno maħor kurno.
translation: They pulled him outside there, beated him unconscious, took him up and threw him outside.

text: Zownƛax zownanuƛax sis bečedaw žek’u.
translation: Once upon a time there was a rich man.

text: Ɣˤalu seranix indowrizaxosi aħo goƛ’anix xanä wazirbi begirno baynosi, k’oƛin yik’in kidbätow serun.
translation: He sent his viziers to call the turkey herder to open the saddle, but his daughter came running and opened it.

text: Q’ˤanoqu nukar xanä egirno elo qunyoxor c’ohores ʕagarłi biyrno biqiraniƛäy.
translation: The king sent forty henchmen to that farmstead in order to find out and catch the thief's relatives.

text: Eniwn, užin, barun bečełiƛ’ zown, miskin xalq’imor sadaq’a teƛxo žeda ʕaƛos žamaʕattown bečed bodin.
translation: The mother, her son and his wife lived wealthily, and by giving alms to the poor people, they enriched the population of the village.

text: Aƛ’irno nesiqay žan boɣno, roži teƛinč’ey xizor oxirno.
translation: She cheated and took it away from him, not keeping her word, and chased him back.

text: Xexłiƛ’ idurno yik’in, yiłä yedu xabar babiwq esinosi, nesä nesidäɣor esiws uži goƛ’a yedu yegirno.
translation: She went home quickly, and when she told this story to her father, he let her invite his brother's son to himself.

text: Were, were, esoƛin, baru ƛ’iri yutin.
translation: "Well well, tell me!", the wife insisted.

text: Xizyo diwanbegä ħukmun beč’no, ža eč’izad yexursiƛin.
translation: Then the judge made the decision that she was to be killed by stoning."

text: Howži min barun ʕaƛaɣor bik’in.
translation: You and your wife go into the village now.

text: Puräza barun hokomƛ’on zown ik’ixƛax.
translation: Apparently he was riding on a carriage next to his wife.

text: Uži mažgityaɣor keca ik’ixƛin oqnosi, yedu ƛ’iri yutin ƛirba kecanix mažgityaɣor egiranix elude lamusaw šebin yołƛin.
translation: When the boy was about to go to the mosque to sleep, she insisted: "It is a shame for us to send a comrade to the mosque for him to sleep!"

text: Užä guluq esirno, yedu šebi šebinƛin.
translation: The boy asked the horse: "What thing is that?!"

text: ʕOloqza esirno:
translation: The people asked:

text: Xizyogon žukani halmaɣä eƛin, deber bišʷa retinäy, roɣno mi där sis ozuri neƛƛin.
translation: Then the bad friend said: "If you want food, take one eye out and give it to me!"

text: Xizyogon xanä dey oz bišroƛin eƛinosi, žoyä meɣyoqorno eƛin žedun bišerno.
translation: Then, when the king said: "Let my army eat!", the boy said [it] to his udder and let them eat.

text: Łˤono äƛiruni bełiqa qˤaƛin, insan esunäy roži roɣo, bełi besunäy ela c’eruc’a caƛixƛin.
translation: The third hunter shouted: "If you are a human, say something; if you are an animal, we will shoot you with the bow!"

text: Eɣeni esiwq xanä deber łinax riyä kut’yoƛ’aza puƛ’aza nełaƛ’ ƛ’iri nuci zawrułiƛin esirnosi, nesä eƛin:
translation: When the king asked the younger brother, how he knew that it had honey on its right side, he said:

text: Howži yeda q’ebedes uži ƛ’irir aynosi, zeyän, ziran yisiq harizi rodin howziri łˤonon: gulun, ħurženyabin, k’ukno eluƛ’ riłixor rułirƛin.
translation: Now that the son of the smith came, the bear and the fox asked him: "Share these three items: the horse, the bags and the cap among us equally!"

text: ƛˤebaƛ’aza žedur uži odin.
translation: After a year, they got a son.

text: Xanä nesiq esirno, didur debeq hawšinaw šebin roda k’ʷeze roqäƛin.
translation: The king asked him: "How could you do so many things?!"

text: Aždaħƛ’or batiruni bikori nexxo yoł, madazaħ räsuru šinaw šebin neła rac’xosi yołƛin!”
translation: A snake that turned into a dragon is coming and it will eat up everything it finds!"

text: Žek’alini nukarä eƛix teqno, nukarza debeq heresin esis, ža łin žeda esnabi bičixozo mečoƛ’ay ric’si, žedu xexbi debikin besač’ineyƛin.
translation: They heard the beaten henchman say: "The henchmen lied to you, they filled that water from the place where the siblings live; couldn't these children perhaps be yours?"

text: Xizyogon nesä dowqor ħoyä tełäy magalu boɣoƛin eƛinosi, hemedurtow sosi mi boɣo xizyo di boɣanƛin eƛo.
translation: Then, when he asks you: 'Take the bread out of the oven!', say likewise: 'First you take it out, then I will take it out!'

text: Žedu q’ˤunesno daʕba boqno, elo tełsi mesed boƛi-bok’łinč’ey.
translation: These two disputed because they could not divide the gold equally.

text: Užä ža ragin łiyrxo rukaynosi, bazarganä yeda xizor oxirno.
translation: When he saw that the boy was eating it up, the merchant chased him.

text: Yeda marɣalyos k’icu yołalini debi uži yołƛin, eƛin neła nełaz xediwƛ’or bašan boyn ukarno.
translation: And he is your son with the pearl tooth.", she said and pointed her finger to her husband.

text: Sida quł užä eniwqay zap’ana hisos k’apyan bisno, žan t’aħin, c’eruc’a rodin.
translation: One day the son took a strong wool thread by his mother, strained it and made a bow.

text: Łaz ƛ’ˤuƛ’o c’eruc’a c’oxxo, hemeła ɣˤutkay kid yowa yołƛin.
translation: Whose roof you hit with the bow, the daughter from that house you will marry.

text: Mi t’or aždaħes xexoy ba di c’unzi yoda, howlo sidaɣ yołƛin teqsi där aždaħno, nełdegon sadaq łˤono xexoynƛin.
translation: Bring me a dragon's young to guard me; I heard somewhere there there is a dragon and beside it three youngs."

text: Hemełƛ’o Beqes ʕUneyzatä kidbez q’ˤimƛ’ay pardagi roɣno caƛin eƛin, yedu yoł mesedyos žežu yołalini debi kidƛin.
translation: Thereupon Sun's Uneyzat threw away the scarf off the girl's head and said: "She is your daughter with the golden ponytail.

text: Nełaz q’ˤimqon lagiqnotown c’i łeɣin.
translation: Its head and its body caught fire.

text: Sasaqosi zira eƛin: ƛa ħažƛ’aɣor rik’an q’anon sadaqƛin.”
translation: In the morning, the fox said: "Come, let's go on hajj (pilgrimage) together."

text: ʕAƛoz raʕalłixorgon baynosiƛin, eƛin babiya, elude dandir šuša žek’u ižin bik’ixozo xalq’imoqgon Aħmadä esirsi, eƛ’iyä yeda žek’u exasi, yagi žiƛboyäƛin.
translation: "When we came to the edge of the village," said the father, "Akhmed asked the people that were coming towards us carrying a man to bury him: 'Has this man died last year or this year?'."

text: Xizyogon esiwn č’ariłno.
translation: Then his sister woke up.

text: Oč’ira mučaris bahana besun,
translation: She found the excuse for the nine cumin cakes,

text: Neła quł sasaqosigon esnabi madaħor boqnosi, eɣeni esiw qˤabani guluƛ’no zown, q’ˤuya šeƛ’un šeƛ’un, yaraɣno lagiqor kurno ik’in.
translation: The next morning when the brothers went outside, the young brother rode the black horse, put on different clothes, threw a weapon around his body and rode off.

text: Zeyä ža neła nełdäɣor yižin.
translation: The bear led her to himself.

text: Mekodäsin, akiłäsin, tatu bexasin yeda elo tełxor ik’in.
translation: Being hungry, tired and at the end of his powers, he went inside.

text: Elo yizar boc’as berten besun.
translation: There they found the wedding of wolves.

text: Q’orolayz xabaryoƛ’ay yiłar riyn neł ʕaƛa teł nasin sida bečedaw žek’uz bertenyołer boqxosi yałrułi.
translation: From the widow's telling, she knew that everyone in this village was going to the wedding of a rich man.

text: Ža nediw tamašałi rukaynosi, acno yaɣˤin, idurno ik’in, pečmaɣor łoħros qal kurno.
translation: Having seen such a wonder, he opened the door, went home and threw the frog's skin into the oven.

text: Howži tełxorno goƛ’in, neła nełaz xediwqor yiła eƛin, howła xizaɣorzo zamanał deber baqru q’ʷariłiłäy mi däq esoƛin.
translation: Now she invited her own husband in and said: "Tell me about the bad things that had happened to you lately?"

text: Ža debi ƛe didiw zown?
translation: "What kind of bridge was that of yours?

text: Sis isno adäz boc’no egirno yisä Muħamad, bexayn žan, ħalalawni reƛ mežan rac’no, ħaramawni där raƛin.
translation: He let Muhammad drive a bull ahead: "Slaughter it, and [y'all] eat the permitted meat, and bring the prohibited [meat] to me!"

text: Xizyo babiwzno yedu kid rok’ƛ’or yaysƛax.
translation: Afterwards also the father remembered that daughter.

text: Nagaħ ža riyrinč’inäy dowde dandir di ƛˤori boda yołƛin.
translation: "If you don't find it out, I will start a war against you."

text: Sasaqosi maħor oqƛ’oräy, eɣeni esiw gondaɣor ʕoƛ’un.
translation: In the morning when he went outside, the young brother fell into the hole.

text: Zownƛax zown anuƛax onočun, mamalayn.
translation: Once upon a time there was a hen and a rooster.

text: Nesis haq’u bełiq’ay bišxo zown.
translation: His family made a living from the hunting.

text: Neła quł sasaq, esiw bełiƛ’ ik’inosi, ža q’orolay žigon yayn k’oƛin idur.
translation: The next morning when the brother had gone hunting, that widow ran back to their home once again.

text: Bilq’isdä dibirqor eƛin, mežur di ʕaƛos xanłi rodan, žamaʕatyo di äƛiru łˤono šebin rodanix ƛ’iri risnäyƛin.
translation: Bilqisdi said to the imam: "I will take over the kingship over the village for you all, if the population does the three things that I say."

text: Neširu, sasiłnosi, ʕomoybi yałru rečuxor boc’i bayn.
translation: In the evening, after it got dark, the wolf came to the cattleshed where the donkeys were.

text: Yedu risxozo žek’uqor mi eƛoƛin, eƛin kidbä nełqor, debeq yiłas bašiqˤoy baha bäzinč’iƛin.
translation: "Say to the person who wants to buy it:" the girl told her, "'You won't affort the price.'

text: Babiwr kidno, esiwr esiyn, xedyor barun, žar eniwn č’agu yoqnoƛax.
translation: For the father his daughter, for the brother his sister, for the husband his wife and for the son his mother was alive.

text: Hemeł zamanał xanes roƛno.
translation: At this time the king got sick.

text: Xizyo xanä naysinän xabaryabin regirno, kaɣatyabin caxno doxturbi goƛ’in.
translation: After that, the king sent the news and wrote letters everywhere and invited doctors.

text: Elaygon bitor čanyabi k’edix ik’iƛ’oräy, neside dandir čarman ricin nexxo ziru č’aq’ˤin.
translation: While going away from there searching the goats, a fox with a turban ran into him.

text: Yisiz bertenyołer xanno, nesä saɣ adiruni ža nesis užin, žasgon ža barun yayn.
translation: The khan and his son, who was cured by this doctor, came to the marriage with his wife.

text: Oƛixatow žek’u oƛixxo zownanu.
translation: But there was no such man at all.

text: Q’ˤimde sadaq lagan šušin, eɣeni esiya rok’un hiƛirno.
translation: The young brother buried the body together with the head and calmed down.

text: Suƛitow eženi esiwn ayrno, gulun bayrno.
translation: And it immediately brought his big brother and the horse.

text: Insan mi esunäy däz kʷayär horo, yałi, eli maxsaryoƛ’ bisinč’ey, xizor bik’iƛin”.
translation: "If you are a human, come into my hand, or else this joke won't bluff us and we'll go back"

text: Yeženir barkalan bodin, yizi keca bik’in.
translation: He thanked the old woman and they went to sleep.

text: Ela elus halmaɣłi rexura raynč’i.
translation: We mustn't destroy our friendship.

text: Hemełƛ’o bečedawni žek’a eƛin, mi dey kid saɣ yodinäy, maharno erno debex ža di yegiran, retinäy boƛi bok’no dey maʕišat neƛanƛin.
translation: Afterwards the rich man suggested, "If you cure my daughter, I will set up the wedding and if you want, I will give you one half of my fortune."

text: Q’ˤano äƛirugon ɣudƛ’o, buq bayru mixƛ’o xediya eƛin, žaqˤułgon dunyalkinwa yedu qˤašixanuƛin.
translation: On the second day, at the time the sun came out, the husband said: "And today even the world doesn't seem to dawn at all."

text: Yezunosi yiłar bercinaw, gelmač’u, ozuri bic’äsi uži ukayn nełał adäzarno oqno ičäsi.
translation: She looked around and saw a boy with handsome, round eyes having come in front of her.

text: C’ik’iwtow humerno, šeƛ’un xiszi rodin, nełdäɣorno ik’in esirno, ɣamas yodixosi žek’u etinaƛin.
translation: He changed his entire face and his clothes, went to him [the dragon] and asked if he wanted a man that makes boxes.

text: Tawadiz harimoxor Ražbadinä nesirtow hič’č’a eɣeni užin xecin.
translation: According to Tavadi's request, Razhbadin left the youngest son to him.

text: Neła žaqor eƛin, t’ay ik’iƛ’orey, deber bˤeƛ’xo aħo äsu.
translation: She said to the boy: "When you go away from here, you will find a shepherd with a flock of sheep.

text: Yeda uži, bišʷannan rok’ƛ’ay šuƛ’in, q’ˤuna anƛ’ima hunix ik’in.
translation: The boy forgot about food entirely and went on his way for two weeks.

text: Igtow q’ˤimłerno aɣˤun babiw idur ayn.
translation: The father came home very puzzled.

text: Howži aħyabin rˤoƛ’urno xanes uži yeda ičix zawru mečoƛ’or ayn.
translation: Now, the prince hang his head and went to the place where he was living.

text: Boc’a rigtow rˤuƛ’no.
translation: The wolves were very afraid.

text: Howži bełiqanes ʕuraw rok’un raq’ˤiłno, roda šebinon koƛ’inč’ey ƛexun yeda.
translation: Now the hunter got very upset and remained not knowing what to do.

text: Hemeła ɣudƛ’ot’a q’ˤuya ulkał ičixosi Muħamadƛin ci yołäsi muʔmin žek’u elor aħnayx zown.
translation: On each of those days, a pious man who lived in another country came walking there.

text: Sida puƛ’aza užibi, sida puƛ’aza žeduz annazaqar annabin rutirno kidbabi.
translation: On one side the boys had gathered and on one side, knee next to knee, gathered the girls.

text: Ža kusaq eƛin łiyzaƛ’ortow, ʕomoyä ħoʕni rizirno.
translation: Even before the beardless man had finished saying this, the donkey gave out a shout.

text: Sasaqgon miƛ’i šudaƛ’or ciyo raganix bayn.
translation: And on the next day, the ram came to the graveyard to lick the salt again.

text: Šet’ur ezuzaƛ’, sis ƛ’iri yałru as taraw rukayxanuƛax elo.
translation: When looking around, he didn't see anything else there than the sky that was above him.

text: Eɣeni esiws c’eruc’a resatow resux zownanu.
translation: The younger brother's bow didn't find anything at all.

text: Elay cołizin roqno nasin halmaɣbi rizin zeyƛ’ c’oxno.
translation: Then all the friends united, got up and pounced on the bear.

text: Idur yaynosi, užigon kecäsi esunosi, asoqtow yitin.
translation: When she came home, she found the boy sleeping and was in seventh heaven.

text: Ža uži zey iłetown aq’ˤiłxo ik’ix zowno.
translation: That boy grew up just like the bear.

text: Xedya mežay bitor zigara bizira baybik bodin.
translation: The husband began to moan from his mattress.

text: Yedu kidgon Ražbadinqor baqru ʕišq’ud, netey yedu berten bodixƛin, iduɣor yik’ixosi hunin šuƛ’in qˤilo yok’ełxo.
translation: The girl was so in love with Razhbadin, and because she prepared that marriage, she forgot the way home and got lost.

text: Žedu c’aq’ yaziqłiƛ’ zown.
translation: They were very poor.

text: Eženä yeda yisä yisir užiłun xecin.
translation: The old man adopted him as his own son.

text: Hoboygon žar ʕoƛnontow čara bexun.
translation: Now the boy totally ran out of possibilities.

text: Ha hoboy, dey mesedyos saħno, t’asno, reƛ’iqoyn, pandurno neƛƛin”.
translation: "Well now, give me back my sah of gold, my washbowl, my glove and my lute."

text: Sisgon mołin neła, elorno ezun xizor ik’iƛ’oräygon, nesä debeq esär, šebi deber t’ay neƛanƛin.
translation: She taught him another thing: "While you go back after having looked, he will ask you what he shall give to you.

text: Kidbä his xuxiƛ’oräy, nełaƛ’ yałru aq’ˤuban, q’ʷariłin bukaynosi, yisiq reže qoqoƛu regin.
translation: While the girl was combing the wool, he laughed loudly, when he saw the torture and sadness that was upon her.

text: Hudayzikogon neširu xanä baɣmoxor oƛosini uži egirno.
translation: Next day in the evening the khan sent his middle son to the garden.

text: Nesän hemedurtow esirno užiq, ža ziys ħoši teƛxanaƛin.
translation: He asked the boy likewise: "Aren't you going to sell that cow hide?"

text: Zeyä žedaqor eƛin, hemeł beženi ʕaƛa łi anuƛax.
translation: The bear said to them: "It's said that there's no water in this big village.

text: Suƛƛi yeda Ismaʕil ozyoł łic’axrun, t’it’in bešerxotow bižix zown yisä xanes oz.
translation: And immediately that Ismail joined that army and fell, cut down and took out the king's army.

text: Hemełƛ’o ƛ’irir ayn k’oƛin kot’u yexorani eženi žek’u.
translation: On that moment appeared the old man with the long beard.

text: Žeda isno hokomƛ’o ħaliłäsi xan eqerno.
translation: They took the drunken king and put him onto the carriage.

text: Moƛał rukayru šebinon hemedurtow eluq riqixosi anuƛin, Pat’imatä žedu xizor boxirno.
translation: "Likewise we cannot get something that we saw in a dream.", Fatima said and chased them back.

text: Nesä žawab teƛno:
translation: He answered:

text: Žeda besurozaƛ’ay ʕumru boyx zown.
translation: They were living from fishing.

text: Yedugon łinarey rˤoƛ’us?
translation: And why the heck has this fallen down?

text: Izin užä neł imyoq ħobo bok’nosi, nazay eƛa riynč’ey reƛ’iqoy rˤoƛ’un.
translation: When he got up and kicked the pole, a glove fell down he didn't know from where.

text: K’uruč’ k’eč’ ɣudi rocu, buq bay.
translation: All's well that ends well.

text: Asoq itix qazaq, halmaɣä ža xabar esinosi.
translation: The Georgian was on cloud nine, when his friend told him the story.

text: Yoƛozoni esiya eƛin, däqor at’es sis saħ neƛnäy, debez ozyortow ʕeze roqace magalabi rodan diƛin.
translation: The middle sister said: "If you give me one sah of wheat, I will make breads enough for your army."

text: Nełaƛ’ razin yoqinč’ey, Beqes ʕUneyzatä mesedyos q’ˤuri imyoqor yicin zawruni ɣˤanazaƛer teƛno, micxiryosni - užiƛer, pakmosni - kidbeƛer, qacisni neła nełƛer risno.
translation: Sun's Uneyzat didn't agree to this and gave the golden chair to the girl that used to be tied to the pole, the silver one to the boy, the copperone to the girl and the wooden one she took for herself.

text: Gulu bišan begirno puƛ’or ʕoƛ’unosi, yeda moƛa iqirxo.
translation: He let the horse eat, fell onto the side and the sleep took him away.

text: Neła ƛˤorłäyn teqno ƛ’irir yayru nesi žaz eniya nesiqor qˤaƛin, iħäzoni łiyä bown kurali mežikin sida ħala bičix yołiƛin.
translation: The moter of that boy heard of their battle and came up and shouted to him: "You two, brought by the river's water and thrown here, be normal!"

text: Q’orolayä nełaqor eƛin, deber žukłi rodanix xalq’i urɣizi boqäsi yołƛin.
translation: The widow told them: "Some people are thinking of harming you.
