text: Neła quł sasaqosigon babiya eƛin, ɣudegon nazorno ʕaƛor ʕuraw pulaw riqir, yałi exura yołƛin.
translation: The next morning, the father said: "Bring alot of pilaw to everyone in the village tomorrow, or else I will kill you."

text: Eɣeni esiwq xanä, däz šadał aqˤuros maħ nazay boqa bayxƛin esirnosi, nesä eƛin:
translation: When the king asked the younger brother, how it came that his wine smells like urine, he said:

text: Rukarƛin, yizi ʕuraw ƛ’iri butinosi, butnoƛ’ pun bok’no ičin zawruni žek’a eƛin, di bitaɣorno ik’in ayzaƛ’or, yedu k’uk biqirno bičinäy, mežuq di t’o yałru šebin rukaranƛin.
translation: "Show it!", [they said and] when they insisted heavily, the man lying on the burka said: "When you hold my cap while I will go away and come back, I will show you the thing that is here."

text: ƛ’irir ik’in sis xexoyn biqirno, et’un guluƛ’orno k’oƛin, sosi čuret’ bok’no.
translation: He came closer, grabbed one young dragon, jumped onto his horse and cracked the whip once.

text: Esiya eƛin, rigutow ħadurłi rodaniƛäy ħonƛ’ay bˤeƛ’ rowa ik’an diƛin.
translation: His brother said: "Because this has to be well prepared, I'll go bring a flock of sheep down from the mountain."

text: Sis zown eniwn nełasgon łˤono kidno.
translation: Once there was a mother who had three daughters.

text: Bełiqanqor ezun mažgitägon dibirno zown ičäsi, yeda dice žaqˤuł kʷat’izi oqxoƛin.
translation: And in the mosque the imam was waiting for the hunter, [saying:] "How late he is today!"

text: ʕIšq’a halag adiru ʕoloqan žek’u darsimoƛ’orä reʕizi oqxo.
translation: The young man, being excited by love, how could he have time for lessons?!

text: Haqan bogurno tełer ezun elo čanyazas ruɣutow rukaynč’izaƛ’, nesä ža zey begirno.
translation: He let him gape, looked into his mouth, and because he didn't see any footprints of the goats there, he let that bear go.

text: C’irduxno, nesis esnabin buqˤełno nełaƛ’ bezun bičäsi zown.
translation: Tsirdukh and his brothers hid and then were looking.

text: Miƛ’i boƛäxrun, et’un guluƛ’orno k’oƛin oqno xizay.
translation: Just when the ram appeared, he jumped onto his horse and began to ride behind it.

text: Ža xabarno teqno obiwn, užin bezanixƛin bik’inosi, yisir suƛƛi yukayruntow yiyn yedu yisis baru yałru.
translation: Having heard of this story, the father and his son went to have a look and he immediately knew when seeing her, that it was his wife.

text: Hič’č’a bercinawni t’awus yisä boƛƛäy boɣƛ’oräy, qarawulza iqirno nesiq esirno, mi nazay, łinar ayäsi žek’u yołƛin.
translation: After he has seized the most beatiful one, the guards caught him and asked, "Where did you come from and why?"

text: Xizyo žeduł xizay bik’ix zawru ɣˤʷayn bexurno, nełas rok’un rižin ik’in babiwder, di esiw yexursiƛin eƛanix.
translation: Afterwards, he killed a dog that was following them, he took out its heart and went to his father to say: "I killed my sister."

text: K’oƛin yedu yäk’iru, q’ˤuriƛ’orno izin meč’oxor q’aq’at’u kurxo esun.
translation: She ran away and he had already gotten on a chair and thrown a noose around his neck.

text: Q’ˤuyan, adäzar keze baqru, ħalt’i boyx zown nesä.
translation: He was also doing other work that he could get.

text: Łox äƛirugon, akäzno yoƛixno, tełxor oqƛin eƛin.
translation: But at the third time, she appeared in the window and said: "Come inside!"

text: Qˤaƛin Q’urʔan t’et’ra oqno dibir.
translation: The imam began to read out the Qur'ân loudly.

text: Sida quł rocazo ɣudƛ’o yizirabi hunix boqxo.
translation: On a clear day then, they got on their way.

text: Bečedawni maduhal yiłdetow sadaq iduɣorno nexno, roɣno ža pardagin ik’in, dey zows žaƛin.
translation: But the wealthy neighbor came home together with the mother, took away the scarf and said, "It was my scarf."

text: Neširun biša bik’inosi, esnazar howžatow žek’u esun q’ˤunan reƛ’izad q’ˤimno riqirno, bišadyoƛ’or aq’ˤułno ičäsi.
translation: When they came to eat dinner there, the brothers found that man there with his two hands supporting him and sitting over his food.

text: Elo teł min uqˤno, ža ɣamasno hokomƛ’o eqerno, di xanes ez yałza mečoƛ’ar ik’an.
translation: I will hide you inside it, put that box onto a carriage and go to the place where the king's fortress is.

text: Tełxor yeda äk’iruntow, gulu begirnon xecin kidbä, xizyo qˤaƛin babiwqor, gulu boxin bik’isƛin.
translation: Right after he went inside, the girl let the horse go and then shouted to her father: "The horse has run away!"

text: Bisno žoyä c’udani heneš teƛno.
translation: The boy took a red apple and gave it to her.

text: Dibirno goƛ’in, nuʕbin bayrno, neła quł neširutown Ismaʕilä ʕArabuzan yown.
translation: They called the mullah, let witnesses come and on that very day in the same evening, Ismail married Arabuzan.

text: Neła qaciz ruk’uqorno icin iħułxor kurxosi yałrułin čuqˤno, ža qˤaƛanada oqno, di ža uži anu, di bˤeƛ’xosi aħo yołƛin.
translation: They tied him to that bundle of wood and when he noticed that they were about to throw him into the river, he started yelling: "I am not the boy, I am a shepherd with a flock of sheep!"

text: Xizyo yizi uynon xanez ɣˤutka tełxor boqno.
translation: Then the four of them went into the king's house.

text: Xanä yisił adäza sual erno, kid yižiƛ’oräy bodač’in berten, nagaħ yoxiraniƛ’or yaynäy, hemełƛ’o bodanƛin.
translation: The king asked him a question: "While I am going to marry the girl, I will not make a wedding; if I have to chase her away, I will do it."

text: Ixdax, isi yecinosi, nasin ciqes, posu yiła maħor regirno.
translation: In spring, after the snow had thawed, she let all the wild animals out.

text: Nełƛ’osi baq’ˤu zaman bik’izaƛ’ortow, łox äƛirugon acmoq deqƛin.
translation: And alot more time after that, it knocked on the door a third time.

text: Xanez užä nesix esiw yegirnoƛax.
translation: The prince married his sister off to him, it is said.

text: Zownƛax, zown anuƛax eniwn užin.
translation: Once upon a time there was a mother and her son.

text: Elay bitor išno-utin ik’inosi, hemełtow azbaryoxor ayn.
translation: He ate and from there he went further and came to the same palace.

text: Neła harizi rodin, habsaʕat maħorno yoqno yayan diƛin.
translation: She pleaded: "I'll get outside and come back!"

text: Nexanƛin yigtow yedu kid ƛ’iri yutinosi, žoyä pandur teƛno ičin yiłar.
translation: "I will come", the girl insisted very much, and the boy gave her the lute.

text: Sasaq nexno maħlay xanqor qˤaƛo mi, c’ik’iw raładyołäy besurobin roqno, žeda debi räƛiru šebin rac’noƛin.
translation: "Come tomorrow and shout from the court tomorrow, that all the fish got out of the sea and ate what you planted."

text: Elus q’ut’i zownč’a där rätiru šebin sadaq rižanix regira, din där rätiru šebin hokomƛ’o ersiƛin.
translation: "Didn't we have an agreement so you let me carry all the things I wanted? I put the things I wanted onto the carriage."

text: Xanä bełiquz žaqor eƛin, sida ezä teł aɣi yołƛin teqxo, där howžan bown horo miƛin.
translation: The king said to the hunter' son, "Inside a fortress there is a bird, I hear; bring it to me!"

text: Bitaɣor bok’äłrun, žeda žeduƛ xabarno boqno, ħumukulizaƛ’osini boƛik’u mesed teƛno yołin howža hibo bisan nesi žaqay, nełas elur beže raħat biča yoł, šidaƛin eƛiłiri ačqˤaynosi ža bak’za ħaƛanix łin roƛixnäy, mekoynosigon hemedurtow bišʷan riqinäy, t’ok’ow elur q’ʷˤariłi łinasƛin.
translation: After they disappered into the distance, they told themselves: "We will buy that stick from this boy for half of the gold on the camels; it will be such a relieve for us! [why?]", they would say(?), "Why would we be anymore sad, if, when we are thirsty, we hit it and water appears for drinking; and when we are hungry, we get food that way."

text: Yiła nesiqorno eƛin, dey xediw yoł, žan ičin di q’ˤuyzix yik’a behizi yaqinč’iƛin.
translation: She said to him: "I have a husband, and I won't be allowed to go to someone else."

text: Aždaħä bišanix baybik bodin.
translation: The dragon began to eat.

text: Howži yeda bˤeƛ’de sadaq ʕaƛoxor ayn.
translation: Now he came to the village together with the sheep.

text: Nesä ik’in xanez kidbes räxuruni ozuras meča ɣʷays ozuri ernosi, nełar nełaz ozuriƛ’aygon rig rukada roqno.
translation: He went and after putting a dog's eye in place of the princess' dead eye, she began to see well again from her eye.

text: Eniwqno nesi užä esix zownanu babiya ža micxir c’idaɣor kurxoƛin.
translation: The son didn't tell his mother that the father was throwing the money into the fire.

text: Elay bitor, ɣˤaɣˤun bizin, łˤonon iduɣor bik’inosi, yeženi esiw yesun, idu yiłe, q’ˤidan yičin yičäsi.
translation: The three of them were very delighted, so when they went home, they found the older sister, sitting at home waiting.

text: Ražbadin igtow pašman oqno, yedu šebi däq roqäƛin.
translation: Razhbadin became very sad: "What happened to me?"

text: Xizyo iguni halmaɣƛ’o mekʷan c’oxno, žukaniq bišʷan esirno, sosigon bišno žedu.
translation: Afterwards, the good friend got hungry and asked the bad one for food, and they ate first.

text: Ža maxsarno bacin, łox äƛiru hunix Zeyes Aħya bˤałayn risno oč’ino q’ˤim yołali aždaħno bexurno xalq’imoƛ’ay yedu ɣudi roɣanƛintow ik’in.
translation: Being bothered by this mockery, Bear Ear took his dagger and went to kill the dragon with the nine heads and to take this burden off the people.

text: Mamalayä nełar t’ok’ow bik’ač’inƛin rexuxanusi rožin teƛno, nece bayn čerayzaƛ’ onoča ža idur begirno.
translation: The rooster gave his word, that he would not go to her anymore, and since he begged so much, the hen let him home.

text: Sidaquł yiłar mesedyos nak’ila yołäsi kid yodin.
translation: One day she gave birth to a girl that had a golden pinky.

text: Encegon ƛ’iray reku roɣnosi, čanyas šilabi maħor roƛixno.
translation: They took away a little more manure from above and a goat's horns appeared.

text: Hemeł mixƛ’o bˤeƛ’es reqenno adäz boc’no aħo oƛixno.
translation: At that time, a shepherd appeared who drove a flock of sheep ahead.

text: Łˤono äƛiru ɣudƛ’ogon ziru ɣunoxorno bayn aɣixor qˤaƛin, kurnäy där xexoy kuro, yäłi ɣunno rutirno minnan nełde sadaq bac’a yołƛin.
translation: On the third day, the fox came to the tree again and shouted to the bird: "Throw me down your nestling, or else I will throw the tree over and eat you together with it!"

text: Boƛƛ’o zaman bik’izaƛ’ortow, meɣu xizor yutin yayn.
translation: Before much time had passed, the udder came back.

text: Žedu xabaryayxno teqno, bełiqa baruqor eƛin, mi uži bełiƛ’ egir, ža bełiƛ’ ik’inč’inäy, q’ˤuyzadäzay hudun elur ħak’omƛer šebin räqinč’iƛin.
translation: When he heard them talking, the hunter said to his wife, "Let the son on a hunt, if he won't go hunting, we won't have anything for our khinkali anyway."

text: Xizyogon oƛƛosini uži łic’oxno, debi baru yoł, yedu di yowa yayƛin.
translation: Then the middle son intevened: "You have a wife, I have to marry her!"

text: Xizaɣor yiła harizi rodin nesiq, mi howža žek’u exurnäy, deber di barułun yičanƛin.
translation: Finally she asked him: "When you kill that man, I will be your wife."

text: Žan eƛin ozƛäy bok’ełno.
translation: Having said this, it flew out of sight.

text: Nełaƛ’no cey zek’ beginanu.
translation: And even then the eagle didn't get startled.

text: ʕArabuzan yayn Ismaʕileł adäza yičin.
translation: Arabuzan came before Ismail.

text: Dahawgon q’ʷarid oqno uži, yedu šebi däq roqä, šida di ceyq ʕenekizi oqinč’uƛin.
translation: The boy became a little sad, [saying:] "What happened to me; why didn't I listen to the eagle?"

text: Łäs q’ut’un bic’no xizor butin nexzey, ħonbi dandir rik’in c’oxsiƛax, ceyes k’onč’aqaygon ret’un reƛno rik’isƛax.
translation: He filled the jug with water and when when they returned, the mountains went together and closed and some flesh came off the eagle's leg.

text: Idur aynosi, yisä Paxrudinqor eƛin, bˤeƛ’ hohomłix ocä xeca rayƛin.
translation: When he came home, he said to Pakhrudin: "The sheep must be left on the threshing floor in the cool air."

text: Łˤono äƛiru ɣudƛ’ogon č’urqˤotown q’ˤanon bežet’ani esnabi hunix boqno, yaqˤułgon yiqixana eluq yok’ek’anix sistow sis ƛäxuruni xanes kidƛin.
translation: The third day then, in the morning, the two older brothers went on their way: "Today we might succeed in stealing the single remaining daughter from the king."

text: Rigutow riguƛin rok’ƛ’orno rayn, maħorno yoqno yiła guluzaƛ’ay q’ayn boɣno pikru bodin, Ražbadinqo iłäsi iłewa yeda där iyäƛin.
translation: "Very good", she thought, came outside and took the articles off the horse and thought: "He looks like Razhbadin."

text: Ɣunoxor baynosi, osaxorno bizin cey ƛ’iräy ƛ’iyor tupäsi hil biłen nexno ɣunoł c’oxno.
translation: When they came to the tree, the eagle got up into the height, came downwards from above like a gun's bullet and pounced on the tree.

text: K’uruč’ k’eč’ ɣudi rocu, buq bay.
translation: All's well that ends well.

text: Hemełƛ’o ceyä eƛin, hi debi deber k’onč’aƛ’osi reƛno, yedun yałza caƛo, ƛ’irigon debez min zam-zames łi riħo, däzno hemedurtow riħoƛin.
translation: Then the eagle said: "Take the meat of your own leg and stick it where it used to be! And smear holy water on yours and smear it also on mine."

text: Bełiquza nełaq, nazay ža t’or yayäƛin esirnosi, hemedur eniwn yexun, best’alłiƛ’no ƛexun bet’erbaqi bodanix maħor yoqäsiƛin esin.
translation: When the hunters asked her, from where she came, she said that her mother died and she lived in orphanhood and went outside to make a living.

text: Elaygon izin ik’in yeda hemełatow iħuz lilyox qoqbi yałza mečoƛ’or ayn.
translation: He got up from there, went along that river bank and came to a place where a cliff was.

text: ƛˤorłäy xizor utin aynosi, nesir barun užin besunanu.
translation: When [the husband] came back from the war, he didn't find his wife and son.

text: Xizaɣor, ƛ’iri baru yutizaƛ’, xediw mešori bexada razi oqno.
translation: Finally, because the wife convinced him, the husband agreed to slaughter the heifer.

text: Aqƛin, eƛin roƛik’no žaƛer q’ˤurin teƛno, q’ˤida ičirno.
translation: "You may." she said, produced a chair, put it under the boy and sat him down.

text: Hudun, xizaɣor aħozä biyrno čanya bik’ixosi moči.
translation: Nevertheless the shepherd finally found out the place where the goat went to.

text: Yaqˤułno yołƛax Bac’ali aderzoxortow čanyabi kikix, sasaq roxirno, neširaɣor žedar žedu rayn.
translation: Today, it's said, Batsali is living just as before feeding the goats, chased the in the morning and they came back towards the evening by themselves.

text: Žedudäɣor ik’ałxor ħal anu deyƛin, xanä mušin kurno eƛin.
translation: "I do not have the strength to go to them," the king sighed and said.

text: Di debi kid, mi dey eniw yiča yoł howžäy soderƛin.
translation: From now on, I am your daughter and you are my mother.

text: Č’irin č’iki ɣudi rocu, buq bay.
translation: All's well that ends well.

text: Ža kid idutow yesun.
translation: The girl was at home.

text: Yizi nexxo bukayrun, žas babiw žedus hunärƛin nexƛ’oräy, užä xexłiƛ’ ħumukuliƛ’ay q’ayn boɣno bazargan xizor utirno.
translation: When the boy's father came on their way as he saw them coming, the boy quickly took the goods off the camel and the merchant went back.

text: Bełiqanä nełas äƛirun rodin ža axar begirno.
translation: The hunter did what it said and let her into his belly.

text: Nełƛ’ay neširu, sasiłnosi, rečuƛertow nexnäy rigu ana deberƛin.
translation: Above that, wouldn't it be better for you if you come into the cattleshed after it got evening?"

text: C’ik’iw qizanyos q’ʷariłi C’irduxƛ’or bˤoƛ’un.
translation: All the family's sadness fell unto Tsirdukh.

text: Hoboy eli sidqo sisno biłis, däƛ’ ƛ’iräy urɣelno bok’ełsiƛin.
translation: Now we became similar to one another and the sorrow went away from me."

text: Hemedur anƛ’ima baħnayn žeduq sosikin bełi biqinanu.
translation: So they walked around for a week and at first didn't get any prey.

text: Žigoƛ’ay ɣun q’ˤunader č’ˤaƛiłno roƛƛi regin.
translation: This time the tree broke apart into two halves.

text: Gugyoq ričin łi ħaƛa koƛ’ixanuzogon žek’a eƛin, di ʕumruł sid žek’ur ħaƛa łi teƛinč’uƛin.
translation: The man who couldn't drink the water on his back said: "I didn't give water to anyone for drinking, in my life."

text: Bayn yizi aziroyn xalq’imołäy berobi raqru ħonƛ’or.
translation: They came to the mountain, which arose from the ice of the frozen people.

text: Nesi žoyä nełar boƛik’no čantay aluk’ani heneš teƛno.
translation: The boy gave her the white apple that he took out of his pocket.

text: Elay oqno nexno yeda idur ayn.
translation: He went away from there and came to his home.

text: Nełaƛ’no razi oqinč’ey, xanä Qartay nesider yayrno gurow kidbabi regärinč’iƛin sisgon žeduł adäza misal erno.
translation: Then once again the king didn't agree, he put another problem before him: "I won't send girls to you, except for if you bring Qartay."

text: Bišadyoƛ’orƛin yizä reƛ’a regirƛ’oräy, Beqes ʕUneyzatä xizor roɣrerno yiziq, dahaw zamanał bičiƛin.
translation: When they reached for the food with their hands, Sun's Uneyzat ordered them to take them back: "Wait a little!"

text: ƛ’iräy ħulin kurno, bercinawt’a kidbazaƛ’orno rutin, ɣˤayz orezna teł žedu esanadanix roqno.
translation: Having thrown off their feathers, they turned into beautiful girls and began to bathe in the pond of milk.

text: Yeɣeni esiwde sadaq žan yuqˤno, gulun rečuƛ bicin, bežet’ani esnabi bayaniqor idu ezun ičäsi zown.
translation: He hid her together with her younger sister, tied the horse to the shed, and waited at home for his older brothers to come.

text: Zownƛax zown anuƛax ʕoƛno esiwn, žeduł tełgon sis esiyn.
translation: Once upon a time there were seven brothers, and among them was one sister.

text: Ža kidno esnazade xecin, yaraɣno ħiroq caƛin, guluƛ’no zown Ismaʕil oqno ik’in sida ʕaƛar ayn.
translation: Ismail left that girl with her brothers, threw his weapon over his shoulder, mounted his horse, rode off and came to a village.

text: Wizirä xanqor eƛin, hemeła qalamä teł sis aɣi yoł, nełasgo bik boqnäy, debez bokor ʕeze baqƛin.
translation: The vizier said to the king, "In that fortress there is a bird, if you can get its hide, it will be enough for your coat."

text: Elo nazosno daʕba boqno, howži yisi xanqor eƛin:
translation: There everyone argued and then they said to the king:

text: Łox esirno dandir žawab biqinč’izaƛ’, yizirabi kecsi howžiƛin, ɣoɣoräk’ru ħos risno užizasƛin eƛin kidbazas meč’oƛ’no eqerno, yik’in kecno.
translation: Because she had asked three times without getting an answer, [saying] "Now they're sleeping", she took the red-hot stick and - thinking it were the boys - put it onto the necks of the girls and went to sleep.

text: Šebi royłin yeda uži ik’inč’ey ičinanu.
translation: No matter what to do, the boy had to go.

text: Sida quł ečruni ɣˤanaza maħor yaqrus riłen rodin, xalno bodin yičin.
translation: One day the old woman pretended to go outside, but waited and payed attention.

text: Ozzaq ža yiħinosi, Ismaʕiler č’ura rukayn, hudun yisis ozurabi rošun c’oxno, iyxanusi iłe oqno.
translation: Ismail smeared it on his eyes and could see again, but nevertheless, his eyes became slanted and he was like someone unknown.

text: Xexłiƛ’ t’ay xizor ik’iƛin, qˤaƛanayn žedu q’ebedzo užiqor.
translation: Get back away quickly!", they yelled at the smith's son.

text: Sasaqosi elo baɣmaz egin ik’ix zawru žek’uqor yisi doxturä eƛin, mi äƛiruce mesed neƛan deber di, där šeƛ’un rown mi nexnäyƛin.
translation: In the morning the doctor said to the man passing through the garden, "If you will bring me clothing, I will give you as much gold as you say."

text: Onoču sosisi begirxanuƛin inkarno boyn bičäsi zown, yaqˤułno łoħrodär bik’iƛin.
translation: "I won't let you in", the hen rejected at first, "Go to the frog again, today!"

text: Hič’č’a adäza bäqiruni guluƛ’no zown, xanes uži hawaƛ’ k’oƛin ik’in t’awus bičixozoni qoqxor.
translation: He took the first horse and rode to the cliff where the peacock lives.

text: Hemeła quł Okur čuqˤno, yisi užä nesiqay ʕilmu yiqärułi.
translation: That day, Oku noticed that the boy took away the science from him.

text: Best’al eniwn, bužin erno nełade sadaq egäruni žek’un guluzaz maħizaqorno bicin ʕaƛay maħor boxirno.
translation: The step-mother and the man she sent together with her, who was put hope into, were tied to horses' tails and were chased out of the village.

text: Kidbä eƛin, žan nesis ruyxor yołƛin.
translation: His daughter said: "That of him is true."

text: Sida quł, ɣudod, maħazayzo ɣuɣaymo yedu moƛaxay č’arik’no.
translation: One day at midnight a noise from far outside woke her up from her sleep.

text: Yeženigon esiya, šebin resun yisirƛin, bak’ima tełäyn begirno aɣi q’ˤidar kurnosi, nełłäy qˤirič’ roqno.
translation: The older sister said "He found something!", let the bird out of the coop and threw it on the ground, so he turned into a pair of scissors.

text: Elo sida ixiw aħinoƛno biqin łˤono q’ˤim yołäsi bikori č’ač’aƛix yisir bukayn.
translation: There he saw a three-headed snake that got under a big rock and was squeaking there.

text: Užä nesä nesis murad esinosi, besura hoqoƛno iqirno, raładyoz iƛ’aqaɣor ižin ža.
translation: After the boy had told his wish, the fish took him into its mouth and carried him to the bottom of the sea.

text: Baq’ˤu xalq’i bexurxo bukaynosi, ƛäxuru boxin bik’in.
translation: Having seen the many killed people, the remaining ones ran away.

text: Sis aždaħes xexoy biqirno ent’or bownäy, neła ʕazara roqˤuma igor nexa žek’u egirxosi anuƛin.
translation: If you take away one of the dragon's youngs and bring it here, it will not let any man come closer than a thousand steps.

text: Babiya žaqor eƛin, elin bežet’a bizin ži, deberno maʕišat betix boqzaƛ’, q’ebedłiƛ’or ruhun oqƛin.
translation: The father said to his son: "We are getting old now, when you begin to want [to make] a fortune, learn to work as a smith!"

text: K’emotya kidno yuqˤek’no yičin zown nełas eniw.
translation: The girl's mother locked her away in a chest.

text: Budumä äsiru xabaryoƛ’ xan c’aq’ ʕažaibno oqno, nesir micxirno teƛno, ƛ’irigon bigu saɣyatno bodin, žan egirno.
translation: The king was very surprised about what the muezzin told, he gave him money and nice presents and let him go.

text: Xanes sis ħalikataw madaxosi žek’u zown.
translation: The king had a mean guard.

text: Neła qułno idur q’ʷaridaw yeda ayzaƛ’, bara esirno, yaqˤułgon łinax q’ʷarid oqnoƛin.
translation: That day, because he came home said, his wife asked: "Why did you come home sad today as well?"

text: Howžin hemedurtow yołƛax žedu.
translation: And it's said they still are like that.

text: Ža balah žeduƛ’ ƛ’iräy bok’ek’zaƛ’ ahli ʕuraw ɣˤaɣˤun bizin, nełƛ’ay soder žeda Zeyes Aħya narnokin egirinč’ey, nesis adabno, ħurmatno bodin ičira yołƛin ƛ’iri risno.
translation: The people were very glad that he had taken the misfortune from them, and after that, they didn't want to let Bear Ear go anywhere, gave him respect and esteem and insisted in keeping him.

text: Ža łi micxiryoƛ’or rutin.
translation: And this water turned into money/jewelry.

text: Žan eƛin Tawadi maħor oqno.
translation: Tavadi said that and went outside.

text: Hemeła zamanał, łaci yiłe yaƛˤo neładerno yik’in, ʕArabuzanä xanes kid yown Ismaʕilzo guluƛ’ caƛin.
translation: In that moment, Arabuzan went to him quickly as the wind and brought the king's daughter and threw it onto Ismail's horse.

text: Xizyogon howžatow geresni hibo aždaħä ɣoɣorik’ƛ’orey, C’iɣruk’ä ħamori kurno.
translation: Again, when the dragon heated that iron staff, Tsighruk coughed.

text: Ža kaɣat yaynosi babiya uži egirno, ciqqorno yižin esiwn yexurno nełas rok’u däq rukaranix ħamayn roɣno raƛin.
translation: After reading the letter, the father sent his son: "Lead your sister into the forest and kill her, take away her heart out of her chest and bring it here to show it to me."

text: Sida quł eƛnoq yaq’ˤu, žek’u maħor oqełxanuzo ħalał, isi yayn.
translation: One day in winter it snowed so hard, that people couldn't even go outside.

text: Esa koƛ’inč’izaƛ’, heresi mec esa baynč’i.
translation: "When you don't know what to say, you shouldn't tell a lie.

text: ʕAƛoz xalq’imon, ʕagarłän t’ok’ow nełqay reƛ’an kurno, howłaƛ’aygon žuk yičiƛin.
translation: The people from the village and the relatives all took their hands from her[, saying]: "Live worse than that!"

text: Xexzaqno ħaƛurno, nełarno teƛno yeda uyrax äƛirugon łi ric’a ik’in.
translation: He gave the child to drink, and he went to give her water for the fourth time.

text: Xan, di nexäsi yoł čara, res anusi debi kid däz Goqix esiranixƛin”.
translation: "King, I have come to you in order to ask for your daughter to be married to my Goqi."

text: Oh, mi nedurä q’urumsaq zownƛin, bizirno bok’nosi, yisägon yedu bečnabi cuciłace raƛ’moƛxor šiširno.
translation: "Oh, what villain are you?!", he lifted and beat the dragon into the ground until his knees were stuck.

text: Sidaquł sasaqosi akbar roɣa di minaraƛ’or izis.
translation: One day in the morning I went up the minaret to call for the prayer.

text: ʕOmoyzaƛ’osi cirabin riyrno, boc’i boqno ciqaɣor bik’in.
translation: Having learned the names of the donkeys, the wolf went into the forest.

text: Nasin xalq’i bezun ƛexun, sosisi yisanixƛin beč’ułno, nesä baq’ˤu micxir esizaƛ’, ɣamas yisełxosi žek’u oƛixno anu.
translation: All the people came out to look and buy it immediately, but when he said how much money it costs, there was no man who could buy the box.

text: Yeda, łukin tełxorno goƛ’inč’ey, best’alawni iłe kawumox hečk’er ƛexun.
translation: Because no one invited him inside, he stood there upright at the gate like an orphan.

text: Kusa urɣizi oqno, šida ža ʕabasi yaqˤuł nesä cuƛer kuräƛin, hudun nesir nełas maʕnatow biyn anu.
translation: Kusa thought: "Why did he throw that coin up today?", nevertheless he could not know its sense.

text: Łiboƛ’ iziƛ’orey, mi kidbez hut’-mˤalär äzuno, yałi nełaz bercinłä akik’no mi q’ˤidar ʕoƛ’arƛin, mołin yiła yisi užiq.
translation: In the fight, don't look into the girl's face, or else her beauty will tire you and make you fall down.", she explained to the boy.

text: Q’ˤida muqno boɣno ʕantawnä nełaq riyrno, enda bexurno ent’aɣor yoqnäy yexura yołƛin.
translation: The stupid one drew a line on the floor and explained to her: "When you cross over this line, I will kill you."

text: Ax roqace reƛ rac’a egirno xecinäy, neƛanƛin eƛin užä.
translation: "If you let me eat a belly-full of meat, then I'll give it to you.", said the boy.

text: Nełƛ’ay soder žedur žedu bič’zin boqno, t’ok’ow mamalay iħuxor łoħroderno bik’ix zown anu, netin quqäsi gurow qacan rayrxo zown anu.
translation: After that, they understood each other, the rooster didn't go to the frog at the river anymore and always brought dry firewood only.

text: Kidbä eƛin, xanes łiboqan nesistow kid yołƛin, nełde łiboƛ’ izanix mitow oqa ayƛin.
translation: The girl said: "The king's wrestler is his daughter. You have to get into a wrestling fight with her.

text: Yoƛo ƛˤeb yik’izaƛ’ortow yizir užin odin.
translation: After less then a year had passed, a son was born to them.

text: Rätiƛin, žawab teƛno xanez užä.
translation: "I want to.", answered the prince.

text: Sida quł xexzade k’ešonayƛ’oräy, sida xexzas yisiq žirbabi recun.
translation: One day, while playing with other children, one child broke its ribs.

text: Yisiz gugyoƛ’or bˤaƛ’un butni bayn.
translation: And a burka fell over his back.

text: Diƛa mi yowanƛin, - eƛin Musa, - debeztow babiya yegirnäy”.
translation: "I will marry you", said Musa, "if you father lets you go."

text: Xanes kidgon ʕiyayxtow ʕiyayx yičäsi zown roda šebinon łaħin.
translation: The king's daughter, who was just crying and crying, didn't know what to do.

text: Bayn ziru elus ħon sis xecin, žek’us q’ˤano xecin, iħubin, ciqbin xecin sida keroqor bayn.
translation: The fox passed our one mountain, another two, rivers and forests and came to a ravine.

text: Xanzo madaxor yaynosi, yiła teł iduɣor xanqor qˤaƛin:
translation: When she came to the king's court, she shouted to the king inside the house:

text: Elorgon yeda moƛa iqirno.
translation: There the sleep took him away.

text: Yeda iduɣor nexa keze oqno sida žopono ciqłäza egin.
translation: In order to get to his home, he had to walk through a dense forest.

text: Ža nesiqor äƛiru šebin rodaniƛäy, xanä nesiqor nesä äƛiru gulun, tupin teƛno.
translation: For doing what he said, the king gave him the horse and the rifle he had asked for.

text: Howži yedu micxir łar biča bayƛin bara esirnosi, yisä där biča bayƛin eƛin.
translation: When the wife asked, "For whom is that money now?", he said: "It must be for me."

text: Žedun adäz roc’no Alimaħama ħonƛ’ay ƛ’iyor oqno.
translation: Alimagomed drove them ahead and went down the mountain.

text: Elor baynosi, yizi q’ˤanon bik’in hiɣa bixił kecno.
translation: When they came there, the two of them went into the hayloft to sleep in the hay.

text: Dahawgon ik’inosi neside guluzaxosi aħo keze oqno.
translation: When he went a little further, a shepherd with horses came towards him.

text: Nesä hut’bi ragi-rac’xotow išanixno oqno ʕAt’idzo čanta yałru kinaw šebin łiyrno.
translation: Licking his lips, he began to eat and finished everything that was in Atid's pocket.

text: Hunix bik’iƛ’oräytow, raƛ’moƛ tełxor gondu yesun.
translation: While they were going on their way, the found a hole that lead inside the earth.

text: Qˤišdäy sodersi zaman baynosi, yizirabi rešt’izi boqanix sidaɣ sanaʕataw mečoƛ’or bayn.
translation: When the afternoon had come, they came to a comfortable place to take a rest.

text: K’oxäƛirun ʕaq’lus xalq’i butirnosi, xanä cuƛer ʕabasin kurno hemedurtow eƛin, k’ešonad mi, pakmos ʕabasi, łinarnotow hunarno anu debi, siskin šebinon deber roda koƛ’ixanuƛin.
translation: The second time the king gathered his advisors, he also threw a coin up and said likewise: "Play, copper coin, you have no talent to whatsoever, you don't know what to do!"

text: Kidbä gugu caƛiza mečoƛ’ ac yoqno.
translation: On the place where the girl threw her back to, a door appeared.

text: Butnoƛ’ pun bok’no äčiru žek’ur bazarganbi igoxxo bukayruntow, q’ecno rutirno, nełaƛ’ ƛ’iri k’ukno eqerno, yizi bayace butnoƛ’ puƛ’orno c’oxno, k’ukyoƛ’or ezun ičin.
translation: When the person lying on his burka saw the merchants approaching, he collected dirt, put a cap over it and lied down on the burka again until they came; then he waited watching his cap.

text: Nesiqorno nexanƛin eƛin, mesedyos saħ neƛnäy.
translation: "I will come," she said also to him, "when you give me a Sah of gold."

text: Sis zown eniwn užin.
translation: There was a mother and her son.

text: Mežuz pikruxor, žedu łˤorłäyn tełäy baħarčiłi yołäsi šebi iča ayx:
translation: "In your opinion, who of the three must be the bravest:

text: Adäza q’ˤidan ičirno Bilq’isdä nesiq esirno, howła ɣudzał mi meq’aw šebin rodäsi zowwäƛin.
translation: Bilqisdi sat him down before her and asked him: "Have you done wrong things in these days?"

text: Bełiqu nełaƛ’ razi oqno.
translation: The hunter agreed on that.

text: Doxturza nesiqor eƛin, budalazaqor (łarašaw ɣˤanazas axʷay bäžiru xexbi) bexada bäqinč’iru bełis reƛ rac’näy, mi saɣ aqƛin.
translation: The doctors said to him: "If you eat meat of a deer which did not get slaughtered for the budalays, you will get cured."

text: Žedu ʕuraw ɣˤaɣˤu rizin ƛirbabi bayzaƛ’.
translation: They got very happy, when guests came.

text: Debez eniwq baqru hunarä däq boqxanuƛin ɣirno bisno iħaɣor łi rowanix bik’in.
translation: "Your mother's talent isn't mine", [he said], took a sieve and went to the river to fetch water.

text: Ražbadinno goƛ’in nesiqor eƛin, eli howži halmaɣbin sadaq bik’a bayxosi, gulubi ħadur rodoƛin.
translation: He called Razhbadin and said to him: "My friends and I must leave now, prepare the horses."

text: Där sis debi haraƛ’ teqra bäti, behizi roqnäyƛin”.
translation: I want to hear your voice, if I may!"

text: Deber mi ħak’o t’alam bodaniƛäy eynodanix debi zaman baynƛin.
translation: The time has come for you to work for the khinkali yourself."

text: Žeda elo sis ixiw ziru č’aq’ˤirno.
translation: They killed a big fox there.

text: Šebin roƛor risno yedu suħmat royx mi.
translation: You took the thing to the middle and organize this celebration.(?)

text: Miƛ’iryoz ɣunoƛ’ ƛ’iri heqäsi yisir bercinawt’a aɣbi rukayn.
translation: He saw beatiful birds sitting on the peach tree.

text: Nełagon žedas xawoc’in rodin, hi rac’oƛin, babiwł adäza eqerno.
translation: And she made and omelette out of them and put it before his father: "Here, eat!"

text: Elo žeduz ɣˤutkar yaynosi, kid idu yesun.
translation: When she came into the house there, she found the girl at home.

text: Ħibaƛ’ k’oƛin bayn zeyä esirno, šebi deber däqay retixƛin.
translation: The bear came running and asked, "What do you want from me?"

text: Ža kaɣat kidbeqor teƛƛ’oräy Tawadä eƛin, di maqˤir ok’łacen yičin yedu kaɣat Ražbadinqor teƛ.
translation: While he gave the letter to the girl, Tavadi said: "Until I got far away, wait and give this letter to Razhbadin.

text: Xanä žedu łˤonon esnabi nesä nesiz iduɣorno bižin paraq’atno boyn, žedude adäz bišʷan ħaƛin ža maħor oqno.
translation: The king invited the three brothers into his home, made an arrangement(?), organized a meal for them and went outside.

text: Xedya, ƛˤoreł ik’a adäza, sida božaraw žek’ude sadaq barun užin bertenyołxor begirno.
translation: The husband, before he went to war, sent his wife and son to the marriage together with a reliable man.

text: C’iɣruk’a eƛin, däz eniya moƛał iħäy ɣired łi rowx rukaysƛin.
translation: Tsighruk said: "I dreamed how my mother was bringing water from the river with a sieve."

text: Žedus zownƛax q’ˤimaku, xedyox regiracet’a roqäsi łˤonon kidbabi.
translation: They had three daughters that were in the age of getting married.

text: Sida quł babiya užin goƛ’in nesiqor eƛix, uži, di ži eže izäsi oqzaƛ’, eynoda iyxanu.
translation: One day the father called his son saying to him: "Son, because I have become old now, I can't work anymore.

text: Elo äsuruni žek’u guluƛ’no zown, bˤałno žan bižin ik’iƛ’oräy, sidaɣ mečoƛ’ aħinno yizirno, wˤałzoni dunyalƛ’aɣor oqno, nesiłgon xizay wˤałzoni dunyalƛ’aɣor xanes užin ik’in.
translation: The man he had found got on his horse, and while he got away, he lifted a rock in some place and got down into the underworld, and the prince followed him and went into the underworld as well.

text: Neła bertenyoł din zows, elay izin berten łiyzaƛ’ortow nexa ays yedu imadu mežuq esanix.
translation: I was also there by that marriage and so long as the marriage did not end I had to come to you and tell this tale.

text: Boc’a razi roqno.
translation: The wolves agreed.

text: Hemełƛ’aza oqno esa beɣula mi laɣes maħ yołäsiƛin”.
translation: That might be why you seem to smell like a slave."

text: Eniya užir aq’ˤlu bodin, nagaħ xalq’imo hunizax ruħč’agoyaw šebin q’ʷarid royx resunäy, mi ža xʷasar rodoƛin.
translation: The mother gave the son an advice: "If someone on your way hurts an animal, rescue it!"

text: Sideƛ’ Ħasanƛin, sideƛ’ Ħusenƛin cirabin cedin.
translation: They named one Hassan, and one Hussein.

text: Hemedur Okä babiw mak’ur odin guludetow sadaq moƛ’oqˤoyn łera ʕazara łera bišomra ɣˤurušyoƛ teƛanix, dicegon užä moƛ’oqˤoy täƛnoƛin eƛinłin.
translation: So Oku persuaded the father to sell him the horse together with the bridle for 5,500 rubles, no matter how much the boy told him not to sell the bridle.

text: Q’arƛ’o izin maħorno oqno ezuzaƛ’, yisir rocasi ɣudin resux, c’oxäsigon boboru buqno bukayx.
translation: When he got up early and went outside, he found a clear day and the hot sun shining.

text: Nesił xizay ʕiyayn, däzno ozzałäy kanłi bissi, uyn ečriłsiƛin, babiya zigara boɣno.
translation: I bemoaned his loss, and lost my eyesight; I indeed got old.", complained the father.

text: Łox äƛirugon yeda ʕuqła ik’inosi, bełiquz užä zeyes mus bik’urno.
translation: After the hunter's son went hiding for the third time, he burnt the bear's hair.

text: Din žeduƛ’ xal bodin ičäsi zows.
translation: And I watched them as well.

text: Amma žoyä esinanu meɣyoqor šebi eƛa rayxosi yałrułi.
translation: But the boy didn't tell what one has to say to the udder.

text: Bazarganä bisno adäza ħaƛin nucos bic’äsi q’ut’u.
translation: The merchant took a jug filled with honey and pushed it in front of him.

text: Buyxor xabar esoƛin, nesä baħaray puħor yiq’ˤakin yegirxanu.
translation: Tell the truth!", he didn't even let the bride come closer to his side.

text: Nełałäy bäk’iru iya šet’urtow oreznabi rodin, howlortow aždaħno bexun.
translation: The blood that came out of it, formed ponds all around, and there the dragon died.

text: ʕOmoyqor cek’ä maqˤäytow qˤaƛin, were, were däł xizay k’oƛin zey nexxo ac yaɣˤo.
translation: The goat kid shouted to the donkey from far away: "Well, well, a bear is running behind me, open the door!"

text: Elo äsuruni žek’a išno xizor ƛäxuruni šebin eƛuk’yaɣorno rutirno, ħurženya tełxor eqerno.
translation: The man he found there ate and gathered the leftovers into a leather sack and put it into his bag.

text: Sida zamanałäza yeda Beqes ʕUneyzates ɣˤutkoł adäzar ayn.
translation: After a while he came before the house of Sun's Uneyzat.

text: Sida quł ciqaɣor qaca roda uži ik’inosi, nesir aɣzaz xexoyzadäɣor ɣunoq bizix bikori bukayn.
translation: One day, when the boy went to the forest to get firewood, he saw a snake getting up a tree towards some bird chicks.

text: Ozzałäy č’uran risno esun yisir babiw.
translation: He found the father having gone blind.

text: Rigutow rigu hoboyƛin, rok’ƛ’orno rayn žoyä ɣunoqosi aluk’anin, c’udanin henešno but’in oqno xanzo kidbedäɣor ik’in.
translation: "Very well, now.", thought the boy, plucked a white and a red apple from the tree and went to the princess.

text: Nesis zown łˤono uži.
translation: He had 3 sons.

text: Boƛik’u xanez maħišatyoce micxirno teƛno, yisno xanä ɣamas kidbez idu eqerno.
translation: The king gave half of his fortune, bought the box and put it into his daughter's room.

text: Zownƛax zown anuƛax sis xan.
translation: Once upon a time there was a king.

text: C’irdux bˤeƛ’es aħołun keze oqno.
translation: Tsirdukh got to work as a shepherd.

text: Neła łera ukrułäy sis Žamaler teƛsi, sideni Arsener teƛsi, sidenigon Pat’imater teƛsi.
translation: Of those five silver (coins) I gave one to Jamal, another one to Arsen and another one to Fatima.

text: Howži xaner retäsi zown neła aɣez bikyos boko bodanix.
translation: Now the king wanted to make a coat out of that bird's hide.

text: Xizyo žeda eƛin:
translation: Afterwards, they said:

text: Žedur xexbi rodix zownanu.
translation: They didn't get any children.

text: Łˤonon užibi bezunč’ey Tawadiƛ’ ƛ’irirno bik’in, nesiqor babiya yegaru kaɣat teƛno.
translation: Without looking, the three sons went to Tavadi and gave him the letter sent by their father.

text: Šida ža debeq łek’irełxanuƛin, hoq’ˤot’ani esnabi bik’in.
translation: "Why can't you stir it?", the other brothers went to him.

text: Izin neł t’asmar kakičurin royn, elosi łi maħor čˤaƛin.
translation: He got up to that bowl, did his ablution, and poured the water out again.

text: Sida zamanałäy, Ħusenä Ħasan hunix ik’iƛ’tow iqirno.
translation: After some time, Hussein caught Hassan while he was walking on the way.

text: Xizyogon bayn žedu ʕoƛiran esnaza nełaq esirno, ya le q’orolay, debi užitowwa ukayxanu, na yoł žaƛin.
translation: Then came the seven brothers and asked her: "Well, hey widow, we don't see your son, where is he?"

text: Neła quł neširutow Ražbadinä dibirno goƛ’in Tawadiƛ’ mahar erno.
translation: That day in the evening Razhbadin invited the imam and they set up the wedding for Tavadi.

text: Bik’in žedu mesedyos nak’ila yałruni kidbez eniwder bayn.
translation: They went on and came to the mother of the girl with the golden pinky.

text: Žan hemedurtow nełaz esnazaden yuqˤno, šeƛ’un nesä nesis šeƛ’un, gulun rečuƛ buqˤno, idu q’ˤidan ičin žedu bayaniqor ezun ičäsi zown.
translation: He likewise hid her next to her sisters, put on his own clothes, hid the horse in the shed, sat down at home and waited for them to come.

text: Žedur užin odin, nesiƛ’ Ħon-Bahadurƛin ci cedinƛax.
translation: A son was born to them and they named him "Mountain~Hero".

text: Esin šebi, xecin šebi sis zown xan.
translation: What is to be said, what is to be left out -- there was a king.

text: Ħon-bahadurä sosi rak’ru mač’id łˤonon q’ˤim nełas reč’no roɣno, k’ox äƛiru rok’nosi hemedurtow łˤonosinigon q’ˤim reč’no roɣno, łox äƛirugon regirnosi, aždaħes oč’inon q’ˤim reč’no.
translation: Mountain~Hero hit him once with the dagger and cut three heads off of him; when he hit him for the second time, he cut three of his heads off as well; and when he did it for the third time then, he cut off all the nine heads.

text: C’iɣruk’es xizyogon aždaħtow bok’ek’anixƛin rok’ƛ’or pikru bayn.
translation: Tsighruk thought of stealing from the dragon again.

text: Ganƛ’on zown neširur yiził xizay yik’in, raładyoł taqˤorno boqa biynč’ey sidaɣ gogaƛ’ rešt’izi boqäsi besun.
translation: And on the bull.calf, he went behind them in the evening and found them taking a rest on a meadow somewhere, not knowing how to get over the sea.

text: Xediw maħor ok’äłrungon, ɣamasya tełäy maħorno boqno elay boxin bazarganbin bok’ełno.
translation: Right after the husband went outside, the merchants got out of the box and ran away.

text: Didur, šebi roqäƛin nesiq esirnosi, nesä heresi esin, raładno k’ek’-bik’no gaman butin debi baru ɣanq’izi yoqsi, uži däq xʷasar oda k’ʷeze oqsiƛin.
translation: When he asked: "How and what happened?", he lied: "The sea was moved and the boat turned around, your wife drowned; I was able to rescue the boy."

text: Dibir haqa ogun ƛexun, eƛa rožin riqinč’ey.
translation: The mullah was gaping in surprise and had no word to say.

text: Yeženä esin nełaq hemeła ziys ƛuzabi eqärza hisonär yezanix yik’iƛin.
translation: The old one told her: "Go look at the trough were you put that cow's bones!"

text: Eli keca adäz, eniya bexayn baħirno c’uda ƛeli neƛxosi.
translation: "Before we slept, our mother slaughtered, cooked and gave us the hot lamb.

text: Hemełƛ’o kidbä eƛin, nediw hunar yołäsi t’or siskin uži ayäsi zownč’uƛin, uyraxäƛirugon ʕuqła yeda egirnäy rigu räči yisirƛin.
translation: The girl broke in, "None of the boys with such potentials came here. It will be good to let him hide for the fourth time."

text: Ečruni žek’a esin yiłaq, dey łˤonon uži tusnaq’ä teł yoł, žedu maħor boɣanix micxir anusi di ƛexäsi yołƛin.
translation: The old man told her: "My three brothers are in prison, and I don't have the money to buy them free."

text: ƛ’irir yayru žaz eniya eƛin, xexzas žirbabi recurxo ičinč’ey, yok’ek’no yäžiruni debez mi esiws ʕadlu šida boyxanuƛin.
translation: The boy's mother came and said: "There child's ribs were not broken, why don't you fulfill your stolen sister's orders?"

text: Kurno užä c’eruc’as hil, ciqaɣorno rezun durimoƛ’ boc’an rik’in, zirun žedaqay xʷasar boqno.
translation: The boy shot the arrow from his bow, the wolves ran towards the forest and the fox got rescued from them.

text: Šebi ža nediw baħarči oƛixa, owo ža ent’o däderƛin”, - amru bodin xanä neła q’orolaqor.
translation: "Who is this brave young man? Bring him here to me!", ordered the king the widow.

text: Zira bigza ħalał ƛ’en bagin, zamanaƛ’azay xexoybin roɣno.
translation: The fox licked enough malt and after some time, it got youngs.

text: Xizyogon žedar hunix k’et’u keze boqno.
translation: Then they met a cat on their way.

text: Kusa ʕuraw ɣˤaɣˤu izin, nece beže maxšel yołäsi žek’u kumakyor iqizaƛ’ƛin.
translation: Kusa got very happy because he got such a skilled man for help.

text: Maħor äk’irusno rodin, acxazay ezun ičin.
translation: He did as if he went outside and looked behind the door.

text: Šebi deber roqno, šida mi šiqˤonyayxƛin?”
translation: "What happened to you, why are you limping?"

text: Elo ɣeduƛ q’ˤida ičäsi yałzay, yisir taqˤaza oc’ino ħumukulin bown hemeł buħima nexxo oc’ino žek’u ukayn.
translation: While he was sitting in the shade, he saw ten men with ten camels coming in the heat.

text: Howži xaner riyn ža äɣurułi.
translation: Now the king knew that he had been defeated.

text: Xanä wazirzaqor amru boyn:
translation: The king commanded his viziers:

text: Ɣudin qˤašin, sasaqosi zamanno bayn, buq boƛixno.
translation: The day dawned, the morning time came and the sun appeared.

text: Hudayzikotow ixiw zigolun yayn, qˤiči rexunč’ey žek’ukin ƛexun anu.
translation: The next day came a big hailstorm and there remained no man whose harvest didn't die.

text: Žeduz kidberno retisƛax esiwq yiłanix, din xec elo ciqqoƛin, yedu babiwq harizi yoqno.
translation: Their daughter wanted to be like her sister and requested before her father: "Leave me there in the forest as well!"

text: ʕAƛoz raʕalłix äxuru žek’u šuša ižin bik’ix yizide dandir žamaʕatyos xalq’i c’oxno.
translation: At the edge of the village, some people from the population came toward them carrying a dead man to bury him.

text: Elo xediwn xecin maħor oqƛ’oräy, Bilq’isdä nesiqor eƛin, deber rac’anixno, ħaƛnixno t’o ʕeziyaw šebin yoł, di yayace t’ay äk’inƛin.
translation: She left the husband there and while she went outside, Bilqisdi said to him: "There is a lot to eat and drink for you here, do not go away until I come back!"

text: Suƛƛi ža yukayrun, Ħusenłäy nesä esirnosi, kidbä elo ɣˤutka teł kinaw raqru šebinno esin, nesä q’ˤida q’ˤiryoł caƛiru c’eruc’az hilmołäzay bešnoyx iyon bukarno.
translation: Immediately when he saw her, he asked for Hussein, and the girl told everything that happened inside the house there, and he showed the blood that was dribbling out of the bow's arrow that was shot down on the floor.

text: Xan c’aq’tow ħikmałiłer egin, nece ʕaq’ilaw yizi xexzar didur di diwan bodanƛin.
translation: The king stood still in astonishment about how he could make a judgement for these so intelligent children.

text: Eniyagon reže sadaq’a rułirno:
translation: And the mother shared large alms:

text: Yizir ža besuroz haqa raładyoz iƛ’aq ukayn.
translation: They saw him in the mouth of the fish at the bottom of the sea.

text: Q’orolaz maduhalłiq sis baru anusi žek’u ičix zown.
translation: In the widow's neighbourhood there was an unmarried man.

text: ʕAƛtown zigaraƛ’ bayn xizyo yisider.
translation: Then the villagers came for condolence.

text: Žeda eniwqor eƛix teqno, bˤaƛo bäk’ino, eli akiłxoƛin.
translation: He heard them say: "Don't go so quickly, we are getting tired!"

text: Babiya esin xanä nesiq ƛ’iri risäru łinałäy.
translation: The father told about what the king told him to fulfil.

text: Adäz keze baqru halmaɣłi iłeda yeda esanƛin rok’ƛ’ort’a rayx zown žas.
translation: The boy thought several times: "Maybe he will be just like the friends he met before."

text: Yisiz rok’ƛ’osi šebin eženi žek’ur riyx.
translation: The old man knew what he thought.

text: Ža nesä risno čantaɣor eqärun, regirno xecin qema.
translation: As soon as he took them and put them into a pocket, it started to rain.

text: Recenyozaz ɣomał cucixäsi yisir cey besun, žeda ƛ’irirno rutin bac’xo zown ža.
translation: He found an eagle burried in an anthill, and they were already climbing upwards to eat it.

text: Di xanłi royxanu.
translation: "I won't be king.

text: Łox äƛiza, užin iqirno ceyä yisiq esirno, ʕuƛ’xo zowäƛin.
translation: On the third time, the eagle took up the boy and asked him: "Were you afraid?"

text: Yisän sida kidbän ʕišq’us łin ħaƛun, yisis bašiqˤoy nełas bašiqor rayn, nełasgon Ismaʕilzo bašiqor raynƛin.
translation: He and some girl drank a love potion, his ring came onto her finger, and hers came on Ismail's finger."

text: Din Tawadin elor baya yołƛin.
translation: Tavadi and I are going to go there."

text: Elay bizin bik’in yizi xanez madaxor bayn.
translation: The left and went on and then came to the king's door.

text: Eženi esiya eƛin:
translation: The eldest brother said:

text: Ha, miyä ža dey tušman, oqčo howži maħorƛinno!” - eƛin yedu aždaħ maħor boqno.
translation: "Well, so you are my enemy, then come out now!", said the dragon and got outside.

text: Nełƛ’ay soder užän, baran kʷarkal anusi ʕumru bižin.
translation: After this, the boy and his wife lead a life without obstacles.

text: Neła quł sasaq, č’urqˤotow, yeda ʕUmar izin oxin.
translation: The next day in the morning, Umar got up and ran away.

text: Maħor boqno łiyzaƛ’ortow, nesä žedu tełxorno biħurno žigon oqno ik’in xanez kidbeq pandur bukaranix.
translation: Before all of them could get outside, he instructed them to go back inside and once again went to the princess to show her the lute.

text: Žigoƛ’ay nełader xanez madaxosini žek’u ayn.
translation: This time the king's guard came to her.

text: Žan bown iduɣor nexƛ’oräy, dandir c’axru xalq’imo sida esirno, sida esirno yiłqay ħaƛanix łi.
translation: And while coming home with it, of the people that she met one asked her, if he could drink some water from her.

text: Qˤašizaƛ’or, nesiz babiwz ɣˤutkoƛ’ay baybik bodin, nazon ʕaƛa yałru ɣˤutkozas akizax ʕeze boqacet’a k’udi besun.
translation: Before dawn, he began at his father's house and there was enough vine in all the houses' windows.

text: Yisił dandir c’axrun eženä eƛin, horo ela gulubi ħažan, yiła sis čuret’ bok’näy ʕoƛno ħon xecixosi, ži di eže izäsi oqzaƛ’, yedu deber biča bayƛin.
translation: Right when he came towards him, the old man said: "Come, let's exchange horses! When I beat the whip, he lets seven mountains behind him; because I have become old now, he shall be yours."

text: Ħalt’in bodan dandi keze roqnäy, barubin rižanƛin, eƛin C’irduxä nełqor.
translation: "When we find some work to do [regularly?], we will find some wives.", said Tsirdukh to her.

text: Nesä žawab teƛno, meža šebigo royłin k’ukyoƛ yałru šebin di mežuq rukarinč’iƛin.
translation: He replied: "Although you do what[ever?](?), I won't show you what I got under the cap."

text: Aždaħä eƛin, yedu mi däz rok’ƛ’ositow šebinwa esirä.
translation: The dragon said: "You have asked exactly what I had on my mind.

text: "Mi yowan yoqinč’ey q’ˤuya ɣˤanabi yowłiri, sosi din ałni debeq bic’reran, nełaq bic’erzaƛ’orƛin".
translation: 'If it happens that I have to marry another woman, I will also first come to you to embrace you, before I embrace her.'

text: Dibirän šidaƛa nesiƛ’ c’aq’ xal bičirno ičix zown.
translation: And the imam for some reason was watching him.

text: Uži yołi isin, qeman raynč’iza paraxat oqno ičäsi zown sasaqosi ɣudi qˤašaceƛin.
translation: The boy however calmly waited where the snow and rain didn't come to until the day set in the next morning.

text: Qˤašizaƛ’or rišin neła rut’ugabi, łˤonoqu äƛiru rut’ugar dahaw moxon ʕeze boqinč’ey, q’ˤuyaniƛ’ay ža q’ˤuna bašice k’ot’ono roqno.
translation: Until dawn she weaved carpets, but the thread was a little too short for the sixtieth carpet, so it became two fingers shorter than the others.

text: Mesedyołäy sis saħ bok’äk’ru iča ayx mežus eɣeni esiwƛin”.
translation: "Your younger brother is the one who stole the one Sah from the gold."

text: Eniya kʷaraħid et’no, ɣired rown łi ħaƛurxo zawru zamanno rok’ƛ’or bayn moƛutow yayxanuƛin, eƛin yisä.
translation: "I cannot sleep because I remember the time that our mother poured water with a colander and brought it to drink in a sieve.", he said.

text: Hunix bik’iƛ’oräy, sasiłnosi yizi nasin sidaɣ łin, c’in, qacan anuza gogaƛ’ rešt’izi boqno.
translation: While they were on their way, it got dark and they all took a rest on a meadow without any firewood, fire or water.

text: Hemedur dandixno yizi.
translation: So they came together.

text: Elay bitor boxin bik’ix boc’in axoł šäširuni xarcelid bix kosix, xizazgon et’unč’ey neła kasiruni bix rutirxo din ik’ix zows, neduräy žagon bˤeƛ’än rac’xo zows.
translation: From there the wolf ran away with the scythe being stuck in the belly mowing grass, but I didn't stay behind but went gathering the mowed grass; so the sheep were eating it.

text: Zownƛax, zown anuƛax sis best’al uži.
translation: Once upon a time there was an orphan boy.

text: ƛ’ˤuƛ’ay łinaƛa haraƛ’ bizirsiƛin, q’orola kid yegirno, šebi elo roqnoƛin yezu-yoƛ’ˤanix.
translation: Because something produced a noise from the roof, the widow sent her daughter to check out what was happening there.

text: Q’ˤunan aɣzas bik bokorno, k’ukyorno ʕeziyaw boqno.
translation: The hide of the two birds was enough for the coat and the cap.

text: Howlo xizyo yizi q’ˤanon esnabi akiłno moƛan biqirno, łerakin ɣudi č’ariłnoanu.
translation: Afterwards these two siblings got tired and caught some sleep, and didn't even woke up for five days.

text: Nedur resunäy, mi di eƛixosi šebin teqerno iči.
translation: "If that's the case, listen carefully to what I am saying!

text: Neła (gula) užiqor eƛin xecin, xizor butin nexƛ’oräy däƛ’ gurow zawnoƛin.
translation: It (the horse) said and left: "When we come back, only ride on me!"

text: Boƛƛo baq’ˤu zaman bik’izaƛ’ortow xaner uži odin.
translation: And before too much time had passed, the king got a son.

text: Neła eƛin, beže taliħ boqsi debi, yaqˤułäy soder mi dey uži yoł, howži debi mi murad eso däqƛin.
translation: She said: "You have good luck, as of today you are my son; now tell me your wish!"

text: Sasaqosi esnazar łuq’zi boqali ceyłäy bäk’iru iyon, nełqay rˤaƛ’uru lelabin rukayn.
translation: In the morning the brothers saw the blood that came out of the wounded eagle and the feathers that fell from it.

text: Mada gulun bicin tełxor ik’anix ac žek’nosi, neła žedu q’ˤimbi taxeƛ ruqˤno.
translation: He tied the horse outside to the gate and when he knocked on the door to go inside, she hid the heads under the sofa.

text: Elay bitor bik’in yizi sida ʕalazanyoz lilyoxor bayn.
translation: The went on and came to the coast of a lake.

text: Yiła esin, bełiqa di iduɣorno bižin, kikin saɣ boysƛin.
translation: He told [him]: "A hunter led me to his home, fed and cured me."

text: Bäziƛin nesä eƛinäy, ža mi däder aƛin.
translation: When he says: 'I will!', bring him to me!"

text: Nełƛ’orsi kʷaxatow Ražbadinno barun Tawadiz žar saɣatyabin rižin Telawäɣor bik’ix.
translation: Soon after that Razhbadin and his wife went to Telavi with presents for Tavadi's son.

text: Barun užin yisä rok’u rayru sida žek’ude sadaq bertenyołxor begirno.
translation: His wife and son he sent to the marriage together with a reliable person.

text: C’iɣruk’ä ixiw ɣamas yodanix bašla bodin.
translation: Tsighruk began to make a big box.

text: Di rac’inč’uƛin, žawab teƛno neła.
translation: "I haven't eaten them", he answered.

text: Nukarä ža tełxor yegirinč’ey yičiräsi zown.
translation: The henchmen were not letting her get inside.

text: Sidaquł žedu nasin sadaqorno rutin ħukmu boyn:
translation: One day they gathered together and made a decision:

text: Walla, debi kidno yetin nełał xizay nexäsi yołƛin, žawab teƛno užä.
translation: "By God, I want your daughter, I have come for her," the boy answered.

text: Raħäruni reƛno rac’no, ence mazaħarno xizor utin iduɣor nexƛ’oräy, yiziz ɣˤutka tełäzay maħor yoqxo howža q’orolay yukayn.
translation: He ate the cooked meat and while coming home again, he saw that widow getting out of their house.

text: Elo tełer zeyn, zirun, boc’in rayn.
translation: There a bear, a fox and a wolf came inside.

text: Hemełƛ’o xizyo eɣeni esiw k’oƛin ik’in xander.
translation: After that, the young brother came running to the king.

text: Sasaqosi maħor oqnosi, xaner rukayn, nesiz gulur radiru inžitłi.
translation: When he went outside in the morning, the king saw the abuse done to his horse.

text: Bikorä bełiqanes č’aɣˤiryabi rac’a bašla bodin zown yałi.
translation: Then the snake started to eat the hunter's bowels.

text: Žan rac’no łiyrno, p’es anusi c’ik’iwtow bišʷa rac’a ħuruƛ’or ik’iƛ’oräy, sid bełiqanä ža t’omceni žek’u oxira aqrun mešok’lałay boɣno musno, ɣunoqor icin kurno.
translation: When he had finished eating it and went to the basin to eat all the food without saying anything, one hunter began to chase the span-sized man away and he took a hair out of his moustache and tied him to a tree.

text: Žedu bigtow q’ʷaridno boqno bičäsi boqzaƛ’, eɣeni esiya neł ečruni ɣˤanazaq esirno, žedu šida nece q’ʷaridno boqno bičäsiƛin.
translation: Because they were very sad, the younger brother asked that old woman: "Why are they so sad?"

text: Xizyo nełar nesis ɣalat’yabi riyn.
translation: Then she knew his mistakes.

text: Bayn bełiqanza bišʷa esirnosi, nesän žeduq raqru šebin esin, hemedurtow mamalayƛ’no zown t’omceni žek’un ayn, nasintow bišʷa rac’a ža oqnosi elay ža oɣƛ’oräy, mešok’lałäy musno boɣno nesä di ɣunoqor icisƛin mus bukarno.
translation: When the hunters came and asked for food, he also told them what had happened: "So a span-sized man came riding on a rooster, and when he began to eat up all the food and I wanted to take him away from there, he took a hair out of his moustache and tied me to a tree." and he showed them the hair.

text: Ukrus bic’no q’ili neƛanƛin, - žawabno teƛno xanä esirno, - šebi aždaħer teƛanix kidä yowäƛin?”
translation: "I will give you one qili of silver", answered the king and asked: "What did your daughter get to give to the dragon?"

text: Yuyxor yutinosi kidbä esnazaqor eƛin:
translation: When the girl sat up again, she said to the brothers:

text: Nagaħ dey wasi debeq t’ubazi boda k’ʷeze boqinč’inäy, ƛexeƛ yałru akamxor ročno regirno ɣanq’izin oqno exuƛin.
translation: Suppose you can't fulfill my testament, then bind a rope to a hook under the ceiling, hang yourself and die!"

text: Ričas ži babiya bayru wasimoxorƛin”, - eƛin užä.
translation: Now father's will will be fulfilled.

text: Ric’äsi mesedyosno, micxiryosno ixinno sadaq risno, ža kid saɣ yodanixƛin xander ik’in.
translation: He took the entire bag filled with gold and money and went to the king in order to cure his daughter.

text: Bełiqanza bayn bišʷa esirnosi, nesä raqru šebin esin, hemedur mamalayƛ’no zown, ruk’ice mešok’la yołäsi t’omce žek’un ayn, kinaw mežur radiru bišʷan rac’no ik’isƛin.
translation: When the hunters came and asked for food, he told what had happened: "So on a rooster came riding a man in the size of a span, with a moustache in the size of a cubit, ate up the entire food that I made for you, and went away."

text: Becawni žek’a ža ozzaq yiħinäy, neła kanłi bayrxoƛax.
translation: If the blind man smears them onto his eyes, it brings back his eyesight.

text: ʕOƛiran esnaza radiru rešonin sidaƛ’or rutirno, ixinno nełas ric’no, žan risno hunix oqno.
translation: He gathered the garbage that the seven brothers had produced in one, filled a bag with it and took it on his way.

text: Di baħarčiłi etixozonis rik’zi radiƛin”.
translation: "I admire the lover's braveness."

text: Hunix ik’iƛ’oräy, yisir łˤonon roƛnoxut’a bazarganbi keze boqno.
translation: While walking on his way, he encountered three sick merchants.

text: Nełqay roxin rik’iƛ’oräy, yeženi esiya babiya neƛno zawruni č’ikay q’ˤidar kurno.
translation: While they were running away from it, the eldest sister threw down the mirror that their father had given her.

text: Mi rac’no resunč’inäy, ezur di haqarƛin, ƛ’iri Bac’ali utin.
translation: "If it's not the case that you have eaten them, let me look into your mouth!", Batsali insisted.

text: Eɣeni esiya ƛ’iri risno, žigoƛ’ayƛa didurnokin iča anu nesi žek’uƛ’ yałru q’ʷariłi biyrinč’eyƛin.
translation: The younger brother insisted: "And now there is no way whatsoever to find out what is bothering this man."

text: Šomon ʕoloqan baħarči ik’ix zown žedun esirno, kidbabi didurnokin razi roqxo zownanu.
translation: So many brave young men were coming and asked them out, but the girls refused them.

text: Kidbä žawab teƛno, dey eniwn anu, babiwn anu, ʕoƛno esiw yołƛin.
translation: The girl answered: "I have neither mother nor father, [but] I have seven brothers."

text: Yeda ʕaq’lu mołixozo ečruni žek’uder aynosi, ža zikru eƛix esun.
translation: When he came to the old man who was to teach the advice, he found him reciting religious chants.

text: Nesi užä mežus ʕumru xʷasar bodäsi boqzaƛ’, nesiłno, Beqes ʕUneyzatełno esnałin risno žeduz bertenyołxor bik’i, ent’aytow ħaƛu-bišanix šebinon rižin.
translation: Because that boy saved your life, you should fraternize with him and Sun's Uneyzat and go to their marriage after taking something to eat and drink with you."

text: Sidaɣ gogaƛ’ kikxo išanixƛin yeda q’ˤida ičinosi, ƛ’irir sis ƛ’oxono zey bayn.
translation: As he sat down to eat near the spring on the meadow, a bear came.

text: K’ox äƛiru ħaƛunosin, hemedurtown yic’no.
translation: When he drank once more, it filled itself again.

text: Nesis q’ˤano gani zown.
translation: He had two young bulls.

text: Sida mixƛ’o maduhalłiq yałru xanä qacis gulu begirno bayrno Musa tełer karuni xanƛ’or (nesiƛ’ ʕAwdunasirƛin ci zown).
translation: One evening, the neighbouring king came sent a wooden horse to the king who threw Musa into jail (his name was Abdulnasir).

text: Howži dey murad yoł, där saɣłin, wacłin radiru baħarčix däxtow yowx zawruni debi kid Tawadix esirno nexäsi yoł di.
translation: Now this is my wish; I have come to ask you to marry off your daughter who was to marry me, to Tavadi, the brave young man who acted so well and brotherly towards me.

text: Qˤašizaƛ’or mi guluƛ’or izinč’inäy, qalamxo bičixozo qarawulza mi iqärƛin, eƛin babiya uži hunix egirno.
translation: If you don't get on your horse before it dawns, the guards who are waiting at the fortress will catch you!" the father said and sent his son away.

text: Eniw yayrun uži exura k’oƛin.
translation: As soon as the mother came, she jumped to kill the boy.

text: Ele hemediw yoł dey ganiƛin, eƛin užä.
translation: "Well, so is my bull.", said the boy.

text: Tełxor egirno zamanaƛ’azay roč k’ek’-rik’ärun, yizä ža raƛ’moƛ’aɣor izirno.
translation: They let him inside, and just when after some time the rope was moved, they lifted him onto the earth.

text: Łas bertenƛin, doxturä esirno.
translation: "Whose wedding?" the doctor asked.

text: Howlo ɣeduƛ žan eqro iduɣor yik’i, dey kid, t’o yičinäy mi beqä yäsƛin, qˤaƛin yiła, dahawgon bix kosa yiħun.
translation: "Put it into the shadow and go home, my daughter, if you sit here, you'll get a sunstroke!" she shouted and began to gather the hay a little.

text: Sida mixƛ’aza hawaƛ’ k’oƛin łˤono t’umi bayn.
translation: After a little while, the three pigeons came flying.

text: Ža kid žeda netintow pečmox ħošin šeƛ’urno xecix zown.
translation: They always kept her at the oven and dressed her in a skin.

text: Nełƛ’ay soder eniwn, užin łinasnotow urɣel anusi bičix zown.
translation: From that moment on the mother and the son lived careless.

text: Sida quł bełiƛ’ äk’izay xizor utin nexƛ’orey, neside dandir bikori c’oxno.
translation: One day, when he was coning back from hunting, he ran into a snake.

text: Neła äƛirun aħyałer regirinč’ey, Alimaħama ħonƛ’or ayn.
translation: Not hearing what it said either, Alimagomed came to the mountain.

text: Elo yisir zirun, zeyn besun daʕbał gulun, ħurženyabin, dabaɣyołäy bodalini k’ukno riłu rodin žeda žeduƛ’ rułira koƛ’inč’ey ƛexasi.
translation: There he found a fox and a bear arguing, as they didn't know how to share among them a horse, bags and a cap made of leather equally.

text: Nagon ža äči, bˤeƛ’ez reqenyołäy, miƛ’bin aqonibin hok’xo mecrax yołƛin.
translation: "Where should he be, he's in the sheep pen seperating the rams and the lambs from the flock of sheep."

text: C’iɣruk’ä xizyogon ħukmu bodix aždaħes mesedyos teł ric’alini sabani rok’ek’a.
translation: Tsighruk then decided to steal the blanket filled with the dragon's gold.

text: Encetow bitaɣor bok’ełnosi, maħiq micxir t’aħiruni ɣˤʷayä yeda ƛ’irizoni dunyalƛ’aɣor izirno.
translation: After they had escaped into a little distance, the dog with the money tied to its tail lifted him into the upper world.

text: Xizyo žoyä babiwqor eƛin, hemesi Ismaʕiles zawru yaraɣno, gulun däqor neƛnäy, howža ʕArabuzan q’ˤidan caƛin nełas meč’ beč’a mi di egiranƛin.
translation: Then the son said to his father: "If you give me that Ismail's weapon and horse, I will throw that Arabuzan down and let you cut her neck."

text: Xizyo nesä esin neł kidbeq, kinaw raqru łanałäyn, didur nesä ža k’onč’ałer łuq’zi adirułäyn.
translation: Afterwards he told that girl everything that had happened and how he hurt his leg.

text: Neła kidbä Ħon-Bahadurqor reƛ’i-bašid, xizor oxiƛin, eƛix zown.
translation: That girl was telling Mountain~Hero with her hands and fingers to run back.

text: Šebi žigon nesir retäƛin?”
translation: "What does he want again, now?"

text: Elo tełäygon Kusan oqno.
translation: And Kusa got out of it.

text: Sida zamanałäy, ža bˤeƛ’ ɣudƛ’ay ɣudƛ’or dahełxo rik’a roqno.
translation: After a while, the sheep began to become fewer from day to day.

text: Nasin ƛ’irin butin žan bexurno, tełäy maħor q’ˤanon isno boɣno, łenon užin oɣno.
translation: They all got onto it and killed it, then pulled the bulls and the five sons out of it.

text: Yizis xabar xanez kidberno teqno, yedu maħor yoƛixno, didiw ža dey eniw yiča yayxƛin, kid maħor yoqno.
translation: This story was overheard by the king's daughter, who appeared outside: "Which one is my mother?!", the girl went outside.

text: Žan hemedurtow ešurno yizä yiził, roqno elay bitor uynon sadaq rik’in.
translation: So they took him along with them as well and from there the four went further together.

text: Yisä esirno, nesiz idu žek’u yołäƛin.
translation: He asked: "Does anyone live at his home?"

text: ʕAƛar ayrun, bełiqanä idu nesä nesirsini but’an eqerno, mažgitäɣor ik’in.
translation: Just when he came to the village, the hunter put the part that was for himself down at home and went to the mosque.

text: Boƛƛ’or bayrułer, nesir yizis gama tełxor ƛisix sis kid yukayn.
translation: When they came to the middle [of the sea], he saw a girl pulling their boat downwards.

text: Iduɣor yizi boqnosi, zeyä yeda žeduz ɣˤutkoxor ayrno.
translation: They went into the house and the bear brought him to their house.

text: Waħ, žigon šebitow nesir retinƛin, esirno neła.
translation: "Oh, what does he want again now?", she asked.

text: T’ok’ow boda darun koƛ’inč’ey, čaran bexun, ƛ’iri šet’u butix ceyno yołƛax.
translation: Not knowing what to do - the situation was desperate - the eagle circled around, up there.

text: Eniw yežigon ħayran yoqno, nece baq’ˤu micxirno bayrno uži idur ayzaƛ’.
translation: His mother was even more astonished, because her son had brought so much money home.

text: Yisä yeda onočaƛ’or, ƛelƛ’or, cek’ƛ’or bełiƛ’or, beƛ’q’ˤuƛ’or, ziyƛ’or, isƛ’or utanix ruhun odin.
translation: He taught him how to turn into a hen, a lamb, a goat kid, a deer, a sheep, a cow and a bull.

text: Zamanaƛ’aza yeda sida šebi eƛa äynč’iru žek’a tełxor ižin, sida ɣˤanazagon šešoƛ’no oɣno.
translation: After some time, a man unbeknownst to him lead him inside and a woman invited him to dance.

text: Mežuqay dar žäd ħažataw šebin anuƛin, - eƛin žoyä, - meži hoboy tełxor biħuƛin”.
translation: "There's still nothing necessary from you for me", said the boy, "go back inside again, now!"

text: Neła kidbä täƛruni łˤorat’a ɣˤurušyozaƛ bazaryay risxosi šebinabin risno, esnabi urɣizi boqno ʕaƛaɣor bik’a.
translation: Having bought the things for the 3 roubles each, that the girl gave to them, they thought about going (back?) to the village.

text: Howda lel yaƛ’ˤuru aɣi eliz baɣmart’a bayx zownƛin, žimäy yoɣno ža lel babiwq yukarnosi, ɣˤutkutow č’urno rik’in.
translation: "This feather has fallen from the bird, which comes to our garden," and as he took the feather from the pocket and showed it to his father the feather illuminated the whole house.

text: Yeynoyxosi aɣˤur yičinosi, maħorno yoqno habihanes baru yezun.
translation: When the working mill stopped working, the miller's wife went outside and had a look.

text: Mi nesiqor eƛo, sosi mitow reč’oƛin.
translation: Say to him: 'You chop first!'

text: Q’ˤunan ozzar rukaynosi, aɣˤuxay bitor hunix oqno yeda zeyä äƛiruni hemeł ʕaƛaɣor.
translation: When both of his eyes saw again, he got on his way away from the mill to the village that the bear had talked about.

text: Elor aynosi, ža uži qˤaƛanada oqno, were meža di aħinyoqorno icin iħułer kuro, qaciz ruk’uqorno icin karnoƛin.
translation: When he arrived there, the boy started to yell: "Well, tie me to a rock and throw me into the river, but don't tie me to a bundle of wood and throw me in!"

text: Nełas där maʕna bič’zi boqxanu.
translation: I don't understand what this means.

text: Zownƛax, zown anuƛax sida ʕaƛa eže žek’u.
translation: Once upon a time in a village there was an old man.

text: Bˤeƛo buqˤełno, ažoz k’odrezaxazay aħyabin roƛik’no kecno bičin, k’et’u c’idox tataniłxo zown.
translation: The pig was hiding behind a bunch of trees, pushing out its ears and sleeping, while the cat was warming itself by the fire.

text: Waʕaleykum salamƛin, eƛin bełiqanä.
translation: "And peace be with you!" said the hunter.

text: Xizaɣor bertenno bodin, kid bečedawni žek’us žax yegirno.
translation: Finally they married and sent the girl to the son of the rich man.

text: Hudayzikotow Ražbadiner bodixosi berten Tawadiƛ’ar butirno.
translation: The next day the marriage which was prepared for Razhbadin turned to Tawadi.

text: Neła quł yeda idur mix bexunosi ayn.
translation: On that day he came home only after a long time had passed.

text: (Nełas xediw ensi žek’uq iłäsi zownƛin, neła nełas babiw ukarno).
translation: ("Her husband resembled this man", she pointed to her father.)

text: Nełƛ’aza ʕaƛar aynosi yeda xalq’imorkin iynanu.
translation: Because of that, the people didn't even recognize him, when he came into the village.

text: Esinč’ey ičäsi zown Muħamadä, xizyo, necetow ƛ’iri xan utizaƛ’, däz kidbä esisƛin eƛin.
translation: Muhammad wasn't telling it, but then, because the king insisted so much, he said: "My daughter told me."

text: Raładyołäza egin gamiƛ’no zown sidade yeda ik’a ayx zown.
translation: At one place he had to go by boat on the sea.

text: Tawadä eƛix yisiqor, mi däde sadaq ƛˤeb yaɣrus yedu rok’uɣʷey rodixƛin.
translation: Tavadi said to him: "You and me, we will prepare the banquet of our spending together one year."

text: Dibirä äƛiruxor, kikxay neła ɣˤanaza yik’in łi ħaƛun.
translation: As the imam said, the woman went to drink water out of the spring.

text: Osaxorno bizin ceyä wˤałer kurxo zown uži, nesiƛ’ay aderno bik’in roładyołer ayzaƛ’or iqirxo izirxo zown.
translation: The eagle flew into the height and dropped the boy, flew down and before he [the boy] came to the sea, it catched him and lifted him up.

text: Elo ƛirbałix sida ɣˤanazader keze oqnosi, yisir neł ʕaƛnos xalq’i sidaɣor butäsi bukayn.
translation: When he became the guest of a woman there, he saw the people of the village gathering in some place.

text: Howži xanä Kusar teƛno zawruni iłno buci łiyxosi zaman bayn.
translation: Now came the time that the six months that the six months the king had given Kusa ended.

text: Neła esirno, bitazoni puƛ’aɣorä oɣan, ent’azoni puƛ’aɣorä oɣanƛin.
translation: It asked: "Shall I take you to the next side, or to this side here?"

text: Xanes nukarbi, nełaƛ’ bužzin boqno, xizor bik’in.
translation: The king's henchmen believed her and went back.

text: Dicegon ža soder bizanƛin hunƛ’izi boqłin, pˤaƛirno bižin yałza wˤałertow bayx zown.
translation: But how much it tried to get upwards, it always glided off and came down where it was.

text: Kidbä bełis oɣˤno teƛno, q’orolay iduɣor yegirno.
translation: The girl gave the widow a deer's thigh and sent her home.

text: Hudod elor zeyn, boc’in, zirun rayn.
translation: At night, the bear, the wolf and the fox came there.

text: Cimyoqaysi xanä ža kid qaciz k’emot’ya tełerno kurno, yoɣno łäłxor yižirno.
translation: The malicious king locked his daughter in a wooden chest and let her throw into the river.

text: Xizyo žedu teƛanix bazaryaɣor bik’in.
translation: Afterwards, they went to the marked to sell them.

text: K’uruč’ k’eč’ ɣudi rocu, buq bay.
translation: All's well that ends well.

text: Neła idur bown zawruni onoču reč’yałer kidbeƛ’orno butin, iduzasi royƛ’orey, k’oƛin yik’in ečruni ɣˤanaza ža kid yiqirno.
translation: The hen that she had brought home quickly turned into a girl and while she did the house[work] then, the old woman ran and caught this girl.

text: ʕAƛoxar baya adäza sidade bikori žek’xo ʕoloqbi besun.
translation: At one point, before coming to the village, he found young people beating a snake.

text: Esnaza yisir ʕuraw micxir teƛsiƛax, taliħ neƛoƛ deber eli aħinyoqorno bicin iħułxor kuroƛin.
translation: It's said that the brothers gave him much money: "May you be given happiness, but tie us to a rock and throw us into the river!"

text: Moƛa iqirno ƛexun, xizyogon di oč’ino q’ˤim yołäsi aždaħ bexursiƛin, esin yisä.
translation: "I was sleeping, and then I killed a nine-headed dragon.", he told.

text: Užir retäsi zown xanes kid esiranix eniw yegira.
translation: The boy wanted to send his mother to ask for the king's daughter.

text: Łinaƛ’aza däł laɣes maħ yołƛin mi eƛäƛin?”
translation: "Why did you say that I smell like a slave?"

text: Howlaytow aɣˤʷarno ik’in, nesä nesis ozurabin roɣno, mogolarno izin ičin.
translation: And so, from there he [the other guy] went into the mill, took his eyes out and got up into the granary.

text: Žamaʕatyor riyx zows yołi žeduz ʕaƛoł boƛƛo sida ixiw aħinyoƛ teł kik yałrułi, elur ža dawla biqa zownč’uƛin.
translation: If the population knew that in the middle of their village under a rock there is a spring, we would not get the prey."

text: Sis zown eniwn yexun best’alłiƛ’ ƛexasi, sideni q’ˤano äƛiru barułäysi zown.
translation: One was then orphan whose mother had died, the other one was from his second wife.

text: Howži sida quł yiła yisiq esirno, mi baru yowäsi yołäƛin.
translation: However one day she asked him, "Do you have a wife?"

text: Žan yałza łeɣirno, ceyä ƛ’irigon mecno biħin, ʕAliqiličes k’onč’un saɣ yoqno.
translation: Holding him where he was, the eagle licked above him with his tongue, and Aliqilich's leg got cured.

text: Yedu yukayrun, yeženä bašiqˤoy erali reƛ’a nełar rukadałxor soder rizirno.
translation: After she saw her, the old woman lifted the hand with the ring put inside, so she could see it.

text: ƛˤeb yik’inosigon, užił xizay xan ayn.
translation: One year later the khan came to pick up his son.

text: Žedus zownƛax sis biƛixosi močin, sis gulun, sisgon bˤeƛ’es reqenno.
translation: They had, as is said, a field for plowing, a horse and a flock of sheep.

text: Sosisi łäł taqˤor oqanix yisis pikru boqäsi zown.
translation: His first idea was to get through the water.

text: Bizin ʕAliqiličzo raƛ’mar bayanix oc’ino gali ƛäxurun ceyez gugyoƛ’ erno zawruni šebin łiyn.
translation: They flew upwards and just ten steps before coming up to Aliqilich's country, the things that were on the eagle's back ended.

text: Roƛiqur raynosi yeda sida ʕaƛarno ayn, adäza räsuru ɣˤutkaɣor ik’in.
translation: At midnight he came into a village and went into the house that lay before him.

text: Kidbä eniwq esinƛax kinaw nełał raqru łinałäyn.
translation: The girl told her mother about everything that had happened to her.

text: Sidaquł yiłar ciqqo Allahes ašuni bukayn.
translation: One day she saw a rainbow in the forest.

text: Nesä nełqor eƛin:
translation: He said to her:

text: Nuʕłimorƛin yisä nełaz bašiłäyn roɣno bašiqˤoyn sadaq risno, žežułäy beč’no kodin bisno, gedmoqaygon yeč’no č’irno yisno.
translation: As a proof he took a ring from her finger and took it with himself, he cut a hair from her ponytail and took it with himself, and he cut off a ribbon from her shirt and took it with himself as well.

text: Babiw idu anunč’izay, igorno yayn, Okuz kidbä nesiqor eƛin, were mi dicegon yaq’ˤu ʕilmu deber yiqiłin, mi siskin šebin riyxanuƛin iča ay.
translation: While her father wasn't at home, Oku's daughter came nearer and said to him: "Well, no matter how much science you will get, you must say that you don't know anything!"

text: Yedu debeq di äsiru šebin łaqnokin äsinƛin eƛin, eženi oqno maħor ik’in.
translation: Don't tell anyone what I told you!", the old man told and went outside.

text: Adäzar q’oldenno kurno sosigon yisä rok’no mač’a.
translation: He threw his armor in front of himself and once again stroke with his saber.

text: Ža där riys hunix kut’yoƛ’aza puƛ’aza nucoƛ’osi t’ut’ bik’ix zawruƛ’azaƛin”.
translation: This I know because there flew a bee on the right side of the way.

text: Behizi raqƛin, razi oqno yeda.
translation: "I permit it.", he agreed.

text: Dowƛ’or ħal bayza quł, enła ɣˤutka yałru imyoq ħobo bok’oƛin”.
translation: "On a day when you have trouble, kick against a pillar that is in the house."

text: Sis elus ħon xecin nesä, q’ˤano žek’us ħon xecin, sis kero boɣno, q’ˤano kero boɣno xizaɣor xanes uži sida yeže gogaƛ’or ayn.
translation: He left our one mountain behind, left two other mountains behind, crossed one ravine, crossed two ravines and finally the prince came onto a big meadow.

text: Zownƛax, zownanuƛax sida ʕaƛa q’ˤano q’orolay.
translation: Once upon a time there lived two widows in a village.

text: Magalabin nesä hemedurtow nesä nesiz nukarzaƛ’ rułirno.
translation: The bread he also shared among his own henchmen.

text: Siskin ɣudi rik’izaƛ’ortow, nełaz bercinłä ʕadalno oyn c’oxno nełaq.
translation: Even before any day has passed, her beauty drugged him and he pounced on to her.

text: Sida zamanałäy, xexbi bežet’a boqnosi henešyabi täƛruni eženi žek’us xabar bayn, sissi uži nesir egiroƛ, nesiqor ezun ža sida kikxo äčiƛin.
translation: At one time, when the children had become older, the story came [to them] of the old man who gave the apples, and one boy was to be sent to him and he would wait on a spring looking for him.

text: Łˤonoqu nukar egirno xanä zam-zames łäł xizay.
translation: The king sent sixty henchmen behind the holy water.

text: Walla, xan, howža ƛeli bayruntow, nełas eniw bexus.
translation: "By God, o king, right after the lamb was born, its mother died.

text: Eženä eƛin, debi xabar Ismaʕilzoq biłäsiwa yołƛin.
translation: The old man said: "Doesn't your way of speaking resemble Ismail's?"

text: Neła žedu esnabi iduɣor goƛ’in, där nešurur rac’anix šebin riqisƛin.
translation: He invited the brothers into his home: "I got something for dinner."

text: Q’orola xanqo mołin, c’ohor nexxozo mečoƛ’ mi nucos bic’äsi q’ili eqroƛin.
translation: The widow explained to the king: "Put a pot filled with honey to the place the thief came to."

text: Isno ceyä bac’no łiyrno, t’ekzo šilazaƛ’ ƛuzabi ƛexun.
translation: When the eagle finished eating the bull, the bones remained on the goat's horns.

text: Mi šebi yołƛin?”
translation: "Who are you?"

text: Neła qułtow neširu užä q’ˤida ħiƛ’oqun raħno, eƛin, eliz madaxay bečedawni maduhalzo madaxor mesedyos qˤiƛurabi rodoƛin.
translation: On that day in the evening, the boy laid down the handkerchief and said: "Build golden stairs from our door to the wealthy neighbor's door!"

text: Elo šeƛ’anix däq ec’no šeƛ’u anuƛin, eƛin kidbä.
translation: "I have no new clothings to put on there", said the girl.

text: Zamanałäy bik’in q’ˤunesno ƛirbabi yałza sida ʕaƛar baynosi, yizi q’ˤunesno daʕba boqno, däz ƛirbadäɣor bik’ixƛin bazargan zown, ay däzodäɣorƛin uži zown ičäsi.
