translation: After some time, when they came to a village where they both had comrades, the two of them got into a dispute: "We are going to my comrade.", said the merchant and "Come to mine!" said the boy.

text: Elay bizin bik’iƛ’oräy žedude dandir nexxo iłno q’ˤim yołäsi aždaħ bukayn.
translation: From there they got up, and saw a six-headed dragon coming towards them.

text: Ža q’orolay xanez ɣˤutkoz madaxor yik’in.
translation: That widow then went to the gate of the king's house.

text: Nesis Pat’imatƛin c’aq’ bercinaw, c’odoraw, ʕaq’ilaw kid zown.
translation: He had a very beautiful, clever and intelligent daughter named Fatima.

text: Nełde sadaq egirno zawruni žek’u nesir teqno xizor utin aynƛin.
translation: The man who was sent together with her - he heared - has returned.

text: Q’ˤidayn izin xanes užin išno.
translation: Up also got the prince and ate.

text: Neła ħurmatyor mustaħiq’aw žek’u anu diƛin, q’ˤidar egirerno užä.
translation: "I'm not a man suitable for such respect!", the boy let them let him down.

text: Dey yoł yeduƛin sis, dey yołƛin sis.
translation: "It is mine" said one of them, "it's mine" said the other.

text: Esnabi zawruni ɣˤutkoz madaxor xanä wazir egirno, žeda elo teł badiru xabarno teqerno xizyo nesiq esanix.
translation: The king sent a vizier to the door of the room where the brothers were, to overhear their conversation and to tell him about it afterwards.

text: Qˤaƛin haraƛ’ bodinosi, raładyołäy howžatow ɣˤanabi yoƛixno.
translation: He shouted and out of the sea came that woman.

text: Neł reƛiłer ɣˤʷays maħ nazay baya bayxƛin?”
translation: "Where does the dog's smell in the meat come from?"

text: Ža bexuranixƛin yisä c’eruc’a rutirnosi, ceyä eƛin, mi di bexarno, deber di sis bercinaw pardagi neƛanƛin.
translation: As he directed his bow to kill the eagle, the latter said, "Don't kill me, I will give you one beautiful scarf."

text: Ceyä di isno own minaraƛ’or egirsi.
translation: The eagle picked me up and brought me to the minaret again.

text: Nesis łˤono ʕomoy zown.
translation: He had three donkeys.

text: Hemeł zamanał, sidaɣ mečoƛ’ č’ʷadma teł gondu yoqno.
translation: At this time, a big hole appeared somewhere in the ruin.

text: Zownč’uƛin, et’un k’oƛin ičin xedyos esiw.
translation: "No, I haven't.", said the husband's brother jumping(?).

text: Zownč’uƛin, eƛin gamiƛ’ozoni žek’a.
translation: "I haven't.", said the sailor.

text: Xizyo royn ičäsi hudun muk’ur oqno uži, neł hiboreƛ q’aymos bic’äsit’a łeno ħumukuli neƛanƛin bazarganä eƛinosi.
translation: Then the boy agreed nevertheless, when the merchant said that he would give him the load from 5 camels for the stick.

text: Elay bitorgon bik’ixozo q’ˤuya gamiƛ’aɣorno yizin ža xʷasarno yoqno, nełaƛ’town raładyoz lilyoxaɣor yok’ełno.
translation: She got to another boat that was going from there to somewhere else, got rescued and escaped on it to the seashore.

text: Neła quł yizis ʕaƛoxaza egin nesä nesiz q’ʷariʕelyoł xan ik’ix zown.
translation: On that day, the king was going through their village for his own needs.

text: Deber rätiru šebinon risno debez mi eni- obiydäɣor yik’iƛin”.
translation: Take whatever you want, then go to your parents.

text: Yisä yałruxor kinaw raqrun, nesa rayrun łinałäy esin nesiq.
translation: He told him about everything that had happened and that he had done.

text: Žedu q’ˤanon xexbin rižin q’orolay duri bižin režet’ani esnazadäɣor yik’in.
translation: The widow carried the two children and ran to the older sisters.

text: Qazaqzar roda šebinon koƛ’inč’ey, sasiw ɣudin roqzaƛ’, xizaz k’oƛin bik’iƛ’orey, yizi rešt’izi boqno Ražbadin aqˤza mečox igo.
translation: The Georgians didn't know what to do because the day got gloomy; and while running behind them they took a rest near the place where Razhbadin was hiding.

text: Žeduq bexurno xabar łiynosi, c’aramałäz begin ža č’iki maħorno boqno, t’umƛ’orno butin boržizin boqno bik’in.
translation: After they finished talking, the flea went outside through a crack, turned into a pigeon again and flew away.

text: Zamanałäy, howžatow miskinawni uži oƛixno, di ža yeynodaniƛ’or ruhun yodanƛin.
translation: After a while, that poor boy appeared[, saying]: "I will teach her how to work."

text: Xizyo eniwn, užin aderzoxortow miskinłiƛ’or bayn.
translation: Afterwards the mother and the son lived poor again.

text: Neširu idur aynosi, qidozaq rixäsi xaličabin, butnin bukaynosi haqa ogun ƛexun.
translation: In the evening, when he came home and saw the mats hanging on the wall and the burka, his mouth gaped.

text: Aħozä eƛin, adäz di bˤeƛ’ žek’uz mečołer regirxo zowsƛin, neła howži bišʷa rac’akin utik’xanu gugyoq roqłin.
translation: The shepherd said: "I sent the sheep to another man's field before, that's why I don't have time to eat food, although it's on my back."

text: Sasaqaza bišʷan risno xediw q’ˤuyzaɣaɣor ik’ixƛin baruqorno eƛin, ik’in ciqqo ɣuna teł uqˤełno ičin.
translation: The next morning, the husband took some food and said to his wife: "I'm going somewhere", went to the forest and hid inside the tree.

text: Boƛik’a ħošos ʕaƛoz xalq’imoqor bokobi roqno, boƛik’unigon sida q’orol ɣˤanazas kuła rodacekin ʕeze boqno anu.
translation: One half of the hide became fur coats for the people of the village, the other half wasn't even enough for the sleeve of one widow.

text: Buħima halizi bodinosi, idurno bik’in sabaniƛ’or boƛ’ˤun.
translation: When the heat excited him [the dragon], he went home and fell onto the blanket.

text: Xan ʕuraw ɣˤaɣˤu izin kidno, yisiƛ’ay q’ʷariłi bok’äk’runi misikin užin saɣłiƛ’, nesis muradno t’ubazi bodin xizor butin bayzaƛ’.
translation: The king got very happy that his wish came true, that his daughter and the young boy who helped him out of distress came home safely.

text: Sida quł Mirza qaca rowanix łˤonontow ʕomoybin rižin ciqaɣor ik’in.
translation: One day, Mirza lead his three donkeys into the forest in order to bring firewood.

text: Ɣˤaɣˤu äziru Žabulawägon yisix Baxumesedun yegirno, yisiqor naibłin teƛno, ɣˤutkoƛ’orno egirno.
translation: Jabulaw, who became happy, gave Bakhumesed's hand to him, gave him the governorship and sent him to a house.

text: Harihun xabaryayx baƛ’ix žedu bik’iƛ’oräy, nełał xizay qˤaƛanayx durimoƛ’ k’oƛin kid yayx yukayn, eni, howlo yiči, oč’ira däz mučariłäy sis kamiruni mučari miyä risäƛin.
translation: While they were going slowly and talking, they saw her daughter running and yelling behind them: "Mother, stay there, did you take the one cumin cake that got lost from my nine?"

text: Elay bizin žamaʕat bik’in.
translation: The people got up from there and went to him.

text: Šebi deber retinƛin, cey hawaƛ’ bayn k’oƛin.
translation: "What did you want?", the eagle came flying.

text: Yedu t’akan łu t’or yayräƛin nesä bayan bisnosi, oƛixno k’oƛin nesistow wazirä di yowsƛin eƛin.
translation: When he wanted to know: "Who brought this glass here?", his vizier came running and said: "I brought it!"

text: Sidaquł bara yisiqor eƛin:
translation: One day his wife said to him:

text: Xedyos kid bercinaw zown.
translation: The husband's daughter was beautiful.

text: Araq’i yołƛin, esin užä.
translation: "It's vodka", told the boy.

text: Hič’č’a adäz eženi esiwr bazarya łˤora ɣˤurušyoƛ teƛxo q’ut’u bukaynosi, nesä bazarganqo esirno:
translation: First of all, the eldest brother saw a jug on the market for three rubles, and asked the merchant:

text: Waħa keran c’oxno, zeyn bexun.
translation: The bear fell into the ravine below and died.

text: Žaq ʕaƛno ƛˤeb yayzay soder, idur own muʕalimno, xanä ža ʕilmuƛ’or oɣno.
translation: When the son became seven years old, the king brought a teacher into his home and he spent time on science.

text: Allahes q’adar nediw boqzaƛ’, žedu q’ˤanon sadaq, Bilq’isdin xediwn, howlotown bexun.
translation: And because Allah's fate was such that the two of them, Bilqisdi and her husband, died there together.

text: Kurzak’uƛ’orgon Okun utin, nełał xizay k’oƛin.
translation: And Oku turned into a falcon and followed it.

text: Esan retin anu, esinč’ey xecan retin anu, sis endiw šebin roqnoƛax.
translation: I didn't want to tell this; I didn't want to leave this untold, but such a thing did happen.

text: Yiła eƛin, mi razi oqnäy, dowxor di sis bercinaw kid yiqiranƛin.
translation: "if you agree, I will find a beautiful girl for you," the woman said.

text: Eɣenägon eƛix teqno:
translation: And he heard the younger brother say:

text: Sida quł žedu sadaq hunix bik’a keze boqno.
translation: One day they happened to go somewhere together.

text: Iduɣor ayƛ’oräy, ačqˤaq quqix kikoƛ’osi xexbin risno sida q’orolayä yoƛixno esirno:
translation: When coming home, he saw the widow with her child (at her breast), which almost died of thirst, and asked:

text: Xaner bič’zi roqno elo q’ˤano c’ohor zawrułin, sisinigon elay oxin äk’irułin.
translation: The king understood that there were two thiefs and the other one had run away from there.

text: Elus esiw na yołƛin neła esirnosi, q’ˤuya šaharyaɣor ik’äsiƛin, yaƛ’irno nesä ža.
translation: When she asked: "Where is our brother?", he lied to her: "He has gone to another city."

text: Nełƛ’ay soder łinasnokin ħinq’i anusi ciqaɣor ʕomoybin rik’ix zown.
translation: After that, the donkeys went into the forest without any danger.

text: Eniw tamaša yoqno ƛexun, nay ža deber nece his riqäƛin.
translation: His mother was astonished: "Where did you get so much wool from?"

text: Izäsi C’irdux, q’ˤuyasinibi kecäsiƛin, C’irduxä žawab bodin.
translation: "Tsirdukh is awake, the others are sleeping." answered Tsirdukh.

text: Enduräza rik’in tamadaqorno eƛin, äƛiruxorno bexurna yizä boc’iƛin, žedułäy sis bezanix madaɣor bik’in.
translation: They went that way and said to the toastmaster: "[???]"; one of them went outside to took whether they had killed the wolf as they said.

text: Cek’i guzqay guzqor k’oƛix boč’ik’xo bičäsi zewn.
translation: The goat kid ran from rock to rock and felt cold.

text: Hemełaƛ’ ʕoloqan žek’a eƛin, howži dey ruħ boɣno xot’ozaxor baysƛin.
translation: Then the young man said: "Now my soul has been taken away and it has come up to my feet."

text: Hič’č’a bercinawni kid šeƛ’un resunč’ey, howlotow ƛexun.
translation: The most beautiful girl didn't find her clothes and thus stayed there.

text: Ixinabin ric’no yizä ža ukrun, micxirno iduɣor bižin.
translation: Having filled their bags, they brought the silver and money home.

text: Ič’an yeženä eƛix teqno, deygon hunar šebi, šeƛ’ures bic’äsi gama ƛisiƛ’orey, dey kodin bet’urno, daqay gama baɣruni užix yik’a räti darƛin.
translation: He heard the oldest one say: "What talent do I have? I'll want to marry the boy who took the boat full of clothes away from me after he pulled out my hair, while I was pulling on the boat."

text: Yeda ʕuraw ɣˤaɣˤun izin, hoboy ik’an di xanes kid esiranixƛin, oqno maħor.
translation: He got very happy, said: "Now I will go to the princess and ask her [to marry me].", and he went outside.

text: Sidaquł yizä ža k’et’u bižin ciqor begirno.
translation: One day they took the cat out and sent him into the forest.

text: Sida maqˤizoni ʕaƛa bečedaw žek’us ɣudƛ’ay ɣudƛ’or ħalaq’ oqxo uži ik’ix zown.
translation: In a village far away, a rich man's son was getting thinner day by day.

text: Hemedur sadaq ʕumru bodix zown eɣeni užän, łoħrołäy yaqruni bercinawni kidbän.
translation: So the young boy and the beautiful girl that became of the frog were living together.

text: Žan eƛin Ħusen hunix oqno.
translation: Hussein said that and went on his way.

text: K’uruč’ k’eč’ ɣudi rocu, buq bay!
translation: Kurch, pearl, may the weather be good and the sun come out.

text: Hemedurtow xanes yoƛƛosini kidno qalamäy yoɣno, xizor utin iduɣor nexno.
translation: In the same way he took the king's middle daughter from the fortress and came back home.

text: Di dowzo Goqix kid egiran, amma där ža ukada ayƛin”.
translation: "I will send my daughter to your Goqi, but I have to see him."

text: Eludegon ƛirbałix keze oqno idu teł kecnäy lamus bičixosiƛin, oqno ičin.
translation: And he was like: "And for us it's a shame if someone is a guest and sleeps in one's home."

text: Rac’inč’uƛin, boc’ä ot’ bisno.
translation: "Didn't eat them," the wolf rejected.

text: Sosi bic’no ik’inosi, aždaħä yisiqor roži eƛinanu.
translation: When he first filled the jug, the dragon didn't say a word to him.

text: Bertenyołer goƛ’in zawruni žedu aħozä eƛin, eluz bˤeƛ’xo imadabi esanix beže maxšel yołäsi sis žek’u yoł.

text: Yizi nasin sidaɣ aɣˤuxor rayn.

text: Ceyn neła nełaz raƛ’maɣor boržizin boqno bik’in, yedan iduɣor nexno.

text: Di ža ħoyä tełerno kurno ik’ursiƛin, eƛin Ħusenä.
translation: I threw him into the oven and let him burn.", said Hussein.

text: Howda boqłin bižan zirurƛin boržizi boqno ɣʷˤadi iduɣor nexƛ’oräy, nełał adäzar osiw ħon keze yoqno.

text: Igaɣor bayƛ’oräy, hič’č’an yeɣeni esiyagon, babiya nełqor teƛno zawruni, hiƛu kurno.
translation: When it came closer, the youngest sister threw down the comb that their father had given to her.

text: Howži, nesä bexaru xabaryoƛ’ Ħasanä imanno erno, razin oqno, yisi Ox̌oyode zendi ečula ʕUmarno egirno.
translation: Now, Hassan believed in the what he got told, he agreed and sent his grandchild Umar together with Ohoyo.

text: Ɣʷˤadi ɣunoƛ’ neła nełaz mežä kecno bičäsi zown.

text: Nełaz meča sis ɣˤʷayn bexurno, nełas rok’un roɣno, babiwder ik’in, ele däz esiws rok’uƛin.
translation: Instead of her, he killed a dog and took out its heart and went to his father: "Here, my sister's heart."

text: Sidaɣ osiw qoqƛ’or raynosi, zira, ent’az rigu huni yołƛin, zey qoqƛ’ay wˤałer begirno.
translation: They came to a high cliff in one place, so the fox said "Here is a good way!", and let the bear go down from the cliff.

text: Xanä nesiqor xabar boynosi, uži razi oqno.

text: Yisä yisiz madax łäs bic’äsi q’ut’u biħurnosi, zam-zames łäs kik boƛixno.
translation: After he poured out the water-filled jug in his yard, a spring of holy water appeared.

text: Esnaza nesiq žeda žedus murad esinosi, xanä žeduq sis imadu esin:

text: Ħoyäy magalu boɣoƛin Ħusenqor eƛinosi, mi bukarƛin ičin Ħusen.

text: Elo dey recenyocet’a isʷa zows.

text: Hoq’ˤonägon eƛin, di yisiqay kapekkin yisäsi anuƛin.

text: Ele žeduz ʕaq’lu anunč’uru xabaryoƛ’ dey qoqoƛu regis.
translation: There, I'm laughing about their mindless gossip.

text: Hunix egirƛ’oräy, bełiqa žaqor eƛin, hunix šebigon dandir c’oxłin, mi nełar kumak bodoƛin.
translation: While sending him on his way, the hunter told his son, "No matter whom you may meet on your way, help them!"

text: Elay ža yoqno etixozoder yik’in.
translation: From there she went to her lover and they came together.

text: Neła ɣunoƛ’ ƛ’iri ceyes xexoyabi teł yołäsi muži zown.

text: Žoyän nesiq neširu raqru łinałäy esin.

text: Ža yoxärus, äƛiruxor, xanä bertenno bodin, deber rätiru šebinon riso iduɣor yik’iƛin riyrno.
translation: The king, as he had said, organized a wedding on the [day of the] divorce and explained: "Take everything you want and go home!"

text: Užä nesä nesis oˤɣqay reč’no reƛ łeɣirsiƛax ceyez k’onč’aq.
translation: The boy cut flesh from his own thigh and glued it to the eagle's leg.

text: Sida quł ziru čanaqanłiƛ’ bik’in.
translation: One day the fox went hunting.

text: Ħusen igtow ƛ’irin utin ža xizor utiranix eƛin, dowƛ’osi q’ʷariłi bisanir di ik’ix mi xizorno uti, di aceł caƛäsi c’eruc’a yoł, nagaħ där sis šebin roqnäy, nełas iyo boƛäx, c’ik’iw šebin ruyxor rik’inäy ɣˤay yešnayƛin.
translation: Hussein insisted and said to make him turn back: "I am coming to take away the sadness on you, go back! I have shot with the bow at the door; suppose something will happen to me, it's [the arrow's] blood will appear, and if everything will go alright, then milk will drip out."

text: Zownƛax, zownanuƛax sis xan, nesis zown sistow sis kid.
translation: Once upon a time there was a king who had just one daughter.

text: Bečedawni žek’a nesi žaq q’ˤanontow ganbi regirno.

text: Hemełƛ’ay soder q’orolaz užis raħataw, paraxataw ʕumru zown, ħalikatawt’ani halmaɣzaqay bat’an oqno.
translation: After that, the widow's son lead a serene, quiet life, bereft of mean friends.

text: Žaz bara eƛin, bercingo eƛi- mołinäy posukin ʕenekizi roqxonč’eƛin.

text: Nesä nesiz nukarzaƛ’ xaličabin rułirno.

text: Nesirno howžatow sayɣatno boyn, hemedurtow nesiqorno eƛin.
translation: To him he gave the same present and said the same thing.

text: Mi nełqor eƛo:

text: Ħažataw q’ʷarʕaraw q’ayn sidaƛ’or butirno, maħor boqanix ħadur boqnosi, Beqes ʕUneyzatä eƛin, däz guluƛ’ zown bik’anƛin.
translation: They gathered all necessary articles together, and when they prepared to go outside, Sun's Uneyzat said: "We will ride on my horse."

text: Zamanałäzay boržizin boqno q’ˤano t’umi bayn.
translation: After some time two pigeons came flying.

text: Yeda di ciqez xaner bižix, min nełar sis is baƛin”.
translation: "I'm bringing this to the king of the forest, bring him a bull!"

text: Mi däƛ’ ƛ’iräy oqƛin.
translation: Forgive me."

text: Hemedur ƛ’irizoni dunyalƛ’or yedan ayrno, xizor butin ceyn bik’in.
translation: So the eagle brought him into the world above and turned back.

text: Izin akiqno t’it’aħin, tełxor q’orolas reƛ’a roƛik’no, elo žan c’unizi yodin bäčiruni nukarza, rok’no mač’an reč’no roɣno.
translation: He got up and clutched at the window, and let the widow's hand appear inside, and the henchmen who were guarding her cut it off with their saber(s).

text: Neł kawumoz acmoq rexoriłno zawru ɣˤʷak’un riqirno yizanƛin yaqru ža, ƛ’irir ža yoq’ˤugani acno yoƛ’ˤun Qartayn ƛ’iƛ’iłno yexun.
translation: Qartay took the hook that hang at the door of that gate, began to get up; the heavy door fell down, Qartay got crushed and died.

text: Sis zown babiwn nesisgon łˤono užin.

text: Zamanałäygon neside puħo, puräzagon t’asno eqerno, ža ɣˤanabin q’ˤida yičin.

text: Łox äƛirugon mač’an risno ik’in ža kikxaɣor q’ut’u bic’a.

text: Łˤora beciz bolžalyos hunice maqˤi deber yetixosi kid yoqłin, neł č’ikayär ezunäy, debeł ža adäz yičirxosi”.

text: Elaygon beƛ’q’ˤun bižin aħołun ik’is.

text: Yizi didiwtow xalq’i, ziys ħošos reɣet’a t’it’urbi bazarya teƛanix nexxosi, yizä elułäy ʕabdalbitowwa boyxƛin, nasin xalq’in ƛ’iri butin, ʕoƛnon esnabi nocubi caƛix bazaryay boxirno.
translation: "What kind of people are they, coming to sell small pieces of cow hide on the market; are they making fools of us?!", all the people insisted and spitting on the seven brothers chased them off the market.

text: Ža aq’ˤiłxotow ik’ix zown, sida ɣudƛ’or buci bayruce, sis buci baynosi ƛˤeb yayru iłe.

text: Nesiqor gulun, šeƛ’un neƛa ray miƛin”.
translation: You must lend him clothes and a horse."

text: Xizyo nesi eženä reč’no ža ɣun.

text: Tełäy aqrun, nesä šeƛ’un xiszi rodin, sis ganin biqirno, ozyoł xizay oqno.

text: Neširutow moƛaxayn bizirno, sosigon bišerno yiła yizi kecerno.

translation: The young people of the village were blaming Razhbadin for sending his children for them to get killed, whereas he, however, choose friendship.

text: Ismaʕilä eƛin, di meka isäsi yoł, ence där rac’a šebin neƛƛin.

translation: She said to her mother: 'When somewhere some young people were killing me, the boy who is standing over there outside came about, gave them three rubles and saved [me].'

text: Yisä žedus šebin elo- t’o esaniqno bˤuƛ’no, c’ohorza mesedyos yic’äsi k’emot’ yisiden xecin, boxin bik’in.

text: Bercingo xal boynosi, besuroz qeqa teł t’akan c’oxäsi yesun.

text: Hudunon xexzaqor babiya moƛałt’a eƛix zown, q’ˤanoqu ɣudi ret’ace däz šudaxor baħnaynäy, mežur ža di äƛiru ukru bäqiƛin.
translation: Nevertheless, the father said to the children in their dream: 'When you come walking to my grave before 40 days have passed, I will get said silver for you.'

text: Yexursiƛingon žawab nesän teƛno.
translation: "I killed her.", he answered.

text: Mesedyoztow ƛäƛ’ kidbedäɣorgon ik’inosi, elo mesedyos ric’äsi istowli resun.
translation: When he went over the golden bridge to his daughter, he found a table filled with gold there.

text: Xizyo ža ɣˤʷay kidbeƛ’orno butin, neła esirno, hemełƛ’o däz babiwz ƛ’irizoni ɣˤutka teł suratyoƛ’ yukayruni kid deber yiqa rätiyäƛin.
translation: Afterwards that dog turned into a girl again and asked: "Then, do you want to ge the girl you saw on the picture inside my father's upper room?"

text: Sasaqtow moƛaxay izizaƛ’or, nesä äƛiruxor rodäsi reže ɣˤutkun, nesiz maday kidbez madär kuräsi mesedyos ƛen bukayzaƛ’, xan haqan ogun ezun ƛexun.
translation: The next morning after getting up from sleeping, he built a big house as said, and when the king saw the golden bridge erected from his door to his daughter's door, he remained looking at it with an open mouth.

text: Meži łˤonontow esira däder nexłin, mežurno riyx ža, hudun di mežułäy sider gurow yäqinč’i.

text: Kid q’arƛ’o saɣ yoqno.

translation: When they went home together, his wife reminded him: "There might be a sense to this hook under the ceiling."

text: Xot’on pˤaƛirno ʕoƛ’un iħułxor yeda ik’iƛ’oräy, reƛ’iƛer yäqiru t’it’oraq exoriłno ƛexun.
translation: His foot glided off and while he was falling into the river, he could hold to a bush with his hand.

text: Nediwt’a panduryabi elzox šomoƛała besʷanƛin”.

text: C’i rit’roƛin.”
translation: Extinguish the fire!"

text: Gulu begirno bayäru xanä ʕAwdunasireł adäz masala erno: neł guluƛ’no zown nesider ayru žek’ur xanłi teƛa yołƛin, amma guluƛ’no zown egirno xanä žek’u ayrinč’inäy, nesis kid q’ˤuyza xanzo žaxno yegirno, raƛ’no roɣno xanłiƛ’aygon žan kura yołƛin.
translation: The king who had sent the horse assigned Awdunasir a task: He would give the kingdom to the man who came to him riding that horse, but if it does not bring a man, then he would send his daughter to another king's son, take away his land and throw him off the throne.

text: Sasaqozox žarno yiynč’ey maħorno yoqno, q’orola žaz babiwq esin nesiƛ’ yałru q’ʷariłiłäy.
translation: In the morning, she secretly went outside; the widow told the boy's father of the sadness that was on him.

text: Howžin yołƛax žedu łinasnokin ħažałi anusi, paraxat babiwde sadaq ʕumru boyx.
translation: It is said they're still alive now and are living together with the father a calm life without a need for anything.

text: Nesä babiya adäz äru masalałäy esin.
translation: He told about the task that his father had put in front of him.

text: Bara eƛin, yaqˤuł žaq bełi č’aq’ˤin anu, sis bercinaw aɣin č’aq’irno ža idur aysƛin.

text: Xizyo xaner riyn, c’ohoresno, nesiz nukarzasno qunyox raqru łinałäyn.
translation: Then the king found out what had happened to the thief and to his henchmen on the farmstead.

text: Ɣowlotown eɣeni esiwn muk’ur oqno.

text: Hudaygon yeda łäƛ’or tungin bižin ik’in, nełaƛ’no aždaħä yisiqor roži eƛin anu.

text: Ža abužahalno adäzay ok’ek’no, nesiz iduza ezu-ʕoƛ’uƛ’oräy, q’ˤidaza insanes ƛuzabi rukayn.
translation: [He] took away this devil, and while he was examining his home, he saw human bones down there.

text: Sida quł čanyabi raynč’ey ričin.

text: Xizyogon esirno nesä, nesi t’omceni žek’ur roqäsi šebintow yołä, łuq’zinan oqäsi zowä žaƛin.

text: Eniwn goƛ’in esir mi, neła deber neł sualyor žawab naƛƛin”.

text: Sasaqosi rečuƛer yezunosi, elo mesed besun anu.
translation: In the morning the mother looked in the cattle shed and did not find gold.

text: Zamanałäy q’ˤimes idusi žek’u iłe oqxo yisis Ražbadin.
translation: After a while Razhbadin became like a member of his own family.

text: Yaqˤułƛa bok’ełsi mi däqay, ɣudegon baynosi ezʷan dowƛ’o diƛin pikrun boyn, iduɣorno ik’in yeda ħadur oqno ičin.
translation: "Today you escaped from me, but tomorrow I will see you when you come", he thought, went home and prepared oneself.

text: Adäy aderno ik’in ečruni žek’a nesiz baruqor eƛin, mežoxor Ražbadines ƛirba aynƛin.
translation: The old man went ahead and said to his wife: "A comrade of Razhbadin has come to you."

text: Xexoyä neła nełas eniwqor eƛin, wey eni, yisi žaƛ’ yałru ɣudi, babiya yeda ozurabin roɣno ent’o icin xecäsi yołƛin.
translation: The chick said to its mother: "Hey mother, this boy is so tortured, his father pricked his eyes out and left him tied here."

text: Žoyä eƛin, howda ɣˤutku k’emot’ya tełer rutira k’ʷeze roqinč’ey ƛexasiƛin.
translation: The boy said: "I can't turn this house into the wooden case anymore."

text: Debex yižixosi kid qazaqxo yegira mi razi oqzaƛ’, diyä razi oqač’inƛin.
translation: But if you agree to send the girl that should marry you to a Georgian, how could I not agree?"

text: "Xedyo yegäru miƛin, - eƛin nesä, - din yegiranƛin".
translation: 'Your husband let you go,' he said, 'so I will let you, too.'

text: Xediya äƛiru rodo miƛin, yeda baruƛ’ ƛ’iri utin.
translation: "Do what your husband said!", he insisted on his wife.

text: Qˤašiƛ’ɣor hawaƛ’ k’oƛin bayn asasi t’awus ɣunoƛ’ bičin.
translation: At dawn the heaven peacock came and sat at the tree.

text: Ža šinaw šebin eniwq esinosi, ħiƛ’oqus ħal biyranix hemedurtow užä q’ˤida kurno.
translation: After he had told his mother everything, in order to show the handkerchief's condition, the boy spread it out.

text: Nełaƛ’no zowerno yeda ƛ’irizoni dunyalƛ’aɣor egirno.
translation: They seated him onto it and sent him to the world above.

text: Azbaryoxor aynosi, yeda ħayranno oqno ezun ƛexux.
translation: When he came to the palace, he remained looking at it in astonishment.

text: Žeduqor big bičiƛinno eƛin, bełiqan hunix oqno nesä nesiz ʕaƛaɣor ik’anix.
translation: The hunter said to them: "Farewell!" and got on his way to go to his own village.

text: Bežet’ani esnaza yeda hemedurtow egirno anu.
translation: The elder brothers didn't let him do so.

text: T’omceni žek’u aynäy, nesä mi exarƛin.
translation: When the span-sized man comes, he will kill you!"

text: Nełagon roɣno neła nełaz bašił erno.
translation: She [Umukus] took it out and put it on her finger.

text: Yizir ža xan łinax iyx yizis babiw yałrułi.
translation: How should they know that this king is their father?

text: Nesä žazar wasiyat bodin:

text: Maħama žigon aƛ’in Aħmadqor, mi ʕabdal esunč’inäy eƛ’isi riƛu žiƛbo räƛiyäƛin.
translation: Again now Magomed scolded Akhmed: "If you weren't a fool, would you plough last year's field this year?"

text: Bilq’isdis xedyos eɣeni esiw ičix zown netey xalq’imor žukłi roda riqanƛin, žedu sidä sidƛ’or codix.
translation: Bilqisdi's husband had a younger brother, whenever possible he harmed people; and he incited them against one another.

text: Nełagon eniwqay izmun bisno, ža t’umi bexurač’inƛin nełqay rožin riqirno, akin raɣˤin tełxor begärun, bik’in kidbez q’ˤimƛ’o heqno.
translation: It [the pigeon] asked permission from the mother and took her word that she would never kill this pigeon; she opened the window and let it inside, and it flew in and sat down on the girl's head.

text: Žedu bičix zown ciqo sidaɣ qunyox.
translation: They were living on a farmstead somewhere in the forest.

text: Nełƛ’ay soder, dicegon Alimaħama qoqoƛiłin, šida mi qoqoƛixƛin, bara esirno anu.
translation: After that, no matter how often Alimagomed laughed, his wife didn't ask "Why are you laughing?" ever again.

text: Debin barusno c’ok’ini raqƛin, eƛin bazarganza.
translation: "You will have a quarrel with your wife." said the merchants.

text: Babiw ƛ’irir ayrun, žoyä esin nesiq eniya äƛiruxor, wallahi eniwn alžanä yoł.
translation: When the father came, the boy told him what the mother had said: "By God, mother is in heaven!

text: Neł mixƛ’o dibirä eƛin, žeda debeq c’aq’tow bit’araw esin.
translation: Thereupon the imam said: "They told you the very truth!"

text: Sida quł yeda žek’u ganzaq ƛexyabin ƛisiran, riƛułno ƛeɣanƛin oqnosi, didurnokin yizä yisiqor meč’ teƛnoanu, ƛ’iri reƛ’u äruntow, žeda boda t’ira xecix zownanu.
translation: One day the man started to utilize the bulls to pull logs and to plough the field, but they didn't comply at all, and when he put the yoke on them, they didn't feel like it at all.

text: Xizyo ža ela cimyoqaysi becurno boɣsiƛin.
translation: Afterwards we broke it out of rage and threw it away."

text: Nesä eƛin, tełäzay roč k’ek’-rik’ernosi, di meža izirƛin.
translation: He said: "When I move the rope from down there, lift me up!"

text: Maħlar bayruntow, xanä eƛin, eloš neła imyoqor yicalini ɣˤanazar naʕanan bodo, nocun caƛo nełasƛin.
translation: When they came into the court, the king said: "Curse and spit on that woman over there, who is tied to that pole!"

text: Ža hunin risno ik’iƛ’orey, sida maydanƛ’or keze oqno.
translation: When he took the way, he came to a clearance.

text: Łinasnokin pikru boynč’ey, esnabi ɣˤulabi caƛix qidobi kuša boqno.
translation: Without thinking, the brothers began demolishing the wall by throwing stones.

text: Elon eɣeni esiya žedur sis ħilla urɣizi bodin, araq’is bic’äsi q’urec’nin bižin ik’in, xanes nukarbin ħalik’no, elay eženi esiws cudi bok’ek’anƛin.
translation: There the younger brother thought of playing a trickery on them: he would lead a mule packed with vodka and would "water" the king's henchmen and then would steal his older brother's corpse from there.

text: Ele di t’o yoł, däder biħadanix boq maħorƛin”, - eƛin nesä žawab boyn.
translation: "Here I am, come out to me and fight!", he answered.

text: Däz halmaɣer sisgon uži odinƛin” - eƛin zira.
translation: "My friend has born another son", said the fox.

text: Nesiqorno hemedurtow, mesedyos saħ neƛnäy nexanƛin, eƛin.
translation: And she said to him as well: "If you give me your Sah of gold, I will come."

text: ʕAƛos nasin žamaʕatno butirno, reƛis ric’xot’a ħurubi eqerno žeduł adäza.
translation: They gathered the village's entire population and put basins filled with meat before them.

text: Užizar ža xabarno teqno, žedu daʕbałer begin, di ik’ixƛin Ħasan, ay di ik’ixƛin Ħusen.
translation: When the boys heard that, they started a dispute: "I am going!", said Hassan; "No, I am going!" said Hussein.

text: Ħalarno izin xedya eƛin, c’udani mamalays ħuli ozqo biħinäy, ozłer kanłi bayxƛin där teqäsi zows.
translation: The husband got up to help and said: "If one smears the red rooster's feather on the eye, the eyesight comes back... I've heard."

text: Łaymoƛ’or aynosi, Ismaʕil izin ʕArabuzande igorno ik’in xanqor eƛin, horo hoboy yiłas meč’ beč’oƛin.
translation: After he came to, Ismail got up and went to Arabuzan and said to the king: "Come now, cut off her neck!"

text: ƛirbas ħurmat bodo, baru, dey tatu łiyxƛin.
translation: Respect the guest, wife, my power is ending."

text: ƛ’irigon ħažat roqxosi šebin idun yoł.
translation: Furthermore, a home is a necessary thing.

text: Bˤałno gulun yeda qalamäɣor, nesä nesiz baruzadäɣor ezunč’ey ik’in.
translation: His horse rushed on to that fortress and he went to his wives straight without looking.

text: Žan bisno ik’in, łˤonon užibin tełäy boɣno, kʷaxatow ečruni žek’u xizor utin ayn.
translation: The old man took it and went away, freed his three sons and quickly came back.

text: Zey, däz halmaɣer uži odin.
translation: "Bear, a friend of mine has born a son.

text: Neła quł ħaƛ’ala yisiq łˤono besuro biqin.
translation: On that day, he hardly caught three fish.

text: (Ža kid c’aq’ bercinaw, ƛ’iräy ozuri boɣa retixanusi yiłe zown.)
translation: (That girl was so beautiful, that one wouldn't want to keep the eyes from her.)

text: ʕAƛar aynƛin teqzaƛ’ortow Ražbadin yizzoxor oƛixzaƛ’, kidbez babiya bigtow tamašan bodin, yisiz ħurmatyoxay rigu bišʷan rodin.
translation: Because Razhbadin appeared at their place before he heard that he had come to the village, the girl's dad was very surprised and out of respect for him he prepared a good meal.

text: Elay yoqno yik’in Bilq’isdi sida ʕaƛar yayn.
translation: From there Bilqisdi left and came to a village.

text: Dey ħumukuli bukaynč’uƛinno eƛix meža, nełas c’ik’iw sipatno esix.
translation: "You say that you haven't seen my camel, and you tell me its entire appearance.

text: Nedur resunäyƛin, Maħama kidbeqor eƛin, akrošaw magalun bižo, yik’in nesiqor eƛo, asno eräsi yoł, bucin žäd bic’äsianuƛin.
translation: "If that is the case," Magomed said to his daughter, "take this cheese bread, go and say to him: ''Fog has come up, and the moon is still not full."

text: Howži hudu xanä nesiqor eƛin, nedur resunäy, hi, enda micxir beč’xosi kesun biso, iłira becider mi nełdär ezäɣor izinč’inäy, debi di meč’ beč’a yołƛin.
translation: Then the king said to him: "If that's the case, here, take the coining form, and if you haven't gotten up the fortress to her within six months, I will cut off your neck."

text: Nagaħ uži miskinłiƛ’or aynäy, babiyä kobora teł eqerno xecäsi micxiryosno, mesedyosno ric’äsi ixin zown.
translation: In case the boy would hit poverty, there was a bag filled with money and gold left inside the granary, put there by the father.

text: Mač’a meč’ beč’oƛ, tupi rok’eł c’oxoƛ, ašunä oƛƛo eč’oƛƛin.
translation: May the dagger cut his neck, may the rifle hit is heart and may the belt cut him in the middle!

text: Žin Q’arum howlo žäk’ruxaysi saɣ oqaniƛax.
translation: They say that Qarum still didn't recover from the beatings.

text: Neła quł yiził siskin ciqes šebin dandi keze roqno anu.
translation: That day, they didn't encounter anything at all in the forest.

text: Best’al kidbes sis ziya zown.
translation: The stepdaughter had a cow.

text: Reƛno łiyn k’onč’aqay reč’no kuräsi zows, howlaytow iyos zok’in ric’äsi zowsƛin”, - esin yisä.
translation: "When the meat ended, I cut some from my leg and threw it to you, and from there I filled the blood into a mug", he told.

text: Xizyogon eženä, ac yaɣˤoƛin, eƛin.
translation: Then the old one said: "Open the door!"

text: Buq bätuza puƛ’azay guluƛ’no zown yisir eže žek’u nexxo ukayn.
translation: In the south he saw an old man riding on a horse coming towards him.

text: Xizyo yiła užiq esin, howži eliz guluz hisona teł mesed besuxanuƛin.
translation: Then she told to her son, "Now there is no gold in the trough of our horse."

text: Žas baru, hawšinaw q’ʷariłin biqin, xizaɣor idur yutizaƛ’.
translation: The son's wife, having endured so much sadness, has finally come back home.

text: Xizyogon ʕUmar ƛelƛ’or utin, Ox̌oyo ziruƛ’or utin, cek’ƛ’or ʕUmar - yeda ceyƛ’or, beƛ’q’ˤuƛ’or yeda - boc’ƛ’or Ox̌oyo, ziyƛ’or ʕUmar utin - zeyƛ’or ža.
translation: Then Umar turned into a lamb, Ohoyo turned into a fox, Umar into a goat kid - he into an eagle, he into a sheep - Ohoyo into a wolf, Umar turned into a cow - he into a bear.

text: Ayn žan eženi esiw ayruni kikxortow.
translation: Also he came to the spring the older brother came to.

text: ƛ’irigon C’iɣruk’ä acno yuqin naysinan ok’bi rok’no.
translation: But then Tsighruk closed the door and put in nails everywhere.

text: K’ox äƛirugon uži uqˤełanix ik’in.
translation: For the second time, the boy went into hiding.

text: Xizyogon c’urun-c’aranƛin” eƛixosi k’emot’ wˤałer yayn.
translation: Then the crate came down that she said "Tsurun-tsaran!" to.

text: Neł mixƛ’o ceyä žaqor eƛin, di dowqor yeɣe k’emot’ neƛan, ža mi dowz ɣˤutkoxor ayzaƛ’or yaɣˤinƛin.
translation: At that time the eagle said to the boy: "I will give you a small wooden case; don't you open it before you get to your house!"

text: ʕAbdal di zows yołƛin, eƛin ʕomoyä, ciqqotow łemuri mi di bac’ƛin.
translation: "If I were a fool," said a donkey, "you would've eaten me in the forest."

text: Elo q’ebedes užin xecin xizyo qartay ħadur yoqno yik’anix.
translation: And the witch prepared to go away, leaving the smith's son there.

text: Nesiq reƛ’an ata regirinč’ey Tawadä eƛin, yeda eluł teł sisxoli yoł.
translation: Tavadi didn't let them touch him and said: "He is alone among us."

text: Xizyo babiwn oqno ža biqiranixƛin.
translation: Then the father began [trying] to catch him.

text: Yoƛƛo ƛˤeb yik’izaƛ’ortow žedur užin oynƛax.
translation: After less than a year has gone by, a son was born to them.

text: Eluqƛa iziräłinč’i mi elo ƛ’irir, hudunon eloš neła ceyqo harizi rodan elaƛin”, - eƛin xanä.
translation: "We cannot lift you up there, so we will beg that eagle over there", said the king.

text: Didur ža reč’xosi där riyxanu, sosi mitow reč’oƛin, eƛin Ħusenä nesiqor.
translation: "I don't know how to chop it down, you chop first!", said Hussein to him.

text: Boƛƛ’o zaman bik’inosi, kidbä neła nełdaɣor eniw yižin.
translation: In the meantime, the girl took her mother to her own place.

text: Elaygon bitor hunix ik’iƛ’oräy, sidaɣ yisir raqˤäsi bix resun.
translation: While he went on on his way from there, he found thick grass in one place.

text: Ozuri c’axrun kidber riyn ža nełas bašiqˤoy yałrułi.
translation: When the girl saw it, she immediately knew that it was her ring.

text: Buqbetaxor yizi sida iħuz lilyoxor baynosi, muši begiranix bazarganbin, yedan q’ˤida bičin.
translation: Toward sunset, when they came to a river bank, the merchants and he sat down to take a break.

text: Łˤora äƛiru ɣudƛ’ogon xanä bertenno bodin yeɣeni esiw yown.
translation: And on the third day the king arranged a marriage and married the young sister.

text: Hoboyƛa yedu bišʷa ħadur radiru žek’u iyrinč’ey xecač’inƛin, oqno yeda uži.
translation: "Now I will find out the man who has prepared this food," began the boy.

text: Mekos nece ħal boqzaƛ’, roɣno ozuri teƛnosi dandi, žukani halmaɣä igunir rac’a biša teƛno.
translation: Because the hunger's strength got so big, he took away his eye and gave it to him, and the bad friend gave the good one food to eat, right away.

text: Dicegon bazarganä ħaƛoƛin c’ok’inayn yaħmoƛ’ ayra oqłin, uži et’un k’oƛin ičin ħaƛuxanuƛin.
translation: How much the merchant scolded him to bring him to his conscience: "Drink!", the boy ran away [saying]: "I don't drink."

text: Din zows elo, nucin ragis, zerun ħaƛus, ruy mešok’labi taraw at’iłinč’u.
translation: I was there as well, I ate honey, I drank booze, and indeed only the moustaches got wet!

text: Yigtow yedu ƛ’iri yutinosi, wacʕal žedzoxaɣor ik’a muk’ur oqno.
translation: When she insisted very much, the cousin agreed to go to their [place].

text: Sisgon čuret’ bok’nosi, raładyos bitazoni lilyoxar boqno yizi.
translation: He beat the whip once more and they came to the other coast of the sea.

text: Ža madaxor yaynosi, oƛixno k’oƛin užä, tełxor yoqƛin, iduɣor goƛ’in.
translation: When she came to the door, the boy came running and invited her into the home: "Come inside!"

text: Därgon nesi užix yik’a retin.
translation: And I want to marry that boy too."

text: Eženi esiya beɣet’ani esnazaqor eƛin:
translation: The eldest brother said to his younger brothers:

text: Hemedur neła ɣˤanaza k’edin, bercinaw, q’ˤimaku kid doxturä yown.
translation: So and so, that woman found a beautiful girl for the doctor and he married.

text: Elo yisir aɣbi žeda žedaqor xabaryayx teqno, yedu ahli łinaqor rezun ričäsi žin močibi riƛinč’ey.
translation: There he heared the birds talking among themselves: "What are those people looking for, why didn't they plow the fields already?

text: Xizyogon ža uži butnoƛ’ pun bok’no äčiru žek’uder ayn.
translation: Then the boy came to a man who was lying on his burka.

text: Q’ˤano äƛiru ƛˤeb yac’nosi, dahawgon bikoris tatu łiyn.
translation: When he ate the second leaf, the snake's power ended a little more.

text: Gogaƛ’ äsuruni žek’a eƛin, yedu dey kero yoł, debi esiwn t’o yołƛin.
translation: The man he found on the meadow said: "This is my ravine, your sister is there as well.

text: Xizyo neł kidbeƛ’or keč’ boɣno:
translation: Afterwards he sang a song about this girl:

text: Šebi zowä deber, nełƛ’asi eso miƛin, eƛin ečruni žek’a.
translation: So tell me what you have to tell!", said the old man.

text: Madax gulun bicin xecin, uži Beqes ʕUneyzatdäɣor ik’in.
translation: The boy left the horse tied to the door and went next to Sun's Uneyzat.

text: Bełiqa eƛin, eloša ƛ’iri iƛ’u yołäsi čanta neƛ där, dowqay di q’ˤuya šebin räsič’iƛin.
translation: The hunter said: "Give me the pocket with the lid above, there; I won't take any other thing from you."

text: Hemedur yeda zawzay, pandurya tełäzay q’ˤanoquno oc’ino łelaw, q’ˤanoquno oc’ino guluƛ’no zown saldatbi boƛixno.
translation: When he was like this, 50 soldiers by foot and 50 soldiers on horses appeared out of the lute.

text: Neširu Alimaħama sadaq yałru hudulzaqor eƛin, sasaq ħonqaɣor bˤeƛ’ rižinäy elurtow rigu räčiƛin.
translation: In the evening Alimagomed said to the friends who were with him: "If we lead the sheep to the mountain tomorrow, it'll be better for us."

text: Wayčo, baħarči, egixwa mi däqƛin!” - aħi bodin ceyä.
translation: "Hey, brave young man, I cannot keep you anymore!", warned the eagle.

text: Nesä sadaq royxosi šebin neła ɣˤanazar rukayx zown.
translation: That woman was seeing all the things he did.

text: Nexno nesä guluƛ’no zowerno ciqaɣor esiwn yižin ik’in.
translation: He came, seated his sister onto the horse and lead her into the forest.

text: Yisiqor yigza ħalał baru c’ok’inayn, ƛelin maħ xecin nexzaƛ’.
translation: His wife scolded him quite much, because he came and left the lamb outside.

text: Yaqˤułtow ħonƛ’ar miƛ’eł xizay boc’in begirno, neła ža bayrnosi, nasin šet’un rutin riša yołƛin.
translation: Today the wolf will be sent into the mountains to bring a ram, and then it will be eaten.

text: Elo k’edanayx ɣˤʷay yałzay, sidaɣaza got’eł gondun yoɣno, ziru bixiq c’i atagon reʕizi boqno.
translation: While the dog was being searching there, the fox made a hole in some place in the haystack and hurried to set fire to the hay.

text: Xizyo ƛelza eƛin, wey, eni, dice igu aħo yoł elusƛin.
translation: Then the lambs said: "Hey mom, what a good shepherd we have!"

text: Idur bayruntow dahaw mušin begirno, bertenyołer butin yizi.
translation: Right when they came home, they took a little rest and returned to the marriage again.

text: Žan ayrno eɣeni esiw aynosi, c’ik’iw ʕaƛos xalq’i ɣˤaɣˤu bizin.
translation: When the young brother came bringing him, all the village's people got happy.

text: Užide igorno ayn, šebi mi t’o rodixƛin, nesiqay bayan bisno.
translation: He came near the boy and wanted to know from him: "What are you doing here?"

text: Xizyo xanes kidbä yik’in Musaq raqru łinałäyn esin.
translation: Afterwards, the king's daughter went and told Musa about what had happened.

text: Zeyä šebi ci cedäƛin esirnosi, zira boƛƛäyr bayäru cedisƛin eƛin.
translation: After the bear had asked, what name they gave him, the fox said, they named him 'up-to-the-middle'.

text: Sida quł yiła Ox̌oyo šebi žek’u yałrułi ʕUmarqo esin.
translation: One day she told Umar what man Ohoyo was.

text: Howlotow neła babiwqor maħor čˤaƛanix t’asno teƛno, eƛin, baba, yiła ɣˤutka eynoyxosi gurow žek’u išxosi anuƛin.
translation: There she gave her father the bowl in order to pour it outside and said: "Daddy, in this house only someone who works, gets to eat!"

text: Nesiqor užin teƛno, gama tełäyn yoqno ža raładyołer k’oƛin.
translation: She gave him the son, got out of the boat's inside and jumped into the sea.

text: Q’ˤiday bet’un k’oƛin xediwn užin nełaq łeɣin, babiwn esiwn ʕuraw ɣˤaɣˤu bizin.
translation: The husband and the son jumped up and grasped her, the father and the brother got very happy.

text: Užä eƛin, wallah, di debi kidno esirno nexäsi yołƛin.
translation: The boy said: "By God, I have come to ask for [the hand of] your daughter."

text: Žedu bužzi boqnoanu, mi iłeni łaciraqa xanes kidbabi rawnč’i, nediwt’a gulubi yołi nay deber riqixƛin.
translation: They didn't believe him: "A light-headed person like you wouldn't bring the king's daughters, however, where did you get such horses from?"

text: Ɣˤanazader ɣuron regirno, eluq ħalikataw šebin deber roqsiƛin ƛ’iräy žan yoɣno, nełar q’omexno bodin, roqno žeda žedaz q’aymozaƛ’ar ciqaɣor rik’in.
translation: The cows were sent to the woman, "We've done something mean to you", they said; they apologized to her, said farewell to her and began to go towards the forest over their own settlement.

text: Sida quł madaħazay yeda ayzaƛ’or šet’ur bac’ad rodäsi ɣˤutku resun.
translation: One day, when he came from outside, he found the house all cleaned everywhere.

text: Doxturä eƛin, min t’o ičin di äšinč’i, mi dey tušman yoł, mi baq’ˤu ʕazab neƛsiƛin.
translation: The doctor said, "You are my enemy and as long as you are here, I will not eat. You caused me much torture."

text: Xizyo reƛ’an reč’no, waħozoni ɣˤutka q’orolan šušin, k’uläɣor žamaʕatyołxor ik’in.
translation: Then he cut off her hand, and burried the widow in the cellar, then he went to the village's assembly in a side street.

text: Hemełƛ’otow xanä nukar egirno, bełiquz žał xizay.
translation: Upon that the king sent a henchman after the hunter's son.

text: Yizirabi q’ˤanon Ražbadinzo barudäɣorno, xexzadäɣorno bik’in.
translation: And the two of them went to his wife and the children.

text: Mi esiwq eso, sidaɣ mečoƛ’ sis aždaħno, nełasgon łˤono xexoyn yoł.
translation: Tell your brother that there is a dragon and its three youngs in some place.

text: Łarašawni ɣˤanazas maduhalłiqtow esiws kid zown.
translation: In the pregnant woman's neighbourhood lived her brother's daughter.

text: Boc’ägon neła nełde sadaq kumakyorƛin zirun bižin.
translation: The wolf took the fox with him for help.

text: Howži yeda ʕoloqbin bižin iħuxorno ik’in, eč’izas šemrebi rutirno žeda.
translation: Now he lead young men to the river and they collected piles of pebbles

text: Žan di riža retinč’ey, dey boyxosi ħalt’i bexuxƛin, xizay yayn.
translation: She came behind us, because she doesn't want me to take it with me, because my work that I do is coming to and end.

text: Yik’inƛax yedu, resun elo ec’no, rigu šeƛ’u.
translation: She went and found new, pretty clothes there.

text: Hič’č’a yeɣenigon hardizi oqa oqno, däx horoƛin.
translation: Then the youngest one began to request: "Come to me!"

text: Sisgon ƛˤeb yet’urno yiħinosi, hoq’ˤonigon ozłer kanłi bayn.
translation: He pulled out a single leaf and after smearing it [onto his eye], the other eye got its eyesight back.

text: Žedu barubi Ismaʕilä babiwder rižinč’ey, qalamä teł ruqˤno xecin.
translation: These wives Ismail didn't lead to his father, but left them hidden inside a fortress.

text: Mesedyosno micxiryosno ric’äsi reže ixinno gugyoq caƛin aw boƛixno ƛ’ˤuƛ’orno bizin beqa kecno bičin.
translation: The mouse with the big bag filled with gold and money threwn over [its back] got up halfway on the roof and went to sleep in the sun.

text: ʕArabuzanno qalamäy maħor yoqno, Alay-bulayƛin eƛixozoni nełaznitow guluƛ’no zown.
translation: And Arabuzan came out of the fortress, riding on her horse named Alay-Bulay.

text: Dibirä eƛin, mužmarƛ’o yik’in hemeła kikxay mi łi ħaƛunäy, deber kid yadiƛin.
translation: The imam said: "When you go on Friday and drink water out of that spring, you will have a daughter."

text: Šida mi nece q’ʷaridno oqno ičäsiƛin.
translation: Why have you become so sad?"

text: Ismaʕilä eƛin, där moƛał yukayru kid yiqizaƛ’or, di ɣˤanazaq reƛ’a atixosi anuƛin.
translation: Ismail said: "Until I get the girl that I saw in my dream, I will not lay hand on any woman."

text: Madaxaɣor isno boxirno, yisä kidbeq esin, žigoƛ’aygon hemediw šebin daq ƛ’iri risrersiƛin.
translation: He chased the bull to the yard and told his daughter: "And once again he lets me fulfil such a thing."

text: Yayn sida yeže ɣˤanaza yiłaq esirno, mi šida bertenyołxor yik’ixanuƛin.
translation: An old woman came along and asked her: "Why don't you go to the marriage?"

text: Deber di dice yetix, där mi nełƛ’aygon aq’ˤu etix.
translation: However much you love me, I love you even more than that.

text: Babiw pikruzał ik’ix zown, didurey yizidäy hozur ok’łanƛin.
translation: The father was going with the thoughts: "How can I get rid of them?"

text: Łinasnotow čaran bexun, rok’un roč’iłno yizis ža łi riqanixaysi, xizor butin nexƛ’oräy, yizi esnabi bičixozoni mečoƛ’or bayn.
translation: Their chances died and their hearts freezed from not getting that water, and while they were returning, they came to the place where the brother and the sister lived.

text: Žedu xexoyabi rac’anixƛin bizixƛax ža bikori.
translation: The snake got up to eat the young eagles.

text: Kid elaygon waħozoni dunyalƛ’o yexurno...”
translation: Our daughter got killed in the world below that one..."

text: Nesä q’ebedzo žaqor eƛin, sosi däq yałru micxir bac’no łiyran, xizyo dowqosi łiyranƛin.
translation: He said to the smith's son: "First we'll spend my money, then we'll spend yours."

text: Yisił dandir nuci teƛxosi bazargan c’oxno.
translation: He met a merchant who was selling honey.

text: Howžin yiłas siħru bäsuƛin habihanes rok’ƛ’orno rayn, bižin ƛ’e yałza bˤoƛ’ä teł eqerno.
translation: "Now it might be his slyness again." the miller thought, carried it and put it into the larder where the malt was.

text: Neła qułno yizi esnabi q’ʷarid boqno bayn, sistow sis kid xanes hoboy ƛexus, žakin yiqixana elułäy sidernoƛin.
translation: And the next day, the brothers came home sad: "One single daughter of the king remain's now, and just one of us will get her."

text: Sasaqosi moƛaxay yäziru eniw, ɣˤutkoł adäza mesedyos ƛ’ˤu yołäsi ɣˤutku rukayn.
translation: The next morning the mother who got up from her sleep, saw a house with a golden roof in front of her house.

text: Ža xabarno bexurno baħaray Tawadidäɣor bitoxƛ’oräy, hobolo q’ˤida yičin yičiƛin, yisä igor nexa yegirxanu.
translation: When the bride had finished her story and approached Tavadi, "Sit down there and wait!" he [said and] didn't let her come closer.

text: Hemełƛ’o łˤono bełiqubi boƛixno.
translation: At this time, three hunters appeared

text: Howt’o aɣˤʷa teł žek’uyey zowsƛin.
translation: And there seemed to be a man inside this mill!"

text: Žeda ža yižin eɣeni esiwqor yayrno.
translation: They carried it away and brought it to the younger brother.

text: Yeda neła gogaƛ’or aynosi, naysinan c’oxäsi buqno yołƛax, elozagon k’ešonayx xexoybin resun.
translation: When he came on that meadow, the sun shined everywhere, and he found the youngs playing over there.

text: Ža łi ƛ’iri riħinosi, siskin raqinč’iru riłe užisno, ceyesno raqˤyabi ixin.
translation: He smeared the water on it and the boy's and the eagle's wounds healed like nothing had happened.

text: Kid yukaynosi, eniw haganiłno ƛexun, becä žedzox yičix zawru kid nece bercinaw yałru nełar howži gurow riynč’izaƛ’.
translation: Having seen the girl, the mother was amazed that the girl was living with them for one month and because she only now noticed how beautiful the girl was.

text: Ža C’irdux yałrun iyn, łinasnotow čaran bexun p’estow anusi ɣamasya teł Qartayn ƛexun.
translation: She [then] knew it was Tsirdukh and that there was no way out, and without any sound, Qartay stayed in the box.

text: Zozis ƛ’ˤu yołäsi qoš zawru, howži elo rigu bercinaw ɣˤutku yołƛax.
translation: Where the shelter with the moss roof used to be, there was now a good, beautiful house.

text: Užä eƛin, šida mi di bexura bayx, dowƛ’äygon q’ʷariłiƛ’ din oqzaƛ’ƛin.
translation: The boy said, "Why would I kill you, when I'm much more sad than you?"

text: Xizyo neła eƛin, bik’anon bik’in debi mi mec bitir nesizoq, riya rodanixgon nesis hibon bisno däqor baƛin.
translation: Then she said: "Go, go, and touch his tongue with yours, and take his stick and bring it to me so I can mark it."

text: Nełaƛ’no mamalay q’oq’oƛixosin, akbar roɣxosin bičinč’izaƛ’, šudno rič’in tełer yezunosi, elo yiłar bercinaw kid yesun.
translation: Then the rooster still didn't stop crowing and cackling, so she dug out the grave, looked inside and found a beautiful girl.

text: Žigoƛ’ay uyn oƛixno k’oƛin nełas xediw.
translation: This time her husband jumped in indeed.

text: Howlotow erno maharno, nesi žax baħarayn yegirno.
translation: There they set up the wedding and the bride was married off to the boy.

text: Igza ħalał žan akik’no, gulu biqa bičin.
translation: The horse tired him well, and then let him catch itself.

text: Mi däƛ’ ʕayibno badin, di dowder harizi oqno aysƛin.
translation: Don't blame me [that] I came to ask you.

text: Užä žawab teƛno, meža neła quł däz raɣä režigon raq’ˤu rešoni roys yołi ƛuzabin čˤaƛin, režigon raq’ˤu his räqi där bazaryayƛin.
translation: The boy answered: "If you had produced more garbage and threw the bones onto my veranda that day, I would've got much more wool from the market."

text: Yizä eli inžit bodixƛin, nasin xalq’i ƛ’irin butin, hut’-mˤalä nocu caƛix bazar xecirerno žeduq.
translation: "They are disgracing us!", all people insisted and spitting in their faces caused them to leave the market.

text: Užä nesä nesidäɣorno rižin ik’in, siskin žedar rac’anix šebin teƛinč’ey quqa łera ɣudi rečuƛ rišin ričirno.
translation: The boy led them to himself and gave them nothing to eat for 5 days, making them hungry, and he drove them into the shed.

text: Sida quł kidbä nesi žaqor eƛin:
translation: One day the girl said to the boy:

text: Sida quł užiz rok’ƛ’or rayn, netintow däde sadaq babiwn äčinč’i, din besurobi riqiranix ruhun oqa ayƛin.
translation: One day the son thought: "My father won't always live with me, I need to learn how to catch fish."

text: Yedu Ismaʕiles qˤaƛu yołƛin, łˤonon barubi ret’un k’oƛin maħor roƛixno.
translation: "That's Ismail's shouting!", the three wives ran outside.

text: Ceyä ža ħonƛ’orno bižin, sida ixiw t’ekzo šilazan eqerno bac’xo zown.
translation: The eagle carried it onto a mountain, put it on the horns of a big goat and ate.

text: Eƛ’isi riƛu žek’us žiƛbosi q’ˤimes ričixosiƛin.
translation: "Last year's field was someone else's, this year's field is his own."

text: Xizyogon nesä xanqo esirno, hudu mi šida eƛa, k’ešonad mi, pakmos ʕabasi, debeq hudun siskin šebin roqxanuƛin.
translation: Then he asked the king: "So why did you say: 'Play, copper coin, nothing can become of you anyway!'?"

text: Däz eniya neširuxayt’a bexayn miƛ’in eli bišerxosiƛin, C’iɣruk’ä žawab bodin.
translation: "My mother slaughtered a ram every night and fed us", he replied.

text: Xizyo užä eƛin nełqor:
translation: After this, the boy said to her:

text: Bežet’ani esnabi baynosi, yisä yiziq esin, žedu łˤonon gulun dey zows, žedu łˤonon xanes kidbabin qalamzo ƛ’ˤuƛ’azan roɣno rok’ek’no di rowsƛin.
translation: When the older brothers came home, he told them: "These three horses are mine, and these three daughters of the king I stole from the roof of the fortress and brought them here."

text: Neła karu rok’xayzo mušimo ħalaq’awni gulus maħi ƛ’iräytow ret’urno roɣno.
translation: Its breath, exhaled from the heart, pulled out the tail from the thin horse.

text: Žeda esnaza biłe sadaq ʕumru bodix zown.
translation: They were living together like brothers.

text: Elo räsuru ɣˤutkaɣor ik’inosi, teł qartay yołƛax, q’ˤunader čˤaƛin kočorin q’ˤidan yičin yičäsi.
translation: When he went into a house that stood there, inside there was supposed to be a witch, that was sitting down with hear hear spread.

text: Bełis reƛno teƛno kidbä q’orolay yegirno.
translation: The girl gave the widow deer meat and sent her away.

text: Zamanałäy yizi yeda rešt’izi aqza mečoƛ’or ačqˤayx, mekoyxno bayn, salamno teƛno žaq ħaƛanir łi esirno, deber ela micxir neƛanƛin.
translation: After a while, they were thirsty and hungry and came to where he was resting, saluted him and asked the boy for some water to drink: "We will give you some money."

text: Allahes ħukmu nediw zownƛin šukrun bodin, nełaz ža q’aʕidaƛ’ yisä yizi buyn šušin, elaygon bitor ik’in.
translation: "So was Allah's decision.", he [said,] showed patience, burried them in its manner and went away from there.

text: Nełaƛ’ barun razi yoqno.
translation: Also his wife agreed on that.

text: Habihanƛ’osi q’ʷariłin bukayn, ziru ƛ’iräyn boqno, xanzo kidbederno bik’in eƛin, ƛ’iräy yoq, mi di yaƛ’irsi.
translation: The fox saw the sadness that was upon the miller, forgave [him], went to the king's daughter and said: "Forgive [me], I cheated you."

text: Howža xabarno boqno, boƛƛo baq’ˤu zaman bik’izaƛ’ortow, yedu kid q’ˤuyazix yegirno.
translation: Not much later after this conversation, that girl was given to someone else.

text: Neła rac’ru bišʷan rac’erno yičiräsi zown kid.
translation: He fed the girl with the food that he ate and kept her.

text: Xanä eƛin, mi dey uži saɣ odinäy, roƛik’u dey bečełi deber neƛanƛin.
translation: The khan said, "If you cure my son, I will give you one half of my riches."

text: Xexbi anuru bazarganƛ’o ci Žungutaw zown.
translation: The name of the merchant who had no children was Jungutav.

text: Ričiƛ, mi äƛiru rodanƛin habihan razi oqno.
translation: "So be it, I will do what you said.", agreed the miller.

text: Moƛaxay izinosi, xan ħayran oqno ƛexun puħo t’akan yukaynosi.
translation: When the king got up from sleeping, he was surprised when he saw the glass at his side.

text: Nełaz gugyoƛ’ zawrunigon šebin din riyranƛin:
translation: And I will explain what was on its back:

text: Žigoƛ’aygon bełiqanza bišʷa rodanix q’ˤano žek’u xecin, hudukinda žeda ħal bayranƛin.
translation: This time, the hunters left two men there to make the food, so that maybe in this way they would be strong enough.

text: Riƛu riƛixoza eƛin, žiƛbosi riƛixƛin.
translation: The plougher said: "I'm ploughing this year's."

text: Nesiqorno bezun bičin yiziq ʕuraw mix bik’in.
translation: Waiting and looking for him, much time passed for them.

text: Sosisi ʕomoyn, mešin ričäsi zown regirxanuƛin, mežan ela riłetow eƛnoqor ħadurłi roda yałiƛin.
translation: At first, the donkey and the calf remained not letting them in: "You must as well, just like us, make preparation for the winter!"

text: Boɣno kurno c’iday hibon esirno, šebi deber retinƛin.
translation: He took the stick out of the fire, threw it away and asked. "What did you want?"

text: Hudayzikogon ʕaƛoz posurxo best’al eniya neła nełas kid yegirno.
translation: The next day the stepmother sent her own daughter to the village's cattle.

text: Mi moƛaxay yiziranix ħažałi zownč’u, baq’ˤu xalq’i däz kumakyor bayäsi zows, dikin žeda eynoda egirinč’uƛin, eƛin yisä.
translation: "There was no need to wake you up from your sleep, many people were coming to help me and they even didn't let me work.", she said.

text: Raqru šebin nełaqno esin, ik’in yeda iduɣor.
translation: He told her what had happened and went home.

text: Sidadäza puƛ’aza ɣˤays iħu boƛixno, sidadäzagon łäs iħu boƛixno.
translation: On the one side a milk river appeared, and on the other side a water river appeared.

text: Idu yäsuru kidbä eƛin, elin hemedur meži riłetow hunin łaħin rayäsi.
translation: The girl that they found in the house said: "Just like you, we lost our way and came here.

text: Hawaƛ’ k’oƛin bik’in ɣʷˤadi.
translation: The crow flew.

text: Howži t’o roda šebin anuƛin, ik’in yeda elay ayno sida šaharyar.
translation: "Now there's nothing I can do", he said, and so he went on from there and came to a city.

text: Šebi retinƛin, cey hawaƛ’ k’oƛin bayn.
translation: "What do you want?" the eagle came flying.

text: Žaqˤułno esix žedu elo yołƛin.
translation: It is said that they are still there today.

text: Elo rišxo žedaƛ žedu ʕomoybi yałzay, ƛ’irir bayn boc’ä eƛin, meži di howži rexurno rac’a yołƛin.
translation: While the donkeys were among themselves there are ate, a wolf came along and said to the donkeys: "I will kill and eat you!"

text: Kecno ƛexunƛin”, - eƛin mamalayä.
translation: "I slept in", said the rooster.

text: Žan bit’araw yoł nesisƛin, eƛin kidbä.
translation: "That of him is true.", said his daughter.

text: Nesi žader yik’anir xedya nełar izmu teƛno.
translation: The husband gave her the permission to go to this boy.

text: T’eq’erbašiƛ’orno ičin ʕAt’idä bok’no hibon howlortow aw bexurno.
translation: Atid waited cautiously, hit the stick on it and killed the mouse.

text: Hemełƛ’o bexayn zawruni ƛelez reƛiłer maħ nazay baya bayxƛin?”
translation: "Where does the smell of this lamb's meat, that has been slaughtered, come from?"

text: Łeno ƛˤeb yayzay sodertow yisir guluƛ’ zown aħnada koƛ’ix zown.
translation: After he became five years old, he understood to ride around on a horse.

text: Hudun eženi esiya xecinč’izaƛ’, nesä äƛiruxor rodin.
translation: Nevertheless, because the older brother wouldn't leave it be, he did what he said.

text: Sida quł neła ʕaƛa sida bečedaw žek’a beže berten bodix zown.
translation: One day a rich man in that village was making a big marriage.

text: Iħäy łi ħaƛanƛin yeda wˤałer užinosi, q’ˤimƛ’ay bˤoƛ’un bik’in k’uk, ƛ’iräy wˤałer łiya bižin.
translation: With the intention to drink from the river, he bowed down and the cap fell down off is head and was carried downstream by the water.

text: Neła ƛuza radiru qˤodaza beqes rešambi c’oxno.
translation: Through the hole that the bone had made, the sun's ray shined.

text: Oƛƛozo eƛin:
translation: The middle one said:

text: Neła ža yižin idu eqernosi, acno serun nełde puħorno ik’in, royxosi šebinon royn, mi tełerno oqno ɣamas yexar, xizyo žeda ža q’ač’azi yodanixƛin elay xizor yegärƛin.
translation: After she carried you inside, unlock the door, and go to her side, do what is to be done, go back inside and destroy the box; afterwards they will send it back to be repaired.

text: Elo yisir q’ˤim anusi lagax laga besun.
translation: There he found a body without a head.

text: Aluk’ani biqirxoƛin zawru yeda yisis reƛ’a qˤabaniƛ’ č’aq’ˤin.
translation: When he was catching the white one, his hand touched the black one.

text: Exaniƛ’or aynosi, eženä žaqor wasi bodin, di exunosi łinaqnokin baraxšizi oqinč’ey sadaq’an rułir, buq boƛäxza puƛ’aɣor gurow saparno bäcinoƛin.
translation: When his dead came, the old one made a testament to his son: "After I have died, do not pity anything but share the alms and go on a journey only to the place where the sun goes up!"

text: Žan bižin ziru ɣʷˤadidäɣor bik’in.
translation: And the fox carried it to the crow.

text: Xizyo babiya žaqor eƛin, ele uži, di eƛix zownč’a dowqor ža bercinawni aɣi bexarno mi, šomoraxno žuk roqa yoł deber, howži rok’ƛ’or ayxa di.
translation: Afterwards the father said to his son, "There, son, didn't I say to you not to kill that beautiful bird, or bad things would happen to you ever so often, have you understood this now?

text: Hemełƛ’o yisä besuros mus bik’urno.
translation: At that point he burned the hair of the fish.

text: Ričas ži babiya bayru wasimoƛ’ req’unƛin”, - eƛin uži reƛ’iqoyä tełer ezunosi, magalazaƛ’ rayzaƛ’or, elo teł anuru šebintow zown anu.
translation: "May it be according to my father's testament, now.", said the boy and when he looked inside the glove, and it was nothing inside except for bread.

text: Elor bayrun, yizi žazas šudabi rukaranixƛin bižix.
translation: When they arrived there, they were immediately guided to their sons' graves.

text: Xanä bełiqus uži nesä nesider goƛ’in.
translation: The king invited the hunter's son to himself.

text: Hawaƛ’ k’oƛin rik’a adäza q’ˤunan esnaza yiłaqor eƛin, howži mi eladay hozuƛ’or k’oƛis, ƛ’irir ayn sida bahadurä yäži miƛin.
translation: Before the two sisters flew into the air, they said to her: "Now you seperated yourself from us. But one hero came around to take you with him."

text: C’udani guluƛ’no zown, c’udagon šeƛ’un šeƛ’un, yaraɣno koyä bisno yeda xanzo qalamqaɣor ik’in.
translation: The red one he rode, put on red clothes, took a weapon in his hand and rode to the king's fortress.

text: Yisä eƛin, eloš neła guħxaza regin däł xizay bikorbi nexxoƛin.
translation: He said: "Over there, behind that hill, snakes are coming behind me."

text: Horoƛin, eƛix xizyo Ražbadinä halmaɣłiqor, Huläɣor eli łinar bik’an, howt’aytow bˤeƛ’no rok’ek’no xizor butin rigu anaƛin.
translation: "Come", said Razhbadin to his friends afterwards, "why shouldn't it be better for us to go back to Georgia, steal some from the flock and then return?"

text: Elaygon bitor ik’iƛ’oräy nesi užił sis ɣˤanabi c’oxno.
translation: While he went further from there, that boy met a woman.

text: Žeda äƛiruxor, yisä yedu sidaɣ ciqa teł maqˤiƛ’or yižin ik’in.
translation: According to what they said, he brought her far away into some forest.

text: Łˤonosi žiman eqerno iduɣor bowƛ’oräy, kak royxo Riħoxaz äčiru q’ˤidarno bˤoƛ’un t’ok’ow dicegon k’ediłin biqinč’u däq.
translation: I put three (coins) in my pocket and when bringing them home, I did a prayer in Rikhokh and they fell down and I couldn't find them anymore no matter how much I searched.

text: Di iłeni žek’a rac’xoƛin, et’un k’oƛin žawab teƛno Ismaʕilä nełar.
translation: "A man like me is eating it!", Ismail answered and jumped toward her.

text: Zamanałäy, łarnokin yukaynč’ey, yedu kid iduɣorno yik’in, šeƛ’un roɣno neła nełas ħošin šeƛ’un, yałzatow pečmox kecno yičin.
translation: After a while, the girl went home with nobody seeing her, took off the clothes, put on her hide, and went to sleep at the oven where she was.

text: Hemełaquł sida sirya k’eč’oli bodin.
translation: That day, one mare gave birth to a foal.

text: Łˤoran esnazaq aždaħ čerayx zown, raładyoł boqanix där kumak bodoƛin.
translation: The dragon was begging the three sisters: "Help me getting over this sea!"

text: Bizin aɣˤuz mogolar bezunosi, nełar elo žek’u esun.
translation: When he got up to look into the mill's granary, he found the man there.

text: Nełaq aw biqix zownanu.
translation: He didn't catch any mice.

text: Sida zamanałäzagon xanes roƛno.
translation: After some time, the king got sick again.

text: Šebi roqäƛin esirnosi, neła esin, där žukłi rodanix xalq’i urɣizi boqxoƛax, mi bełiƛ’ ik’inosi, di t’o sisxoli yuƛ’ˤxo.
translation: After he asked: "What happened?", she told: "Some people are apparently planning to harm me; after you went hunting, I was afraid here alone.

text: Sasaqosi xanes uži q’ʷarid oqno ičin.
translation: In the morning the prince was sad.

text: Žeduł teł yałru hič’č’a bexorani bˤeliz ɣunä eƛix teqno, šet’urtow yałru ažomzas ʕadlu däq boqxo, sistow sis yałru barus ʕadlun boqinč’ey, yeda ʕabdal ħonƛ’aɣor ik’ix, nesä nesił xizay rexada šebin rowanixƛin.
translation: And he heard the highest poplar trees saying among themselves: "I can control the trees that are around me, but this fool cannot even control the single wife that he has, and he is going onto the mountain to bring something to slaughter after himself."

text: Gamiƛ’no zown žedu raładyołäza bik’a bayx zown.
translation: On a boat they had to cross the sea.

text: Sasiłace yaħna-yik’ix madazaħno yičin, xizor yutin idur yaynosi, žeda kidbazaqor yiła eƛin, howžär di mežar užiłun zows, amma di kid yołƛin.
translation: Until it got dark she was outside walking around, and after she came back home, she said to those girls: "Until now I was a boy for you, but I am a girl."

text: Aždaħä ʕAliqilič izirno ok’no xot’obi cuciłace raƛ’moƛxor šiširno.
translation: The dragon lifted Aliqilich and beat him into the ground until his feet were stuck.

text: Ceyä eƛin, howži däłer ħal bays, mi däƛ’ ƛ’iri zowƛin.
translation: The eagle said: "Now my strength has come back to me; ride on me!"

text: Salamno bisno, kidbez babiya esirno, šebi retin nexä, šebi debi muradƛin.
translation: The girl's father answered the greeting and asked, "What have you come for, what is your wish?"

text: Xanno, barun q’ˤimes ɣˤutka bičin, mezarno kidberno ec’no ɣˤutku rodin, Beqez ʕUneyzatä radiruni ɣˤutka žan, xediwn bičin.
translation: The king and his wife lived in their own house, for the son-in-law and the daughter they built a new house and in the house that Sun's Uneyzat built lived she herself and her husband.

text: Bełiqanä yisir ƛoħay boɣno sis ħak’on, nełdergon met’rin teƛno.
translation: The hunter took one khinkali out of the pan for him and gave a piece of meat next to it.

text: C’iɣruk’ägon ža sabanin risno oxin ik’in elay.
translation: And Tsighruk took the blanket and ran away from there.

text: Nesiƛ’ cigon ʕUmarqiličƛin zown.
translation: His name was Umarqilich.

text: Ražbadinä, yisä royxosi šebinno bič’zi roqinč’ey, esirno yisiq, ya žax baruwa yowxanu mi, ya xedyox kidwa yegirxanu.
translation: Razhbadin, who did not understand what he [Tavadi] was doing, asked him: "You neither mary off your son, nor your daughter.

text: K’eneč’ k’eč’ ɣudi rocu!
translation: May the day be as bright as egg and bead!

text: Xizyo neła babiw is bexada egirno.
translation: Then she let her father slaughter the bull.

text: Xizyo cey maħorno boqno neła nełaz gugyoƛ’ ƛ’iri užin zowrno, boržizin boqno iħubin, ħonbin xecin bik’in raładyoƛ’ ƛ’irir bayn.
translation: Afterwards, the eagle went outside, seated the boy on its own back, flew away past rivers and mountains and came to the sea.

text: Sida qułgon bełiƛ’ ik’iƛ’oräy, cey žek’xo yisir xexbi besun.
translation: One day when he was on a hunt, he found children beating an eagle.

text: Elin dowde sadaq bižoƛin, bazarganbi harizi boqno.
translation: "Take us with you, too!", the merchants requested.

text: Risno yeženi esiwqor xanä rut’ugabi rišanix hisos ric’äsi urgu teƛno.
translation: The king gave the oldest sister a sack filled with wool for her to weave carpets.

text: Łinaƛ’nokin qoqoƛinč’uƛin, yisä ot’ bisno.
translation: "I laughed about nothing.", he rejected.

text: Sis zown bečedaw žek’u.
translation: There was a rich man.

text: Ik’in eženi žek’a nesir rätiru łinałäyn yisiq esinosi, yeda uži sosisi teƛa rätinč’irus riłen zown, yedu hibon teƛno di mekoyxno, ačqˤayxnoyä ičanƛin.
translation: When the eldest man went and told him what he wanted, the boy pretended to be not wanting to give it to him at first: "When I sold you the stick, will I remain hungry and thirsty?"

text: Ža łas ħalt’i yałrun čuqˤno k’oƛin yedu xizay.
translation: Just when he noticed who's work this was, he ran behind [him].

text: Xizay ezun yedan ƛexun.
translation: And he stood there looking behind it.

text: Sida ɣudin bik’in, q’ˤuna äƛiru ɣudƛ’o yizirabi łebelawzas sida šaharyar bayn.
translation: They went one day, and on the second day arrived at the city of the Tlyarata people.

text: Łi rowa ik’iƛin babiwqorno eƛin, Ismaʕilä bišadyoƛ’ ƛ’iri ʕArabuzanä teƛno zawruni bašiqˤoy łox šet’u rutirno.
translation: "Go and bring some water!", Ismail said to his father and turned the ring that Arabuzan had given to him over the food three times.

text: Nełał xizaygon zirun zeyn durimoƛ’ k’oƛin rik’in.
translation: The fox and the bear went running behind it.

text: ʕArabuzanä nesiqor eƛin, babiwz rok’ƛ’o yoł deber ħilla bodanix, gaƛ’izar ik’inč’ey ičanixgon resno anuƛin.
translation: Arabuzan said to him: "Your father has the idea of tricking you!", but it wasn't possible to not go to where he was invited.

text: Elo žedas žedaƛ xabarno boqno zeyä eƛin, ħuł di eƛin zawruni ʕaƛa žaqˤuł łi roƛixnoƛin.
translation: There, they spoke with each other and the bear said: "Yesterday there appeared water in the village I had told you about."

text: ƛ’ariyawt’ani bˤeƛ’ rayn Alimaħamade puħo ričin, gušiwt’ani ħonƛ’otow ričin.
translation: The fat sheep came and stood next to Alimagomed and the thin ones remained on the mountain.

text: Neła esiwz xedyos kid zown rätiru łinaƛ’or yutanix hunar yołäsi.
translation: That sister's husband had a daughter, who had the talent to turn into whatever she wanted.

text: Wazirzagon ozno boɣno nesił xizay bik’anƛin.
translation: The viziers led the army out and went to follow him.

text: ʕOmoybin rišʷanix ʕalaxyoƛ’or regirno, ža qaca roda ciqełxor oqno.
translation: He let the donkeys onto a clearing so they could eat, and went into the forest to collect the firewood.

text: Xizyo nesiz aħya tełer kičuričus biłe haraƛ’ bayn.
translation: Then, a noise like an earthquake came into his ear.

text: Žigoƛ’aygon dunyalƛ’otow ci rik’äsi doxtur goƛ’in nesider.
translation: But this time he called the most famous doctor in the world to himself.

text: Q’ˤida yuyoƛ’or žan kurno, ħoyaƛ’aygon nesiƛ’or rutirno ƛoħon kurno, xediw nesä nesiz mežä ik’in kecno.
translation: And the husband threw it down onto the floor and the pan off from the oven onto him, and went to sleep on his own mattress.

text: Nesiz eniya esirno:
translation: His mother asked:

text: Tungin bic’no nexƛ’osi, hemedurtow hunix dandir c’axru xalq’imo ħaƛun łiyra, žan lamusyoqay, dahawtow łi xecäsi zown.
translation: Right when he came from filling the jug, the people that he met on the way drank up, and from this conscience, leaving hardly and water.

text: Žeduq yeda eč’ra k’ʷeze oqnoanu.
translation: They could not stop him.

text: Bertenyoł zawru nasin xalq’i ħayran boqno ƛexun.
translation: All people on the marriage were surprised.

text: Xediya eƛin, babiya däł adäza zaħmataw masala ersi.
translation: The husband said: "My father has put a difficult task before me.

text: Diwanyoƛ’or goƛ’inosi, Kusa eƛin, di xanez kidbede zows, nełar nuʕłi rodanix šebin däq yołƛin, yisä nełas bašiqˤoyn, žežuqay räč’ru musrabin, gedmoqay yeč’no zawruni č’irno yukarno.
translation: After Kusa was invited to the court, he said: "I have been with the king's daughter, and I have things with me to prove that!"; he showed her ring, the hairs cut from her ponytail and the ribbon that he cut from her shirt.

text: Bełiƛ’ay xizor utin idur aynosi, hemedurtow yisir q’ʷaridno yoqno yičäsi esiw yesun.
translation: When he came back home from hunting, he found his sister once again being sad.

text: Yedu kid debex yegira yołƛin, eƛin xanä.
translation: "I will marry her off to you", said the king.

text: ʕUraw q’ʷarid oqno ža, šet’ursi dunyaltow qˤabir butin nesir nece yetixozo baruƛ’ay bat’a oqzaƛ’.
translation: He became very sad, the world around him turned black because he got seperated from the wife he loved so much.

text: Elo ža qˤirič’ onočaƛ’or rutin.
translation: There these scissors turned into a hen.

text: Sideni saħ ługon boqłin elułäy, sidä bisäsi biča bayxƛin”.
translation: It must be the case that someone of us has taken the other Sah."

text: Eženi žoyä ža q’ut’u bisno nesi bazarganqay.
translation: The eldest son took the jug from the merchant.

text: Best’al eniya nełaƛ’ rayrxosi ɣudin q’urƛ’or rizin, kidbä ħukmu boyn, eniwsni bašiqˤoyn nełqay riqirno, yoxanixƛin.
translation: The fact that the stepmother bothered her so much went on her nerves and she decided to take her mother's ring away from her in order to run away.

text: Ħon-bahadurä eƛin, debi eniwn yexursi, min bexuran, mi biłet’ani bišom bexurełxosi bahadur oqzaƛ’ nexsi di t’orƛin.
translation: Mountain~Hero said: "I killed your mother, and I will kill you! I have come here, for I am the hero who has killed a hundred just like you."

text: Eɣeni esiw siskin eynoynč’u.
translation: "Our younger brother didn't work at all.

text: Ža gurow łin žeda maqˤiƛ’ay rixra rayx zown.
translation: Except of that, they had to bring the water from far away.

text: Xizyogon Maħama akiłno q’ˤida bičinosi, Aħmadä eƛin, howt’o deber zowa gulu biqixyołiƛin.
translation: Again Magomed got tired and when they sat down, Akhmed said: "If we only could get a horse for you to ride on it..."

text: Howsi xaneł laɣes maħ yołƛin”.
translation: "That king smells like a slave."

text: Elo žedu sida ixiw keroqor bayn.
translation: There they came to a big ravine.

text: Yedu šebi ʕalamałi riča rayxƛin yeda bazaryar ik’inosi, elon siskin besun anu:
translation: "What wonder could this be?"; when he went to the market, he didn't find anything there:

text: Naysinan k’edinosi, aɣˤuz kobora uqˤłäsi Q’arum esun.
translation: After they had searched everywhere, they found Qarum hiding in the mill's granary.

text: Žedu łˤonon haq’ubi ʕaƛa teł taliħawn, bigut’aƛinno esix zown.
translation: They said that these three families in the village were very happy and good.

text: Zira ħal bayrinč’ey yeda xandäɣor oɣno.
translation: Nevertheless, the fox pesuaded him to go to the king.

text: Howži uži ozzał ogun.
translation: Now the boy was making big eyes.

text: Ražbadinzo pikruƛ’ esnabin c’aq’tow razi boqno.
translation: His brothers heavily agreed on Razhbadin's idea.

text: Ža mesedyos mač’an mi raqˤza yoł, mač’a meč’ beč’oƛ, tupi rok’eł č’aq’ˤiƛ, ašunä oƛi eč’oƛƛin eniya eƛisƛin.
translation: Mother said, that golden dagger is where you hide it, may the dagger cut your neck, may the rifle hit your heart and may the belt cut you into halves."

text: Debez ħumukulis kut’yoƛ’asi k’onč’u šiqˤono zowwäƛin?”
translation: "Was your camel's right foot lame?"

text: Howt’ay yäk’irun mi babiwqor där moƛał rukaysƛin eƛo, łˤora ɣudi žedu gulubi rišerłin, łi ħaƛurinč’eyn xecin, iħä łäƛ’or žedu rižinosi, ʕoloqanawni gulu łi ħaƛa boƛƛäɣor bäk’i, ečrunä lilyoxaytow ħaƛuƛin.
translation: "When you go, say to your father that I saw it in a dream: Although you feed those horses for three days, you don't give them water to drink, then you lead them over the water into a river; the young horse will go to the middle to drink water, the old one will drink from the riverbank instead."

text: Elozo xalq’imor łi teƛxanuƛin exurno ža.
translation: He was killed because he didn't gave water to the people there.

text: Kidbez eniya nesir ya micxir, ya mesed, ya q’ˤuya riguƛ’ay rigu šebinabi teƛxo zown, hudun užä žedu šebinabi risnoanu.
translation: The girl's mother was about to give him money or gold or other precious things, but the boy didn't take those things.

text: Babiya sida quł uži idurno goƛ’in eƛin, howła ʕaƛor ʕuraw k’udi bayrinč’inäy, mi di exura yołƛin.
translation: The father one day invited the son to his home and said: "If [you] don't bring many vines to that village, I will kill you."

text: Sida quł bara yeda išrinč’ey quqa xecin.
translation: One day the wife didn't prepare food for him and left him hungry.

text: Žagon ečruni ɣˤanaza iduɣor bižin yik’in.
translation: And and old woman carried it to her home.

text: Xedyosni yołi maʕdinaw tarbiya yołäsi kid zown.
translation: The husband, however, had a daughter of pretty upbringing.

text: (Ža ensi žek’uq iłäsi zownƛin bašan boyn neła nełas esiw ukarno).
translation: ("He resembled this man.", she pointed her finger onto her brother.)

text: K’ox äƛirugon uži kikxor łi ric’a ik’in.
translation: A second time the boy went to the spring to fill it with water.

text: Nazay eƛa iynč’ey ayn dibirä yiłaƛ’ mahar erno.
translation: Unknown from where there came an imam and set up the wedding for them.

text: T’umzałäy xabar täqruntow, akäłru iłen oqno Ħon-Bahadurä eƛin, di žedał xizaynč’e oqäsiƛin.
translation: When Mountain~Hero heard the story about the pigeons, he wasn't tired anymore and said: "I will search for them!"

text: Neł mixƛ’o kidbä eƛin, debeztow žukzar ža mi qal c’idaɣor kursiƛin.
translation: At that time the girl said: "You threw the skin into the oven to spite [me]!"

text: Q’orolas reč’alini reƛ’an rižin, yeda mažgityoz akixor ik’in.
translation: He took out the cut-off hand of the widow and went to the moque's window.

text: ƛ’ˤaz č’imix yedu yałzay, qurun-qaranƛin” qˤaƛix ƛ’iräy ƛ’iyor sis k’emot’ yayn.
translation: When she was on the roof's edge and was shouting: "Kurun-karan!", one crate came down from above.

text: Kikxor ayruntow aždaħä ža č’aguyawtow axaɣor igin kurno.
translation: And immediately when he came to to the spring, the dragon swallowed him alive.

text: Ik’in yisä reƛ’a atinosi, mesed nˤoƛaƛ’or butin.
translation: When he went and touched it with his hand, the gold turned into ashes.

text: Sosisi esinč’eyn bičin, xizaɣor esin, sis bercinaw ɣˤanabi ela gamiƛ’ozoni žek’ur teƛsiƛin.
translation: At first they didn't tell, but finally they told: "We sold a beautiful woman to a sailor."

text: Keč’oz reƛ’id neła neƛƛ’oräy, eɣeni esiya kut’yozod neƛƛin eƛin.
translation: When she stretched out her left hand, the younger brother said: "Give me your right hand."

text: Iħuƛ’ boqƛ’oräy, ƛen bˤoƛ’un dahaw šebin ƛexus Goqi łiyä ižanir.
