translation: When we wanted to cross the river, the bridge broke apart and Goqi was almost carried away by the water.

text: ʕEze roqace łin rixerno, xizor butin nexƛ’oräy, nukarza hunixtow ħaƛun łiyrno ža łi.
translation: They took as much water as would be enough, and when they were going back, the henchmen drank up the water on their way.

text: Neła ɣˤay xirerxo zown bazarganä ciqqo eqäruni žaq.
translation: It was giving milk to the boy who was put into the forest by the merchants.

text: Babiwrno, kidberno esuxanuzo mečoƛ’ mi di uqˤek’a ayƛin, raqru nasin šebin yisä ziruq esin.
translation: He told the fox what has happened and said, "You must hide me in a such place, where the father and his daughter will not find me."

text: Kecnosi zira riłiqosi hut’-mˤali ragi-rac’ƛ’oräy, zeyä esirno:
translation: After they went to sleep, the fox licked up butter from his face, and the bear asked:

text: Žedu q’ˤanon hunar xanqo bukarnosi, łˤono äƛirugon nasi debeq di hunar bukaranƛin uži xande igor ik’inosi, nesä eƛin, däz łiboqande c’oxranix debi mi łiboqan aƛin.
translation: Having shown these two talents to the king, the boy went to the king: "Which is the third one I shall show you?", and he said: "Bring your wrestler in order to fight with my wrester."

text: Žedu aždaħzo ɣutkoƛ’ ƛ’irir bayn.
translation: They came to the house of the dragon.

text: Nesis rok’ƛ’or rayn, nełaz eniya däqor neširur oč’ino q’ˤap’o boyxosiƛin eƛizaƛ’ di ʕadadawa bužzi oqinč’ey ičäsi zown.
translation: It came onto his mind: "I seemingly didn't believe in vain that her mother said she could prepare nine spindles until the evening.

text: Yisägon ħiƛ’oqadno rizirno yałza mečoƛ’ eqerno.
translation: And he lifted it with a handkerchief and put it back to the place where it was.

text: Sistow sis kid nesix yik’anix razi yoqno.
translation: One single girl [finally] agreed to come to him.

text: Tełerno ik’in babiwqno, kidbeqno esirno yisä, mežur di ukayyä žigoƛ’ayƛin.
translation: He went into the house and asked the father and the daughter, " Did you see me again?"

text: Łas yałru esinäy, biša yołä mežiƛin, esirno habihanä.
translation: "If I tell you whose you are, will you eat?", asked the miller.

text: C’aq’ req’ezi roqxo, dey kidƛin, babiyan žawab teƛno.
translation: "It fits very well, my daughter", answered the father.

text: Xizyogon, hemedurtow dice ixiw ʕaq’lu yisä där žigoƛ’äygon bodäƛin nesiz rok’ƛ’or rayn.
translation: Again, he thought: "What a similarly big advice did he give me once again?!"

text: Užä ʕon rok’no bikorin bexurno boɣno kurno ik’anƛin aqru yeda, xexoyza eč’erno žedas eniw yayace t’o ičiƛin.
translation: The boy struck his ax, killed the snake, took it and threw it away and thought of going, but the chicks stopped him until their mother would come.

text: Hudayziko ža ziya bexayn.
translation: The next day the cow was slaughtered.

text: Xizyogon q’ˤida bäčiza nesä eƛisƛin, esin babiya, howt’o deber zowanix bigu gulu biqixyołiƛin.
translation: "Then he said, where we were sitting:" said the father, "'If we only could get a horse for you to ride on it...'."

text: Nesir ža yukayrun roƛ’in boqno ža kid nesä barułun yižin.
translation: When he saw her, he immediately fell in love with her and took her as a wife.

text: Xedya yisno tełxor yayrno k’emot’.
translation: The husband took the wooden case and brought it inside.

text: Elay yizin yedu howlo igozatow yałruni ħonƛ’aɣor yik’in aħoziz q’aymoxor yayn.
translation: From there, she went further toward a mountain nearby and came to a shepherd's shelter.

text: Bigza ħalałno ħaƛun, q’ˤimär baynosi, utin c’oxno mežä kecno ičin.
translation: Having drunken heavily, and after it got into his head, he went to bed and slept.

text: Žan c’unzi yodin elogon xanä nukarbin xecin.
translation: To guard her, the king left his henchmen there.

text: Oƛƛozoni esiya, di bisäsi anu elay mesedƛin, inkar boyn, hemedurtow eɣenän, xizyogon eženi esiyan ot’ bisno.
translation: The middle brother rejected this, saying that he were not the one who took the gold, and so did the younger brother; after that also the eldest brother rejected it.

text: Bara xanqor hoboy eluƛ’or oz nexxowaƛin eƛinosi, nesä eƛin, nexłin žedur huni räynč’i.
translation: When the kings wife asked the king: "Is the army coming towards us now?", he said: "Although they will come, they won't know the way.

text: Hemeła qułtow žeda pikru bodin, yaqˤuł elas esiya xexbi roda rayxosi anaƛin.
translation: On that very day they thought: "Shouldn't our sister give birth to the children today?"

text: Žeda žedudäɣorgon eniwn yown paraxat bercinłiƛ’ bičix zown.
translation: They brought the mother to themselves and lead a beautiful and calm life.

text: ʕOmoyza nełxor eƛin, howži mi eli rexurnäy, Mirza min bexar.
translation: The donkeys said to him: "Now, if you kill us, Mirza will kill you too.

text: Ža kaɣat t’et’ernosi, Tawadi igtow ɣˤaɣˤu izix.
translation: When Tavadi read the letter, he became very happy.

text: Neł q’ut’oƛ’ Ħasanno razi oqno.
translation: On that agreement also Hassan agreed.

text: Oƛƛozo esiwq esirno xanä:
translation: The king asked the middle brother:

text: Žeda ža hemece etizaq ƛ’at’umoƛ’no egirno siskin nesir q’ʷariłi bukara begirinč’ey q’ˤida äčizar kikix zowno.
translation: Because they loved him so much, they let him be capricious, had him sit down and fed him, without showing him anything sad.

text: Dey biłe bäsunč’i mežzoxosin t’asƛin!”, - eƛin užä.
translation: The washbowls that you have are not like mine.

text: Eli beɣusƛin, tełxorkin boqinč’ey, badiru q’ut’imoxor, łˤonon q’urec’nin, žedaƛ’ ƛ’iri yałru q’ayn howlotow xecin, bazarganbin xizor bik’in.
translation: "We have lost!"; without even going inside they left the three mules and the wares on them there, to fulfill the agreement, and went back.

text: Sida zamanaƛ’aza ʕArabuzanƛin eƛixosi ɣˤanabi yayn, didiw žek’a ža dey simildi rac’xoƛin.
translation: After some time, a woman named Arabuzan came: "What man is eating my corn?!"

text: Łiƛa yoł eludeƛin, - yizä yizis q’ʷariłi esin, - amma nełaƛ’ ƛ’iri aždaħ baq’ˤułno bičäsi yoł.
translation: "We have some water", they told with grief, "but a dragon is covering it."

text: Xizaɣor užiq ža muk’urno yoqno, yisiz guluƛ’no zown bik’in.
translation: Finally she became persuaded by the boy and they both sat on his horse and they went away.

text: Sida quł neširu aħna-ik’anix maħor oqnosi, reɣeni xaličan, beɣenigon magalun bäqiruni xanzo nukarer q’orolaz idäzay beɣe xexzas guru teqno.
translation: One day, in the evening, one of the king's henchmen who went outside to walk for a while and got a small mat and a small bread heard the crying of young children from the home of the widow.

text: Nesis eniw čamaligo ƛˤebał adäz yexasi zown.
translation: His mother had died several years before.

text: Gulučini esnazałäy eženiq oc’ino ƛˤeb zown, nesiqayzoniq - oč’ino ƛˤeb, aqugani esiw žeduł teł hič’č’a yeɣe zown.
translation: Of the brothers, the old one was ten years old, the other one was nine; and the sister was the youngest among them.

text: Nazołno adäzagon kidbä sist’a nigaɣˤič’is t’it’u eqerno, mežuz k’icazał räk’iru reƛ roɣno enłaƛ’ eqroƛin.
translation: In front of everyone, the girl put a piece of cloth: "[Y'all] take the meat that is stuck between your teeth and put it on it."

text: Zownƛax zown anuƛax sis žek’u.
translation: Once upon a time there was a man.

text: Nesä yiłaqor eƛin, sasaq q’arƛ’o ʕoƛno put yołali kedonno yisno din ayan, biƛno put yołäsi yisno min horoƛin.
translation: He said to her: "Tomorrow in the morning, I will come with the seven-pood sledgehammer, and you come with the eight-pood sledgehammer!

text: Žin yołƛax žedu hemedurtow bercinłiƛ’.
translation: And now, they say, they also live happily.

text: Sida zamanałäy maqˤi riłe yizar c’i rukayn.
translation: Suddenly, they saw a fire far away.

text: Q’urec’niƛ’ ƛ’iri q’ˤano t’ik’in caƛin, yeda oqno qunyoxaɣor ik’in.
translation: On the mule he threw two waterskins onto the mule and went began to go to the farm.

text: Yisä yizi ʕoƛnon esnabi sist’a aħinyoqorno bicin, iħuz iƛ’aqaɣort’a begirno.
translation: He tied these seven brothers one at a time to the rock and sent them to the bottom of the river.

text: Xediw zown ʕaƛatow ci rik’äsi q’ebed.
translation: The husband was a famous smith in the village.

text: Elay bik’in idur bayrun, esnazar ža Ražbadin yałru iynosi, sidä sis ałnoxay begirinč’ey zown ʕuraw mixƛ’or.
translation: Immediately when they came home, they knew it was Razhbadin, they didn't let each other out of the arms again for a long time.

text: Ža c’aq’ bercinaw, ʕadanłi yołäsi, Allahä hari q’abul boyxosi ɣˤanabi zown.
translation: She was a very beautiful and respectful woman whose prayers Allah received.

text: Ayn ža sidaɣ ixiw gogaƛ’or.
translation: And he came to some big meadow.

text: Užä eniwqor eƛin, koyä bisanix kikiw, rac’anix t’aʕam yołäsi biša där ero, di esiw k’edanix ik’anƛin yoł.
translation: The boy said to his mother: "Prepare me some food that's light to take into the hand but tasty to eat; I will go to search my sister."

text: Nełaz k’onč’azax šudaƛ’osi šeƛ’u rutix zown.
translation: It came back with the clothes from the grave around its feet.

text: Roc’no isʷan, babiwz ʕaƛaɣorno yezun Pat’imatno yik’ix zown.
translation: Fatima drove the bulls and went looking toward her father's village.

text: Łox äƛiru ik’inosin, neła yisiqor xabarkin boyn anu.
translation: Also the third time when he went, he did not say anything to him.

text: Där howža qacis gulun neƛ, din hunix egirƛin”.
translation: Give me that wooden horse and let me go."

text: Xizyogon nesä äƛi, magalu ešadƛin, mi žan ešayn, yałi nesä mi ħoyä tełer karƛin.
translation: Then he will say: 'Bake bread!', and when you don't bake the bread, he will throw you into the oven."

text: Siryos bet’erhanesno, hokožek’usno k’eč’oliƛäy bitor q’ecen boqno:
translation: An argument over the foal arose between the mare's master's and the coachman:

text: Žek’uz raƛ’ma xanłun ičizaƛ’or, däztow raƛ’ma laɣłun rigu, mežus res besunäy, di däztow raƛ’mar ayroƛin”, - harizi rodin yisä.
translation: "Before getting the kingship over someone's country, it's better to be a slave in my own country; if you have the ability, bring me back to my country!", he begged.

text: Yizä izirinč’izaƛ’, bełiqan ƛ’iyor, gonda tełxortow, ik’in.
translation: Because they didn't lift him up, the hunter went downwards into the hole.

text: ƛ’ˤu bešnada boqnosi, neła q’orolayä kidbeqor eƛin:
translation: When the roof began to leak, the widow said to her daughter:

text: Walla, dey uži, debi babiwn mi iłetow xan zows.
translation: "By God, my son, your father was a king, just like you.

text: Yeda sidaɣ ciqo č’iħodyos ažombin šet’ur, roƛƛo azbarno yałza mečoƛ’or ayn.
translation: In some place he found a forest with fruit trees all around, and in the middle he came to a place with a palace in the middle.

text: Di dowdäɣor nexƛ’oräy, madazax zawruni ʕoloqza žeda žeduqor eƛix, Ražbadinłäy baħarčiƛinä eƛan, nesä nesix yižixosi baħaray šebi eƛa äynč’iru qazaqxo yegirxoƛin.
translation: When I came to you, the young people who were in their yards, were saying to each other: "Razhbadin is a brave young man, to send the bride he was to marry to an unknown Georgian."

text: Ozatawni eniya eƛin, ʕaƛaɣor ik’iłin, deber mežiz iday gurow kanłi bukaynč’i.
translation: The blind mother said: "Although you go into the village, you will only see the light of your home.

text: Ozurabin roɣno, aɣˤʷarno ik’in, mogolarno izin ičinäy, hemediw maʕišat neƛxoƛin.
translation: After you took my eyes, I went into a mill and when I got up into the granary, I was given such a fortune.

text: Elay bitor ik’iƛ’oräy akiłno, mekoyn bełiqus tatu łiyn.
translation: While he went away from there, he got tired and hungry and the hunter's power ended.

text: Ɣudƛ’ay ɣudƛ’or uži ħalaq’ oqxo ik’ix ukayn, sosigon babiya esirno nesiq, šebi deber roqno, šida mi ɣˤuč’ iłe oqxo ik’ixƛin.
translation: Seeing that from day to day the son went on to become thin, so the father asked him again: "What happened to you? Why are you becoming like a stick?"

text: Žedude igor aynosi, salamno teƛno, nesä žeduq esirno:
translation: After he came closer to them, he said hello and asked them:

text: Sosiƛa ƛirba, k’oxƛa ƛirba, łoxno ƛirba, hudaygon šebi retäsiƛin!
translation: "The first time a guest, the second time a guest, and the third time a guest, what do you want the next day?"

text: Aħugon łäxor qˤaydegon sadaq zirun bik’in elo bäsuru k’et’uq mi šebiƛin esirnosi, k’et’a di žek’u yołƛin eƛin.
translation: Again, the fox, together with the hare, went to the water, where the cat was, and asked: "Who are you?" - "I'm a man!" said the cat.

text: Sida quł bełiƛ’ äk’iru žaq didurnokin bełi č’aq’ˤin anu.
translation: One day the son who went hunting somehow didn't manage to shoot a deer.

text: Xanä ixiw bertenno boyn, indowrizaz aħozix kidno yegirno.
translation: The king organized a big wedding and married his daughter off to the turkey herder.

text: Ayn yisä yiziq esirno, mežur di ukayyäƛin?
translation: He came and asked them, "Did you see me?"

text: Q’arum ħayran oqnon ƛexun ʕAt’idzo bečełiƛ’, esirno nesiq, didur mi nece bečed oqäƛin.
translation: Qarum was astonished about Atid's riches and asked him: "How did you become such a wealthy man?"

text: Žedus iłno uži zown.
translation: They had six sons.

text: Hawaƛ’ łox šet’un butin, cey bayn užide puħo heqno.
translation: The eagle turned circles in the air three times and then came to sit down next to the boy.

text: Xanä nesix ixiw bertenno boyn kidno yegirno, boƛik’u nesis ric’q’in, ɣˤutkun, posun teƛno, howlotow xande puräza iguni žek’un ičin.
translation: The king organized a big marriage and married off his daughter, gave half his wealth, his house and cattle to him and the good man was to the king's side.

text: Hoboy mi, eni, eloš nesi bečedawni maduhales kidno däx esirno yik’iƛin, eƛin užä.
translation: "Now you, mom, go over there and ask the wealthy neighbour's daughter out for me!", said the boy.

text: Xizyo nesä kinaw raqru šebinon esin, xaner nesäq nesä łi ħaƛuxosini t’akan yetinƛin esirno.
translation: Afterwards, he told everything that happened and the king asked for the water-drinking-glass that he wanted.

text: Ukru däqay kʷaxatow łäy, aq’ˤlu yołi netäy netintow däde sadaq ƛäxuƛin, Ħusenä žawab teƛno.
translation: "Silver will soon end from me; an advice, however, will always remain with me.", answered Hussein.

text: Xizyo žeda eƛin, nesä elo č’ʷadma čanya bicäsi zown besunäy, ela isʷa ricanƛin.
translation: Then they said that if he tied the she.goat to the family's house, we will tie a bull to it.

text: Nełƛ’ay soder žedu kidbabi ħalaxor regirnokin ciqaɣor rik’ix zownanu.
translation: After that, the girl couldn't even with force go into the forest anymore.

text: Bertenyoł best’alawni esiw neła nełaz esiwde q’ˤida yičin.
translation: On the marriage, the stepsister sat down next to her own sister.

text: Hudayzikogon ɣurox uži egirnosi, hemedurtow boƛixno aɣi užiqor xabaryayn:
translation: The next day the boy was sent to pasture the cows, when the bird appeared again and told him:

text: Elozo ʕaƛoz žamaʕatyo łiyä rayäru qaciƛ’ay bet’erbaqi boyx zown.
translation: The population of this village there made a living from the wood brought along the river.

text: (Ža yołƛax ensi baħarčiq iłäsiƛin, neła nełas xediw ukarno).
translation: ("He resembled this young man", she pointed to her husband.)

text: Sisgon ʕaq’lu mołoƛin ƛ’iri uži utinosi, hunix ik’izaƛ’ deberno ħažat yaq ža xizor ƛäxuruni sisini ɣˤurušƛin, endurtow esin eženi žek’a ʕaq’lu, boƛ’araw royxozo łinałno cim xizor biq’ˤir, sabru ader adek’ƛin.
translation: When the boy insisted: "Give me another advice!", the old man said the advice that way: "When you go on your way, the remaining ruble will become necessary for you. Anything evil that might be done should be moved aside, and patience should be pushed forward."

text: Rečuƛ gulun bicin, žaqor ezun ičin, ayxana yeda žigonƛin.
translation: He tied the horse to the cattleshed and waited for his son: "Why isn't he coming again now?"

text: Nasin butin žan žek’no, nesiqay łin roɣno, žan rižin nukarbi bik’in, elur resus yedu łiƛin eƛin.
translation: The henchmen all turned back, beat him up and carried away the water from him, and said: "We found this water!"

text: Žäd mi eɣetow yołƛin, eniya eƛin.
translation: "But you're still young," his mother said.

text: Xizyo Bilq’isdä bazaryarno yik’in, ʕoloqzas šeƛ’un risno, xizor yutin iduɣorno nexno, žan šeƛ’un, maħor yoqno.
translation: Afterwards, Bilqisdi went to the market, bought men's clothings, came back home, put it on and went outside.

text: Nedur resunäyƛin, eƛin boc’ä, meža mežaƛ’osi cirabi esoƛin.
translation: "If that's the case," said the wolf, "tell me your names!"

text: Yoƛo ƛˤeb yik’inosi, Tawadi ixiw suħmat roda iħux.
translation: After a year had passed, Tavadi began to organize a big celbration.

text: Ža c’aq’ c’odoraw ħaywan zown.
translation: It was a very clever animal.

text: Hemedur yiła kinaw raqru šebin esin.
translation: So she told everything that had happened.

text: Xizyo ža c’ohor k’edin esuranix di ƛ’iri risxo, daruyar ħumukulis reƛ där retinƛin iduzan yaħnaynƛin.
translation: Afterwards I will take actions to search and find the thief, walking around the homes [saying] that I want camel meat for a medicine."

text: Nełasno sis kid zown.
translation: She also had one daughter.

text: Yeženi esiya eƛin, hisos ric’no urgu däqor neƛnäy, nazon debez nukarzaz guluzaƛ’ ƛ’iri kuranix xaličabi rišan diƛin.
translation: The oldest sister said: "If you fill a sack with wool and give it to me, I will weave mats to put on all the horses of your henchmen."

text: Žuka žek’ur rigu ɣudi rukaynoƛ!
translation: Let a bad man not see a good day!

text: Q’orola nełqor eƛin, łäxor mi yik’iƛ’oräy yok’ek’anix ʕoloqbi urɣizi boqxo, hudu mi esiwqor eƛo, zam-zames łi et’näy, madaxtow nełas kik boƛixxosi, nełał xizay ik’iƛƛin.
translation: The widow said to her: "Some young men are thinking about kidnapping you when you go to get water, so tell your brother that if he pours out holy water, then outside a spring of it will appear, behind which he should go."

text: Oƛixno sida užä tupin caƛin dey bašan bašiqˤoyn sadaq bet’ursi, xizyo däqgon qeman riħunč’u.
translation: And a boy appeared and with his rifle shot my finger and my ring off and afterwards the rain began.

text: Boƛƛo baq’ˤu zaman bik’izaƛ’ortow žedur q’ˤanon užibi bodin.
translation: After some time has passed, two sons were born to them.

text: Nesiqor didiwnokin xabar boynč’ey, c’odorawni esiw dibirzo rečuƛerno ik’in, sis čanya bexurno elo rekuyał šušin, elayn yoɣno q’ˤuya mečoƛ’ dibires barun šušin yisä.
translation: Not telling him anything, the clever brother went into the mullah's shed, killed a goat and burried it in the manure there and took out the mullah's wife from there and burried her in another place.

text: Xizyo nełas esiw idur yayn.
translation: Afterwards, he sister came home.

text: Hemełƛ’o xizyo aždaħ č’ariłno.
translation: After that the dragon woke up.

text: Esiw nesä howlotow ciqqo xecin.
translation: His sister he left there in the forest.

text: Nełde sadaqno zown ɣˤʷay.
translation: A dog was together with her as well.

text: Elo ɣˤutkaɣorno boqno, žeda žedur maʕišat boyx bičin q’ˤanon esnabi.
translation: Then moved into the house there, and lived there engaging in their household.

text: Yiła eƛin, eloš neła łˤora t’alamezni ɣˤutka deber retinäy, łˤora ƛˤeba mi elo doxturłin roynäy, deber di kumak boyxosi ɣˤanabiłun yičanƛin.
translation: She said, "If you want to live in this three-floored house and work as a doctor for three years, I will help you."

text: Ħolimoƛ’äy muhubi rut’ixozo aɣä hoq’ˤoniqor eƛin, q’ˤidasini howlotow räči ža, ħolimoƛ’osi muhubi rut’oƛin.
translation: The bird who pecked the granules from the mat said to the other one: "They will remain down there anyway, collect the granules on the mat!"

text: Šibaw ɣudƛ’o šudaƛ’ay ciyo raganix sis ixiw miƛ’it’a bayx zown.
translation: Every day a big ram was coming to lick the salt from the grave.

text: K’uruč’ k’eč’ ɣudi rocu, buq bay.
translation: All's well that ends well.

text: Bˤeƛoqgon ixiw qˤaƛun regin, ciqaɣor boxin.
translation: The pig emitted loud shouts and ran away into the forest.

text: Babiya bayru wasimoxor łˤoran esnaza irsi bułirno nazoƛ’no sist’a saħ bayn.
translation: According to the father's testament, the three brothers should share the heir and they should receive one Sah each.

text: Sis žek’un ayn howlor hemecetow ƛ’iri utizaƛ’, moƛ’oqˤoyn sadaq däq teƛno rok’ełsi.
translation: And there came a man and because he insisted so much, I sold also the bridle and lost it.

text: Ħurženyaɣor reƛ’a regirnosi, xanzo užir hemesi žek’uzoq riłäsi eƛuk’ resun.
translation: The prince reached his hand into the bag and found a leather sack that resembled the man's.

text: Sida ƛäxuru muhumołäy ʕUmar kurzak’aƛ’orno utin, onočaƛ’or atiruni Ox̌oyoƛ’ c’oxno.
translation: From a single remaining grain, Umar turned into a falcon again and pounced upon Ohoyo who had turned into a hen.

text: Howža uži ent’o däder ayr; nesiqkinda dey kid yuyxor yutanƛin”.
translation: "Bring that boy to me; he might be able to turn my daughter back."

text: Nedur resunäy, eso huduƛin”, - eƛin nesi žek’a.
translation: "If that's the case, then say so!", said the man.

text: Aɣä esin, ɣunoxorno bayn, zira maħin rok’no rutirno žan caƛin, nasin xexoyabi roɣa yołƛin, däqay q’ˤanon xexoy boɣsi, sistow sis därno xecinƛin.
translation: The bird said: "A fox has come to the tree and wanted to throw it down with a beat of his tail to take away all my nestlings, two nestlings he took, so just a single one he left for me."

text: K’uruč’ k’eč’, ɣudi rocu, buq bay.
translation: All's well that ends well.

text: Užä eƛin, di c’eruc’as hil kuran, meži nełał xizayn k’oƛin, adäza hilno yayrno xizor butin bayrur di ziru neƛanƛin.
translation: The boy said, "I will shoot an arrow from my bow and you will run behind it; to the one who first brings it back to me, I will give the fox."

text: Elo xizaɣorsi yeda uyn išno, bišʷan łiyn.
translation: There he finally ate and finished up his food.

text: Howži yisi žoyä, t’ok’ow urɣel čučzi boda nesis igorsi žek’un oqinč’izaƛ’, yacʕalqo kinaw raqru šebinno esin, eƛin, yaqˤuł dey xizaɣorsi ɣudi yoł ƛexasi.
translation: Now that boy, because there was no close person to him to relieve his sorrow a bit more, he told his cousin everything that has happened and said: "Today is my last day.

text: Ungotow mi č’agu yołäƛin, esirno ʕArabuzanä yisiq, igor aynosi.
translation: "Are you really alive?", asked him Arabuzan, when he came close.

text: Yaqˤuł yiła däz bara oč’ira ɣˤanazacewa ħalt’i bodin.
translation: Today my wife worked like nine women.

text: Ruyxor esinäy, elur xexbi rodixanu.
translation: "To tell you the truth, we don't get any children.

text: Xanes ɣˤutkoł adäza reže baɣ zown, elo anunč’uru č’iħotow zownanu.
translation: There was a big garden before the khan's house, where the fruits grew.

text: Žigoƛ’ayn Kusa urɣizi oqno, šebitowey yiłas maʕna boqxoƛin, hudun yisiz q’ˤimłer šebintow rik’in anu.
translation: And also now Kusa was thinking: "What sense does this make?", but nothing came into his head.

text: Elo Paxrudinƛin sida žek’a yisir ƛirbałi royn.
translation: There a man named Pakhrudin welcomed him as a guest.

text: Šebi deber roqäƛin, yisä esirnosi, nesä esin, ʕažaibaw maxšel, hunar yołäsi uži zows dey.
translation: "What happened to you?", he [the son] asked, and he [the father] said: "I had a talented son with amazing skills.

text: Ža gulu debeq biqinäy, nełaƛ’no zown mi q’ˤuya qoq yałza mečoƛ’or ik’a ay.
translation: When you get that horse, you must ride to another cliff.

text: Mi där eloša maħiq micxir t’aħiruni ɣˤʷay neƛƛin, esirno užä.
translation: "Give me the dog with the money tied to its tail, there", the boy asked.

text: Nedur resunäyƛin, eƛin bara, t’ay deber di uyno ɣˤurušno neƛan, nełaƛ ʕaq’lu bisanix mi pulanaw žek’uder sida ʕaƛar ik’iƛin.
translation: "If that's the case," said his wife, "I will give you four rubles from here; go to a village to a certain man in order to buy an advice with it."

text: Elexa, hudulaw žek’u ža Ox̌oyo di yoł.
translation: "Lo and behold, dear man, I am this Ohoyo.

text: (Nesir riyx zownanu ža nesis kid yałrułi).
translation: (He didn't know it was his daughter).

text: Maħazay yutin yedu yayzaƛ’orgon, käcrus riłen rodin mežä utin c’oxno užin ičin, šebičey yiła radiƛin.
translation: Before she came back from outside, the boy layed down on the mattress and pretended to sleep [thinking]: "What the heck will she do?"

text: Howžigonä ža däx nexxanuƛin, t’asno bižin ik’in yisi užä ža xanez kidbeq bukarno.
translation: "Well, shouldn't she come to me now?", the boy carried the washbowl to the princess and showed it to her.

text: Łˤono äƛirunigon hunixoza täƛru łˤeb yac’nosi, t’it’ubin roqno bełiqanez rečnoł bikorin boƛixno maħor.
translation: When he ate the leaf that the third traveller gave him, the snake turned to pieces and came out through the hunter's vomiting.

text: Elo ʕaƛoł roƛƛo ixiw naħuz ɣuna teł łi yołƛax.
translation: There's supposed to be water inside a big oak in the middle of the village.

text: Uži raładyoz ɣˤanaza äƛiruxor ik’in.
translation: The boy went to what the woman from the sea told him.

text: Ɣˤutkaɣor bäk’izaz puƛ’aza q’ˤano rut’ugan rixoƛ ƛ’irin caxno, sidaƛ’ sisxoli xanes kid yayoƛƛin”, sideniƛ’gon nasin xalq’i bayoƛƛin”.
translation: And over the entrance to the house, let there hang two carpets on which shall be written on the one side: "May the princess come alone!" and on the other one: "May all people come!"!

text: Sasaqosi ʕAliqilič nełaz ruɣuł xizay ik’in.
translation: The next morning, Aliqilich followed its prints.

text: Soder ezunosi, ƛ’iräy ƛ’iyor łäs čok boɣäsiƛax, wˤałer ezuzaƛ’ q’ˤidar axukin yayxanuƛax.
translation: When he looked upwards, a stream of water flowed down, but when he looked down, not even a drop of water came down.

text: Howži hudu žedu xexbi ax roqacet’a bišno.
translation: From now on these children ate their bellies full.

text: Nesi žek’a rayäru šeƛ’un šeƛ’un, howži nartow di ik’anƛin yeda zawru, ƛ’irir boƛƛozo rihmos ɣˤanabi yayn.
translation: He put on the clothing brought by that man and thought now, "Where shall I go?"  as a woman of middle age came.

text: Idur aynosi, xanä ec’noni baruqor eƛin:
translation: After coming home, the king said to his new wife:

text: Xizyogon eženi žek’a eƛin, howži deber sis iman yołäsi baru yiča yay, nełał xizay oqa deber zaman bayn.
translation: Then the old man said: "Now you need to have a faithful wife, the time has come to go after her!

text: Elo mušin begirno, howt’aɣor iħun äsuƛin, ašunä ročyoxno icin, t’omceni žek’uz tupi caƛiruni bełiqan tełxor egiranix žeda q’ut’i bodin.
translation: They thought [let the air/breath/smell?] "He appears to have went in there.", tied [...] to a rope in their belt, and they agreed to let the hunter that shot the span-sized man with the rifle inside the hole.

text: Sidä sidqo rak’ru kedonyozaz haraƛ’yo, akiłno (kodin bitin) q’ˤanontow q’ˤidar bˤoƛ’un.
translation: The noise of the sledgehammers, when they hit one another, made the two of them loose conscience and fall down.

text: Ik’in ʕaƛar yeda uži aynosi, nesä nesis ɣˤutku k’edix yałzay, qiƛ’orok’uƛ’ay dandir barun keze yoqno, sadaq iduɣor bik’in.
translation: When the boy came into the village, he met his wife by accident while he was searching for his own house, and they went home together.

text: Neł maydanzo č’imä nesir łˤono t’ala yołäsi ɣˤutku rukayn.
translation: In a corner of that clearance, he saw a house with three floors.

text: Žar gulun teƛno, žedur riguƛay rigu bišʷan erno, xanä reže rok’uɣʷeyn rodin, žedus hunin regirno.
translation: So the king gave the horse to the boy, gave them really good food, organized a large meal and let them go their way.

text: Nesä mi kʷaxatow posurƛ’orno utirno ac’a yoł.
translation: He will soon turn you into a cattle and eat you.

text: Nełas rok’u qepƛix ričäsi zown netiey ɣudi qˤašanƛin.
translation: Her heart was beating and waited for the dawn to come.

text: Zamanałäy kidber eniwdäɣorno, esnazadäɣorno yik’anix rok’u k’oƛin.
translation: Afterwards, the girl wanted to go to her mother and her sisters.

text: Nesis q’ˤano uži zown.
translation: He had two sons.

text: Bikoride igaɣor ayƛ’oräy, neła ceyes xexoy biqirƛ’orey, roɣno mač’an bikorzo rok’no.
translation: When he came closer to the snake as it was taking away the eagle's chick, he took the saber and hit the snake.

text: Zek’ egin xan č’ariłno.
translation: The king shuddered and woke up.

text: Nełƛ’osi baq’ˤu zaman bik’izaƛ’ortow xan exun.
translation: Not much time passed since then, until the king died.

text: Boƛƛo zamanno bik’in, babiw baru yowa oqnosi, nesiqor bašiqˤoyn teƛno, kidbä eƛin, eniws wasi zows dowqor, howda bašiqˤoy c’oxxosi taraw mi baru yawnoƛƛin.
translation: After a while, when the father intented to find a[nother] wife, the daughter gave him the ring and said: "Mother's last wish was that you may only marry a woman who matches this ring."

text: Neła kidbeqor eƛin, ele žigoƛ’aygon deber däƛ’aza aždaħes xexoyn biqis, howži mi esiwqor eƛo, deber halmaɣłun yičaniƛäyn, nesirgon barułun yičaniƛäyn Beqes ʕUneyzat yowanix ik’iƛƛin.
translation: She said to the girl: "Again now because of me you got hold of a dragon's young, now say to your brother that he go and mary the Sun's Uneyzat, so she may become a friend to you and a wife to him."

text: Sosisi xan royxosi anuƛin ičäsi zown, yigtow q’orolay ƛ’iri yutizaƛ’, xanä esin.
translation: At first the king was like: "She doesn't.", but because the widow insisted so much, the king told her.

text: Xizyogon nasin xalq’i butirno nesi xanä, howža uži ƛexuzaƛ’or, kidber daru bodanixƛin.
translation: Then, the king gathered all the people, except for that boy, to cure his daughter.

text: Xedya žawab teƛno, däq q’ˤoq’ˤorut’a magalabi zows, nesir rigu bišʷa retixosi yołƛin.
translation: The husband answered: "I had hollow breads with me; and he likes good food."

text: Zira koyä tupi yołäsi sis zaʕipaw uži iqirno.
translation: The fox found a sickly boy with a rifle in his hand.

text: Ciq łäyzaza puƛ’aza yisił dandir c’oxno oč’ino taraw mešok’las mus ħuħqo anuru sis kusa.
translation: At the end of the forest he met a man who had merely 9 hairs on his cheek where the moustache would be.

text: T’ok’ow elo tełäy boqełxanuzoxor ɣamasya žan bišin C’iɣruk’ä eƛin, hoboy debi ɣudbi łiysƛin.
translation: Tsighruk locked him in the box so that he couldn't get out there anymore, and said: "Now your days are numbered!"

text: Tungi bexunosi, ʕiyayn yičin yedu.
translation: When the jug broke, she was crying.

text: Sidade kikxor baynosi, elo ence q’ˤidan bičin bišƛ’oräy, eɣeni esiws ħamay č’ikay yˤoƛ’un yoƛixno.
translation: They came to a spring; and when they barely sat down and ate, the mirror fell from the younger brother's arms.

text: Nełas q’ˤuya etali zown.
translation: She had another lover.

text: Kidbä goƛ’in ža ƛirbałix ik’inč’izaƛ’, yoƛixno nełaz eniya goƛ’in ižin ža iduɣor.
translation: Because he didn't go in as a guest when the girl invited hem, her mother appeared and invited him and lead him into the house.

text: Elur hunix riƛu riƛix žek’u esus.
translation: "On the way we found a man ploughing a field.

text: Elo qoqä teł miƛ’iryoz ɣunoƛ’ ƛ’iri raq’ˤu aɣbi ričixosi, t’awus hič’č’a bittaza deber bäsu.
translation: There inside the cliff there are many birds above on the peach tree, but you will find the peacock over other side.

text: Yizin maħorno yoqno yezunosi, ɣuros reqenno bayrno rayäsi, eƛnoq yiłas posu rac’runi boc’an, zeybin resun.
translation: After she got up, went outside and had a look, the wolves and bears that had eaten her cattle in winter, turned up leading a cow herd.

text: Qacis gulu łaci biłe hawaƛ’ boržizi boqno bik’inosi, ʕoƛno ħonno xecin sidade gogaƛ’or bayn.
translation: After the wooden horse flew through the air like the wind, it left seven mountains behind and came to a meadow.

text: Išno-utin, partalno rutirno, nesä nesiz hunix ik’iƛ’oräy, aziroyn, k’omo biłet’a boqno bexasi halmaɣbi besun.
translation: He ate, gathered his belongings, and while he went on his way, he found his friends, frozen to death and each one rolled together like a ball.

text: Žedu elozay bok’äłrungon, xanez kidbä k’oƛin yik’in tusnaq’ay maħor uži egirno.
translation: They escaped from there and the king's daughter ran and let the boy out of the prison.

text: Elogon teł sis gondun yič’in, teł yedan uqˤno, ƛ’irigon girimabin kurno, žan ƛ’iqun nełaƛ’ ƛ’irigon zeyä lidu yaq’ˤun.
translation: The bear dug a big hole there, hid the hunter's son inside, threw the poles above it and covered it with his paw as well.

text: Č’ago, saɣłiƛ’ kidbabi rukaynosi, babiw ɣˤaɣˤu izin.
translation: When the father saw the girls alive and healthy, he got very happy.

text: Hi enda ħiƛ’oqu debez mi ozurazaq riħoƛin, žoyä babiwqor teƛno.
translation: "Take this handkerchief and rub it on your eyes.", the boy gave it to his father.

text: Žaz baruƛ’no ezun babiws ʕuraw rok’u ƛexun.
translation: The father looked at the boy's wife and became very envious.

text: Nesiq neła meɣyoqor didur eƛa rayxosi yałrułi mołin.
translation: Him she taught how one has to speak to the udder.

text: Yisän eƛin, meža eluq buyxor xabar esinäy, biša yołƛin.
translation: He said: "If you tell us the truth, we will eat."

text: Assalama ʕalaykumƛin eƛin, salamno teƛno, uži ɣˤutka tełxor oqno.
translation: "As-salam alaykum!" said the boy, greeted him and got into the house.

text: Ay, di ik’ix, nesiƛ’ adäz di exuƛƛin, aceł c’eruc’an caƛin, bˤaƛoyn gulun, et’un k’oƛin nełaƛ’no zown Ħusen hunix oqno.
translation: "No, I am going, may I die before him!", Hussein [said and] shot at the door with his bow, harnessed the horse, and quickly rode on his way on it.

text: Kidbezno, baruzno, nesizno bok’nosigon, žedułäy ħalaq’awt’a ʕomoybi roqno.
translation: And after cracking also [for?] his daughter, his wife and himself, they became thin donkeys.

text: Xizyogon hemesitow xanä begirno q’ˤano t’umi bayrno, howzałäy nasi guluči, nasi aqiw yałru biyrƛin.
translation: Then, that king had two pigeons be brought[ to him, saying]: "Find out, which is male and which is female!"

text: Yiłar yołi qˤišoder riguƛ’ay rigu biša ersiƛax.
translation: For her, however, she prepared the best food for dinner.

text: Ačqˤaynosi ceyä q’ˤʷa-q’ˤʷaƛin eƛinosi, užä ozäy mowt’a yoƛ’ˤurxo zown ceyz haqar.
translation: When the thirsty eagle said "krwa-krwa", the boy let tears fall down from his eyes into the eagle's mouth.

text: Xizyo deber rätiru šebin t’ubazi radi yiła barkatawni ħiƛ’oqaƛinno eƛin, bikorä uži hunix egirno.
translation: "Afterwards this blessed handkerchief will fulfill anything you want.", the snake said and let the boy go.

text: Bełiqubin tamaša boqno ƛexun, nece bercinaw ža kid yesuzaƛ’.
translation: The hunters were astonished that such a beautiful girl existed.

text: Nełƛ’osi kʷaxatow ʕoƛiran esnaza bertenno bodin, barubin rown.
translation: Shortly afterwards, the seven brothers organized a marriage and married.

text: Sida saʕatyacegon mečołno beynoyn zira im-m-m-m, im-m-m-mƛin eƛin.
translation: And after working on the field for one hour, the fox said "Mhmhmh, mhmhmh!"

text: Yiła esirno, šebi deber retinƛin.
translation: She asked: "What do you want?"

text: Nesä nesiq Ħusen äk’iru huni rukarno.
translation: He showed him the way that Hussein went.

text: Ħak’omƛerce šebin riqiraniƛäy, eynodanix močin k’edin oqäsi yołƛin, esin Alimaħama.
translation: "I have come to search a place to work in order to earn some food."

text: K’et’uƛ’orno bezun, tamašan boqno zira esirno:
translation: The fox looked at the cat, was surprised and asked:

text: Deber di łi rowa yik’ixƛin esiwqorno eƛin yedu maħor yoqno.
translation: "I go to bring you some water." she said to her brother and went outside.

text: Zira eƛin: c’idoq gugu yukarčey, hemełƛ’o xizyo łu rägiru räyƛin.”
translation: The fox said: "Expose your back to the fire, afterwards, we will know, who licked it away."

text: Nece xira, łˤora ɣˤurušyoƛ, yedu q’ut’u teƛxo boqzaƛ’, yiłas hunarno eso mi däqƛin?”.
translation: "So expensive, you're selling this jug for three rubles, tell me about its ability!"

text: Xexłiƛ’ yiłzaqay mi ok’łinč’inäy, nesä debeq rigu rukarinč’i.
translation: If you won't get away quickly from here, he won't let you see anything good anymore."

text: Goƛ’in ša boyxozo žek’uq esirnosi, nesä eƛin:
translation: When he called the man who made the wine and asked him, he said:

text: ƛirbas nediw bažarin bukayn, dibirä eƛin, yiła ʕaƛa xanłi roda ray debeqƛin.
translation: Having seen this kind of skill of his guest, the imam said: "You must become king in this village!"

text: Nesider aynosi yisi žek’a esirno nesiq, ha xan, šida kecno ičäsi mi, šebi roqno deberƛin.
translation: When the man arrived at his place, he asked him: "Hey king, why do you sleep, what happened to you?"

text: Esin šebi xecin šebi zownƛax zownanuƛax ya hawaƛ’ anusi, ya raƛ’moƛ’ anusi sidaɣ ħonƛ’o zirun ɣʷˤadin.
translation: Once upon a time there was neither in the air nor on the land, [but] on some mountain a fox and a crow.

text: Babiya äƛiruq’aw, yexoriłxosi, k’ot’oniłxosi, aluk’a qˤaba yoqxosi, barun yiqinanu.
translation: He didn't get a wife that was black and white and longer and shorter, as the father had said.

text: Zamanałäy yisiq ozzałer kanłi bayrxosi ƛˤeb yiqin.
translation: After a while, he picked up a leaf that brought back his eyesight to his eyes again.

text: Elor aynosi, mogolarno izin, kecno ičin.
translation: Having arrived there, he got into the granary and went to sleep.

text: Sasaqosi k’uläɣor ganbi riħada rižinosi, ganƛ’or yutin zawru kid ɣalbac’ƛ’orno yutin, xanez ganzo axarno k’oƛin, mo yac’xo zown.
translation: The next morning, he lead the young bull into the side street to fight, the girl who had turned into a bull turned into a lion then and ran into the belly of the king's bull and was eating its fat.

text: Hoboy yedu däƛ’or nexxoƛin, zeygon ɣunoqayn bˤoƛ’un, q’ˤidayn bizin zebu yoɣno boxin.
translation: "Now he's coming to me!", the bear fell off the tree, got down and ran away quickly.

text: Hudunč’e nucoł his roqux ƛexun mi.
translation: you nevertheless were sticking the wool into the honey.

text: Šebitow hoboy radiƛin, ža uži mekoyx, oč’iłxo ičäsi zown.
translation: "What shall I do now?", the boy said and remained hungry and freezing.

text: Hunixor qˤišon erno, saparyox boqno.
translation: They took some leftovers for the way and went on a journey.

text: Bežet’ani esnazas c’eruc’abi č’aq’ˤin žedu biłetow bečedawt’anizas ɣutkozas ƛ’ˤazaƛ’.
translation: The elder brothers' bows hit the roofs of houses of wealthy ones just like them.

text: Zira hudun zey muk’urno bodin, q’ˤanon žedu hunix roqno.
translation: Nevertheless, the fox convinced the bear so the two of them got on their way.

text: Zamanaƛ’aza cek’ä weeni rizirno.
translation: After a while, the goat kid emitted a bleat.

text: Šida ɣuro kamiräƛin, babiya dahaw ʕedeʕizi oynosi, moƛa iqirno ƛexun diƛin, babiwq heresi mec yesa bayn.
translation: "Why have you lost the cows?", the father scolded a little, so he had to tell his father the lie the he had fallen asleep.

text: Nełas uži ela exuräsi esunäy, bazarganzo čantasi t’aqˤ mežur ruqäsi räsu, neła exuräsi esunäy raɣˤäsi räsuƛin.
translation: If we have killed her son, you will find the knife in the merchant's pocket closed, if she has killed him, it will be open.

text: Šet’ur didiwnkin xalq’i bukaynč’izaƛ’, bełiqanä rekarbin risno kawubi sera iħun.
translation: Because he didn't see any people around, the hunter took the keys and began to unlock the gates.

text: Sasaqay neširur bik’in yizi sida oreznixor bayn.
translation: They went from the morning to the evening and came to a pond.

text: Ži roda šebin anuƛin”.
translation: But now there is nothing to do."

text: Hobolaytow idurno bik’in žedu esnabi, žeda žedus posun rexayn, nełas reƛno rac’no, xizor ƛäxuru ƛuzazas ixinabin ric’no bazaryaɣor bik’in.
translation: From there the brothers went home, slaughtered their cattle, ate its mean and filled bags with the remaining bones and went to the market.

text: Yoƛƛozo kidbä eƛin, di bečedaw žek’us uži ok’ek’äsi zows, nesił xizayn ayn, däqay xot’oqosin roɣno axiru užix därno yik’a rätiƛin.
translation: The middle girl said: "I'll also want to marry the boy who, when I stole the rich man's son, came after him, took my footwear from me and ran away."

text: Xizyo hemedurtow kidbes rožin rodin eƛix teqno, nesir mi etix zows yołi, nesä mi ƛ’irizoni ɣˤutkaɣor ezanix egärƛin.
translation: Afterwards he likewise heard a girl's voice say: "If he loved you, he will let you look into the upper room."

text: Neła quł sasaqosi, qˤašacekin ičinč’ey, č’ʷadma bäciru ƛeli bowa ik’inosi, eɣeni esiwr ƛuzabi gurow resun anu.
translation: The next day, even before it got morning, he went to get the lamb which was tied to the ruin, and the young brother found nothing but bones.

text: Nedur resunäy, di bexunosi, däz iyos zok’in ric’o, däz axʷay č’aɣˤirno roɣo, žubin boɣo.
translation: "In that case, after I died, fill the mug with my blood, take the bowel out of my belly and also take away my liver!

text: Łˤoran ƛˤeba na mi zowäƛin, esirno gula nesiq.
translation: "Where have you been these three years?", asked him the horse.

text: Ixiw berten bodin nesi užis, šet’ur yałruni ʕaƛozałäy xalq’in goƛ’in.
translation: He arranged the boy's big marriage and invited the people from the villages around.

text: Elo igoza ciqqo ečrut’ani xalq’imos qunno, beƛ’ˤes reqenno zown, amma žedus xexbi zownanu.
translation: In the neighboring forest, there was some old people's farmstead with a heard of sheep, but they didn't have a child.

text: Hudayziko esiw maħor oqno neła aždaħeł xizay.
translation: The next day the brother went outside in search of the dragon.

text: Tełerno ik’in, yisä esirno žeduq, di na uqˤäłru iyyä mežurƛin.
translation: He got into the house and asked them, "Do you know, where I had hidden?"

text: - elus maħ yołäsi migon nay t’or keze yoqäsi?”
translation: "And from where did you, having our spirit, get here?"

text: Nasin reƛ’ubin t’aħin ħaƛ’ala aħoziz ozäy ises c’iɣu ƛisin yoɣno.
translation: All the pairs [of buffalos] combined could hardly pull out the bull's shoulder.blade from the eye of the shepherd.

text: Esnabi razin boqno, nełqay łˤonot’a ɣˤurušno yisno, bazaryaɣor bik’in.
translation: The brothers agreed, took the three rubles from her and went to the market.

text: Sida obiws łˤono uži zown.
translation: A father had three sons.

text: Nesirno aħozizar bayru biłetow sayɣatno boyn, bazarganbi howt’aza boƛixnäy, beč’er žeduƛin harizin rodin, roc’no adäz łenon ħumukulibin ʕaƛaɣor esʷałno yedan ik’in.
translation: And he also gave to him the same present as to the shepherds who had come, and begged him: "When merchants appear over there, stop them!"; then he urged the five camels forward and hastily went into the village.

text: ʕOmoyn, mešin yołi tełxorno riħun, eƛnoqor rutäruni bix tatanaxorno roqno rac’xo zown.
translation: The donkey and the calf, however, sat down inside in the warmth and ate the hay that had been gathered for the winter.

text: Sida zamanałäy yiła yeda uži c’enno ʕiyayrno.
translation: At some point in time, she pinched the boy and made him cry.

text: Begirno xecin Bac’alä ziru.
translation: Batsali let the fox go and left.

text: Howži mix bik’äsi zaman yoł, sasaq bukaran ža debeq diƛin, babiya eƛin.
translation: "Now it's late, tomorrow I will show it to you.", said the father.

text: Hemełƛ’o xizyo bečedawni žek’uƛ’or xabar bayn, užä ganbin ruhun rodin, riƛubigon riƛin łiyrxoƛin.
translation: After that, the story that the boy taught the bulls to work and is now plowing all the fields, got to the rich man.

text: Q’ˤanon sadaq ʕaƛaɣor nexxo zown.
translation: The two went to the village together.

text: Šew mi žigon raxƛin, esirno žigon ʕomoyä.
translation: "What are you doing again now?", asked the donkey again.

text: Žedu ret’uranƛin yeda oqnosi, oc’ino q’ˤano q’ˤim yołali aždaħä yok’ek’no yižix sis bercinaw kid yukayn.
translation: When he was just about to pluck them, he saw a twelve-headed dragon, which had kidnapped a beautiful girl.

text: Šibaw ɣudeł bełiƛ’ ik’in dawla biqirinč’ey netikin ža xizor utix zownanu.
translation: Every day he went hunting and didn't return until he got prey.

text: Sida quł bełiquz užä eniwqor eƛin, howži bełiƛ’ ik’iłaniƛ’ dey rok’u rayxƛin.
translation: One day the hunter's son said to his mother, "Now I am convinced that I can go on a hunt."

text: Elay oqno ik’iƛ’oräy, yisir hunix xalq’i keze boqno.
translation: While he was getting away from there, he met some people on the way.

text: Nesä doxturqo esirno, debeq yedu kid saɣ yodäłäƛin.
translation: He asked doctor to cure his daughter.

text: Sida quł žoyä eniwqor eƛin, di eynoda ik’ix, nedur yaziqłiƛ’ netayrce eli bičanƛin.
translation: One day the son said to his mother: "I will go to work; how much longer will we live in poverty like this?"

text: Ħasan idu zown.
translation: Hassan was at home.

text: Elo yoƛi gogaƛ’ Okuƛin eƛixosi žek’u maħor oƛixno ičin.
translation: There in the middle of the meadow, a man appeared who called himself Oku.

text: Didurnokin yiła žedu sidxokin yik’anix roži teƛno anu, dey xediw yołƛin.
translation: However she did not agree to go to anyone of them: "I have a husband."

text: Nełaƛ’ ƛ’iräy ħošin boɣno, q’ˤunader boƛi bok’no.
translation: And they took away its hide and cut it into two halves.

text: Siskintow šebin rac’a riqinč’izaƛ’, xizor butin nexƛ’oräy, hawaƛ’ay sidaɣ ħonƛ’o ɣʷˤader simildis muhu bukayn.
translation: Because the crow didn't get anything to eat, it returned, and meanwhile it saw a maize grain on some mountain from the air.

text: Ža esin nesiq rok’äłruntow, xanä nukar bełiquz žader egirno, ža aɣin bown däder horoƛin, nesiq riyranix.
translation: Right after he told him that, the king sent the henchman to the hunter's son in order to explain to him, "Come to me and bring me that bird!"

text: Yet’un hečk’er k’oƛin eniya esirno, nazay mi t’or ayäƛin.
translation: The mother jumped up and asked: "Where did you come from here!"

text: Elay izin ik’in yeda, ʕazara ƛˤeba ik’in, ʕazara ɣudi ik’in, sida iħuz lilyoxor ayn.
translation: From there he got away and went on a thousand years and a thousand days and came to a riverbank.

text: Neširu beciƛ’ q’ˤanon maħorno boqno, t’akanno yisno užä eƛin, ulkałkin anunč’uruq’aw, xanbin, wazirbin bišxosi, riguƛ’ay rigu teł šebin yołäsi magazin roqoƛƛin.
translation: In the evening by moonlight, the two got outside and the boy took the glass and said: "May there be a shop where the best things are in, which the kings and viziers eat...."

text: Di äƛiru rodinosi, mežiz mada zam-zames kikno boƛixsi.
translation: After you did what I said, a holy spring appeared in your yard.

text: Magalun boynanu yisä.
translation: He also didn't make the bread.

text: Hemełƛ’o xizyo žoyä babiwq esin, ža miƛ’in di zows, gulun di zows, hemediw hunar yoł däz lagiłƛin.
translation: After that, the boy told his father: "That ram was me; and the horse was me; such a talent is in my body."

text: Sasaqor biča moči yołäƛin, esirnosi, neła eƛin, sasaqor biča močiƛa yoł, mežuƛ waħħo kuranix muži anuƛin.
translation: When they asked: "Is there a place [for us] to stay until tomorrow?", she said: "There is some place to stay until tomorrow, but there is no mattress to spread out under you."

text: ʕUmar, sanaʕałi boqno xizaz mi t’ay xexłiƛ’ oxi.
translation: "Umar, when the chance arises, quickly run away from here!

text: Howža yisiq eƛin rok’äłrungon žedu yisiƛ’or butin, eluqgon esinč’inäy, ela mi xaner teƛa yołƛin.
translation: When he had said that to him, they insisted: "When you don't tell us, we will give you to the king."

text: Din siħrus ʕilmu mołixosi yoł, retinäy dädertow egirƛin, eƛin Ox̌oyä.
translation: "I am also teaching the science of slyness; if you want, send him to me!", said Ohoyo.

text: Ža aɣes täqrun zira eƛin, debeq ža äsirus haqu yoč’ik’oƛƛin.
translation: After hearing what the bird said, the fox said: "May the mouth freeze to the one who told you that!"

text: Kidbä eƛin:
translation: The daughter said:

text: Žedu nexxo bukaynosi, yeda neł ixinoxorno oqno ʕuraw q’ʷaridno oqno ičin.
translation: When he saw them coming, he came next to that bag and became very sad.

text: Hi hudu deber nedur resunäyƛin, rok’no mač’an exurno eženi esiwdetow puräza taxeƛno eqerno akäza baɣmaɣorno yezun yičin.
translation: "So take [this], if that's the case!", and she hit and killed him with the dagger, put him under the ottoman to the oldest brother's side and waited looking out of the window into the garden.

text: Elo yisä tełer gondun yič’in, ƛ’irigon zozis ƛ’ˤun bodin, howlo kidno xecin, iduɣor oxin žan nexno.
translation: There inside he dug a hole, made a moss roof on it, left his daughter there and went back home.

text: Xanä eƛin:
translation: The king said:

text: Oč’ira beciƛ’azay, nełar bercinaw kid yodin, ruy nełas sistow sis ƛ’oq’ˤa ozuri zown.
translation: Nine months later, a beautiful daughter was born to her, just that she had only one eye on the forehead.

text: Roƛxosi ahli nesider režigon raq’u raya roqno.
translation: And more sick people began to come to him.

text: Ismaʕil äciruni ɣunoƛ’ ƛ’iri t’umin, nełas xexoyn heqno.
translation: On that tree where Ismail was tied, a pigeon and its chick sat down.

text: Babiya badiru wasimoxor, žedu netintow bełiƛ’ buq bätuzaɣort’a bik’ix zown.
translation: According to the testament that their father gave, they always went hunting in the west.

text: Nesis łˤono kid zown.
translation: He had three daughters.

text: Ža łˤoran esnazas esixosi šebin łiynosi, xanä diwanno beč’no ħumukulis bet’erhan eɣurno.
translation: After the three brothers finished telling the things, the king made the judgement that the camel's owner lost.

text: Istowlizaƛ’ anunč’uru niʕmatyabin zownč’u, dicegon rac’łin axʷarno raynč’u, dicegon zeru ħaƛułin q’ˤimłerno bizinč’u.
translation: There were no blisses that were [not] absent from the tables, so that, no matter how much one ate, they didn't come into the belly, and how much booze one drank, it didn't get up into the head.

text: Oƛƛozonägon eƛin:
translation: The middle one said:

text: Nesä eƛin, šida meža žar teƛxozo reƛił ƛuza xecäƛin.
translation: He said: "Why did you leave a bone in the meat that you gave your son?"

text: Yedu šebin nedur zawrułi där riyäsi zownč’u.
translation: I didn't know that things were like that.

text: Nar yedu yik’a yayxƛin, esnabi urɣelyozałer begäsi zown.
translation: "Where might she have gone to?", the brothers got desperate.

text: Idur ayrun, xediya baruqor eƛin, där ciqqo riynoxu esus, nesir c’ik’iw šebin riyxƛin.
translation: Right when he came home, the husband said to his wife: "I found a sheikh in the forest and he knows everything."

text: Meža mežur rätiru rodo, di sasaq maħor aqƛin, žeduqorno eƛin, saparyox ik’a ħadur oqno.
translation: "Do what you want, I will go out tomorrow.", he said to them and prepared to go on the journey.

text: Xizyo xanä q’ˤano äƛirugon hunar bukar mi däqƛin užiqor eƛin, sasaq däz gulude biħanadanix debi mi gulu baƛin.
translation: "Show me the second talent;", the king said to the boy afterwards, "bring your horse tomorrow in order to fight next to my horse!"

text: Di mi ɣurox egiranƛin odäsi zownč’u.
translation: I didn't give birth to you to have you pasture the cows.

text: Mow yoƛ’ˤaru ozuri yisis bat’yaw roqno, reƛ räč’runi k’onč’un, liɣˤonyayx zown.
translation: His eye, which shed the tear, changed, and the leg he cut the meat from, got lame.

text: Sida quł sis bełiqan idu biša rodan xecin, nasin ciqaɣor bik’in.
translation: One day, they all left one hunter at home to make food, and went into the forest.

text: Aħmadä riƛu riƛixozo žek’uq esirno, žiƛbosiyä, eƛ’isiyä yedu mi riƛu riƛixƛin.
translation: Akhmed asked the man who was ploughing the field: "Are you ploughing this year's or last year's field?"

text: Elo yisir imyoq bicäsi q’ˤim anusi esiws žanaza bukayn.
translation: There he saw the headless corpse of his brother tied to a pole.

text: Łˤono äƛiru t’alamzo čardaħäɣor oqnosi, raɣiƛ aɣzas šeƛ’u ƛ’iräy roɣxo łˤonon bercinawt’a kidbabi rukayn.
translation: When he came to the balcony on the third floor, he saw three beautiful girls undressing of birds' clothes under the veranda.

text: Asaqozagon łˤono henešno bayrsi, sis yedu kesu urɣizi badirur, sis äsirur, sideni teqärur.
translation: And the people from Asakh brought three apples; one from the one who invented the story, one for the one who told this story and another one for the one, who listened to the story.

text: Yiłaz babiwr čuqˤno, ža ɣˤʷay esiranix xanes uži neła kidbä rok’uƛ’ esärułi.
translation: Her father noticed that the daughter asked on her heart(?) that the prince should ask for the dog.

text: Neširuzoƛ’ ʕaƛa šibawz ɣˤutkoz akä neł ɣˤanaza näƛru muhumabin eqerno, eɣeni esiw iduɣor ik’in.
translation: In the evening the younger brother put the grains that woman had given to him into the windows of each house and went home.

text: Howži deber retinäy bexad, retinč’inäy bexaynƛin, eƛin riynoxa mešorłäy.
translation: Now if you want to, then slaughter it, and if you don't want to, don't slaughter it!", said the sheikh about the heifer.

text: Łˤoran ƛˤeba yeda elo kecno ƛexux.
translation: And he remained there sleeping for three years.

text: Łi teƛƛ’oräy, kidbez bašił nesir nesätow barur sayɣat radiruni bašiqˤoy rukayn.
translation: When he gave her the water, he saw on the girl's finger the ring that he himself had given to his wife as a present.

text: Rok’un q’ʷarid roqno ʕAliqilič ʕiyada iħun.
translation: His heart became sad and Aliqilich started to cry.

text: K’uruč’ k’eč’ ɣudi rocu, buq bay.
translation: All's well that end's well.

text: Łoxnon łox nesä nełaz ɣˤul caƛin, sosikin c’oxinč’izaƛ’, aždaħä Ħusen gulunnantow sadaq igin kurno.
translation: Three times in a row he threw the stone at it and because he didn't hit it, the dragon swallowed Hussein along with his horse.

text: Ɣˤaɣˤun yizin ža xabar esanix eniw bečedawni maduhalzaxar yik’in.
translation: The mother was happy and went to tell the wealthy neighbors about this.

text: ʕOloqan užiƛ’orgon utin ƛ’irir Okun ayn.
translation: Oku turned into a young boy and came upstairs.

text: Roƛƛ’o q’ˤanogon anƛ’ rik’inosi, hemedurtow riyrinč’ey rekan riqirno, dice reƛ ƛexunƛin xal bodinosi, sida quł rac’ace ƛexasi resun.
translation: After two weeks had passed, he took the key away without letting her know in the same way, and took a look how much meat would remain, and he found that it would remain to eat for one day.

text: Ɣudes neširuxay elay sist’a heneš kamäsi besux.
translation: Daily in the evening one apple disappears.

text: Buyxor laga baynosi, bełiqanä žeduq esirno, mežuƛ’ ci šebiƛin.
translation: When he came to senses, the hunter asked them: "What're your names?"

text: Aɣzas ža xabar teqnosi, Alimaħamas qoqoƛu regin.
translation: When Alimagomed heard the birds talking, he bursted into laughter.

text: Idur aynosi užä nesä nesis esiy guluƛ’no zowerno ciqaɣor yižin, hudun rok’u gurħizi roqzaƛ’, yexurnoanu.
translation: When the son came home, he seated his own sister onto a horse and led her into the forest, but because his heart felt sorry, he didn't kill her.

text: Mi radiru łinaƛ’no di razi yołƛin, doxturän eƛin.
translation: "I fully agree with you," the doctor said.

text: Doxturno neła q’ut’oƛ’ razi oqno.
translation: The doctor agreed with it.

text: Digon ayäsi elo žeduz bertenyołer nesi ʕAliqiličä där łeno ukru neƛsi yedu kesubi caxxosi oqzaƛ’.
translation: And I came to their marriage as well, and Aliqilich gave me 5 silver (coins), because I am writing down those tales.

text: ƛ’ˤuƛ’or bizin bezunosi, elo yizir gondu yesun, elo tełer bezunosi, aždaħ bukayn.
translation: They got up onto the roof, had a look and found a big hole; when they looked into it, they saw the dragon.

text: Suƛƛitow zira aħi boyn, haxar, et’o teł žek’u yołƛin.
translation: Immediately the fox raised an alarm: "Now, there is a man inside here!"

text: Eɣeni esiya, när meži bik’äsi zowäƛin žeduq esirłin, žeda esix zownanu, ža debi iš anu, ħažat anuru šebin eluq esärnoƛin.
translation: Although the younger brother asked them: "Where are you going?", they didn't tell him [but said]: "It's not your business, don't ask us unnecessary things!"

text: Howlaytow yiza aħi begirno elo igo yałru ʕaƛołer.
translation: From there they alarmed the nearby village.

text: Bełiqanä žeduq didur bikorä nesiz gugya kuła rayrułäy esin.
translation: The hunter told them how the snake deceived him.

text: Elo yiła xaner bokon, k’ukno baqˤun.
translation: There she sewn a fur coat and a cap for the king.

text: Zirulaw, mi šebi rac’xoƛin?”
translation: "My dear fox, what are you eating?"

text: Elo idu besun iłno užin, mežä k’ʷač’nox yiziräsi ɣˤanabin.
translation: There at home they found six sons and in the bed a groaning woman.

text: Hudun meža di berten bodinč’ey xecinč’u.
translation: "Nevertheless you all didn't let me *not* have a marriage.

text: K’uruč’ k’eč’, ɣudi rocu, buq bay.
translation: All's well that ends well.

text: Žubi bac’näy, debi ʕumru bexoräł, č’aɣˤir ašuƛ ricinäy, mi q’uwataw aq, däz lagiłäy boƛäxru zok’ice iyo lagiq biħinäy, mi łarnokin ukayxanusi aq.
translation: When you eat my liver, your life will become longer; if you tie my bowel around your waist, you will become strong; and when you smear blood from my body from the mug on your body, nobody will see you.

text: Hemeł qułtow ečruni ɣˤanaza naysinan ʕaƛozał xabar begirno, däde idu aq’iw kid yołƛin.
translation: On this day, the old woman spread the story everywhere in among the villages: "I have an alien girl at home!"

text: Neła eƛin, aždaħ bexaruni uži elzox yołƛin.
translation: She said: "The boy who killed the dragon is living at our place."

text: K’uruč’ k’eč’ ɣudi rocu buq bay.
translation: All's well that ends well.

text: Reɣet’a ƛuzazas ric’äsi ɣutkutow rukayn elo yisir.
translation: There he saw that the room was filled with small bones.

text: Mi dey č’iħo rac’iyäƛin, esirno xizyogon nełä.
translation: "Did you eat my fruits?", she asked again.

text: Yisän eli baƛ’irsiƛin, elaygon bitor bazarganbi hunix boqno, waranibin rižin äk’iru žaz ʕaƛaɣorno bezun.
translation: "He cheated us as well," the merchants [said and] got on their way away from their and looked for the village of the boy who went leading the camels.

text: Q’orolay žaz idurno yik’in, nesir yiynč’ey neširu ža kecxozo taxeƛno yuqˤełno, ʕenekizi yoqno yičin.
translation: The widow went to the boy's home, without him knowing, and at night hid under his bed while he was sleeping, and listened.

text: Neła quł hudayziko sasaqasi zaman baynosi, žedur bišanix retin, hudun Q’arumä nesiqosini biša ader roƛik’noanu.
translation: A day later, when the morning time came, they wanted to eat, but Qarum didn't take out the food that he had.

text: Hawaƛ’no, raładyołno, raƛ’moƛ’no žeda di esursi, ana debi q’ˤuya ħilla- makruƛin, esirno užä.
translation: "They have found me in the air, in the sea and on the earth, do you have another trick?" the boy asked.

text: Xizyo xanä eƛin:
translation: Then the king said:

text: Łox äƛirugon boc’ä rečuƛxor qˤaƛin, qˤaƛanayxosi, di tełxor begirƛin.
translation: For the third time, the wolf shouted into the cattleshed: "Shout, let me inside!"

text: Zownƛax zown anuƛax sis ɣˤanabi.
translation: Once upon a time there was a woman.

text: Hudayƛ’aygon hemedurtow ročyoxno icin egirnosi, ƛ’iri yałruni bełiqanzas q’ut’i boqno, žigoƛ’ay roč k’ek’erłin ela ža izirač’inƛin.
translation: The next day[time?], when they let him [down], tied to the rope, the hunters that were above, agreed: "When he moves the rope again, we won't lift him up."

text: Sida ečru žek’ur ciqqo qacis łˤono hoko t’ät’irus łˤono ɣˤuruš yiqäsi zows, hudun iduɣor ayƛ’oräy, bikori bexurxo ʕoloqbin besun, ža begirno xecaniƛ žedur łˤonon ɣˤuruš teƛsi diƛin, esin žoyä.
translation: "I got three rubles for felling three carriages full of firewood for an old man; but while I was coming home, I found some young people who were killing a snake and I gave them the three rubles for letting go of it.", he told.

text: Neła babiwqor eƛin, howža best’alawni esiw yexura yay, ža č’agun yičin dey dunyalƛ’o netikin taliħ boqa anuƛin.
translation: She said to her father: "You must kill this stepsister, when she is alive, I will not get happy again ever in my world."

text: Diwanbegä nesiqor eƛin, mi iłet’a ʕorƛ’ilawbi bižin iħuxay eč’ibi rutirƛin.
translation: The judge said to him: "Bring some fine men just like you and gather pebbles at the river."

text: Xanä aƛirun rodin, nukar bełiquzaxar ik’in.
translation: The henchman did what the king said and went to the hunters.

text: Meži di bižan mežuq yałru kinaw micxir där neƛnäyƛin.
translation: "I will take you with me, if you give me all the money you have."

text: Maduhalłiq yałru xande ħalt’oł ža zown: nesis azbarno c’unzi rodin.
translation: He was working at the neighbouring king: He guarded his palace.

text: Išamt’a bisxo caƛin, łoxnon łox c’oxerno.
translation: Taking aim each time he threw, he hit it three times in a row.

text: Ceyä eƛin, hoboy mi däƛ’ ƛ’irin zow, elor bayrun mi q’ut’u bic’no, di xexłiƛ’ xizor butanixƛin.
translation: The eagle said: "Ride on me now, so that I can return quickly, right after I come to there and you filled the jug!"

text: Sis zown xan.
translation: There was a king.

text: Mi eli baƛ’irxoƛin, bełiqanbi bužzi boqnoanu nesä äsiru łinaƛ’no.
translation: "You're cheating us!", the hunters didn't trust in what he had told them.

text: K’ox äƛizayn hemedurtow xalq’imon ħaƛun Zeyes Aħyaq idur zok’ice dandi łi rayn.
translation: Also the second time the people drank it up likewise, and Bear Ear could only bring enough water for a mug home.

text: Way, dey užiƛin, eƛin yeženä, eluz xanez kidbes anƛ’imasi berten boyx, nesi baħarawx di yik’ixanuƛin yedu yičäsi.
translation: "Oh my son!", said the old woman, "They're celebrating our king's daughter's marriage the whole week, she was like: 'I won't go to that fiancé!'."

text: Sida quł ža t’o ʕaƛay ik’inosi, där žek’ude kecan retin di debez babiwz laɣde kecsi.
translation: One day, after he had left this village, I wanted to sleep with a man, and so I slept with your father's slave.

text: Łinaqey ža ʕiyayxƛin žas eniya esirnosi, yisir dowqosi pardagi retinƛin eƛin.
translation: When the boy's mother asked: "Why does he cry?", she said: "He wants your scarf."

text: Qˤišodes zaman baynosin hemedurtow ičin Q’arum.
translation: And when the time for dinner came, Qarum was likewise.

text: Žeda žedude sadaq, aħoziz ozzäy landi roɣʷa, qura oc’ira gamušyozas reƛ’un rižun.
translation: They came together with 200 buffalos, to remove the splinter from the shepherd's eye.

text: Łinaƛ’notow raħmu bisxosi xexbi zown ža.
translation: He was a boy who had mercy with everything.

text: Eženi žek’a mołin, eloš gugu caƛoƛin.
translation: The old man explained: "Throw your back over there!"

text: Šebi mežar roqäsi, łinaq meži ʕiyayxƛin?”, - esirno yisä.
translation: "Whay happened to you, what are you crying about?", he asked.

text: Xanä q’ʷaridno oqno wˤałer rˤoƛ’arzay q’ˤimkin rizirxo zownanu, t’ok’ow roda šebin koƛ’inč’ey.
translation: The king became sad and while he hung his head, he didn't lift it up again and didn't know what to do anymore.

text: Di debeq riyrno xecix, hemeł reƛiłäy encekin šebin žek’ur riqinäy, daru baqinč’iƛin.
translation: I warn you; if anyone else will get even a little piece of that meat, the medicine won't work.

text: Žeduł tełäy hič’č’a ʕaq’ilawƛin ci rik’äsi zown Kusaƛin ci yołäsi sida wizirłäy.
translation: Among them, the one who was famous as the most intelligent was a vizier with the name Kusa.

text: Yisiqor žan teƛno, ziru ozƛäy gugin.
translation: The fox gave it to him and disappeared.

text: ʕOloqanawni žoyä didiwkin nesir žawab teƛnoanu.
translation: The young boy didn't give him any answer.

text: Dandir c’axru bazarganza esirno, ža tupi teƛxosi ana miƛin.
translation: Some merchants whom he met asked him: "Aren't you selling this rifle?"

text: Hemedurtow din bˤuƛ’si, mi däƛ’or c’eruc’a rutirnosiƛin, eƛin ceyä.
translation: "I was afraid likewise, when you turned the bow unto me.", said the eagle.

text: Užä žawab teƛno, meža aħinyoqorno icingon di iħułxor kursi yołi, bežigon ixiw reqen där bäqiƛin.
translation: The boy answered: "If you had thrown me into the river tied to a rock, then I would've gotten an even bigger flock."

text: Hemedur miskinłiƛ’no, best’alłiƛ’no sisxoli eniwde aq’ˤiłno zawruni žoyä, xanno muk’ur oyn, nesis kidno yown, ixiw bertenno bodin.
translation: That's how the boy who grew up in poverty and orphanhood by his single mother persuaded the king, married his daughter and prepared a big marriage.

text: Ħon-bahadurä rok’no mač’an łˤonon bikoris q’ˤim reč’no kurno.
translation: Mountain~Hero beated with his dagger and chopped off the tree heads of the snake.

text: Eloša raładyołsi aluk’ani gagalin betin yeda ʕiyayxƛin”.
translation: He is crying because he want's to have that white flower over there in the sea.

text: Nesä Bilq’isdiqor eƛin, mi däx nexa yayƛin.
translation: He said to Bilqisdi: "You must come with me."

text: Hemedurtow nesiƛ’or nełas eniwn yutin, mi ža kid yexurinč’inäy, dey di kidno yižin dowdäy yoxa yołƛin.
translation: Her mother likewise turned to him: "If you don't kill the girl, I will take my daughter with me and run away from you."

text: Xizaɣor hudun yisä sis sanaʕałin biqirno suƛƛitow nasin q’ˤimbin reč’no, aždaħ bexurno łaƛ’ay boɣno kurno.
translation: Finally nevertheless he took a chance, cut off all the heads at once, killed the dragon and threw it off the water.

text: ʕAƛos ahli ziyaratyoƛ’t’a xizyo bik’ix zown žeduz šudaƛ’or.
translation: And the people of the village were coming to visit their grave.

text: Mesedyos mač’an, ašunin babiya raqˤza yoł.
translation: The golden dagger and the belt are where your father hid them.

text: Kʷaxatow xizyo baruq regin, xec di yiča yegirno, t’ok’ow esirač’inƛin.
translation: Soon from his wife could be heard: "Enough, let it be, I won't ask anymore!"

text: Xecač’inƛin inkar bodin žeda, bexurinč’ey xeca betinäy, łˤono ɣuruš neƛ elurƛin boqno bičin yizi.
translation: "We won't leave it.", they rejected, "If you want us not to kill it, give us three rubles!".

text: Zownƛax zownanuƛax ader zamanał Ceƛ’o sida ʕaƛa Ražbadinƛin ci rik’äsi bahadur.
translation: Once upon a long time ago in the Tsetlo village there was a hero who went by the name of "Razhbadin".

text: Hudu mi nedurä zown, k’ačaɣˤƛin, bizirno bok’no meč’oƛäyrcen cucik’no, mač’an roɣno maħor bexuranixƛin oqno yeda.
translation: "You meant like this, you bandit?", Aliqilich lifted him, and beat him in up to his neck, then drew his dagger and was about to kill the dragon.

text: Že kid ełu yut’ix soder yizixno yičin, kidbazadäy yok’ełno.
translation: While the girl collected bilberries and got higher, she got lost from the girls.

text: Sida mixƛ’o moƛaxay Ražbadin č’ariłxo.
translation: At some point in time, Razhbadin woke up from his sleep.

text: Nełas łˤono kid zown.
translation: He had five daughters.

text: Di mečołxor yik’isƛin.
translation: I went to the field."

text: Mesedyos saħno, t’asno, reƛ’iqoyn, pandurno boɣno xanez kidbeqay, xanägon nesi žax žan yegirno.
translation: The king took away the sah of gold, the washbowl, the glove and the lute, and sent it back to the boy.

text: Assalamu ʕalaykumƛin salamno teƛno, babiwderno, žaderno dandirno nexno nesä esirno, meža sapar naɣor bicäsi yołƛin.
translation: "Peace be with you!", he greeted and came with the father and his son and asked: "Where are you heading?"

text: Roƛno mežiƛ’or ik’inosi, yisi ʕUmarqiličä ʕIsaqiličqor eƛin:
translation: After he became sick and went to bed, Umarqilich said to Isaqilich:

text: Howži q’ʷˤaɣˤƛin bezun bitor kurno anu reƛ, q’ʷˤaɣˤƛin bezun ninor et’no anu iyo.
translation: Now when he screaked and looked to the one side, he didn't throw in any meat, and when he screaked and looked to the other side, he didn't pour in any blood.

text: Deberno p’es anusi bičinäy, rigu räčiƛin, - eƛin yeda tungi bic’a iħun.
translation: It will be good also for you to be quiet", he said and came to fill the jug.
